name: Skyrim Script Extender (SKSE64)
summary: The Skyrim Script Extender (SKSE) is a tool used by many Skyrim mods that expands scripting capabilities and adds additional functionality to the game.
description:
The Skyrim Script Extender (SKSE) is a tool used by many Skyrim mods that expands scripting capabilities and adds additional functionality to the game.

Once installed, no additional steps are needed to launch Skyrim with SKSE's added functionality. You can start the game using SKSE from skse64_loader.exe.

[b]Compatibility: SKSE64 supports the releases of Skyrim on Steam and GOG. Other stores (EGS, Windows Store, Game Pass) are not supported.[/b]

[size=4]Installation (Manual)[/size]
[list]
[*]Download the SKSE Build for your game version from the Files tab.
[*]Extract all files inside the top-level folder in the ZIP to your game folder (where Skyrim.exe is located).
[*]Run the game using skse64_loader.exe.
[*]To confirm it is working, open the console with the tilde (`) key and type [i]getskseversion[/i]. This will display the version number of the current SKSE build. 
[/list]


[size=4]Install with Vortex[/size]
[list]
[*]Click the "Vortex" button in the top-right of this page.
[*]Once installed and enabled, ensure you have deployed it by clicking "Deploy Mods" on the Mods toolbar.
[*]Use the shortcut on the dashboard to start the game with SKSE64.
[/list]
author: SKSE Team

name: SSE Engine Fixes (skse64 plugin)
summary: skse64 plugin to fix various issues with the Skyrim Special Edition engine.
description:
[center][b][size=6]Engine Fixes for Skyrim Special Edition[/size][/b][/center]
A WIP skse64 plugin for Skyrim Special Edition to fix bugs and patch issues.

The majority of current work on this plugin (5.0+) is being done by Ryan, who deserves more credit than you can imagine :^)

[size=4][b][center]Installation[/center][/b][/size]
[list]
[*]Install the Part 1 zip as you would any SKSE plugin, be that through your mod manager or directly to your data folder.
[/list] ﻿The "_preload.txt" file is required to be in the directory if you want to use the memory manager patch, so if your Mod Manager warns you about it, make sure it gets added.[list]
[*]Install the Part 2 zip by extracting it to your Skyrim SE folder (the three dlls should be alongside SkyrimSE.exe, etc)
[/list]

For configuration see the INI Settings below. The defaults should be stable for all users.

[color=#ff7700][b]If you prefer SSE Fixes' version of the same fixes to this one, you can install them together and Engine Fixes will automatically disable its conflicting patches :)
[/b][/color]

[b][size=4][center]INI Settings/Configuration[/center][/size][/b]
[b][size=3]EngineFixes[/size][/b]
[list]
[*]VerboseLogging (default: false) - Enables more verbose logging in the log file.
[*]CleanSKSECosaves (default: true) - Deletes SKSE cosaves with no matching save on launch.
[/list]
[b][size=3]Patches[/size][/b]
[list]
[*]DisableChargenPrecache (default: false) - "Precache Killer" function, duplicated from RaceMenu.
[*]EnableAchievementsWIthMods (default: true) - Enables achievements when you have mods installed.
[*]FormCaching (default: true) - Caches recently accessed forms. Similar to SSE Fixes.
[*]MaxStdio (default: true) - Raises max file handle limit from 512 to 2048, which should fix "False Save Corruption" bug in most cases.
[*]RegularQuicksaves (default: false) - Makes quick saves use the regular save handler.
[*]SaveAddedSoundCategories (default: true) - Saves volume settings for mod-added sound categories to a separate ini file. This functionality is the same as Audio Overhaul for Skyrim SE's dll, but they will not conflict.
[*]ScrollingDoesntSwitchPOV (default: false) - Makes it so using your scroll wheel won't swap between 1st/3rd person. See LE mod [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrim/mods/95800]here[/url]﻿
[*]SleepWaitTime (default: false) - Lets you change the time it takes to sleep or wait. See LE mod [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrim/mods/95796]here[/url]﻿
[*]SleepWaitTimeModifier (default: 0.3) - The in-game default time is 1.0; smaller values speed up the sleep/wait time, higher values slow it down
[*]TreeLODReferenceCaching (default: true) - Requires FormCaching. Fixes the very slow Tree LOD function that causes framerate drops most noticeable in Riften. Similar to SSE Fixes.
[*]WaterflowAnimation (default: true) - Decouples water flow animation from in-game timescale, so that decreasing/increasing your timescale doesnt mess with the water animation speed.
[*]WaterflowSpeed (default: 20.0) - Sets the waterflow speed for the animation patch. The default of 20 is equal to the default timescale, and is probably OK unless you want the water to appear slower/faster for some reason.
[/list]
[b][size=3]Fixes[/size][/b]
[list]
[*]ArcheryDownwardAiming (default: true) - Fix a bug where projectiles sometimes don't fire properly if you're aiming downward. Ported from Cobb's LE fix (see [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrim/articles/52111]here[/url])
[*]AnimationLoadSignedCrash (default: true) - Fix an improper unsigned/signed conversion. Should allow loading more animations before a CTD. [b]If you use [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/31146]Animation Limit Crash Fix SSE[/url] I suggest disabling this to use that mod's fix instead.[/b]
[*]BethesdaNetCrash (default: true) - Fixes a crash on startup caused by improper handling of non-ASCII characters in Bethesda.net HTTP response headers.
[*]BSLightingAmbientSpecular (default: true) - Fixes a bug where lighting template Directional Ambient Specular & Fresnel Power are broken
[*]BSLightingShaderForceAlphaTest (default: true) - Forces alpha testing in the lighting shader. Fixes object LOD reflections on water.
[*]BSLightingShaderParallaxBug (default: true) - Fixes a bug with parallax lighting shaders
[*]CalendarSkipping (default: true) - Fixes the bug listed under Notes on [url=https://www.creationkit.com/index.php?title=GetCurrentGameTime_-_Utility]this page[/url]
[*]DoublePerkApply (default: true) - Fixes a bug where NPCs in the player's cell will have their perk effects applied twice on game load
[*]EquipShoutEventSpam (default: true) - Fixes a bug where the 'Equip Shout' script would fire a shout equipped event even if the equip failed.
[*]GetKeywordItemCount (default: false) - Fixes the condition function "GetKeywordItemCount" returning incorrect values in some situations.
[*]GHeapLeakDetectionCrash (default: true) - Fixes a crash where scaleform attempts to report a memory leak but the code doesnt exist in Skyrim
[*]LipSync (default: true) - Fixes the lip sync bug, same as LE bug fixes.
[*]MemoryAccessErrors (default: true) - Fixes a handful of out-of-bounds or use-after-free bugs. Required for experimental memory patches.
[*]MO5STypo (default: true) - Fixes a typo in the ARMA (Armor Addon) form loader preventing 1st person female alternate texture sets from being loaded.
[*]PerkFragmentIsRunning (default: true) - See [url=https://github.com/SniffleMan/MiscFixesSSE/blob/master/README.md]Misc Fixes SSE's readme[/url]﻿ for details.
[*]RemovedSpellBook (default: true) - See [url=https://github.com/SniffleMan/MiscFixesSSE/blob/master/README.md]Misc Fixes SSE's readme[/url]﻿ for details.
[*]SaveScreenshots (default: true) - Fixes save game screenshots being blank when TAA is disabled.
[*]SlowTimeCameraMovement (default: true) - Fixes camera movement being slow during slow time effects.
[*]TreeReflections (default: true) - Fixes tree LOD reflections. No conflict with ENB.
[*]UnequipAllCrash (default: true) - Fixes a crash when invoking "Actor.UnequipAll" on an actor without an AI process.
[*]VerticalLookSensitivity (default: true) - Makes vertical look sensitivity not tied to framerate.
[*]WeaponBlockScaling (default: true) - Fixes weapon blocking so it [url=https://en.uesp.net/wiki/Skyrim:Block#Skill_Usage]correctly scales off of the blocking actor's weapon[/url].
[/list][b]
[b][size=3]Warnings[/size][/b]
[/b][list]
[*]DupeAddonNodes (default: true)
[/list]
ADDN (Addon Node) forms have a node index that must be unique across your entire load order. A warning box will pop up if you have a conflict in your load order, and the conflicting mods will be logged to your Engine Fixes log file. To resolve this, you need to contact the mod author(s) whose mods have conflicts, and one of them will have to change which node index they are using. Please be courteous :)

[list]
[*]RefHandleLimit (default: true)
[*]RefrMainMenuLimit (default: 800000)
[*]RefrLoadGameLimit (default: 1000000)
[/list]

For detailed information on this engine limitation, see [url=https://www.reddit.com/r/skyrimmods/comments/ag4wm7/psa_the_reference_handle_cap_or_diagnosing_one_of/]my reddit post[/url]﻿.

This will pop up a warning box if your reference handle limit is above the configured warning levels at your main menu and whenever you load a save game.

Over the course of a 100+ hour game session, it is easily possible to build a large reference count in your save game, so the Main Menu limit is by default configured to 800k, which is 250k~ below the actual limit. If you don't intend to play long saves you can probably safely raise this warning limit.

The load game limit is set to 1 million (48k below the limit). If your save game is getting dangerously close to the limit it is possible to reduce the references stored in it using [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/5031/]ReSaver[/url]﻿, but keep in mind this can cause things in your game world to disappear/respawn depending on your choices.

Currently, the only solution to this issue is to either remove non-master plugins with large reference counts from your load order, or turn them into master plugins.[b]

[b][size=3]Experimental[/size][/b]
[/b][list]
[*]CellInit (default: true) - Fixes a rare crash where a form does not get converted from an id to a pointer.
[*]MemoryManager (default: false) - Disables built-in memory manager for the game's largest heaps. This is the famous "Use OS Allocators" patch
from LE.[*]UseTBBMalloc (default: true) - If you enable the memory manager patch, this will also replace standard malloc with tbbmalloc. This does nothing if the memory manager patch is disabled.
[*]SaveGameMaxSize (default: false) - Expands maximum save game size from 64 MB (uncompressed) to 128 MB. If you have a long playthrough and your game starts crashing when you try to save, this might fix it. Marked experimental because I can't test it myself, but is most likely safe.
[/list][b]

[b][size=4][center]Credits[/center][/size][/b]
[/b]Nukem -  more stuff than I can mention
Sniffleman/Ryan - Misc Fixes + CommonLibSSE 
meh321 - research into tree LOD function (SSE fixes), bugfixes LE & port permissions
sheson - skse plugin preloader for LE alongside meh
himika - scatter table implementation from libskyrim (LE), plus tons of research function/variable names
kassent - useful information from the source code of various skse plugins 
Kole6738 - cosave cleaner idea+code
LStewieAL - Things ported from [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrim/users/2232669?tab=user+files&BH=0]here[/url][b]

[/b][center][b][size=4]Source & Contributing[/size][/b][/center]
Source is available on [url=https://github.com/aers/EngineFixesSkyrim64]Github[/url]﻿. While I intend to look into some other known bugs, no promises. If you feel like tracking down a bug and theorizing a fix or even implementing one yourself, feel free to contribute; I'm willing to maintain it for future game updates.

Preloader can be found [url=https://github.com/aers/skse64_plugin_preloader]here[/url]﻿.
tbb.dll and tbbmalloc.dll are Intel's [url=https://www.threadingbuildingblocks.org/]Threaded Building Blocks[/url]﻿ library.
author: aers - Nukem - Ryan

name: RaceMenu
summary: Complete overhaul to the character creation menu including new customization features such as multiple RGBA warpaints, body paints, hand paint, and foot paints. (Requires SKSE)
description:
[center][url=https://www.patreon.com/bePatron?u=5334755][img]https://c5.patreon.com/external/logo/become_a_patron_button.png[/img][/url][/center]
Due to popular request, this is a version of RaceMenu that is feature equivalent to the alpha. All non-functional features have been disabled via setting. Features that do not work are denoted by "non-functional". There is no ETA on a feature-complete version.

[center][size=5][color=#ff0000][b]REQUIREMENTS[/b]﻿[/color][/size][/center]
The [url=http://skse64.silverlock.org/]Skyrim Script Extender (SKSE64)[/url], Version [color=orange]2.0.7[/color] (or newer matching the RaceMenu version) is [b]REQUIRED[/b] to run RaceMenu SE.

If you get mismatching version warnings, make sure to delete the following loose files from your data folder if they exist:
[list]
[*]CharGen.pex
[*]NiOverride.pex
[*]RaceMenu.pex
[*]RaceMenuBase.pex
[*]RaceMenuLoad.pex
[*]RaceMenuPlugin.pex
[/list]
Additionally, make sure that you installed SKSE and the SKSE base script files correctly.

Non-functional in RaceMenu SSE
[s]For ECE to RaceMenu migration see:
[/s][list][*][url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrim/mods/61336/?][size=2][s]CharGen Export[/s][/size][/url]
[s][/*][/s][/list][color=#5EA2E5][center][size=5][b]PURPOSE[/b][/size][/center][/color]
The RaceMenu was redesigned with the intention of using SKSE to allow for more in-depth customization of your character. This mod does not fix the problems with the internal class to this menu; it merely redesigns it and adds more features. Please ensure that you don't have problems opening the ORIGINAL RaceMenu before blaming this mod.

[color=#5EA2E5][center][size=5][b]FEATURES[/b][/size][/center][/color]
[color=#5EA2E5]RaceMenu.esp[/color]
[list][*]Numeric display for all sliders[/*]
[*]Categorizes all the vanilla options to the SkyUI look[/*]
[*]Search filter by name [color=#7C7C7C](Same as SkyUI)[/color][/*]
[*]Color ANY tint, including hair with an AARRGGBB value[/*]
[*]Loads/Saves settings when re-opening the menu[/*]
[*]Light On/Off [color=#7C7C7C](Spawns a white light directly infront of your character)[/color][/*]
[*]Zoom distances adjusted to be closer to the face when In and slightly further when out[/*]
[*]Basic plugin interface[/*]
[*]Controller support[/*]
[*]Works with any race, including custom races[/*]
[*]Doesn't modify any vanilla assets [color=#7C7C7C](Unlike my Extended Slider Colors mod)[/color][/*]
[*]Multi-warpaint support[/*]
[*]Warpaint texture hotswapping [color=#7C7C7C](Custom warpaint)[/color][/*]
[*]Display racial skill bonuses[/*]
[*]Save/Load presets to and from windows clipboard[/*]
[*]Supports body/hand/feet[s]/face[/s] overlays[/*]
[*]Save/Load color[/*]
[*]Change overlay glow color[/*]
[*]Change overlay glow strength[/*]
[*]Export head mesh[/*]
[*]Save/Load preset to and from a slot file [color=#7C7C7C](Requires CharGen v2.0.0 or greater and replaces clipboard save/load)[/color][/*]
[*]Show HeadPart name and source mod [color=#7C7C7C](Requires CharGen v2.1.2 or greater)[/color][/*]
[*]Camera move up/down/left/right zoom in/out[/*]
[/list]
[color=#5EA2E5]RaceMenuPlugin.esp[/color] [color=#7C7C7C](Optional)[/color]
[list][*]Adjust player height[/*]
[*]Adjust player bicep size[/*]
[*]Adjust player glute size [color=#7C7C7C](Only works for meshes that support skeletal scaling e.g. TBBP body meshes)[/color][/*]
[*]Adjust player breast size [color=#7C7C7C](Only works for meshes that support skeletal scaling e.g. BBP body meshes)[/color][/*]
[*]Adjust player breast curve size [color=#7C7C7C](Only works for meshes that support skeletal scaling e.g. TBBP body meshes)[/color][/*]
[/list]

[color=#5EA2E5][size=5][b]BodyGen randomize[/b][/size][/color]
There is now new syntax to the previous feature to allow randomization of all actors of a particular gender.
To setup an initial BodyGen file you need to create two ini files at:
meshes/actors/character/BodyGenData/%MOD_FILENAME_WITH_EXT%/
morphs.ini
templates.ini
The templates file outlines BodyMorphs by name
Here is a sample:
[color=#7C7C7C]Sevenbase=7B High@1.0 | 7B Bombshell High@1.0 | 7B Natural High@1.0 | 7B Cleavage High@1.0 | 7B Bcup High@1.0
Breasts=BreastSH@1.0 | BreastSSH@2.0, Breast@1.0, BreastCleavage@0.1:1.0, BreastGravity@0.1:1.0[/color]

The morphs file defines what morphs should be available to what ActorBases, here is an example:
[color=#7C7C7C]Skyrim.esm|F62F0=Sevenbase,Breasts[/color]

For the new All feature, this would say:
[color=#7C7C7C]All|Female=Sevenbase,Breasts[/color]

Make sure you have empty new lines at the end of your ini files.
If you have explicit overrides in other mods they will overwrite by load order, so if you have all in an earlier mod, then have one explicit in a later mod, the ActorBase will use the later setting.

[color=#5EA2E5][center][size=5][b]Equippable Transforms[/b][/size][/center][/color]

What are Equippable Transforms? They are essentially additional data that can be attached onto Armor that will apply an NiTransform internally to the skeleton. What would you use this for? Replacing High Heels of course! The transform is also done natively so it's near instant application.
 
How do you use it? Simple, add an NiStringExtraData named "SDTA" without quotes (Skeleton Data) to your NiTriShape or NiTriStrips of the armor you want to apply it to (You may need to apply to both _0 and _1)
 
The "String Data" will be a JSON formatted string, here is a sample:
[color=#7C7C7C][
{"name":"NPC L Breast","scale":2.0,"pos":[0,0,0],"rot":[0,0,0]},
{"name":"NPC R Breast","scale":2.0,"pos":[0,0,0],"rot":[0,0,0]}
][/color]
The "name" field is the name of the node you are transforming
The "pos" field is a relative translation of the node you are transforming
The "rot" field is a rotation in degress in euler angles (heading, attitude, bank)
 
Here is a sample for replacing High Heels
[color=#7C7C7C][{"name":"NPC","pos":[0, 0, 5.0]}][/color]
You may need to tweak the units as I just eyeballed it for the particular boots I was using.
 
This runs off the existing Transform framework that NiOverride provides, so any modifications to particular nodes will stack with these, you can only have one internal equipped node transform (i.e. two pieces of armor cant modify the same node, overwriting will happen and the behavior will most likely be last written or undefined).

[color=#5EA2E5][size=5][b]Sculpt Mode[/b][/size][/color]

[b]Head Import[/b]
This feature will import the geometry of a nif file that is formatted for NPCs (You either exported it, or you copied an NPC's into the directory)

[b]Sculpt[/b]
This feature is complicated, the tools you have available are Inflate, Deflate, Smooth, Move, and Mask.

[b]Rotating[/b]
Drag with right mouse click

[b]Panning[/b]
Hold the Secondary button and drag with right mouse click to pan.

[b]Inflate/Deflate[/b]
Moves the hit vertices out/in based on an average of adjacent face normals

[b]Smooth[/b]
Moves the hit vertices to an average position of all adjacent vertices, [b]CAUTION[/b] do not use near openings such as the mouth

[b]Move[/b]
Moves vertices in the direction you move the cursor, orthogonal to the view plane

[b]Mask[/b]
Excludes vertices from being altered, including imported geometry, if you are importing a head with a different neck seam you should mask add around the neck area before importing

[b]History[/b]
History will store all previous vertex actions including Clear Sculpt, Import Geometry, and all brush actions

[b]Head Parts[/b]
This is the window for changing the wireframe preview including locking the mesh (so it can't be edited) making the mesh visible, or changing the wireframe color

[b]Clear Sculpt[/b]
This will wipe all sculpting data from all ACTIVE parts (Editable must be checked)

[b]File based Presets[/b]
Presets are now saved to explicit file names and have changed format to json, as such will be saved as .jslot files, if you are replacing an existing slot file, refrain from replacing a .slot file, clicking on a selected file when saving will overwrite the selected file, and the file extension will be for binary but will try to load the json and fail. Binary slot files can still be loaded for legacy purposes, however this format is no longer saved by CharGen.

[color=red]There will not be, nor will ever be controller support for the Sculpt feature, sorry.[/color]


[color=#5EA2E5][center][size=5][b]CHANGE LOG[/b][/size][/center][/color]
See Changes section

[color=#5EA2E5][center][size=5][b]High-Resolution Warpaint[/b][/size][/center][/color]
An INI setting has been added to SKSE to allow for higher resolution warpaint to be applied to your character. The default resolution is 256, this is a terrible resolution if you want to do some really fancy warpaints.
[list][*]Locate your Data directory[/*]
[*]Create SKSE folder if it is not already there[/*]
[*]Create SKSE.ini if it is not already there[/*]
[*]Add "[Display]" category[/*]
[*]Add "iTintTextureResolution=2048"[/*]
[/list]

Your [color=#5EA2E5]Data/SKSE/SKSE.ini[/color] should look something like this:
[color=#5EA2E5][Display]
iTintTextureResolution=2048[/color]
Putting a higher resolution should be possible but I have only tested it at 2048.
[color=#7C7C7C]Note: This may cause low resolution textures to look worse due to upscaling, hopefully this will encourage high-resolution replacements.[/color]

[color=#5EA2E5][center][size=5][b]Keymapping[/b][/size][/center][/color]
Keyboard:
[list][*]Choose Color/Accept - Activate (Default E)[/*]
[*]Done - Ready Weapon (Default R)[/*]
[*]Light - Sneak (Default Ctrl)[/*]
[*]Zoom - Sprint (Default Shift)[/*]
[*]Search - Jump (Default Spacebar)[/*]
[*]Choose Texture - Wait (Default T)[/*]
[*]Load Preset - Quickload[/*]
[*]Save Preset - Quicksave[/*]
[*]Export Head - Shout (Default Z)[/*]
[/list]
Controller:
[list][*]Choose Color/Accept - Activate (Default A)[/*]
[*]Done - Ready Weapon (Default X)[/*]
[*]Light - Wait (Default Back)[/*]
[*]Zoom - Sprint (Default RB)[/*]
[*]Search - None[/*]
[*]Choose Texture - Jump (Default Y)[/*]
[*]Load Preset - Left Stick[/*]
[*]Save Preset - Right Stick[/*]
[*]Export Head - Shout (Default LB)[/*]
[*]Change Sub Category - Left/Right Trigger[/*]
[/list]

[color=#5EA2E5][center][size=5][b]FAQ[/b][/size][/center][/color]
[color=#ff0000]Q[/color]. How do I change the number of overlays available?
[color=#00ff00]A[/color]. Open Data/SKSE/Plugins/NiOverride.ini in any text editor and change the values here.

[color=#ff0000]Q[/color]. Do I need to install CharGen Extension?
[color=#00ff00]A[/color]. Not for versions 2.7 and up, CharGen Extension is completely included, you should uninstall it if you had it prior to installing 2.7 and up.

[color=#ff0000]Q[/color]. I see dollar signs everywhere and many features appear to be missing what do I do?
[color=#00ff00]A[/color]. Install SKSE, or if you have installed it, run your game using skse_loader.exe as you should be.

[color=#ff0000]Q[/color]. Is this compatible with ECE?
[color=#00ff00]A[/color]. Partially, only a few features will work, generally I do not recommend using them together as presets will not work.

[color=#ff0000]Q[/color]. How do I get more Face/Body/Hand/Feet Paint?
[color=#00ff00]A[/color]. Open Data/SKSE/Plugins/NiOverride.ini in a text editor, you will see a section for the amount of paints you can have per type, you can change this up to 128, but it is highly recommended you only set what you intend to use as too many can result in significant performance losses.


[color=#5EA2E5][center][b]Making a plugin[/b][/center][/color]
This short tutorial assumes you have SKSE 1.6.6 (or greater) correctly installed, already have basic knowledge in compiling scripts, using the Creation Kit, can solve compiling errors on your own, and assumes you have already downloaded the modders package.
[list][*]Extract the modders package to your Data folder[/*]
[*]Open Creation Kit (You do not need to load Skyrim.esm)[/*]
[*]Create new quest[/*]
[*]Give quest a unique ID[/*]
[*]Press [color=#5EA2E5][b]Quest Aliases[/b][/color] tab[/*]
[*]Right click the list, [color=#5EA2E5][b]New Reference Alias[/b][/color][/*]
[*]Name the alias [color=#5EA2E5][b]Player[/b][/color][/*]
[*]Tick [color=#5EA2E5][b]Specific Reference[/b][/color][/*]
[*]Press [color=#5EA2E5][b]Select Force Reference[/b][/color][/*]
[*]Select Cell [color=#5EA2E5](any)[/color], Ref [color=#5EA2E5]PlayerRef ('Player')[/color] click OK[/*]
[*]Press the [color=#5EA2E5][b]Add[/b][/color] button in the Scripts group[/*]
[*]Type [color=#5EA2E5][b]RaceMenuLoad[/b][/color] into the filter and click OK[/*]
[*]Press OK in the Reference Alias window[/*]
[*]Go to the scripts tab and [color=#5EA2E5][b]Add[/b][/color][/*]
[*]Click [color=#5EA2E5][b][New Script][/b][/color], give the script a name[/*]
[*]Open the script in your editor of choice and change [color=#5EA2E5][b]extends Quest[/b][/color] to [color=#5EA2E5][b]extends RaceMenuBase[/b][/color][/*]
[*]Copy contents of [color=#5EA2E5][b]PluginTemplate.psc[/b][/color] to your new script[/*]
[*]Read the comments inside of the template[/*]
[*]Click OK in the Quest window[/*]
[*]Save your plugin[/*]
[*]Add your warpaints/sliders to your script[/*]
[*]Compile your script[/*]
[/list]
To package and distribute your plugin you only need to include:
Data/Scripts/YourPlugin.pex
Data/YourPlugin.esp

[color=#5EA2E5][center][size=5][b]COMPATIBILITY[/b][/size][/center][/color]
Enhanced Character Edit is not directly compatible. RaceMenu has compatibility code to accommodate some of the extra facial morphs, install ECE first, then install RaceMenu to take advantage of this. There will be a CharGen verison of ECE sliders soon.
Extended Slider Colors (My previous mod) is not incompatible, but it is highly recommended you uninstall it as it is no longer necessary.

[color=#5EA2E5][center][size=5][b]LOAD ORDER[/b][/size][/center][/color]
Irrelevant, put it anywhere you want it won't make a difference.

[color=#5EA2E5][center][size=5][b]INSTALLATION[/b][/size][/center][/color]
[b]Automatic Download with NMM[/b]
[list][*]Click the Download with manager button on top of the file.[/*]
[*]RaceMenu will appear in in NMM's Mods list once it's downloaded. Double-click the RaceMenu entry to Activate it.[/*]
[/list]
OR

[b]Manual Download with NMM[/b]
[list][*]Start NMM and click on Mods.[/*]
[*]In the left icon bar, click on Add Mod From File and select the downloaded archive file.[/*]
[*]RaceMenu will now appear in the list. Double-click to Activate it.[/*]
[/list]
OR

[b]Manual Installation[/b]
[list][*]Locate the Data/ folder in your Skyrim installation directory. Typically it's found at Program Files/Steam/steamapps/common/skyrim/Data/.[/*]
[*]Extract the contents of the downloaded archive file to your Data/ folder.[/*]
[*]In the Skyrim Launcher, select Data Files and enable RaceMenu.esp.[/*]
[/list]
[color=#5EA2E5][center][size=5][b]SPECIAL EDITION KNOWN ISSUES 0.2.0 to current[/b][/size][/center][/color]
Body morphs seem to lag behind a little bit when scrolling fast through the options, this probably won't be fixed and is a minor inconvenience.

[color=#5EA2E5][center][size=5][b]KNOWN ISSUES 2.0.0 to current[/b][/size][/center][/color]
Body/Hand/Feet paint may appear to be rendered overtop of some effects, this is a known issue. The reason this happens is due to the AlphaProperty flags on the overlay template meshes, changing these to the other set of flags results in overlays occasionally partially rendering on the skin.

The right bicep scale increases strangely. This is a problem with the skeleton and associated mesh, it cannot be fixed on my part and probably won't be fixed by anyone else either. The bug has propagated to pretty much every armor ever made from the base bodies so it's probably far too late to be ever fixed.
[color=#5EA2E5][center][/center][/color]
[color=#5EA2E5][center][size=5][b]TROUBLESHOOTING[/b][/size][/center][/color]
If the last 3 categories are there but you only have "default" to choose from when you change the body texture: You are missing the Overlays Plugin, this plugin contains a number of textures by various authors and are not included in the main mod (simply because they are large and I don't want to be uploading a few MB to update the mod every time; my upload is awful).

If your problem is none of the above you probably have far bigger issues like not even running SKSE with the loader (or not as admin). Or your just simply pushing the game engine way too hard (too many mods, too many high-res textures, too much going on etc). The limitations of the game engine are independent of your PC. If your PC is great save yourself the trouble of typing it out as it's probably irrelevant.

Please make sure your game does not crash opening the original RaceMenu before posting your issue. This mod does not fix the internal class to this menu, it merely redesigns it and adds more features.

If your head appears to be so small you can't see it try cleansaving and installing the latest version.

If your game crashes when you color too many warpaints, this is normal. Currently the game has some sort of hard limit on the number of tints you can have at one time and is currently being investigated.

If your zoom or light toggle do not work this is because there is currently a glitch in SKSE that does not hook the key input system when you start a new game. You need to save your game and reload then showracemenu in the console to fix this. This is corrected for the next SKSE version.

If neither hair color nor tint colors do anything this means that your SKSE scripts are not up to date. Note: This still requires you actually have and run SKSE.

If you are experiencing freezing when opening the RaceMenu, this is due to your game loading many assets all at once and needs to queue drive requests, wait patiently for it to load. If it takes longer than 3 minutes than there is likely an internal issue usually caused by having too many hair mods. This same freezing would happen regardless of having my mod.

If you are experiencing crashing on startup, you likely have a problem with another mod, please take those complaints elsewhere as this mod should not ever conflict.

If there is anything off with the translation and you would like to help correct it, see the [url=https://docs.google.com/spreadsheet/ccc?key=0AhcCUXFKD04_dHIwUXhQV2c3Qkl4RDV6R2UyLUtqc3c]Google Spreadsheet[/url] for the RaceMenu.
author: Expired

name: Caliente's Beautiful Bodies Enhancer -CBBE-
summary: The well-known body enhancer made famous in Skyrim is back and enhanced for Skyrim Special Edition.
description:
[center][color=powderblue][size=5]Problem? Read the FAQ below first.
[url=https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1TbQaDCEIdFMzAAd-0OJnGleYzwMeSi4XqVZlg7S4x-I]~Click here for the mod list~[/url][/size][/color]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/vxxcHmA.png[/img]
[size=4]A female body replacer for The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim Special Edition[/size]
For a safe-for-work (SFW) edition of the mod, go [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/74257]here[/url]﻿.

[color=powderblue]Together with BodySlide, you can customize your character's appearance to the limits of your imagination, and make any outfit fit well!
So be sure to download and install [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/201]BodySlide[/url] for maximum potential![/color][/center]

[color=#b0e0e6]Contents[/color]
[list]
[*]Description
[*]Options
[*]Installation
[*]FAQ
[*]Troubleshooting
[*]Credits
[/list]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/3EXvKwN.png[/img]
CBBE replaces the vanilla female body that all adult female characters (player and NPC) share, with one that is fully customisable with the [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/201]BodySlide[/url] tool. By default there are three nude options available that don't require anything but installation, as well as two underwear styles for these body shapes.

Also included are all vanilla and DLC outfits converted to the base three body shapes, with additional BodySlide compatibility including sliders and many zaps.

The body mesh is completely custom and has 13554 vertices, which is more than 20 times the resolution of the vanilla Skyrim body and 6 times that of the older CBBE. Included are textures made from photographs of real women, and multiple choices of high-res normal maps to fit the body shape you want. Also redone are the mesh weights for animation, offering improved deformation in many poses.

[color=powderblue]Note to modders:
[/color]If you wish to create armour/clothes for the bodies, make use of the conversion references coming with BodySlide in Outfit Studio (the base shape of CBBE, otherwise the "Outfit" presets) or your preferred modelling software. Ideally, create BodySlide projects so that your outfits will fit regardless of shape preset.

[color=powderblue][size=3]Discord[/size][/color]
[url=https://discord.gg/2qg2Rzw][img]https://i.imgur.com/QI1YKyD.png[/img][/url]


[img]https://i.imgur.com/uawVAdg.png[/img]
The following are installation options for different body shapes as well as underwear styles.
All of these shapes support the weight slider from 0% to 100% in the game.

[size=3][color=powderblue]Shape Options[/color][/size]
[color=#b0e0e6]Slim[/color]:
- Default CBBE body with a natural shape of slimmer ladies.
[color=powderblue]Curvy[/color]:
- Based on the original CBBE
- More voluptuous than "Slim", but still realistic in its proportions.
[color=powderblue]Vanilla[/color]:
- Based on the Vanilla body made to work with CBBE textures.
- Thicker waist, lowered breasts and more defined pelvis region.
[color=powderblue]NeverNude[/color]:
- Vanilla-styled underwear.
[color=powderblue]Underwear[/color]:
- Modern style underwear in five colours (black, purple, red, light blue, and a plain grey) and an optional more revealing style.

More options available with [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/201]BodySlide[/url], including zap options (to remove parts) and many outfits.

For body presets, have a look at Jeir's [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/11229]CBBE Presets Compendium[/url] or search Nexus Mods for tons of them.
You are going to find one you love for sure!

[size=3][color=powderblue]Skin Options[/color][/size]
[color=powderblue]Default Skin[/color]:
- Clean, smooth and hairless textures.
[color=powderblue]Pubic Hair[/color]:
- Default textures with pubic hair.

For more texture options, such as innies or dirt, check out the resources in the misc. files section.
If you want to use entirely different skin textures for CBBE, check out the list of compatible mods or search the Nexus!

[size=3][color=powderblue]Normal Map Options[/color][/size]
"Normal maps" are a type of texture that is going to define how your body is being affected by light.
Installing the right normal map is going to make sure the body is being lit/shaded in a way you want it to look like.

[color=powderblue]Base[/color]:
- Default normal map that comes with the main CBBE installer. Works well enough for a lot of body shapes.
[color=powderblue]Petite[/color]:
- Less shading for the breast area compared to the base map. Better for body shapes with much smaller breasts.
[color=powderblue]Curvier[/color]:
- More shading for the breast area compared to the base map. Better for body shapes with larger breasts.
[color=powderblue]Cleavage[/color]:
- More shading for the upper breast area to provide a nice look of cleavage. Works well for outfits.
[color=powderblue]SevenBase[/color]:
- Shading that matches the SevenBase body shape. Recommended to use only with CBBE SevenBase preset from BodySlide.
[color=powderblue]SevenBase Muscular[/color]:
- Same as SevenBase, but with muscle definition.
[color=powderblue]Muscular[/color]:
- Provides abs and a little bit of definition for the inner thighs and butt.
[color=powderblue]Fetish[/color]:
- More defined nipple and areola shape. A little bit of abs and curvy breasts. Recommended to use with matching preset from BodySlide.

[size=3][color=powderblue]Physics Bodies[/color][/size]
After installing CBBE, your BodySlide program will have a new entry "CBBE Body Physics" for basic breast and butt physics.
It's only available in BodySlide, not in the main CBBE installer.

Additionally, there is a "CBBE Body Special" available in BodySlide.
With this body, you will receive a 3D labia mesh. The labia requires the body textures to be compatible with it, so keep this in mind when using third party CBBE textures.

Make sure to check out the [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/30174]CBBE 3BA[/url] or [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/29023/]CBBE SMP[/url] bodies for advanced physics and other additions as well.

[color=#ff0000]WARNING:
[/color]All of these physics bodies absolutely require you to have the [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/1988/]XPMSSE[/url] skeleton installed, or your game will crash!
Additionally, you need a physics system such as [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/21224]CBPC[/url] or [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/57339]HDT SMP[/url].
[size=3][color=powderblue]
RaceMenu BodyMorph[/color][/size]
BodyMorph is a system part of the [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/19080]RaceMenu[/url] mod that lets you morph bodies and outfits live in-game.

To make use of it, choose the "RaceMenu Morphs" option in the CBBE installer and make sure to build all of your bodies and outfits using the "Build Morphs" checkbox in the BodySlide program.

To get load/save functionality of body presets in-game in RaceMenu, the two mods [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/13048]PapyrusUtil[/url] and [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/17561]UIExtensions[/url] are required.
[size=3][color=powderblue]
Plugins[/color][/size]
[color=powderblue]CBBE.esl/esp[/color]:
- Required for rim-lighting version only. Disable if using the non-rim-lighting version. "ESPFE" plugin that doesn't take up a plugin slot (as it's an ESL-flagged esp).
[color=#b0e0e6]RaceMenuMorphsCBBE.esp[/color]:
- Comes with the RaceMenu Morphs option (see above). Can not be converted to an ESL plugin!
[size=3][color=powderblue]
Recommended Mods[/color][/size]
The following are recommended mods to check out that are a good fit for CBBE.
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/90713]Female Hands Redone:[/url] Overhaul for hands with additional BodySlide sliders.
- ﻿[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/78090]NecDaz Feet:[/url] Overhaul for feet with different proportions.
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/798]Fair Skin Complexion:[/url] Overhaul of all textures used for female characters.
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/45718]Diamond Skin:[/url] Female skin textures based on Fair Skin Complexion.
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/65274]BnP - Female Skin:[/url] Semi-realistic, meticulously crafted high-quality skin texture replacer.
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/20078]Bijin Skin:[/url] Female skin textures used by Bijin NPCs.
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/184]Feminine Argonian Textures:[/url] Overhaul for Argonian body textures.
- [url=https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1TbQaDCEIdFMzAAd-0OJnGleYzwMeSi4XqVZlg7S4x-I]List of CBBE-compatible mods[/url]


[img]https://i.imgur.com/xXsc576.png[/img]
Installation of the [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/266]Unofficial Skyrim Special Edition Patch (USSEP)[/url] is highly advised to fix a lot of issues related to vanilla outfits (and the game in general).
It is also highly recommended to use a [b]mod manager[/b] to make it easier to install and uninstall mods.

[color=powderblue]Mod Managers (Vortex, Mod Organizer, NMM, ...)[/color]
The included installer can be used with any mod manager that supports "FOMod" installers, such as the ones listed above.

Simply download the main file to your mod manager and install/enable it.
Make sure to run the installation using the installer and follow its instructions.

[color=powderblue]Wrye Bash[/color]
Download the main file and copy it into the installers tab.

Check the "FOMod" box above the Sub-packages pane for scripted installation, or check the boxes of what you wish to install in the Sub-Packages pane.
Right click the archive and choose "Install".

[color=powderblue]Manual[/color]
If you are an advanced user/modder, the mod may also be installed manually.

Download the main file and open the archive using [url=https://7-zip.org/]7-zip[/url]﻿.
Install the [i]contents[/i] of the "00 Required (Slim)" folder into the "Data" folder of your game installation. You may then install the [i]contents[/i] of one of the 01-05 folders depending on Body shape and type (nude or not) desired, and then the corresponding outfit files for your chosen body shape (06-08, or just 09 if you intend on using BodySlide).

Any other of the numbered folders are optional and may also be installed into the "Data" folder. Ensure that if you install any of the "Underwear" folders (16-21), you also install one of the textures for it as well (22-25).


[img]https://i.imgur.com/lrW7B26.png[/img]
[color=powderblue][size=3]Q1:[/size] Why does this affect NPCs and how can I make this player-only?[/color]
A: All adult female characters use the exact same meshes/textures, bar the Beast and elderly races/characters, who have some texture differences. To make this player-only, one option is to install and play as a modded standalone race or use a unique player mod, but this comes with various issues. Preferably, install a base CBBE body shape (and corresponding outfits) that all NPCs will use, and use the in-game RaceMenu Morphs for your character only. Requires RaceMenu, the Morphs plugin, and BodySlide. This option means you can also use things such as BodyGen, OBody or AutoBody to have further control in NPC body shapes.

[color=powderblue][size=3]Q2:[/size] What are the CBBE.esp or RaceMenuMorphsCBBE.esp plugin files for?[/color]
A: Please check the "Plugins" section under "Options" above for info on what they do and why they exist.

[color=powderblue][size=3]Q3a:[/size] Why are the bodies not changing after I install?[/color]
A1: You didn't install the armour/clothing replacer in this mod (nor a separate one) and/or are not looking at a nude female adult character.
A2: If NPCs have changed but your character hasn't, you're using a custom race (or similar) and didn't copy over the meshes/textures into the race's folders.
A3: You're not using a legal copy of the game. Buy the game legally and you're all set.
A4: If you're using NMM, make sure you're using the latest files from GitHub. If using Vortex/MO2 and BodySlide, make sure those mod managers are using your preset and not a default CBBE body.
A5: If (some) NPCs haven't changed, but your character (and other NPCs) has, you have a mod that makes them standalone (custom followers, possibly 'Bijins'). You will have to manually add the meshes and textures to their folders.

[color=powderblue][size=3]Q3b:[/size] Why are the outfits not changing after I install?[/color]
A: Outfits in Skyrim don't work the same was as in real life. They don't go over the body, they replace the body. Thus the body shape when wearing an outfit will be whatever shape the outfit was made for. If you want the outfits to match your CBBE base or BodySlide bodies, you need to install CBBE or BodySlide-compatible outfit mods. This mod comes with vanilla outfit replacers. Consult the mod list for other CBBE-compatible mods.

[color=powderblue][size=3]Q4:[/size] How do I swap between the different body types?[/color]
A: Reinstall/use BodySlide to generate new body meshes. You [i]will[/i] need to reinstall if you want to change from one texture set to another if you don't know how to do it manually.

[color=powderblue][size=3]Q5:[/size] Is this mod compatible with [i]x mod[/i]?[/color]
A: If it's a female body mod, then no. If it's a face retexture mod, and you haven't installed the FacePack, then yes, as long as it's compatible with the CBBE seams. If it's an armour/clothing mod...consult the CBBE mod list, as only outfit mods that were made for the current version of CBBE, or else don't show much/any skin, will work without major issues. If it's a standalone race mod/follower, you'll need to manually copy/paste CBBE textures into that mod's folders. Anything else will be fine.

[color=powderblue][size=3]Q6:[/size] Can you make a body texture with pubic hair/tattoos/dirt/argonian support?[/color]
A: There are many mods in the mod list that have different features such as scars and dirt, and different skin types.
Some pubic hair/dirt options are in the installer or texture resources.

[color=powderblue][size=3]Q7:[/size] How do I get body physics/jellyboobs/all dem jiggles hurhurhur?[/color]
A: Install [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/21224]CBPC[/url] (or [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/57339]Faster HDT SMP[/url]), [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/1988]XPMSSE[/url], and build the CBBE Body Physics (or Special) body in BodySlide.

[color=powderblue][size=3]Q8:[/size] Why do I get gaps at the hips/misplaced textures when nude?[/color]
A: You are not using CBBE textures on CBBE meshes. Reinstall, and make sure all the necessary files are in the correct place. Argonians do not have texture support with CBBE - you will have to get them elsewhere (see: mod list).

[color=powderblue][size=3]Q9:[/size] Why do I get gaps at the hips/misplaced textures with certain outfits/outfits made for other body mods?[/color]
A: In games, the outfit defines the mesh/shape, but both the body and the outfit use the same skin texture. CBBE has a different UV map to the vanilla/other bodies. UV maps are how the textures line up on the meshes. Due to the different UV maps, CBBE textures will not line up correctly on vanilla/other mod meshes.

[color=powderblue][size=3]Q10:[/size] I have the 'dark head bug'/'light head bug'/face-colour-doesn't-match-body-colour bug. How do I fix this?[/color]
A: If it's with NPCs, then you've altered them in some way (even if it's just removing dirt or changing their weight), and need to generate facegen data. Have a look at various 'how to create a follower/NPC' tutorials for help. Note that this can happen even if you have the facegen data generated, if you've already encountered the NPC in your game. You may also need to edit your load order to deal with conflicts.
Also note that if you have several mods that affect NPC appearance (e.g. ECE, EEO, [b]and[/b] NPC-appearance replacer mods) then the wrong load order with these mods' plugins can also cause issues. You may need to manually move them up/down your load order to find out the right place for them.
If it's with the player character, there's no known fix. If it's with a vampire character, then blame BethSoft as it's an issue with the vanilla game.

[color=powderblue][size=3]Q11:[/size] I tried installing manually, but I can't extract the archive, why?
[/color]A: [url=https://7-zip.org/]Use 7-zip[/url] for manual extraction - it's 100% free, works, and doesn't screw up the archives. 7zip's been the modder's choice archiver for more than two decades, for good reason.

[color=powderblue][size=3]Q12:[/size] B'aww, this mod causes my game to crash![/color]
A: No, it doesn't. There is nothing in this mod that would cause your game to crash, unless your hardware isn't good enough to render the 4k uncompressed ultra textures which are the default ones (fix; use lower res textures); you have mods than add to the game's instability, or you generated the Physics Body in BodySlide without installed XPMSSE. Check your mods, and learn to love LOOT, SSEEdit and Wrye Bash.

[color=powderblue][size=3]Q13:[/size] Can I use outfits/presets/bodies/anything-that-isn't-CBBE-textures made for Skyrim Legendary/'Oldrim's' CBBE with this?[/color]
A: No. You'll break things. Don't do it. They need to be converted to the new CBBE first.

[color=powderblue][size=3]Q14:[/size] Will you put this on Bethesda.net?[/color]
A: CBBE and its outfits are [url=https://bethesda.net/en/mods/skyrim/mod-detail/4063300]now on Bethesda.net[/url].

[color=powderblue][size=3]Q15:[/size] Why doesn't SSE CBBE have a belly node?[/color]
A: In LE, one of the spine nodes was sacrificed to provide a belly node, resulting in sub-optimal animations but providing belly scaling/'physics'. In SSE, all of the spine nodes are used on the spine, meaning animations are as good as they can ever be. Nodes can not be added, and we are not willing to sacrifice one of the spine nodes again. RaceMenu SE has been released with support for body morphs, mods can use this scaling system for the belly instead.


[img]https://i.imgur.com/cSsp41F.png[/img]
CBBE is a simple mod on a technical level. All that is needed for it to work correctly is for it to replace the right files.

Make sure your mod manager is correctly set up and the CBBE mod is installed and enabled.

Don't forget about checking for file conflicts to make sure the body files are overwritten in the order you want your body-related mods to load in.
You can only have one body mod replacing the bodies of default races at the same time!

[b]Check out the FAQ section of this mod page for more help and tips.[/b]

name: Pandora Behaviour Engine Plus
summary: Behaviour engine tool for patching Skyrim Nemesis/FNIS behaviour mods, with full creature support. Clean, responsive UI and error-tolerant, verbose patching. Supported on Windows/Linux/MacOS, open source and intended as an alternative to Nemesis.
description:
[size=3][img]https://imgup.uk/proxy/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fi.imgur.com%2FirfmurJ.png[/img]
A modular and lightweight behavior engine for TES Skyrim SE, for creatures and humanoids. Built with backwards compatibility in mind for [url=https://github.com/ShikyoKira/Project-New-Reign---Nemesis-Main]Nemesis Unlimited Behavior Engine[/url] and FNIS, Pandora Behaviour Engine+ is an alternative engine streamlines both the author and user experience through a simplified UI, robust logging, intuitive formats, and fast patching times.

Pandora runs on Windows, Linux*, and MacOS**
[/size][size=2]* Use Wine or Proton
** MacOS support is untested[/size][size=3]

[img]https://imgup.uk/proxy/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fi.imgur.com%2FTOlwusq.png[/img]
[b] Core:
[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][size=3]Faster patching times (will depend on end user hardware specs). [/size]
[*][size=3]Simpler usage.[/size]
[*][size=3]Various startup arguments to customize output paths and operation.[/size]
[*][size=3]Verbose and precise logging via the output "Engine.log"[/size]
[*][size=3]Modular back-end design; capable of supporting multiple animation/behavior patching configurations.[/size]
[*][size=3]Long-term support.[/size]
[*][size=3]Full creature support.[/size]
[*][size=3]Support for code-based custom behavior plugins.[/size]
[*][size=3]Support for both FNIS and Nemesis mod formats.[/size]
[*][size=3]Strong error tolerance; illegal edits by mods are isolated and reverted to vanilla, without tripping up the rest of the patching process.[/size]
[*][size=3]Descriptive, clear and copy-able program messages.[/size]
[*][size=3]Finds Skyrim SE/AE automatically via Windows Registry; works out of the box in proper mod list environments.[/size]
[/list][size=3]
﻿[b]Quality of Life:

[/b][/size][list]
[*][size=3]Search box for behaviour patches.[/size]
[*][size=3]Light/dark theme.[/size]
[*][size=3]Select and unselect all checkboxes.[/size]
[*][size=3]Grippers for drag and drop priority. [/size]
[*][size=3]Task bar flasher to indicate patching complete.[/size]
[*][size=3]QoL command lines (e.g "--auto_close" to close Pandora after patching)[/size]
[*][size=3]Keyboard based sorting ('-' and '+')[/size]
[*][size=3]Automatic output tracking and old output deletion.[/size]
[*][size=3]Button to view output folder.[/size]
[/list]
[img]https://imgup.uk/proxy/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fi.imgur.com%2FSn443WV.png[/img]

[size=3] For Installation Instructions, please read our [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/articles/11067]Pandora Installation Guide[/url][/size][size=1]

For the (latest bleeding edge) versions of the engine that may not be as stable or tested as the release on this page, go to the [url=https://github.com/Monitor221hz/Pandora-Behaviour-Engine-Plus]github page[/url]. Note that there's not always a version ahead of the release on Nexus.

author: jet

name: Instant Corpse Cleanup (No Papyrus Scripts)
summary: A lightweight, script-free solution for corpse cleanup by using the native game engine setting.
description:
[b]Why this mod? (The Problem with Traditional body cleanup Mods):
[/b]Most cleanup mods use invisible quests and cloaking spells that constantly scan your surroundings. These scripts stay in your save file forever, often causing more "save bloat" than the corpses themselves. Furthermore, using the Delete() function incorrectly can break respawns and cause quest bugs.

[b]The Solution:[/b]This mod features [b]ZERO Papyrus scripts[/b]. It modifies internal game settings to accelerate the engine's natural cleanup process.[list]
[*][b]Real-time / Rapid Cleanup:[/b] Corpses are flagged for removal almost immediately after you leave the area.
[/list][list]
[*][b]No Save Bloat: [/b]No running scripts, no hidden quests.
[*][b]No Quest Bugs:[/b] Uses native engine logic, ensuring locations can still respawn correctly.
[/list][list]
[*][b]Performance First:[/b] Dramatically reduces the load on your CPU and Save Data.
[*][b]ESL Version:[/b] Doesn't take up a mod slot.
[/list]

author: Messiah

name: Permanent Corpses - No More Dead Body Cleanup
summary: Tired of battlefield corpses disappearing the moment you leave the area? This mod overhauls the game engine's cleanup logic to ensure every fallen foe and friend remains exactly where they fell, indefinitely. ESL. It's safe for save files.
description:
[b]Key Features:[/b]
[list]
[*][b]Zero Cleanup:[/b] Prevents the game engine from automatically deleting dead bodies after a given time.
[*][b]No "Dead Body Cleanup Cell":[/b] Unique NPCs will no longer be teleported to the "WIDeadBodyCleanupCell" (the morgue) upon death. They stay in the game world.
[*][b]No Corpse Limit:[/b] Eliminates the hard cap on the number of bodies that can accumulate in a single location or cell. Stack 'em as high as you want!
[*][b]Encounter Zone Lockdown:[/b] By default, dungeons and interior cells will no longer reset their contents, preserving the state of your conquest. However, the optional version support respawn and old corpses will be replaced by new, living enemies.
[/list]
[b]Exceptional Cases: [/b]
[list]
[*][b]Quest-Forced Dungeon Resets:[/b] Some quests are designed to force-reset a dungeon specifically to spawn a random boss enemy for that quest.
[*][b]Wild Random Encounters Cleanup:[/b] Generic enemies spawned exclusively for random encounter events might still disappear when the event script finishes.
[/list][list]
[/list][b]This mod will not cause save bloat or corrupt your save files.[/b]
To ensure performance, I conducted rigorous stress testing as shown in [b]Image 2[/b]. I used the console to spawn and kill a total of [b]2,320[/b] NPCs across 20 different dungeons. After this massive influx of data, the save file size only increased by [b]1.1 MB[/b]. This proves that the impact on your save data is minimal, even with an extreme number of persistent corpses.
author: Moly

name: Ultimate Optimized Scripts Compilation
summary: A compilation of scripts which aims to incorporate optimizations and fixes from various other script mods on Nexus with open permissions.
description:
[size=3]Unlike [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/105144]Script Optimization and Fixes Compilation[/url]﻿, this is not just a compilation of mods. Instead, I merged changes of conflicting scripts and added my own fixes on top.[/size]


[color=#00ffff][size=4][b][i]Included Mods:[/i][/b][/size][/color]
[size=3]---------------------Optimization Mods with Conflicts Resolved----------------------
[/size][url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/54061][size=3]Vanilla Script (micro)Optimizations
[/size][/url][url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/68139][size=3]Vanilla Scripting Enhancements
[/size][/url][size=3][url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/67968]OnMagicEffectApply[/url]
-------------------------------Script Fix Mods Optimized-----------------------------
[url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/1473]Modern Brawl Bug Fix
[/url][url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/38621]Modern Clap Bug Fix
[/url][url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/65849]King Olaf's Fire Festival Not Ending Fix
[/url][url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/80676]Magic Student (WIChangeLocation04) Quest Fix
[/url]﻿[url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/91403]World Encounter Hostility Fix - Performance Version[/url]﻿
[url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/62413]WIDeadBodyCleanupScript Crash Fix[/url] (Version from [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/36042]lilebonymace's patches and xEdit scripts[/url])
[url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/74652]Roggvir's Execution Scene Fixes
[/url][url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/138750]Sawmill Activation Fix[/url] (Without the optional fix which causes issues above 60 FPS)[url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/73890]
[/url][url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/90996]MQ104PlayerScript Fix
[/url]﻿[/size][url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/90955][size=3]DefaultActivateSelf Tweak
[/size][/url][size=3][url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrim/mods/69339]Elder Scroll Reading Fix
[/url][/size][size=3][url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/87940]Dragonactorscript infinite loop fix[/url] (Fail Silently Version)[url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/87940]
[/url][url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/58862]Mage Armor Script Fix
[/url][url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/154661]The Pigeon's Nest - Assortment of small mods and fixes[/url]﻿
[/size][size=3][url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/43038]Hearthfire HouseBards AI Package Fix[/url] (Deleted. This mod adds back a dice roll, which is omitted by Bethesda, to make bards not always sing when you enter Hearthfire houses)
[/size][color=#ffff00][size=3][b]﻿Note: If you have any of the above mods installed, you can safely remove them.
[/b][/size][/color]

[color=#00ffff][size=4][b][i]Own Fixes:[/i][/b][/size][/color][size=3]
[list]
[*][size=3]Fixed a bug that ash piles of world encounter aliases(NPCs you normally see in random encounters) will soon disappear when [/size][size=3]DisableOnUnload property set to True ([b]wealiasscript.pex[/b] and [b]objectreference.pex[/b])[/size]
[*][size=3]Fix a bug that three fence gates won't close when Isran tests whether Gunmar, Sorine and player are vampires in quest A New Order if you have [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/266]Unofficial Skyrim Special Edition Patch - USSEP[/url] installed due to a wrong condition check ([b]default2stateactivator.pex[/b])[/size]
[/list][/size][b][color=#ffff00][size=3]Note: You may find I made edits to ObjectReference.pex, just don't panic. Long story short, in order to fix the first bug, directly making changes to ObjectReference is much easier than other methods. Please see the source for more details.[/size][/color]
[/b]

[b][i][color=#00ffff][size=4]Recommended Mods and Load Order:
[/size][/color][/i][/b][size=3][url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/122999]Ultimate Optimized Scripts Compilation[/url]([color=#ffff00][b]Load UOSC.esl after plugin of USSEP and USCCCP[/b][/color])[/size]
[size=3][url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/97155](SCROTE) - Simply Optimized Scripts AIO[/url]﻿([color=#ffff00][b]Load SimplyOptimizedScripts.esl after UOSC.esl[/b][/color])
[url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/141745]CritterSpawn - Script Call Reduction
[/url][url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/159287]Stormcloaks Fight With Thalmor and Penitus Oculatus[/url]
[url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/166044]Imperial Thalmor Stormcloak Patrols Fixes - Soldiers and Prisoners World Encounters
[/url][url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/81587]﻿Rock Traps Trigger Fixes
[/url][url=http://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/81495]Robber's Gorge Fixes
[/url]﻿
[/size]

name: Pet My Pussy - 4K overlays CBBE-3BA-UBE
summary:
This mod adds 22 racemenu overlays with cute kittens
Racemenu and SlaveTats verions aviable.
description:
[center][color=#f1c232][size=6][/size][/color][/center][center][color=#f1c232][size=6]New in 1.2
[/size]
[spoiler][size=4][img]https://i.ibb.co/dJ6khnj5/Pet-My-Pussy1-2.png[/img][/spoiler]
[/size][/color][img]https://i.ibb.co/99dkJ3M1/RGB-How-To-Use.png[/img]

[color=#6aa84f][size=5]1. Download and install like any other mod

2. Open [/size][/color][color=#6aa84f][size=5]Racemenu[/size][/color][color=#f1c232][size=5]﻿ [/size][/color][color=#ffd966][size=5]Body Paint tab[/size][/color][color=#6aa84f][size=5] and select overlays you want[/size][/color]

[quote][size=4][color=#93c47d]I recomend you to add this line to SKSE.INI in Data/Skse to get better resolution fo overlays[/color]

[color=#ffd966][Display]
iTintTextureResolution=4096
[/color]
[color=#93c47d]Also [/color][color=#f1c232]to get more slots[/color][color=#93c47d] in the Body Paints tab change this [/color][color=#cc0000]Number[/color][color=#93c47d] in skse64.ini located in Data/Skse/Plugins[/color]

[color=#6aa84f][Overlays/Body] ; "Body [Ovl#]" and "Body [SOvl#]"
; Determines how many body overlays there should be
iNumOverlays=[/color][color=#cc0000]18[/color][color=#6aa84f] ; Default[6]
iSpellOverlays=1 ; Default[1][/color][color=#93c47d][/color][/size][/quote]


[quote][size=5]﻿[/size][color=#f1c232][size=5]Please report any issues so I can fix them as soon as possible[/size][/color][/quote][color=#f1c232]
[/color]

[size=5][quote][color=#f1c232]If you have any suggestions, feel free to leave them in the comments.[/color][/quote][/size]

name: Male Npc Overhaul SkyPatched
summary:
Skypatched version of my Male Npc Overhaul,
visual overhaul for 208 male NPCs
description:
[color=#00ff00][size=6][b]Requirement:
[/b][/size][/color][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/106659][color=#6fa8dc][size=5]SkyPatcher[/size][/color][/url]


[color=#ffff00][size=5]this mod will change the apperence of 208 NPCs in Skyrim[/size][/color]


[color=#00ffff][size=3]What is the different bewteen the Skypatched version and the old version[/size][/color]
the skypatched version it only copy the visual to the specific NPC and won't overwrite  npc data  so it will highly increase the compatiblity,that means you won't see the black face bug and you don't need patch for most of  the mod anymore(like USSEP,ai overhaul sse,ect...)


[color=#00ffff][size=3]problem you may see sometimes[/size][/color]
i found this problem in the skypatched version a few times and i don't know why, so you may see the hair floating or invisble sometimes, just go other place and come back , hair will be fixed


[color=#00ffff][size=3]how to remove those makeover you don't like in my mod[/size][/color]
open the '1MNO_face.ini' and '2MNO_body.ini' file from my mod, i already list the NPC name and their line,deleted that line of the npc will make him won't be effect by my mod anymore


[color=#00ffff][size=3]about hairy body[/size][/color]
i use 3 kinds of skin for these npc, because i dont want their body all look the same.some fo them are hairless ,some are hairy.some are very hairy.
but i know some pepole don't like hairy body, so if you want all of them are hairless,just replacer the body textures in my mod(they are here: data/textures/actors/character/bnm/body )you will find 3 folder in the 'body' folder.  ''fs' dolder include hairless textures '2' folder include hairy textures, '3'  folder include heavy hairy textures. so use the body textures in the 'fs' folder  to replacer those textures in the '2' and '3' folder, then everyone is hairless

name: Fully Harvest Hanging Carcasses and Coin Purses - ITA
summary: Traduzione in Italiano della mod "Fully Harvest Hanging Carcasses and Coin Purses", che corregge in maniera immersiva il modo in cui la cacciagione appesa oppure le borse di monete vengono raccolte. È richiesta l'installazione della mod originale.
description:
Traduzione in Italiano della mod "[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/86751]Fully Harvest Hanging Carcasses and Coin Purses[/url]", che modifica in modo immersivo il modo con cui la cacciagione appesa oppure le borse di monete vengono raccolte.

Caratteristiche

> Quando raccogli carcasse di animali morti appesi (conigli, fagiani, salmoni, skeevers carbonizzati), questi scompaiono (fino a quando l'oggetto non si rigenera), invece di continuare ad essere appesi come prima senza cambiamenti visivi.

> Risolto il problema per cui quando prendi le borse di monete la loro collisione rimane lì, il che significa che puoi ancora interagire fisicamente con esso: urtarlo, colpirlo con le armi, lanciargli frecce e le frecce si conficcheranno come se il borsellino fosse ancora lì, e prendere una borsa da una piastra a pressione non la rilascia.

> Risolto il problema relativo alla collisione più precisa per i salmoni appesi. Innanzitutto per DeadSalmon02 (quello più scuro e più piccolo), la cui collisione inizia ben sotto la sua testa tanto che se ad esempio si scagliano delle frecce in quella zona queste resteranno lì fluttuando nell'aria, dondolandosi insieme al salmone.

Grazie per il supporto a [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/25247]Ordine della Runa Dorata[/url], e a [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/users/59086436]lilebonymace[/url] e [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/users/6312426]wizkid34[/url] per la fantastica mod originale.

name: Fallout 4 Script Extender (F4SE)
summary: The Fallout 4 Script Extender, or F4SE for short, is a modder's resource that expands the scripting capabilities of Fallout 4. It does so without modifying the executable files on disk, so there are no permanent side effects.
description:
[color=#ff7700][size=4][b]All native code plugins (.dlls) need to be updated by their creators for 1.10.980+ and 1.11+.
Old native code plugins not loading is not a problem with F4SE.
[/b][/size][/color]
The Fallout 4 Script Extender, or F4SE for short, is a modder's resource that expands the scripting capabilities of Fallout 4. It does so without modifying the executable files on disk, so there are no permanent side effects.

Once installed, no additional steps are needed to launch Fallout 4 with F4SE's added functionality. You can start the game using F4SE from f4se_loader.exe

[size=4]Installation (Manual)[/size]
[list]
[*]Download the F4SE from the Files tab.
[*]Extract all files inside the top-level folder in the ZIP to your game folder (where Fallout4.exe is located).
[*]Run the game using f4se_loader.exe.
[*]To confirm it is working, open the console with the tilde (`) key and type [i]getf4seversion[/i]. This will display the version number of the current F4SE build. 
[/list]


[size=4]Install with Vortex[/size]
[list]
[*]Click the "Vortex" button in the top-right of this page.
[*]Once installed and enabled, ensure you have deployed it by clicking "Deploy Mods" on the Mods toolbar.
[*]Use the shortcut on the dashboard to start the game with F4SE.
[/list]
[size=4]Compatibility:[/size]
F4SE supports the latest version of Fallout available on Steam. MS Store/Gamepass/Epic/whatever else isn't supported.
