name: Unofficial Fallout 4 Patch - UFO4P
summary: A comprehensive bugfixing mod for Fallout 4. The goal of the Unofficial Fallout 4 Patch (aka UFO4P) is to eventually fix every bug with Fallout 4 not officially resolved by the developers to the limits of the Creation Kit and community-developed tools, in one easy-to-install package.
description:
[center][img]https://www.afkmods.com/modbanners/UFO4PVaultBoyBanner920x200.jpg[/img][/center]
[center][color=#ff0000][size=6]Requires Fallout 4 patch 1.11.137 or greater!
Requires *ALL 15* Official DLCs.[/size][/color][/center]

A comprehensive bugfixing mod for Fallout 4. The goal of the Unofficial Fallout 4 Patch (aka UFO4P) is to eventually fix every bug with Fallout 4 not officially resolved by the developers to the limits of the Creation Kit and community-developed tools, in one easy-to-install package.

Features:
- Hundreds of gameplay, quest, NPC, object, item, text and placement bugs fixed.
- Created by the authors of the Unofficial Oblivion and Skyrim Patch series.
- No unsafe changes (such as deletions of stock objects).
- Designed to be compatible with as many other mods as possible.

For more information (ie the recent changelog, mods that UFO4P replaces) see [url=https://www.afkmods.com/index.php?/forum/350-unofficial-fallout-4-patches/] UFO4P's threads on the AFK Mods forum here[/url].

[url=https://www.afkmods.com/Unofficial%20Fallout%204%20Patch%20Version%20History.html]The complete changelog is posted here[/url].

If you're experiencing a bug with Fallout 4, please report the bug to us in as much detail as possible on [url=https://afktrack.afkmods.com/index.php?a=issues&project=32]the UFO4P bugtracker[/url]. Please use search to ensure that you aren't submitting something that is already there.

[url=https://www.afkmods.com/index.php?/topic/4420-frequently-asked-questions-bugs/]Frequently Asked Questions - BUGS[/url]
[url=https://www.afkmods.com/index.php?/topic/4421-frequently-asked-questions-about-the-project/]Frequently Asked Questions - About the Project[/url]

[center][url=https://www.patreon.com/arthmoor][img]https://c5.patreon.com/external/logo/become_a_patron_button.png[/img][/url][/center]

[b][u][size=4]Installation Requirements
[/size][/u][/b]
Fallout 4 version 1.11.137 or greater, plus the following official DLCs:
Automatron
Wasteland Workshop
Far Harbor
Contraptions Workshop
Vault-Tec
Nuka-World
Hellfire Power Armor
X-02 Power Armor
Heavy Incinerator
Enclave Weapon Skins
Enclave Armor Skins
Halloween Workshop
Tesla Cannon
Makeshift Weapon Pack
Enclave Remnants

[color=red]DO NOT extract the contents of the BA2 files into your Data folder as loose files, or let your mod manager perform an equivalent function on the contents of the file. Doing so will cause things to load in the wrong order and break the game. Fallout 4 is intended to load BA2 files alongside their ESM/ESP files. Circumventing this causes issues which we will not provide support for. If you insist on going against this advice, you will receive no support for the problems you generate as a result of this.[/color]

After installing the mods, the following general load order needs to be followed:

Fallout4.esm
DLCRobot.esm
DLCworkshop01.esm
DLCCoast.esm
DLCWorkshop02.esm
DLCWorkshop03.esm
DLCNukaWorld.esm
DLCUltraHighResolution.esm (OPTIONAL)
ccBGSFO4044-HellfirePowerArmor.esl
ccBGSFO4115-X02.esl
ccBGSFO4116-HeavyFlamer.esl
ccBGSFO4110-WS_Enclave.esl
ccBGSFO4096-AS_Enclave.esl
ccFSVFO4007-Halloween.esl
ccBGSFO4046-TesCan.esl
ccSBJFO4003-Grenade.esl
ccOTMFO4001-Remnants.esl
Unofficial Fallout 4 Patch.esp
[Everything else you have installed]

For getting load order set with other mods loaded, please use [url=https://loot.github.io/]LOOT[/url]. It will provide you a solid starting point for everything that you can then tweak as needed.

[b][u]Disclaimer[/u][/b]

This mod is not made, guaranteed, or supported by Microsoft, Zenimax, Bethesda Game Studios, or any of their affiliates.
author: The Unofficial Patch Project Team

name: Mod Configuration Menu
summary: The Mod Configuration Menu is a settings page for mods! It provides a central location for mod configuration, accessible via the Pause menu.
description:
[img align=right]https://i.imgur.com/yThthRV.png[/img]
[color=#f6b26b][font=Courier New][size=5][b]★ About ★[/b][/size][/font][/color]

The Mod Configuration Menu is a settings page for mods!

It provides a central location for mod configuration, accessible via the Pause menu.

ModSettings are stored in per-mod INI files under Data\MCM\Settings, making it easy to backup and restore settings and even change them outside the game if so desired should the mod support it.

Are you a modder? Check out the [url=https://github.com/Neanka/MCM_0.1_AS3/wiki]documentation[/url] for detailed information on creating MCM menus.

[color=#f6b26b][font=Courier New][size=5][b]★ Features ★[/b][/size][/font][/color]

[list]
[*] Configure more aspects of your mods than ever before - the MCM provides a range of controls such as checkboxes, steppers, dropdowns, sliders, buttons, text, and keybinds.
[/list]
[list]
[*] [b]Optimized for PC[/b]. The MCM steps far beyond what is allowed for consoles.
[/list]
[list]
[*] [b]No ESM/ESP[/b]. The MCM does not count towards your load order.
[/list]
[list]
[*] [b]Save-safe[/b]. The MCM never sinks its teeth into your savegame - any modifications, as with most F4SE plugins, are done in-memory.
[/list]
[list]
[*] [b]File-safe[/b]. No vanilla forms or files on disk are overriden. The MCM is completely standalone.
[/list]
[list]
[*] [b]Hotkey support and conflict detection[/b]. Assign hotkeys to mod actions to activate them on-the-fly.
[/list]

[color=#f6b26b][font=Courier New][size=5][b]★ Details ★[/b][/size][/font][/color]

Each mod's menu lives in its own directory in [code]Data\MCM\Config\ModName[/code].

User settings are located at [code]Data\MCM\Settings\ModName.ini[/code].

[b][font=Courier New][color=#6d9eeb][size=4][u]FOLDER STRUCTURE[/u][/size][/color][/font][/b]
[spoiler][font=Courier New]Data/
└── MCM/
    ├── Config/
    │   ├── Mod1/
    │   │   ├── config.json         (Required) Menu Layout
    │   │   ├── lib.swf             (Optional) Image library
    │   │   ├── keybinds.json       (Optional) Keybind Definitions
    │   │   └── settings.ini        (Optional) Mod Setting Defaults
    │   ├── Mod2/
    │   │   └── ...
    │   └── Mod3/
    │       └── ...
    └─── Settings/
         ├── Mod1.ini               Mod Settings for Mod1
         ├── Mod2.ini               Mod Settings for Mod2
         └── Mod3.ini               Mod Settings for Mod3[/font][/spoiler]


[color=#f6b26b][font=Courier New][size=5][b]★ Installation ★[/b][/size][/font][/color]

Install with your preferred mod manager, or copy the files in the download to your Fallout 4 Data folder for a manual install.

You should see a new option in the Pause menu in-game.

If there is no Mod Config option, double-check that you have [url=http://f4se.silverlock.org/]F4SE[/url] installed and running!

[color=#f6b26b][font=Courier New][size=5][b]★ Requirements ★[/b][/size][/font][/color]

Fallout 4 (check Files tab for version requirements).
[url=http://f4se.silverlock.org/]Fallout 4 Script Extender (F4SE)[/url], 0.4.2 or higher.

[color=#f6b26b][font=Courier New][size=5][b]★ Team ★[/b][/size][/font][/color]

[url=http://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/users/17152929/]registrator2000[/url] - Backend: architecture design, F4SE plugin and Papyrus bindings
[url=http://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/users/6719842/]Neanka[/url] - Frontend: MCM interface - everything you see and interact with
[url=http://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/users/5694891/]shadowslasher410[/url] - Frontend: MCM control design, logo & consultation

[color=#f6b26b][font=Courier New][size=5][b]★ More ★[/b][/size][/font][/color]

Please report issues at the [url=https://github.com/Neanka/MCM_0.1_AS3/issues]Github issue tracker[/url]!
Suggestions are welcome - please post them in the [url=https://discord.gg/4VtnEnb]MCM Discord[/url] server.

Source code:
[url=https://github.com/reg2k/f4mcm]Github[/url] [C++] (F4SE plugin)
[url=https://github.com/Neanka/MCM_0.1_AS3]Github[/url] [AS3] (Interface)

[color=#f6b26b][font=Courier New][size=5][b]★ Acknowledgements ★[/b][/size][/font][/color]

Many thanks to the F4SE team for their tireless work extending the core feature-set of the game. The MCM would not be possible without it. Most modders make mods - these people make the tools that make other mods possible. They work behind-the-scenes; outside the limelight. Without them, the modding scene would be decidedly less interesting and very much more mundane.
author: reg2k - Neanka - shadowslasher410

name: Caliente's Beautiful Bodies Enhancer -CBBE-
summary: The well-known body enhancer made famous in Skyrim is back for Fallout 4 debut
description:
[center][img]http://i.imgur.com/k2z0DwA.png[/img]
[size=5]The most customizable body mod available is back![/size][/center]

[center]Together with BodySlide, you can customize your character's appearance to the limits of your imagination, and make any outfit fit well!
So be sure to download and install [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/mods/25]BodySlide[/url] for maximum potential![/center]
[color=#ffff60]Contents[/color]
[list]
[*][color=#a0fff0]Description[/color]
[*][color=#a0fff0]Options[/color]
[*][color=#a0fff0]Installation[/color]
[*][color=#a0fff0]FAQ[/color]
[*][color=#a0fff0]Recommended Mods[/color]
[*][color=#a0fff0]Uninstalling[/color]
[*][color=#a0fff0]Credits[/color]
[/list]
[img]http://i.imgur.com/JxsH2xL.png[/img]
CBBE replaces the vanilla female body that all adult female characters (player and NPC) share, with one that is fully customizable with the [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/mods/25]BodySlide[/url] tool. By default there are three nude options available that don't require anything but installation, as well as [url=http://vintagedancer.com/1950s/1950s-lingerie]lore-friendly underwear[/url] styles for these body shapes (using Beth's own unused underwear meshes).

Included in the main installer are bodies, textures, all vanilla and DLC outfits, and BodySlide addons for the body and outfits.
There are also morph files for the body and the outfits included, which are used for in-game morphing with [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/mods/12631/]LooksMenu[/url] or other script mods.

As of version 2.7, the original 'high poly' body is now only found in BodySlide. If you prefer a minor visual upgrade at the cost of not having dismemberment, feel free to use BodySlide to build that mesh.

[img]http://i.imgur.com/yVLswK8.png[/img]
Please note that these shapes are what you get when the [i]very centre[/i] of the weight/body morph triangle is selected. The shape will then morph according to where ever you select in the triangle, just as the vanilla body does.

[color=#ffff60][size=3]Slim:[/size][/color] Slender shape for fans of slimmer ladies.
[color=#ffff60][size=3]Curvy:[/size][/color] Based on the modern ideal.
[color=#ffff60][size=3]Vanilla:[/size][/color] Default body, but better!
[color=#ffff60][size=3]NeverNude:[/size][/color] The above bodies, but with underwear.
Be sure to grab [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/mods/25]BodySlide[/url] for 100% customisability with CBBE, including body physics!

[color=#ffff60][size=3]Texture Resources[/size][/color]

Included in the optional downloads is a Texture Resource archive providing the source  photoshop .psd files with Diffuse, Normal, and Specular textures.  These files include the base texture data used by CBBE, as well as the following additional options (as hidden layers that can be activated.) 
[list]
[*]a total of 9 pubic hair options... three by me, three by Jeir, and three by XunAmarox (used with permission and originally part of her "CBBE Innies" mod)
[*]pinker nipple option by Jeir, and another nipple variant with diffuse/spec/normals by XunAmarox
[*]An alternative vulva style by XunAmarox. 
[/list]If you like XunAmarox's options, please remember to endorse her mod and thank her! [img]http://forums.nexusmods.com/public/style_emoticons/dark/smile.gif[/img]

Permissions to use these textures to create optional variants, including distributing those variants as mods are granted, provided credits are given, and links to this mod as well as CBBEInnies are provided ( if you use those layers ). Please don't redistribute the source .psd files, though!

[b][i]Installation and use of these texture files is detailed in the readme file included in the archive. Software such as Photoshop or GIMP can open the provided .psd files.[/i][/b]

[img]http://i.imgur.com/VvfymCB.png[/img]
Use NotePad/WordPad to open your "Fallout4Custom.ini" file located in "Documents\My Games\Fallout4". If you use MO2, then you need to edit the one through its built-in ini editor. Once opened, copy the following three lines and paste it into the Fallout4Custom.ini.

[i][Archive]
bInvalidateOlderFiles=1
sResourceDataDirsFinal=[/i]

[size=1](No, that's not a typo. If Fallout4Custom.ini doesn't exist, you need to create it yourself manually by opening NotePad/WordPad, copy/pasting in the three lines, and saving it as Fallout4Custom.ini. Make sure when you save, the file ends with .ini instead of .ini.txt or .ini.ini!
If the lines are already there, you only need to edit their values. Note that this requirement is for all mods that have loose files, not just CBBE.)[/size]

[color=#ffff60][size=3]Manual[/size][/color][list=1]
[*] Download the main file archive and open it with 7zip. Do not use anything but 7zip.
[*] Install the [i]contents[/i] of the 00 Required folder into Steam/SteamApps/common/Fallout 4/Data.
[*] Install the [i]contents[/i] of the body you want (one of folders 01-05) into Fallout 4/Data. Skip this if you only want the nude Slim body.
[*] Install the [i]contents[/i] of the outfit replacers you want (06-17) into Fallout 4/Data, according to what body shape you're using and what DLC you have.
[*] Optional: Install the contents of the BodySlide folders (18-21) into Fallout 4/Data. For BodySlide.
[*] Optional: Install the contents of the Tri and F4EE folders (22-26) into Fallout 4/Data. For in-game morphs. Requires BodySlide folders and LooksMenu.
[*]Optional: Download the Optional CBBE Reduced file and open it with 7zip. Copy steps 2-6 to install these files. 
[/list][color=#ffff60][size=3]Mod Managers[/size][/color]
[list=1]
[*]Install the archive to Vortex/Mod Organiser or other mod managers like you would do with any other mod. Always install Optional files after the main file.
[/list]
Note:
- The CBBE.esp plugin is optional if you have neck-covering hair or don't use the LooksMenu in-game morphs. See FAQ4.
- If you have issues with editing the .ini, [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/articles/3831]check the comments here to see if anything helps[/url].

[color=#ffff60][size=3]Installation Tutorials[/size][/color]
[url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Wkwtgp3x25s]https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Wkwtgp3x25s[/url] by Gopher

[b]Beautiful showcase by Hodilton and an in-depth tutorial by Gopher:[/b]
[youtube]hU8hbYSwivs[/youtube][youtube]Wkwtgp3x25s[/youtube]


[img]http://i.imgur.com/lZ4QzO3.png[/img]
[size=3][color=#ffff60]Q1:[/color] Why are the bodies not changing after I install?[/size]
[color=#a0fff0]A1:[/color] You didn't follow the instructions in the installation section. Make sure you have followed them exactly.
[color=#a0fff0]A2:[/color] If NPCs have changed, but your player character hasn't, you're using a mod that changes the directory for the player body meshes/textures, such as 'Unique Player'. You'll need to either uninstall that mod and use LooksMenu's in-game morphs instead, or install CBBE's meshes/textures manually into the Unique Player folders.
[color=#a0fff0]A3:[/color] If your player character has changed, but NPCs haven't, you're using a mod that changes the directory for NPC body meshes/textures. You'll need to either uninstall whatever mod is causing it, or install the meshes/textures manually.
[color=#a0fff0]A4:[/color] You're not looking at a nude female adult character or are currently wearing an outfit that's not CBBE-compatible.
[color=#a0fff0]A5:[/color] Your mod manager is being buggy or there's a user error. The installer is bug free, or there would be more people reporting issues. If you're using NMM, make sure you're using the latest files from GitHub.

[size=3][color=#ffff60]Q2:[/color] Why am I getting patchwork body textures (black shoulder, nipple on stomach or painted-on underwear)?[/size]
[color=#a0fff0]A1:[/color] You didn't follow the instructions in the installation section. The .ini edits are not optional, and are required for [i]any[/i] mod that has loose files.
[color=#a0fff0]A2:[/color] You have a conflict somewhere. Make sure you don't have any other mods that affect or overwrote the body (e.g Horizon. See sticky in comments to link for fix.).
[color=#a0fff0]A3:[/color] You're not using a CBBE-compatible outfit or texture on a CBBE body. At the very least, you should install the vanilla outfits/default textures found in the main file's installer.
[color=#a0fff0]A4:[/color] You're using a mod that changes the directory for the body meshes/textures (e.g. Unique Player. See FAQ1 A2). 
[color=#a0fff0]A5:[/color] Elderly women are not officially supported by this mod. Install the elderly textures found [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/mods/7432]here[/url].
[color=#a0fff0]A6:[/color] Mannequins (Contraptions DLC) are not officially supported. Try [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/mods/15821]this mod[/url].

[size=3][color=#ffff60]Q3:[/color] How do I fix the texture mismatch at the back of the player/NPC head?[/size]
[color=#a0fff0]A:[/color] Activate the CBBE.esp to fix it. This only works for the player character. To fix it for NPCs, try [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/mods/44553]this mod[/url].

[size=3][color=#ffff60]Q4:[/color] Do outfits or body textures not specifically made for CBBE work with it?[/size]
[color=#a0fff0]A:[/color] Only body textures made to work with CBBE will show up correctly in all places, as CBBE has different UV maps for texture alignment. Outfits that show skin in any way need to be compatible with CBBE to match up. You'll have to (nicely) ask the creator to make a version of their mod for CBBE or convert the outfit yourself using Outfit Studio, or the textures using Photoshop/GIMP/etc.

[size=3][color=#ffff60]Q5:[/color] Will there be a male version?[/size]
[color=#a0fff0]A:[/color] There are several Bodyslide-compatible male body mods linked on the BodySlide mod page.

[size=3][color=#ffff60]Q6:[/color] How do I make this player-only?[/size]
[color=#a0fff0]A:[/color] Use the in-game morphs with BodySlide and LooksMenu to make your character have her own shape, while NPCs will have a different shape (or possibly many different shapes, if LooksMenu's BodyGen is used). While 'Unique Player' and standalone custom races exist, it is not recommended to use them, as they require far more work than simply using the in-game morphs.

[size=3][color=#ffff60]Q7:[/color] Will you put this on Bethesda.net?[/size]
[color=#a0fff0]A:[/color]  CBBE and its outfits are[url=https://bethesda.net/en/mods/fallout4/mod-detail/4061893] on Bethesda.net.[/url]


[img]https://i.imgur.com/D4MMuX6.png[/img]
[url=http://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/mods/552]Young Female Face Textures by Fuse00[/url] (combine with the normal map from [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/mods/7485/]Oni face[/url] to get a nice, smooth, 20-something year old appearance)
[url=http://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/mods/169]FCO - HD Eyes by Drumber[/url]
[url=http://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/mods/288]Higher Female Protagonist Voice by Asterra
[/url][url=http://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/mods/12631/]LooksMenu[/url] and the [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/mods/24830/]LooksMenu Customization Compendium[/url] for a better UI and more options when creating your character's face.

[img]http://i.imgur.com/BFaGwKC.png[/img]
Uninstall using the mod manager you installed with. If you used BodySlide, hold Alt while clicking BatchBuild to delete everything built. If using MO2, make sure you've deleted any related output folders.

author: Ousnius and Caliente

name: LooksMenu
summary: Adds engine modifications to improve character creation.
description:
[center][url=https://www.patreon.com/bePatron?u=5334755][img]https://c5.patreon.com/external/logo/become_a_patron_button.png[/img][/url][/center]
[size=4]Requires [url=http://f4se.silverlock.org/]F4SE[/url] for game version [color=#ff0000]1.5.157[/color] or greater

Features[/size]
[list]
[*]Feature list is much larger, allowing to see more items at once
[*]Facial features can now be selected from the list, rather than selecting the actual face aka select part by name
[*]Presets can now be saved/loaded
[*]CBBE Bodyslide Sliders can be used ingame if properly generated
[*]BodyGen can generate body shapes randomly for NPCs
[*]Allows for hair palette overrides (for more hair colors)
[*]Overlays can be applied overtop of the existing body
[*]Skin can be overridden to another set of textures or mesh
[*]Skin and makeup can be colorized via HSV
[/list]

[size=4]Game Version Table[/size]
[list]
[*]If you're using Game Version 1.10.26 use 1.6.2
[*]If you're using Game Version 1.10.20 use 1.6.1
[*]If you're using Game Version 1.9.4 use 1.5.0
[*]If you're using Game Version 1.8.7 use 1.1.7
[*]If you're using Game Version 1.7.22 use 1.1.5
[*]If you're using Game Version 1.7.15 use 1.1.4
[*]If you're using Game Version 1.7.12 use 1.1.2
[*]If you're using Game Version 1.7.10 use 1.1.1
[*]If you're using Game Version 1.7.9 use 1.1.0
[*]If you're using Game Version 1.6.9 use 1.0.9
[*]If you're using Game Version 1.6.3 use 1.0.8
[*]If you're using Game Version 1.5.416 use 1.0.7
[*]If you're using Game Version 1.5.307 use 1.0.6
[*]If you're using Game Version 1.5.210 use 1.0.5
[*]If you're using Game Version 1.5.205 use 1.0.3
[/list]
[size=4][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/mods/30163/]Tint Injection[/url]﻿
[/size]
See the link above for more information on how to inject tints.

[size=4]Skin Override[/size]

[size=2]This feature allows for an Actor/ActorBase's skin Forms to be overridden to an alternative, see the included Sample Skin Override for more details[/size]

[size=4]Overlays[/size]

This feature is similar to that of RaceMenu, where textures can be applied overtop of the body. Instead of altering the textures individually, it will change the entire material for the overlay. You may use either a BGSM or a BGEM material file as a target. Note that BGSM materials do not support alpha blending (BGEM does). If the texture is using a BGSM and is flagged for Skin Tint, it is possible to colorize the overlay, however colorization is not yet available through the User Interface.

Transforming is a new feature to LooksMenu's overlays where the UV map of the body can be altered for the overlay such that it can be placed where you please. The offset and scale are relative to the assigned values in the material. This means that if you create an overlay with a material where the offset or scale are altered, the overlay will add to the existing offset, and multiply the existing scale.

See the provided sample in the optional downloads section for creating overlays.
[list]
[*]The "id" of an overlay must be unique, this is the unique name used by the Script API and is analogous to "template".
[*]The "transformable" property is intended to enable/disable translation and scaling of an overlay (Full body may not make sense to transform)
[*]The "playable" property is intended to hide the template while in the UI when choosing a template
[*]Overlays can be applied to multiple slots simultaneously where the overlay may span both the body (slot 3) and hands (slot 4)
[/list]
Please note this feature is not intended to use as swappable skin textures, while this may work it is not what it is meant to do. Swapping skin textures is intended to be done with Skin Overrides


[size=4]BodyMorph[/size]

[size=2]Body Morphing is effectively in-game Bodyslide and is unique to a particular NPC. You must already have [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/mods/25/?]Bodyslide[/url] to generate morphs. When building through Bodyslide, select the "CBBE Zeroed Sliders" preset and tick "Build Morphs" this will build the files required to do in-game Bodyslide.

[/size]For male body morphs see [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/mods/24835/?]this page[/url]


[size=4]BodyGen [/size]

BodyGen is a feature that allows for random (or specific) BodyMorph generation based on guided files (Because random everything usually results in a horrifying mess) for particular NPCs, or any NPC. If an Actor is eligible for random generation, when that actor is loaded (when you first see them, or load an existing save where they have no morphs) their morph listing will be computed based on the proceeding files.

[color=#ffd966]Data/F4SE/Plugins/F4EE/BodyGen/%MODNAME%/templates.ini
Data/F4SE/Plugins/F4EE/BodyGen/%MODNAME%/morphs.ini[/color]

[size=2]e.g.[/size]
[color=#ffd966]Data/F4SE/Plugins/F4EE/BodyGen/MyMod.esp/templates.ini
Data/F4SE/Plugins/F4EE/BodyGen/MyMod.esp/morphs.ini[/color]

[size=2]The following is a sample templates.ini[/size]
[color=#999999]Random=7B Upper@0:1.0, 7B Lower@0:1.0, MuscularChest@0:1.0, MuscularArms@0:1.0, MuscularButt@0:1.0, MuscularLegs@0:1.0, Breasts@0.5:1.0[/color]

[size=2]The following is a sample morphs.ini[/size]
[color=#999999]All|Female|HumanRace=Random[/color]

[size=2]Syntax explanation for templates.ini
[/size]"#" (Pound/Hash) indicates the line is a comment (Comments should be separate lines)
[size=2]"=" (Equals) separates Template Name (Left) from the morphs (Right)[/size]
[size=2]"," (Comma) separated values indicate AND[/size]
[size=2]"|" (Bar) separated values indicate OR[/size]
[size=2]"/" (Slash) separated values indicate a "set" where everything between the next "/" or end of line are OR'd as a group[/size]
[size=2]":" (Colon) separated values indicate a uniformly distributed range where, if that morph is chosen the value is random[/size]
[size=2]"@" (At) separated values separate between the Morph name and the Value[/size]
[size=2]Increasing the frequency of the entry effectively increases the probability of that entry[/size]

[size=2]Syntax explanation for morphs.ini
"#" (Pound/Hash) indicates the line is a comment (Comments should be separate lines)[/size]
[size=2]"=" (Equals) separates the target (Left) from the templates (Right)[/size]
"|" (Bar) left of equals separates target parameters, right of equals indicates OR for multiple templates
[size=2]"," (Comma) right of equals indicates AND for multiple templates
Templates can be referenced by name from other bodygen mods[/size]

[size=2]When targeting all NPCs:
[color=#999999]All|Female|HumanRace[/color][/size]
[list]
[*]First entry "All" indicates any NPC
[*]Second entry "Female" indicates any Female or Male
[*]Third entry "HumanRace" indicates
[/list]
[size=2]When targetting a specific NPC:
[color=#999999]Fallout4.esm|2F1E=Random[/color][/size][list]
[*]Bar separates Plugin name from FormID (without mod index)
[*]This would mean only Piper would be generated with the "Random" template
[/list][size=2]More complicated templates.ini example[/size]
[color=#999999]Thick=7B Upper@1.0, 7B Lower@1.0 / MuscularChest@1.0 | Breasts@1.0, MuscularArms@1.0, MuscularButt@1.0, MuscularLegs@1.0[/color]
[size=2]This separates 7B from the Muscular morphs where it's a 50/50 probability of choosing either full 7B or Muscular, and a 50/50 split for choosing MuscularChest or Breasts[/size]

[size=2]A full list of morph names can be found when CBBE is installed at:[/size]
[color=#ffd966]Data/F4SE/Plugins/F4EE/Sliders/CBBE.esp/sliders.json[/color]
[size=2]Where the "morph" field indicates the morph name[/size]


[size=4]TintMask Resolution Feature
[/size]
[color=#ffd966]Data/F4SE/Plugins/f4ee.ini[/color]

[color=#999999][CharGen]
uTintWidth=1024
uTintHeight=1024[/color]

This setting allows you to change the maximum resolution of the face texture and associated tint masks. The game will normally always use 1024 even if you have 2048 or higher resolution face textures. People commonly experience lag when using 2048 face textures, the reason for this is the game is actually downsampling the textures to 1024. This setting allows you to stop that forced downsample and actually use the resolution of the texture you desire for example if you want to use 2048, you change uTintWidth and uTintHeight to be 2048.



If you see a grayed out Preset option, this is due to the plugin not loading, typically meaning incompatible game version

Why does this need F4SE?
For starters the game does not save/load character presets, this is impossible without the included plugin. In terms of selecting part by name the plugin is needed as the interface does not have full access to the internal variable that sets what your current part is. Without the plugin changing "Type" does not work correctly.
author: Expired6978

name: Armor and Weapon Keywords Community Resource (AWKCR)
summary: This is a master file to create a standardize framework for armor so that modders can create and modify armor (like Armorsmith) while making edits compatible with mods that adjust dynamic item naming (like Valdacil's Item Sorting). Also adds a standardized set of crafting menu categories for armor/clothing/weapons and adds two workbenches for m
description:
[url=https://www.patreon.com/Gambit77][img]http://i.imgur.com/phGsbyZ.png[/img][/url]

[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1151/images/6091-0-1458709156.png[/img]

[color=#a4c2f4][size=5]Purpose[/size][/color]
---------------------
[size=4]This resource accomplishes the following things:
1) Creates a standardized framework of keywords for: armor and weapon records for use by dynamic naming mods (and other mods that utilize these keywords to do cool things), crafting menus, and a color swap & decal swap framework.
2) Standardizes apparel slot usage to prevent conflicts between mod authors creations to increase inter usability.
3) Creates standardized armor, weapons, ammunition, and explosive workbenches to be utilized for crafting items added by mods.
4) Creates a collection of object modifications for blade weapons to recreate blade tempering from Skyrim for use by weapon modders.
[/size]

[color=#a4c2f4][size=5]Mod Users[/size][/color]
---------------------
Mods you have installed may require this file as a base resource.  Simply download and install the mod using the standard installation methods (recommended NMM: [url=http://wiki.tesnexus.com/index.php/Fallout_4_Mod_Installation]Newbie Guide[/url]).

[color=#a4c2f4][size=5]Bethesda.net Links[/size][/color]
---------------------
Xbox: All-DLC: [url=https://bethesda.net/en/mods/fallout4/mod-detail/4038275]https://bethesda.net/en/mods/fallout4/mod-detail/4038275[/url]
Xbox: No-DLC: [url=https://bethesda.net/en/mods/fallout4/mod-detail/4038282]https://bethesda.net/en/mods/fallout4/mod-detail/4038282[/url]
Xbox: Automatron Patch: [url=https://bethesda.net/en/mods/fallout4/mod-detail/4038285]https://bethesda.net/en/mods/fallout4/mod-detail/4038285[/url]
Xbox: Contraption Patch: [url=https://bethesda.net/en/mods/fallout4/mod-detail/4038287]https://bethesda.net/en/mods/fallout4/mod-detail/4038287[/url]
Xbox: Far Harbor Patch: [url=https://bethesda.net/en/mods/fallout4/mod-detail/4038293]https://bethesda.net/en/mods/fallout4/mod-detail/4038293[/url]
Xbox: Nuka World Patch: [url=https://bethesda.net/en/mods/fallout4/mod-detail/4038289]https://bethesda.net/en/mods/fallout4/mod-detail/4038289[/url]
Xbox: Vault-Tec Patch: [url=https://bethesda.net/en/mods/fallout4/mod-detail/4038286]https://bethesda.net/en/mods/fallout4/mod-detail/4038286[/url]


[color=#a4c2f4][size=5]Mod Authors[/size][/color]
---------------------
[color=#a4c2f4][size=4]Purpose of this resource:[/size][/color]
Create a standardize framework for armor so that modders can create and modify armor while making edits compatible with mods that adjust dynamic item naming (like Valdacil's Item Sorting).
Adds a standardized set of crafting menu categories for armor/clothing/weapons and adds tow new crafting benches, the Armorsmith and Weaponsmith workbenches.  This was done so that modders could share category menu keywords in order to lower our combined footprint in order to help the community avoid the formlist cap bug.  And as a bonus to consolidate everybody's crafting recipes onto just 2 benches for convenience.

[color=#a4c2f4][size=4]Why create this:[/size][/color]
Problem 1: many users want items sorted better in their inventory
Solution 1: rename items so they start with a tag that groups and sorts them alphabetically
Drawback: modifying the name of the record causes conflict with any other mod that modifies the same record.  As simple rename causes incompatibility

Solution 2: use Fallout 4's dynamic naming mechanism to automatically tag armor and weapons so that the base record need not be modified
Advantage 1: since base records aren't modified, this method is compatible with mods that adjust stats, models, slots, etc
Advantage 2: mods that add new items can be tagged for sorting without addition work or compatibility patches
Complication: keywords on weapons are really consistent, but keywords for armor are far less consistent with many classifications missing keywords altogether.

So this is a resource to fix Bethesda's inconsistent keywords on armor pieces and force all armor items to run dynamic naming rules.  If not using a mod that modifies the dynamic naming rules, then these edits will have no negative effect.  However, by using this resource your mod will the primed for dynamic naming.

Vanilla Fallout 4 gives us the following ways to identify armor
ArmorTypeHat - hats
ArmorBodyPartHead - helmets
ArmorBodyPartEyes - glasses/goggles
ClothingDogmeat + playerCannotEquip - Dogmeat only equipable
ArmorBodyPartChest - chest armor overlays
dn_PowerArmor_Helmet
dn_PowerArmor_LeftArm
dn_PowerArmor_LeftLeg
dn_PowerArmor_RightArm
dn_PowerArmor_RightLeg
dn_Powerarmor_Torso

And that is it.  As you can see we're missing the same left/right arm/leg breakdown for armor overlay pieces (leather, combat armor, etc).  We're also missing an standard keywords on clothing, armor suits, and the like.  The is also no Super Mutant keyword.

This resource solves that problem by adding keywords to tag armor/cosmetics so that mod authors can properly classify their items.  See tutorial article attached to this page for more specifics.

If you are creating a mod that adds new wearable items, use this resource and one of the keywords and follow the instructions outlined in this article, then your new items will be prepared for dynamic naming automatically with no need for compatibility patches.  The resource includes all vanilla items properly tagged and prepared for dynamic naming.

[color=#a4c2f4][size=4]Instructions for Mod Authors[/size][/color]
--------------------
Make ArmorKeywords.esm a master on your .esp and link it as a required file when you upload your mod to Nexus.
To override an item, start with the resource item and copy as override into your .esp.  All pieces to run dynamic naming are already set as detailed below.
To create a new item, start with a resource item that is close then copy as a new item into your .esp.  All pieces to run dynamic naming should carryover.  Adjust community keywords as needed to properly tag and classify your item.

[color=#a4c2f4][size=4]Details[/size][/color]
---------------------
Dynamic naming works off the INNR records in FO4Edit (latest version calls these Instance Naming Rule).  These rules run as If...Then statements looking for keywords on the object and applying a piece of the item's name if a matching rule is found.

However, by default many armor items don't run dynamic naming rules because they didn't have any upgrades available in vanilla.  In order to force them to run dynamic naming rules the INRD of the item records must be set to the EditorID of the rule to run for that item.  Additionally, the OBTS must have a minimal definition as follows (if the item already has OBTE defined, do not change it; below is only for items where OBTE is undefined):

OBTE - Count = 1
OBTS Parts A Count = 0
OBTS Parts B Count = 0
OBTS Unknown (first instance) = 00 00 00 00 FF FF 01
OBTS Unknown (second instance) = 00 00

With INRD and OBTE set, it is then up to the author of sorting mods or mods that modify naming rules how to utilize the keywords the item is tagged with.  In this way, sorting mods do not need to modify armor records to accomplish sorting and mods that add new armor/clothing can be sorted automatically as long both modders use these community keywords.

[color=#a4c2f4][size=4]Mods[/size][/color]
----------------------
This community resource was initially envisioned and realized by Valdacil and Gambit77 in order to make Valdacil's Item Sorting (VIS) compatible with Armorsmith.  Once we got started, we realized this would be a great framework to universalize the armor modding community and make life easier for both armor modders and sorting modders.

name: BodySlide and Outfit Studio
summary: An easy to use tool for customizing bodies and outfits, creating new bodies and outfits, and converting outfits between body types.
description:
[center][img]http://i.imgur.com/ZWYVoWi.png[/img][/center]
[color=#ffff60]Contents[/color]
[list]
[*][color=#a0fff0]Description[/color]
[*][color=#a0fff0]Installation[/color]
[*][color=#a0fff0]Addons[/color]
[*][color=#a0fff0]FAQ[/color]
[*][color=#a0fff0]Uninstalling[/color]
[*][color=#a0fff0]Credits[/color]
[/list]
[img]http://i.imgur.com/JxsH2xL.png[/img]
BodySlide has been updated with the addition of Fallout 4 support, bringing an enormous list of new features once again.
Unprecedented flexibility coupled with marvelous ease of use allows you to have all the outfits and shapes you've ever dreamed of.

For the latest updates, known issues and more, check out the sticky comment and the changes tab!

Note: As of version 3.8 of BodySlide, this utility requires OpenGL 3.3. If your card does not support this (cards that meet the minimum system requirements for Skyrim should), you should not update BodySlide until you can get a better card.

[color=#ffff60][size=3]Discord[/size][/color]
[url=https://discord.gg/2qg2Rzw][img]https://i.imgur.com/QI1YKyD.png[/img][/url]

[size=4]Check out the official [url=https://github.com/ousnius/BodySlide-and-Outfit-Studio/wiki/Guides-and-Documentation]documentation and guides[/url] here on the [url=https://github.com/ousnius/BodySlide-and-Outfit-Studio/wiki]Wiki[/url][/size]

[color=#ffff60][size=3]BodySlide[/size][/color]
[list]
[*]Customize outfits and bodies without the need to edit mesh files!
[*]Use sliders to create your personal shapes.
[*]Fancy outfit filtering quickly shows you the outfits you want, while the textured preview windows show you what you are going to get.
[*]Building morph files allows you to use the sliders in-game using [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/mods/12631]LooksMenu[/url] or other script mods.
[/list]
[color=#ffff60][size=3]Outfit Studio[/size][/color]
[list]
[*]The power of an experienced modder is now in your hands!
[*]Convert outfits between bodies, create your own BodySlide sliders, correct animation weights and edit meshes otherwise.
[*]If you know what you're doing, you can even edit static meshes.
[*]Import and export .FBX, .OBJ and .NIF meshes.
[*]Barely any previous modding experience required.
[/list]
[color=#ffff60][size=3]Physics[/size][/color]
[list]
[*]With the BodySlide addon on the CBBE mod page comes the "CBBE Body Physics" set.
[*]Choose it in the Outfit/Body drop down of BodySlide and build it. Your nude body should now have breast physics.
[/list]
[color=#ffff60][size=3]GitHub Repository[/size][/color]
[list]
[*][url=https://github.com/ousnius/BodySlide-and-Outfit-Studio]https://github.com/ousnius/BodySlide-and-Outfit-Studio[/url]
[*]Source code and similar things (GPLv3+ license).
[/list]
[img]http://i.imgur.com/VvfymCB.png[/img]
Add the following lines to your "Fallout4Custom.ini" file located in "Documents\My Games\Fallout4", or through your mod manager if applicable:

[i][Archive]
bInvalidateOlderFiles=1
sResourceDataDirsFinal=[/i]

[size=1](No, that's not a typo. Yes, all three lines.)[/size]

[color=#ffff60][size=3]Manual[/size][/color]
[list=1]
[*]Download and open the archive with 7Zip, and extract the 'Tools' folder to Steam/SteamApps/Common/Fallout 4/Data.
[*]After you're done, the executable can be found in the "Steam/SteamApps/Common/Fallout 4/Data/Tools/BodySlide" folder.
[/list]
[color=#ffff60][size=3]Vortex[/size][/color][list=1]
[*]Download and install the archive to Vortex like you would do with any other mod and enable it.
[*]Make sure your Vortex deploys correctly by clicking the "Deploy Mods" toolbar button in the "Mods" section.
[*]In the dashboard go to "Add Tool".
[*]In the "Target" field, choose the "BodySlide x64.exe" file from your game installation's real data folder, NOT the Vortex mods folder: "Steam/SteamApps/Common/Fallout 4/Data/Tools/BodySlide".
[*]When BodySlide is open, click on the Settings button and ensure the Game Data Path points to your actual game data folder, e.g. "C:/Games/Steam/SteamApps/Common/Fallout 4/Data/".
[*]Install any BodySlide addons such as body mods and outfits to Vortex as well.
[*][url=https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1704/images/201/201-1537370120-1864925415.gif]GIF of the process[/url]
[/list]
[color=#ffff60][size=3]Mod Organizer[/size][/color][list=1]
[*]Download and install the archive to MO like you would do with any other mod and enable it.
[*]In MO2, go to the Data tab on the right, locate the BodySlide x64.exe in the Tools/BodySlide folder, right click it, and add it as an executable. You can then launch it through MO2.
[*]When BodySlide is open, click on the Settings button and ensure the Game Data Path points to your actual game data folder, e.g. "C:/Games/Steam/SteamApps/Common/Fallout 4/Data/".
[*]Install any BodySlide addons such as body mods and outfits to MO as well.
[*]After BatchBuilding, the files will appear in your Overwrites folder. [url=https://www.afkmods.com/index.php?/topic/8960-slessefo4-bodyslide-and-mo2-what-to-do-with-bodyslide-output-files/]You will need to create a new mod folder, for example 'BodySlide Output', move the files from Overwrites into this folder, and then enable it.[/url]﻿
[*]We do not troubleshoot MO installation issues, please ask for help in Mod Organizer communities.
[/list]

[color=#ffff60][size=3]Updating[/size][/color]
You can uninstall old/install new, or just install the new files and overwrite if prompted. Just be sure to back up your personal presets/groups/bodies/outfits in the SliderSets/SliderPresets/ShapeData/SliderGroups folders so they don't get deleted, whether by accident or buggy mod manager.

[color=#a0fff0]Note:
[/color]If you're experiencing any problems, locate the main Mod Organizer/Nexus Mod Manager and BodySlide executables and set them to always run with admin elevation.

[img]http://i.imgur.com/lZ4QzO3.png[/img]
[size=3][color=#ffff60]Q1:[/color] If I have an issue, what would you suggest I do before anything (including posting questions)?[/size]
[color=#a0fff0]A:[/color] Read the mod description, especially the FAQ. Read the stickies. Reinstall BodySlide. Install BodySlide manually if your mod manager of choice fails to do its main purpose.

[size=3][color=#ffff60]Q2:[/color] Why is there an error when building sets, saving projects or writing other files?[/size]
[color=#a0fff0]A:[/color] You might have to set BodySlide (and tools that launch BodySlide such as NMM and MO) to always run as an admin.

[size=3][color=#ffff60]Q3:[/color] Why are the changes I am building not appearing in-game?[/size]
[color=#a0fff0]A:[/color] Make sure you have added the line "bInvalidateOlderFiles=1" to your "Fallout4Custom.ini" file in the "Documents\My Games\Fallout4" folder.
- Check if your player character or NPC is using a custom race, or your outfit a plugin that points to a different meshes folder (unique player mods).
If so, you will have to manually move the built files to the correct directories for the race/NPC.
- Make sure your character has its weight at the very centre of the weight triangle if you want the body to match what you see in the preview window.
- Take a look at the "last modified" date of the files you built and check if it changed (see Q2).
- If you use MO2, BodySlide will generally place the built meshes into your overwrites folder. You need to turn that into its own mod in MO2 each time you do a Build/BatchBuild.
- If you use NMM, make sure it's the latest version from GitHub.

[size=3][color=#ffff60]Q4:[/color] Why does my BodySlide not contain any bodies or outfits?[/size]
[color=#a0fff0]A:[/color] You need to install addons for BodySlide, such as the CBBE body or various other mods that support BodySlide.

If you're using NMM/Vortex/MO, make sure that you've correctly linked the BodySlide directory/executable to the program and are running it through them properly. Sometimes you may have to redo the shortcuts to BodySlide, especially with MO2. See the installation part of the description. Also, make sure that when you click on 'Settings' in BodySlide, the Game Data Path is your game data path, i.e. "Steam/SteamApps/Common/Fallout 4/Data". 

[size=3][color=#ffff60]Q5:[/color] Why do I get an error saying "Failed to find game install" or "Failed to load Setup.xrc file."?[/size]
[color=#a0fff0]A: [/color]Registry isn't set, or BodySlide wasn't installed properly. Launch Fallout 4 using the Steam launcher instead of F4SE to fix the registry, and try launching BodySlide again. If it still doesn't work, make sure all the files are installed correctly and you're running it as admin. If you have to set the directory manually, ensure it's pointing to Fallout 4/Data. Try downloading and installing the mod with all anti-virus software disabled. If you're using NMM, make sure you're using the latest files from GitHub.
Note that Vortex/MO aren't perfect and may not install all of the files, especially Setup.xrc. If so, you may have to install manually.

[size=3][color=#ffff60]Q6:[/color] Why do my outfits or bodies say "No Image" all over them?[/size]
[color=#a0fff0]A: [/color]You need to install correctly, according to the instructions, and you need to make sure you're installing/using everything through your mod manager. You also need to launch Fallout 4 from the default/Steam launcher (not F4SE) to sort out the registry, or manually set the directory to point to Fallout 4/Data through the 'Settings' button. Only then BS/OS will be able to find and load the textures.

[size=3][color=#ffff60]Q7:[/color] Why am I getting a lot of distortion for very small but detailed meshes?[/size]
[color=#a0fff0]A:[/color] Fallout 4's meshes are saved using 16-bit half-precision positional values. What this means is, for meshes that are very small but at the same time have many polygons, there can be a lot of distortion because it's not precise enough.

The full precision checkbox in the shape properties of Outfit Studio makes the shape it has been activated for save with the full 32-bit single-precision positional values that previous games had, so there will be no distortion. This should only be used when you really need it, though.

[size=3][color=#ffff60]Q8:[/color] How does in-game morphing work?[/size]
[color=#a0fff0]A:[/color] For in-game morphing to function, your bodies and outfits need to have so called TRI morph files built for them.
CBBE and its vanilla outfits already come with pre-built and attached morph files, so you can use it out of the box.

If you are customizing the body or any custom outfits using BodySlide, then you have to check the "Build Morphs" checkbox before building.
Only then the built set can be morphed in-game using [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/mods/12631/]LooksMenu[/url] or other script mods. For LooksMenu, you need the slider definition JSON file coming with CBBE (or potentially other body mods).

[size=3][color=#ffff60]Q9:[/color] Why does the FBX import create weird texture coordinates?[/size]
[color=#a0fff0]A:[/color] You have to split all edges of your mesh along the UV seams. The NIF format only supports one texture coordinate per vertex, so UV seams need duplicated vertices.

[size=3][color=#ffff60]Q10:[/color] Why do I get an error saying "OpenGL context is not OK" for Outfit Studio or the preview window?[/size]
[color=#a0fff0]A:[/color] This means the driver of the graphics chip used doesn't support the version of OpenGL that's required. Make sure to update all of your graphics drivers (manually). In case there are multiple graphics chips built into your PC, set BodySlide to run with the dedicated one only.

[size=3][color=#ffff60]Q11:[/color] I didn't save my preset. Can I get the slider configuration using the created meshes?[/size]
[color=#a0fff0]A:[/color] No. If you don't save a preset, then it's gone. Your only option is to try and recreate it.

[size=3][color=#ffff60]Q12:[/color] I use Vortex/MO, and even though I've installed a body mod, BodySlide is blank/Outfit Studio throws up an error. How do I fix this?[/size]
[color=#a0fff0]A:[/color] You need to make sure the body you have installed is actually BodySlide-compatible, and you need to follow the installation instructions. With Vortex, you need to run BodySlide/Outfit Studio from your actual game folder. With MO, please contact the MO devs/read their own documentation.

[size=3][color=#ffff60]Q13:[/color] There's [s]scary red[/s] shiny gold text at the bottom of BodySlide. Wat do?[/size]
[color=#a0fff0]A:[/color] This was added in BodySlide v.5.5.0. The [s]red[/s] gold colour means you have more than one option in BodySlide for the same thing. If you right-click it, you will see these options. If you click on one, BodySlide will swap to it in the Outfit/Body drop-down menu. If you check the box next to the [s]red[/s] gold text, it will turn blue. This means BodySlide will only build this mesh when you BatchBuild, reducing the number of options you have to select when BatchBuilding.

[img]http://i.imgur.com/BFaGwKC.png[/img]
Uninstalling BodySlide is as simple as deleting the BodySlide folder or mod you originally installed, manually or using NMM/MO.

Removing outfit files created with BodySlide involves deleting the appropriate meshes within the "Data/meshes" directory.
Only you know which ones those might be.

name: High FPS Physics Fix
summary:
Untie the game speed from the framerate. Fixes physical bugs. Reduces loading time.
Now there is a separate version for VR.
description:
[color=#6aa84f][font=Arial][b][size=4][center]Features[/center][/size][/b][/font][/color][list]
[*][size=3]Unlock FPS.[/size]
[*][size=3]Untie the game speed from the framerate, [/size][size=3]allowing you to play at any FPS values at normal speed.[/size]
[*][size=3]Fixed stuttering. The game will become smoother. No more need for iFPSClamp=60 and it works with any FPS.[/size]
[*][size=3]Fixes a problem with dialogs.[/size]
[*][size=3]Jump height fix at high FPS.[/size]
[*][size=3]Reduces loading time. The effect is especially noticeable if game is on SSD.[/size]
[*][size=3]Ability to disable animation on loading screens to [color=#ffff00]significantly[/color] reduce loading time.[/size]
[*][size=3]Ability to shorten post-loading animation.[/size]
[*][size=3]Ability to upscale the game window to full screen when using low resolution.﻿[/size]
[*][size=3]Ability to use G-Sync and HDR in windowed mode.﻿[/size]
[*][size=3]Locks mouse cursor in the game window (fixes a mouse glitch when working with multiple monitors).﻿[/size]
[*][size=3]Fix drop read speed from the disk during loading screens.﻿[/size]
[*][size=3]Fix rotation speed of objects.[/size]
[*][size=3]Fix [/size][size=3]rotation speed in workshop mode.[/size]
[*][size=3]Fixes a problem of stuck NPC and [/size][size=3]getting stuck when you access to the terminal and power armor.[/size]
[*][size=3]Fix lockpick rotation speed and sound during lockpicking.[/size]
[*][size=3]Fix animation speed in Pip-Boy when using FallUI mod.﻿﻿[/size]
[*][size=3]Fix wind speed (clothes and hair).[/size]
[*][size=3]Fixed a bug with a white screen when the player falls (from the third person). [/size]
[*][size=3]Displays FPS, loading time and other information in the on-screen display (OSD).﻿[/size]
[/list]
[size=3]The mod will be useful for any FPS.[/size]
[size=3]Customize mod by editing the HighFPSPhysicsFix.ini file, which is located in game folder Fallout 4\Data\F4SE\Plugins or in mod folder: true - enabled, false - disabled. Also you can create your own settings file 'HighFPSPhysicsFix_custom.ini' and enter only the settings you changed.
[/size][center][/center]

[color=#6aa84f][font=Arial][b][size=4][center]Requirements[/center][/size][/b][/font][/color]
[size=3]Fallout 4 - 1.10.138.0-1.10.163.0, 1.10.980.0-1.10.984.0, 1.11.159.0-1.11.191.0
A version for VR is available.[/size]
[size=3][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/mods/42147]F4SE - 0.6.20+[/url][/size]
[center][color=#6aa84f][font=Arial][size=4][b]Installation[/b][/size][/font]
[/color][/center][size=3]
Dowload the file for your version of the game (in the file description) from the [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/mods/44798?tab=files]"Files" tab[/url].
Use any mod manager ("Mod manager download" button) or unpack the archive manually into the game folder ("Manual download" button).
Download and install [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/mods/47327?tab=files]Address Library for F4SE Plugins[/url] for your version of the game.﻿
Download and install [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/mods/42147]Fallout 4 Script Extender (F4SE)[/url] for your version of the game - Read the installation instructions and always launch the game through F4SE (f4se_loader.exe file).
[b]To make sure that the mod is loaded correctly[/b]:
[list]
[*][size=3]In the f4se.log file (in the C:\Users\<username>\Documents\My Games\Fallout4\F4SE folder) there should be a line "plugin [b]HighFPSPhysicsFix.dll[/b] (00000001 High FPS Physics Fix ... ) [b]loaded correctly[/b]". [/size]
[*][size=3]In this folder there should be a file HighFPSPhysicsFix.log.[/size]
[/list][/size]
[color=#6aa84f][font=Arial][b][size=4][center]Compatibility﻿[/center][/size][/b][/font][/color][size=3]
If using ENB, disable "ForceVSync" in the enblocal.ini file and also set "Vertical Sync" to Application Driven in the driver settings. Manage the "EnableVSync" setting in the mod's settings.

If you are using the "BorderlessUpscale" setting and are having problems with the ENB effects, set the "DisableBufferResizing" setting in the "[Display]" section to "true".
[/size]
[size=3]The mod must be compatible with others.[/size]

[color=#6aa84f][font=Arial][b][size=3][center]Credits[/center][/size][/b][/font][/color]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/users/36620665?tab=user+files]R3zy[/url] for providing the source of [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/18160]Havok Fix[/url] for Skyrim SE.
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/users/5217635?tab=user+files]SlavicPotato[/url] for providing the source of [url=https://github.com/SlavicPotato/SSEDisplayTweaks]SSE Display Tweaks[/url] for Skyrim SE.
F4SE's team.

The source is available on [url=https://github.com/AntoniX35/HighFPSPhysicsFix]GitHub[/url].

name: Just Another Piper Outfit (FG Bodyslide)
summary: A refit of Friffy's Just Another Piper Outfit to Fusion Girl.
description:
While it's a modder's resource now, as [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/mods/21114]Friffy[/url] abandoned it, I found it too nice to be sitting around. So I converted it to Fusiongirl and fixed a few issues, like the Bandana clipping through the collar and there being a second pair of shoes in the outfit. It's a refit suitable for larger sizes you can reach with FG, if you like to use them. Due to game physics, some poses can make the clothes clip through.

This is mainly a bodyslide, so you need the original first (modder's resource or not, they still did most of the work). Then download mine and let it override if it asks. This was a lot more challenging than my previous refits, so I hope you like it, hit the buttons, and all that Jazz.

name: People Live In - Roadside Pines Motel Patch Hub
summary: An automated FOMOD patch hub for conflicts and general integration patches involving People Live In - Roadside Pines Motel.
description:
[list][font=Trebuchet MS][size=3]This mod page hosts a FOMOD auto-patcher for [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/mods/60711]People Live In - Roadside Pines Motel[/url]. This patcher makes it easy and convenient to select and install patches you may need when using the mod.[/size][/font][/list][size=2]
[line]
[/size][b][font=Tahoma][size=5]FEATURES[/size][/font][/b][size=2]

[/size][list]
[*][font=Trebuchet MS][size=3]The FOMOD installer automatically detects supported mods, but allows you to adjust selections as you please.[/size][/font]
[*][font=Trebuchet MS][size=3]In cases where more information is needed, the FOMOD installer will directly prompt you with additional pages.[/size][/font]
[*][font=Trebuchet MS][size=3]Patches are marked as light plugins (esl-flagged esp) for minimal impact on load orders.[/size][/font]
[/list][size=2]
[line]
[/size][b][font=Tahoma][size=5]FAQ[/size][/font][/b][size=2]

[/size][list][size=3][b]Why is this a separate mod page?[/b][/size]

[list][size=3]I felt I had too many individual patches available, so I decided to make a FOMOD installer for them. However, maintaining that FOMOD installer means I need to update the whole thing any time a new patch is installed, and not all mod managers track file versions separately from page versions.

So, for convenience, I moved the patches to this page so that the version number for the patcher can be tracked independently from the main mod's version number. The separate page also allows me to add all of the patched mods as requirements in the nexus UI for tracking purposes without making it seem as though the main mod requires them.
[/size][/list][/list]

[center][url=https://mod.kitchen/][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1151/images/97379/97379-1760798100-1146501245.png[/img][/url][url=https://discord.gg/adCeFQK][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1151/images/97379/97379-1760798106-2064016897.png[/img][/url][/center]
author: Glitchfinder

name: Sandbag Fortifications
summary: Update 5.0 organizes the 50 or so objects via script injection. Constructible military themed structures. Trenches, bunkers, Dragon's Teeth, Sandbag posts, barbed wire fences and barricades, camouflage nets, barriers and more.
description:
[left][size=3]There was a surprising lack of fortifications in the base game with only 2 small guard towers and I felt like more should be added. Over the course of 4 updates/installements we've added 51 constructible fortifications from bunkers, to sandbag barriers, to trenches, and many other things.[/size][/left][size=3]
[b][u][color=#ffff00]PLEASE READ UNINSTALLTION BELOW![/color][/u][/b]
[center]

[youtube]AHT4_iGXX1k[/youtube][youtube]EWQgpy5DDpA[/youtube][/center]

[/size][size=3][center][/center][center][/center][/size][line]
[size=3][center]Structures[/center]
Trenches - Short, long, fortified, and corner trenches - 5 snap-able trenches with which you can fortify your settlements. Build one or two for decent protection or go all out and make a trench system. 

Bunkers - Concrete bunkers for building strongpoints across the Commonwealth. Varies sizes and designs for every situation. 

Heavy Machine Gun Nest - Another strongpoint structure with a unique armor package add-on available for improved protection. 

Sandbag Cover - A stack of snap-able sandbags and a "pillar" for strengthening any area. Works great with the trenches as well. 

Dragon's Teeth - You might have seen these obstacles in pictures of the Siegfried Line, they're designed to stop vehicles or channel them into kill-zones. Perfect for stopping mechanized troops in Power Armor. There are 3 teeth to choose from, each is of different height and slightly different design. 

Barbed Wire Fences and Barricades - 3 fences, 3 barricades, Czech hedgehog (wood and metal), pointed stake - Fences and log structures wrapped with barbed wire deny the enemy of further advance and can be used to tunnel them into killzones. 

Quonset Hut - A medium sized hut made of corrugated galvanized steel. It can be made a living quarter in varies areas of the Wasteland. Thanks to Ethreon for the mesh.

Watchtower - Survey the area and watch out of enemy skirmishers from this fortified position. 

Improvised Defensive Positions - Not like you're going to have a working defense industry 200 years after a nuclear war. Improvise a picnic table, dig a foxhole, or stack some sandbag ups--these positions are combined from the sturdiest materials found laying around.

Field Command - Camouflaged position with enough space to accommodate tables and weapon trunks to plan out your assaults.  

Tent - A sandbag fortified tent.
 
Light Sandbag Post - A lightly protected sandbag and wooden structure good for checkpoints.

Heavy Sandbag Post - A heavily fortified version of the light post; provides good cover in hot zones. 

Camouflaged Post - Camouflaged wooden outpost good for overlooking forest areas in which it can blend in. 

Defensive Positions - Two sandbag mounds provide moderate cover for a single man. 

Camouflage Nets - Three camouflage nets to provide cover for the Defensive Positions, turrets, and artillery howitzers. 

Barriers - Inspired by the Hesco barriers these wooden shell and dirt filled cubes are perfect for thick and high walls.
 
High Sandbag Wall - Good for general cover. 

[/size][line][size=3]
[/size][center][size=3]Design/Features[/size][/center][size=3]
All fortifications are under [i]Defense > Guard Posts[/i] in the workshop. 

Since there's no way to dynamically change the ground in-game so the trenches are above ground. 

A number of structures can be manned by settlers. There are two versions of the Light and Heavy Sandbag Posts and Camouflaged Post--one for assigned guards to and the other so things can be placed on the roofs. 

All the structures can sink into the ground.

Trenches, Sandbag Cover and Pillar, Defensive Barriers, and Barbed Wire Fences can snap at different heights so they can be used on uneven/sloped ground. 

If the defensive position camouflage net combo isn't working try placing the net first and then slide the sandbag mound in. Same goes for the howitzer. 

[/size][line][size=3]
[/size][center][size=3]Uninstallation[/size][/center][size=3]

All menus are added via script injection. Before uninstalling the mod an uninstaller has to be consumed in-game to remove the
menus properly.

1. The uninstaller can be crafted at any Chembench under Utility. It's called "Sandbag Fortifications Uninstaller."

2. Once crafted it will show up under Aid in your inventory.

3. After consuming that you will see the menus will be gone; you can now exit the game-world and uninstall the mod. 




[/size]
author: Nutulator and Ethreon

name: Pump Actions of the Wasteland - RU
summary: Перевод мода | Этот мод добавляет единую модульную платформу, которую можно превратить в 5 различных дробовиков - от любимцев фанатов серии до культовых моделей из истории, видеоигр и кино.
description:
В вашей «[i]«постапокалиптической»[/i][size=1] [/size][i][size=1]ролевой[/size] игре»[/i] НЕТ помповых дробовиков? Это НЕПОПРАВИМОЕ упущение. Теперь в вашем распоряжении сразу ЧЕТЫРЕ помповых дробовика: культовый Remington 870 (он же охотничий дробовик из New Vegas), Mossberg 500 и Maverick 88 («Чейзер-13»), икона хорроров Ithaca 37 и знаменитый SPAS-12. Всё, чтобы ваше сердце забилось чаще.

[size=4][b][color=#f6b26b]Remington 870[/color][/b] [/size]
Тот самый охотничий дробовик из NV [size=1]и Fo76[/size]. Он станет вашим основным охотничьим дробовиком. Наряду с Ithaca, это модель гражданского типа, за исключением того, что у «Ремингтона» есть шанс найти версию с отъемным магазином.
[size=4][b][color=#f6b26b]Spas 12[/color][/b] [/size]
Суперкультовый дробовик Fallout! Прямиком из всеми «любимой» и «абсолютно каноничной» Fallout Brotherhood of Steel! [i]*издает рвотные позывы[/i]*. Из-за своей легендарности он останется верен традициям: это единственный дробовик, который может встретиться со складным прикладом (складывающимся поверх ресивера), из-за чего в игровом мире он реже попадается с оптикой. Также может оснащаться барабанным магазином.
[color=#f6b26b][size=4][b]Mossberg 500/Maverick 88[/b][/size][/color]
Более «военизированная» модель. Чаще встречается с прицелами армейского образца. Maverick 88 и легендарный «Чейзер-13» можно только создать на верстаке, в игровом мире они не выпадают.
[size=4][b][color=#f6b26b]Ithaca 37[/color] [/b][/size]
Похож на Rem 870, но по очевидным причинам не имеет модификаций с коробчатым или барабанным магазином. Неважно, с кем вы сражаетесь - с биологическим оружием или ужасами из глубокого космоса, - обязательно приберегите его для «близких контактов».

[size=4][center]﻿[b][color=#00ff00]Особенности[/color][/b][/center][/size]
[list]
[*]Мод добавляет четыре пушки и их варианты в игру через торговцев и левел-листы с помощью скриптовой инъекции. [b]Рейдеры[/b] используют Remington и Ithaca (гражданские), [b]Стрелки[/b] - Mossberg и SPAS (армейские). Все появляются, начиная с [b]14 уровня[/b]. Гражданские модели в продаже с 14 уровня, армейские - с 25-го. С 10 уровня гражданские модели можно найти в вещмешках и довоенных сейфах, армейские - в довоенных сундуках
[/list][list]
[*]Детали оружия сильно перемешаны, так что вы будете встречать интересные сборки у врагов, даже если не любите сами копаться в модификациях. Но учтите... у всех трофейных пушек будут «не очень хорошие» внутренние механизмы (что-что?)
[/list][list]
[*]Ресиверы определяют модель оружия и его поведение, а урон теперь зависит от слота «Внутренние механизмы». Они заменяют стандартные ресиверы из ванильной игры в плане урона и требований к навыкам).[size=1] Система вдохновлена трилогией stalker.[/size]
[/list][list]
[*]Здесь нет смены калибра. Только 12-й калибр, и точка.
[/list][list]
[*]Если вы посмотрите на скриншоты, то наверняка узнаете знакомые детали из других игр или фильмов. Если нет... неважно, они в любом случае не испортят вам погружение. Если вам любопытно - дерзайте...
[/list][spoiler]- Чейзер-13 - это воссоздание одного из дробовиков из S.T.A.L.K.E.R., а конкретнее - из «Тени Чернобыля» (соответствующая цветовая схема).
- Модификации Sierpinski позволяют собрать именно тот вариант SPAS-12, который был в SIGNALIS.
- Ствол M37 Stakeout и патронташ на цевье взяты из дробовика Stakeout в CoD: Black Ops
- Глушитель наемника (Hitman suppressor) — это копия самодельного глушителя Антона Чигура из фильма [i]Старикам тут не место[/i]. 
- Техника поинтмена - это отсылка к анимации приближения у SPAS-12 в игре F.E.A.R.
- Детали «Survivor» и «Upgraded» - воссоздание обвесов из The Last of Us (Original, Part II и Remake). Вы можете собрать дробовик Джоэла из первой части (Mossberg 500) или Элли из второй части (Remington 870). Также воссоздан Remington 870 из ремейка первой части со всеми его улучшениями.
- Цевье с фонариком - отсылка к конфигурации Remington 870 из Black Ops 2.
- Патрон 000 Magnum - отсылка к патронам из классических частей Resident Evil.
- Патрон 000 Buck shot — отсылка к боеприпасам из Metro Last Light, Metro 2033 Redux и Stalker 2.[/spoiler]

[list]
[*]Типы боеприпасов, не обольщайтесь - мод не добавляет новые патроны как отдельные предметы в инвентарь, но добавляет интересные модификаторы стрельбы: от обычной дроби до пули или «Дыхания дракона»..
[/list][list]
[*]Модуль УСМ, позволяет выбрать ручной режим или полуавтоматический. 
[/list] ﻿Примечание по поводу полуавтоматики
[spoiler]В планах есть отдельный пак только для полуавтоматических дробовиков, но он еще не скоро появится, так что я оставил эту опцию здесь просто ради веселья. Полуавтоматический режим не встречается у пушек в игровом мире (только на верстаке). Поскольку этот пак в основном посвящен помповым дробовикам, специальных анимаций для полуавтоматического цикла не делалось (однако гильзы вылетают корректно). Я ценю веселье и свободу выбора больше, чем идеальную полировку и реализм.[/spoiler]

[list]
[*]ПОЛНОСТЬЮ кастомные анимации от первого лица (обычные и в силовой броне), и ПОЛНОСТЬЮ кастомные анимации от третьего лица. Для третьего лица в силовой броне используются ванильные анимации охотничьего карабина ([size=1]простите, анимации для СБ от 3-го лица - это просто невыносимая боль[/size]).
[/list][list]
[*]Доступна версия в формате ESL.
[/list][list]
[*]Нереальное количество модификаций.
[/list]
[center]﻿[b]Требования:
[/b]Н Е Т У... [/center][center][spoiler][i]...Я всегда держу его под рукой - для близких контактов.[/i][/spoiler][/center]

description:

Hi! I'm KC![b]
I made this modlist entirely by myself & literally taught myself how to mod in order to solve any conflicts!

I hope you enjoy! (:[/i][/b][/quote][/center][/i]
[center]My modlist makes you ask yourself 1 question: [i]"Am I dead yet?"[/i]
If the answer is [i]no[/i], then [b]proceed[/b].
I've tailored [i][b]The SICKnasty Suite[/b][/i] to be a challenging, yet [u]fair[/u], survival playthrough.
If something frustrates you, [u]the difficulty is mostly modular[/u] so you can tweak it to your preference.

[u][b]I've handpicked mods that either:[/b][/u]
- have 0 conflicts with each other
- are explicitly stated to work well with each other
- or that I felt I could make a compatibility patch for

name: Vtaw9 Mega Treasure Hunt
summary: Mega Treasure Hunt to find all 499 Hand Placed Vtaw9 Clothing items, hidden in Custom Treasure Chests, across all the DLC.
description:
[color=#00ff00][size=4]A Mega Treasure Hunt for Clothing...[/size][/color]


Crafting Clothing in the Chem Bench is too easy, and unsatisfying, so there is no Chem Bench crafting of Clothing. Now you have to find them, scattered throughout the Commonwealth, and all the DLC's, in 499 Hand Placed Custom Containers, that are all over the place.

High, Low, in the Rivers, Oceans, Emergency Beacon Rooms, places you generally don't look. On Roofs, there's one even on a Helipad, on a Roof of a Hospital.

From the highest Rooftops, to the Deepest Oceans, inside and outside. Custom Names, Stats, Enchants, along with Legendaries to find. Numbered Tokens added to each Chest, to keep track of which Chests you've found.

New Custom Containers to keep your clothing in, along with a New Till for the Tokens to be kept in. Craftable in Settlements...


[color=#ea9999][size=3]Warning... [/size][/color]This mod contains some very provocative clothing, along with some extremely revealing outfits. Along with Nudity, and Semi-Nudity. It also contains adult themed Audio Holotapes, and Notes, that add to the Lore of Fallout 4.



[color=#00ff00][size=4]Vtaw9 Mega Treasure Hunt.[/size][/color]

[color=#00ff00][size=3]Features...[/size][/color]
[color=#fce5cd]- Chem Bench...[/color] No Chem Bench Crafting of Clothing, you've got to find them all now.
[color=#fce5cd]- Chests...[/color] 498 Hand Placed Custom Containers to find, with 1 or more, re-spawning items of clothing in each.
[color=#fce5cd]- Stats...[/color] Tweaked Clothing Stats.
[color=#fce5cd]- Enchants...[/color] Custom Clothing Enchants, Eg Charisma 5, Endurance 5, Carry +100, etc...

[color=#fce5cd]- Clothing Sets...[/color] Custom, named clothing Sets to find, Clothing mix and match sets, to compliment your looks.
[color=#fce5cd]- Legendaries...[/color] Lots of Legendary Clothing to find.
[color=#fce5cd]- Difficulty...[/color] Regional difficulty, means better stats on clothing. Tougher areas, have better clothing to find.
[color=#fce5cd]- Everywhere...[/color] All DLC's, in Water, on Roofs, Inside, Outside. Most inside Cells, contain Clothing Chests.

[color=#fce5cd]- Shoes...[/color] Lots of Shoe Variants made, most Legendary.
[color=#fce5cd]- DLC's...[/color] All DLC's included.
[color=#fce5cd]- Railroad Ballistic Fiber...[/color] Removed, so it's a tougher game, along with removing the Railroad Weave on Vtaw9 stuff.
[color=#fce5cd]- Tweaked Clothing Slots...[/color] Clothing Slots tweaked, so they make sense. Underwear under Trousers, Bra's under Tops.

[color=#fce5cd]- Audio Holotapes...[/color] Professional quality, Audio Holotapes for some New story driven segments, using AI Voiced v1.3  [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/mods/86525]Falltalk[/url]. Eliza and Ezra Parker Voice Logs.

[color=#fce5cd]- Eliza Parker and Carol Danvers Settlers...[/color] Fleshed out NPC Settlers to find at 2 Settlements, one in a Commonwealth Settlement, one in a Far Harbor Settlement, I love them both. Audio Logs are great for Eliza...

[color=#00ff00][size=3]Managing your collection...[/size][/color]
[color=#fce5cd]- Tokens...[/color] Numbered Token in each Chest, to work out easily which Chests you've found.
[color=#fce5cd]- Token Chest...[/color] New constructible Checkout Till, to keep your Tokens in.
[color=#fce5cd]- 2 Custom Chests...[/color] New constructible, gorgeous looking custom Chests, to keep your Clothing in.
[color=#fce5cd]- List...[/color] in-game List and Hints, to carry with you, to help find items.
[color=#fce5cd]- Printable Checklist...[/color] Clothing Container Checklist, containing Hints on where to find them. Along with Coc Commands.
 
[color=#00ff00][size=3]New...[/size][/color]
[color=#fce5cd]- NEW Ground Models / Preview Models...[/color] All Clothing Items done, NEW Fancy Packs made for invisible items...
[color=#fce5cd]- Nano Pockets...[/color] New Pocket system for clothing, levels 1 -> 14, no more wearing ugly, clipping armour for it's Deep Pockets.
                                 Player Level 1 -> 200+ needed, most wont reach Tier 14 Nano Pockets.
                                 Now you can just play the game, with clothing.

[color=#fce5cd]- Linings...[/color] New Lining system for clothing, Balanced, Energy and Ballistic. All with different requirements to make, allowing you to tweak clothing stats for different enemies. Requiring various SPECIAL Perks to unlock them.

[color=#fce5cd]- Bouy's...[/color] New Buoys, to find underwater Treasure Chests. Recommended mod for clear water, [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/mods/19380]Wasteland Water Revival[/url].

[color=#fce5cd]- Settlement Items...[/color] New Custom Containers, to keep your stuff in.
      Checkout till... for Tokens
      Custom Containers... 2 New Custom Containers, for storing your clothes.

[color=#fce5cd]- Chem Bench Crafting Recipe...[/color] War Nurse Token is now Craftable, with 100 Synth Components.
      Kill 100 Synths, get their Death Item. Aka Synth Component, build a War Nurse Token.
      War Nurse Token, build a Half Scale War Nurse Token Standee Container, Non-Respawning, with free stuff inside.
      [b]Vtaw9[/b] Section in Chem Bench has been added.


- No broken Precombines or Previs's, all cleaned in FO4 Edit.


[color=#f4cccc][b]- SAKR Tags Add-On Patch...[/b][/color] These tell mods what type of clothing they are wearing, Panties, Bra, Skirt, Jacket, Stockings, High Heel Boots. Along with how revealing they are, barely covering, or covering all.

Download on LL. ... along with SAKR main mod, and mods that use it.

[b][color=#f4cccc][size=3]Latex Collar Curse on LL[/size][/color][/b] ... makes full use of all 499 items of clothing that are SAKR, with enforced Random Dress Ups.

Brown face bug... a mismatch of textures, between High and Low textures. Causing a Brown Face on your character, use if you're experiencing it.

IDA body, and Valkyr Face, are two of my favourite recommended textures, that I use for CBBE.

I don't think Fusion Girl experiences the Brown Face Bug... I haven't used Fusion Girl for a while...

Adds lots of Zetan content, new spawns, new downed UFO's, new tweaked weapons, new Magazines to find, Eventually coming...  re-spawning treasure hunt, new characters, new lore, and new indoor locations.
