author: Masuryan

name: Dynamic Wardrobe
summary: A context-aware outfit management system that transforms how V dresses in Night City. Powered by Equipment-EX, it intelligently swaps outfits as you move between locations, apartments, combat zones, and districts. Just name your outfits with simple keywords and Dynamic Wardrobe handles every transition automatically.
description:
[center][/center][center][/center][center][img]https://i.imgur.com/R18hSOA.png[/img]


[color=#b0b0b0]Let your wardrobe adapt to the world around you with automatic,
context-driven outfit swaps powered by [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/cyberpunk2077/mods/6945]Equipment-EX[/url]﻿.

Dynamic Wardrobe transforms how V dresses in Night City —
name your Equipment-EX outfits with simple keywords
and the mod does the rest:

[i][b][color=#555555]●[/color] Location-aware outfit matching[/b][/i]
[i][b] [i][color=#555555]● [/color][/i]Seamless apartment ↔ outdoor transitions[/b][/i]
[i][b][i][color=#555555]● [/color][/i]Scene-aware handling for showers, ripperdocs & more[/b][/i]
[i][b][i][color=#555555]● [/color][/i]Combat & dangerous zone outfit swapping[/b][/i]
[i][b][i][color=#555555]● [/color][/i]Club detection for Night City's venues[/b][/i]
[i][b][i][color=#555555]● [/color][/i]Vehicle outfits — dress for cars, bikes, or specific rides[/b][/i]
[i][b][i][color=#555555]● [/color][/i]Weather & time-of-day outfit modifiers[/b][/i]
[i][b][i][color=#555555]● [/color][/i]Outfit shuffling — rotate looks on a timer[/b][/i]
[i][b][i][color=#555555]● [/color][/i]Pinned outfits — lock any outfit with a ! prefix[/b][/i]

[color=#555555]◉[/color] [b]How It Works:[/b]

[spoiler][color=#555555]●[/color] [b]Apartments[/b]
Walk into any apartment and V changes into home clothes. Walk out and V dresses for wherever they're headed.
All vanilla apartments supported, plus custom apartments via config file.

[color=#555555]●[/color] [b]Clubs[/b]
Step into Afterlife, Lizzie's, or any supported venue and V dresses for the occasion.
Combine "club" with context keywords for location-aware club outfits — "club corpo" only applies at corpo-area clubs.

[color=#555555]●[/color] [b]Locations[/b]
Name an outfit after a district and it kicks in when V arrives.
"japantown", "badlands", "kabuki" — use the debug overlay to see your current location and it just works.

[color=#555555]●[/color] [b]Context Keywords[/b]
Assign outfits to broad vibes instead of individual locations: [b]corpo[/b], [b]street[/b], [b]wild[/b], [b]club[/b].
V dresses to match the neighborhood — one outfit covers an entire part of the city.

[color=#555555]●[/color] [b]Combat[/b]
V gears up when a fight starts and changes back when it's over.
Add location keywords for area-specific combat looks — "combat street", "combat badlands".

[color=#555555]●[/color] [b]Scenes[/b]
V undresses for showers, then puts home clothes back on when the scene ends.
Quest outfits (diving suit, etc.) are always respected — the mod steps back until they're removed.

[color=#555555]●[/color] [b]Ripperdoc[/b]
V changes into something comfortable at the ripperdoc chair, and the previous outfit is restored on exit.
Enhanced with [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/cyberpunk2077/mods/7064]Immersive Rippers[/url] for full undressing animations.

[color=#555555]●[/color] [b]Vehicles[/b]
V changes into driving gear when entering a vehicle and reverts on exit.
Add "car" or "bike" for vehicle-type outfits, or use vehicle context keywords for specific rides.
Prefix with (acc) to layer accessories — helmets, visors, gloves — on top of V's current outfit instead of replacing it.

[color=#555555]●[/color] [b]Weather & Time of Day[/b]
Add "rain" or "dry" to make outfits weather-aware. Add "day" or "night" for time-based outfits.
These act as priority modifiers — a "rain street" outfit beats a plain "street" outfit when it's raining.

[color=#555555]●[/color] [b]Outfit Shuffling[/b]
Enable shuffling to rotate V's outfit on a timer. Context mode picks from the current location pool.
Chaos mode picks from all non-reserved outfits. Timer pauses during combat, scenes, and at home.

[color=#555555]●[/color] [b]Pinned Outfits[/b]
Prefix any outfit with ! to tell the mod "hands off".
Blocks all automatic swaps until V enters an apartment, ripperdoc, or scene.[/spoiler]

[i]New to the mod? Check out the [url=https://strahlimeier.gitbook.io/dynamic-wardrobe/quickstart]Quick Setup Guide[/url].
For details on each feature, see the [url=https://strahlimeier.gitbook.io/dynamic-wardrobe/advanced]full documentation[/url], [url=https://strahlimeier.gitbook.io/dynamic-wardrobe/advanced/priority]priority system[/url], and [url=https://strahlimeier.gitbook.io/dynamic-wardrobe/configuration]configuration[/url].[/i]

[color=#555555]◉[/color] [b]Outfit Naming Reference:[/b]

[spoiler][i][b]Name your Equipment-EX outfits with these keywords:[/b][/i]

[color=#555555]»[/color] [u][i]home[/i][/u] — applied on apartment entry. e.g. "home cozy", "home towel"
[color=#555555]»[/color] [i][u]outdoor[/u][/i] — fallback when no location/context matches. e.g. "outdoor casual"
[color=#555555]»[/color] [i][u]nude[/u][/i] — applied during shower and undress scenes. e.g. "nude"
[color=#555555]»[/color] [i][u]ripper[/u][/i] — applied at ripperdoc chairs. e.g. "ripper casual"
[color=#555555]»[/color] [i][u]combat[/u][/i] — applied on combat/danger zones. e.g. "combat", "combat street"
[color=#555555]»[/color] [i][u]club[/u][/i] — applied on club entry. e.g. "club casual", "club corpo"
[color=#555555]»[/color] [i][u]vehicle[/u][/i] — applied when entering a vehicle. e.g. "vehicle", "vehicle car", "vehicle bike"
[color=#555555]»[/color] [i][u]rain / dry[/u][/i] — weather modifier. e.g. "rain street", "dry outdoor"
[color=#555555]»[/color] [i][u]day / night[/u][/i] — time-of-day modifier. e.g. "night club", "day outdoor"
[color=#555555]»[/color] [i][u]! (pinned)[/u][/i] — locks outfit, blocks all automatic swaps. e.g. "!tactical stealth"
[color=#555555]»[/color] [i][u]Location names[/u][/i] — use the debug overlay to see your current location. e.g. "japantown", "badlands/coastview"
[color=#555555]»[/color] [i][u]Context keywords[/u][/i] — broad categories: [b]corpo[/b], [b]street[/b], [b]wild[/b], [b]club[/b]. e.g. "corpo formal", "wild rugged"

[i]See [url=https://strahlimeier.gitbook.io/dynamic-wardrobe/examples]Examples[/url] for more naming patterns and the [url=https://strahlimeier.gitbook.io/dynamic-wardrobe/configuration/context-map]Context Map[/url] for customizing keywords.[/i][/spoiler]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/dFn3Zyg.png[/img]

[spoiler][color=#B24A4A][b]Will Conflict:[/b][/color] Other Equipment-EX automation mods (e.g. [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/cyberpunk2077/mods/21876]Apartment Outfits[/url]) — both mods try to control outfit changes during scene transitions. If you only want Apartment Outfits to handle home/shower swaps, disable [b]Home Outfits[/b] in DW's Mod Settings — DW will skip all apartment outfit logic and let the other mod handle it.

[color=#6FA37A][b]Generally Compatible:[/b][/color] Equipment-EX, appearance mods (AMM), UI/HUD mods, clothing mods that don't automate EX outfits.

See [url=https://strahlimeier.gitbook.io/dynamic-wardrobe/compatibility]Compatibility[/url] for the full breakdown.

[b]Soft Requirements:[/b]
These mods are not required but unlock additional features:
[color=#555555]●[/color] [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/cyberpunk2077/mods/7064]Immersive Rippers[/url] — adds immersive undressing animations at ripperdoc chairs
[color=#555555]●[/color] [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/cyberpunk2077/mods/10255]Immersive Rippers - Dogtown[/url] — extends Immersive Rippers to Dogtown ripperdocs
[color=#555555]●[/color] [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/cyberpunk2077/mods/18972]Hangout Romances[/url] — enables drive scene outfit handling
[color=#555555]●[/color] [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/cyberpunk2077/mods/27532]Immersive Shower[/url] — eliminates shower scene flash

[b]Known Limitations:[/b]
See [url=https://strahlimeier.gitbook.io/dynamic-wardrobe/issues]Known Issues[/url] for the full list including workarounds.[/spoiler]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/VguXyg9.png[/img]

[spoiler][color=#555555]●[/color] Download all the requirements
[color=#555555]●[/color] Download the main file
[color=#555555]●[/color] Extract it to your game's folder
[color=#555555]●[/color] Configure in-game: Settings → Mod Settings → Dynamic Wardrobe

For setup, outfit naming, and examples see the [url=https://strahlimeier.gitbook.io/dynamic-wardrobe/quickstart]Quick Setup Guide[/url].

[color=#be4c40][b]Updating:[/b][/color]
[color=#be4c40][b]Always delete the old version before installing a new one.
[/b][/color]
Leftover scripts from previous versions can cause conflicts and errors.
[color=#555555]●[/color] Back up [b]CustomApartments.reds[/b], [b]ContextMap.reds[/b], [b]CustomPlaces.reds[/b], and [b]VehicleContextMap.reds[/b] if you've customized them
[color=#555555]●[/color] Delete the [b]r6/scripts/Dynamic Wardrobe[/b] folder and [b]r6/input/DynamicWardrobeInput.xml[/b]
[color=#555555]●[/color] Install the new version
[color=#555555]●[/color] Restore your backed-up config files

[b]You can safely uninstall Dynamic Wardrobe at any time.
[/b]See the [url=https://strahlimeier.gitbook.io/dynamic-wardrobe/save-data]Save Data & Uninstall[/url] page for details.[/spoiler]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/users/4688264?tab=user+files][img]https://i.imgur.com/w95Rju7.png[/img][/url][/color][/center]
author: Strahlimeier

name: Content Patcher
summary: Loads content packs that change the game's data, images, and maps without replacing XNB files.
description:
[b]Content Patcher[/b] loads content packs that change the game's data, images, and maps without replacing XNB files. Content packs can make changes dynamically based on many in-game details like location, weather, date, festivals or events, spouse, relationships, whether you have a Joja membership, etc.

Unlike XNB mods, these content packs get automatic update checks and compatibility checks, are easy to install and uninstall, support localisation automatically, are less likely to conflict, and are less likely to break due to game updates.

[b]This mod doesn't do anything on its own; you need to install content packs separately.[/b]


[img]https://i.imgur.com/suHzq2E.png[/img]
[list=1]
[*][url=https://smapi.io]Install the latest version of SMAPI[/url].
[*]Unzip the mod folder into [font=Courier New]Stardew Valley/Mods[/font].
[*]Install any content packs you want by unzipping them into [font=Courier New]Stardew Valley/Mods[/font].
[*]Run the game using SMAPI.
[/list]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/ig7VIBk.png[/img]
[list]
[*][b]Content packs[/b] let you edit the game's XNB images, data, and maps with zero programming. You can optionally use features like conditions, tokens, and player settings. See the [url=https://github.com/Pathoschild/StardewMods/blob/stable/ContentPatcher/docs/author-guide.md#readme]mod author documentation[/url] for more info and a guide to creating content packs.
[*]The [b]Content Patcher API[/b] lets SMAPI modders create custom tokens for content packs to use. See the [url=https://github.com/Pathoschild/StardewMods/blob/stable/ContentPatcher/docs/extensibility.md#readme]extensibility guide[/url] for more info.
[/list]
[b]? Mod author docs in other languages: [url=https://github.com/Pathoschild/StardewMods/blob/stable/ContentPatcher/docs/zh/README.md]zh (中文)[/url][/b].


[img]https://i.imgur.com/M7KlO44.png[/img]
[list]
[*]Compatible with...
[list]
[*]Stardew Valley 1.6 or later;
[*]Linux, macOS, and Windows;
[*]single-player and multiplayer (see [url=https://github.com/Pathoschild/StardewMods/tree/stable/ContentPatcher#multiplayer]multiplayer compatibility notes[/url]).
[/list][*]No known mod conflicts.
[/list]

[img]https://i.imgur.com/ccJ4SPZ.png[/img]
[list][*][url=https://github.com/Pathoschild/StardewMods/blob/stable/ContentPatcher/docs/release-notes.md#release-notes]Release notes[/url]
[*][url=https://github.com/Pathoschild/StardewMods/tree/stable/ContentPatcher]Source code[/url]
[*][url=https://github.com/Pathoschild/StardewMods#readme]My other mods[/url]
[/list]
[size=1]Tiny reminder: endorse the mod if you like it. It means more people will see it, and it makes the author happy. :)[/size]
author: Pathoschild

name: Generic Mod Config Menu (GMCM)
summary: Adds an in-game UI to edit other mods' config options.
description:
[b]Generic Mod Config Menu[/b] adds an in-game UI to edit other mods' config options. This only works for mods designed to support it.

[b][size=5]Install[/size][/b]
[list=1]
[*]Install the latest version of [url=https://smapi.io]SMAPI[/url].
[*]Unzip the mod folder into [font=Courier New]Stardew Valley/Mods[/font].
[*]Run the game using SMAPI.
[/list]
[b][size=5]Use[/size][/b]
[b][size=3]For players[/size][/b]
You can edit settings from the title screen ([url=https://github.com/spacechase0/StardewValleyMods/blob/develop/framework/GenericModConfigMenu/docs/screenshot-title.png]via the cog button[/url]) or in-game ([url=https://github.com/spacechase0/StardewValleyMods/blob/develop/framework/GenericModConfigMenu/docs/screenshot-in-game-options.png]at the bottom of the in-game options menu[/url]). This only works for mods which were designed to support Generic Mod Config Menu, and some mods may only allow editing their config from the title screen.

Changes are live after saving, no need to restart the game.

[b][size=3]For mod authors[/size][/b]
[list]
[*][b]For C# mods:[/b] see the [url=https://github.com/spacechase0/StardewValleyMods/tree/develop/framework/GenericModConfigMenu#for-c-mod-authors]mod author documentation[/url]﻿.
[*][b]For Content Patcher packs:[/b] you don't need to do anything! Content Patcher will add the config UI automatically for you.
[/list]
[b][size=5]Compatibility[/size][/b]
Compatible with Stardew Valley 1.5.5+ on Linux/macOS/Windows, both single-player and multiplayer.

[b][size=5]See also[/size][/b]
[list]
[*][url=https://github.com/spacechase0/StardewValleyMods/blob/develop/framework/GenericModConfigMenu/docs/release-notes.md#release-notes]Release notes[/url]
[*][url=https://github.com/spacechase0/StardewValleyMods]Source code[/url]
[/list]
author: spacechase0

name: SMAPI - Stardew Modding API
summary: The mod loader for Stardew Valley.
description:
[b]SMAPI [/b]is the mod loader for Stardew Valley. It works fine with GOG and Steam achievements, it's compatible with Linux/macOS/Windows, you can uninstall it anytime, and there's a friendly community if you need help. SMAPI is required for most types of Stardew Valley mod.

[img width=20,height=20]https://smapi.io/Content/images/pufferchick.png[/img]


[size=5][b]Install SMAPI[/b][/size]
See the [url=https://stardewvalleywiki.com/Modding:Player_Guide]player's guide to modding[/url] (or skip to [url=https://stardewvalleywiki.com/Modding:Player_Guide/Getting_Started#Getting_started]install steps[/url])﻿. To update SMAPI, download the new version and run its installer; it'll clean up the old version automatically.

[b][color=Red]For Android phones:[/color][/b] see [url=https://stardewvalleywiki.com/Modding:Installing_SMAPI_on_Android]installing SMAPI on Android[/url] instead. This page is not for Android.


[size=5][b]Get help[/b][/size]
[list]
[*]See [url=https://stardewvalleywiki.com/Modding:Help]Modding:Help[/url]﻿ on the wiki!
[/list]

[size=5][b]See also[/b][/size][list]
[*][url=https://smapi.io/#whatsnew]What's new?[/url]﻿
[*][url=https://smapi.io/privacy]Privacy notes[/url]
[*][url=https://smapi.io/#donate]Donate to support SMAPI ﻿♥[/url]﻿
[*]For mod creators: [url=https://stardewvalleywiki.com/Modding:Index]modding documentation[/url]﻿ and [url=https://github.com/Pathoschild/SMAPI]SMAPI source code[/url]﻿
[/list]
author: Pathoschild

name: NPC Map Locations
summary: See NPC and players on the map, with an optional minimap.
description:
See NPC and players on the map with live real-time positioning and an optional minimap.
[img]https://i.imgur.com/L2T4Wii.gif[/img]

[b][color=#6aa84f]For non-English players, translations are included in the base mod. [url=https://github.com/bouhm/stardew-valley-mods#translating-the-mods]Contributions are welcome[/url]![/color][/b]


[size=5][b]Install[/b][/size]
[list=1]
[*][url=https://smapi.io]Install the latest version of SMAPI[/url].
[*][i](Optional)[/i] Install [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/stardewvalley/mods/5098]Generic Mod Config Menu[/url] to configure the mod.

[*]Unzip the mod folder into [font=Courier New]Stardew Valley/Mods[/font].
[*]Run the game using SMAPI.
[/list]

[b][size=5]How to use[/size][/b]
[b]See [url=https://github.com/bouhm/stardew-valley-mods/tree/main/NPCMapLocations#use]how to use NPC Map Locations[/url] in the readme![/b]

You can install [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/stardewvalley/mods/5098]Generic Mod Config Menu[/url] to configure the mod.


[size=5][b]Compatibility[/b][/size]
[list]
[*]Compatible with...[list]
[*]Stardew Valley 1.6.9 or later;
[*]Linux, macOS, and Windows;
[*]single-player and multiplayer ([b]must be installed by the host player[/b], and also by any farmhand who wants to see NPCs on the map).
[/list]
[*]No known mod conflicts.
[/list]

[size=5][b]See also[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][url=https://github.com/bouhm/stardew-valley-mods/tree/main/NPCMapLocations/docs/release-notes.md#release-notes]Release notes[/url]
[*][url=https://github.com/bouhm/stardew-valley-mods]Source code[/url]
[*][url=https://github.com/bouhm/stardew-valley-mods#readme]My other mods[/url]
[/list]
[size=1]Tiny reminder: endorse the mod if you like it. It means more people will see it, and it makes the author happy. :)[/size]
author: Bouhm and Pathoschild

name: Stardew Valley Expanded
summary: Stardew Valley Expanded is a fanmade expansion for ConcernedApe's Stardew Valley. This mod adds 28 new NPCs,  58 locations, 278 character events, 43 fish, reimagined vanilla areas, three farm maps, a reimagined world map reflecting all changes, new music, questlines, objects, crops, festivals, and many miscellaneous additions!
description:
[center][url=https://twitter.com/FlashShifter][/url][/center][center]﻿[/center][center][url=https://twitter.com/FlashShifter][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1303/images/7608/7608-1610385311-241093688.png[/img]﻿﻿﻿﻿[/url]
[color=#b6b6b6][i]The world of Stardew Valley is awe-inspiring. Relationships, farming, fishing, adventuring...All made by just one person, who has given us the gift of so many memories with this game. But the problem with memories is that we'll never experience that world for the first time again.

I aim to give you, the player, that sense of adventure and unknown once more. Through this mod, I want to immerse you in the world ConcernedApe created.
[/i]
-Flash[/color][/center]



[size=3][center]Stardew Valley Expanded (SVE) stays as true to the source material as it can- from new areas and expanded dialogue and events, to the portraits for a brand new set of characters. My goal, as a modder hobbyist, is this: "Give the player the magical feeling they had when they first played Stardew Valley". To experience that magic as ConcernedApe intended, I recommend playing through vanilla Stardew Valley before jumping into SVE’s expanded world. For players who have already played Stardew Valley: think back to when you first walked into the Secret Woods, completed an elaborate quest, experienced Shane’s six heart event, or met Abigail for the first time. Stardew Valley is full of those inspired moments that enchant you and draw you into its world. By taking that world and expanding it, I hope to create more of those moments, so you can always find something new when you make your way back to the valley. Starting a new save file is required![/center][/size]
[size=3]

[/size][center][size=3]﻿[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1303/images/7608/7608-1610385406-1987767487.png[/img]﻿[/size]
[size=3]Please read the Installation Guide on GitHub![/size]
[size=3][url=https://github.com/FlashShifter/StardewValleyExpanded/wiki/Install-guide]Click Me![/url]﻿[/size]

[size=3]Known bugs, common errors, mod compatibility and troubleshooting on GitHub![/size]
[size=3][url=https://github.com/FlashShifter/StardewValleyExpanded/wiki/Troubleshooting]Click Me![/url][/size][/center]



[center][size=3]﻿[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1303/images/7608/7608-1610385470-1829474198.png[/img]﻿﻿﻿[/size]
[size=3]Follow me on [/size][url=https://twitter.com/FlashShifter][size=3]Twitter[/size][/url][size=3]﻿ for sneak peeks and updates![/size]

[size=3]Join the [/size][url=https://www.reddit.com/r/StardewValleyExpanded/][size=3]Stardew Valley Expanded Subreddit[/size][/url][size=3]﻿ and participate in discussions with other players!

Join the [url=https://discord.com/invite/svexpanded]Stardew Valley Expanded Discord Server[/url][/size][/center][size=3]﻿﻿﻿
[/size]


[center][size=3][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1303/images/7608/7608-1610385458-1214125993.png[/img]﻿﻿﻿[/size]
[size=3]SVE is a free expansion for ConcernedApe's Stardew Valley. Countless hours of brand-new content has been added to the game. There are regular content updates, bug fixes, and transparent communication with the community. Developing SVE requires a lot of time. [/size]

[size=3]Please consider donating to me directly via[/size][size=4] [url=https://www.paypal.com/paypalme2/FlashShifter?locale.x=en_US&fbclid=IwAR3ezr4RKIEXNbjgT1OxdU6dgmLhIdTjhhIhvurkDo0Hyv3tyQfRrsgM1jY]PayPal[/url][/size][/center]




[center][size=3][img width=425,height=250]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1303/images/7608/7608-1610385431-1482639146.png[/img]﻿[/size]
[size=3] SVE adds over a dozen new fully-fledged NPCs, and many other characters. These NPCs come with dynamic dialogue, schedules, back-story, personalities, animations, events, and more. [/size][/center]



[center][size=3][img]https://i.postimg.cc/BnM1J0b4/New-Character-Events.png[/img]﻿[/size]
[size=3]SVE events aim to expand the story of Stardew's characters and the lore of the world. Players can visit the [url=https://stardewvalleyexpanded.wiki.gg/]Stardew Valley Expanded Wiki[/url]﻿ [/size][size=3]for details on how to trigger the new events. SVE as of Version 1.15 adds over 278 new events.[/size][/center]



[center][size=3]﻿[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1303/images/7608/7608-1610385463-2024977734.png[/img]﻿﻿[/size]
[size=3]SVE features an expanded Joja storyline! What will the fate of Pelican Town be if you, the player, side with Joja and allow a corporate takeover of the valley?[/size][/center]




[center][size=3][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1303/images/7608/7608-1610385419-880329289.png[/img]﻿﻿﻿[/size]
[size=3]SVE adds an additional three farm maps to choose from (optional).[/size]

[color=#e69138][size=4][b]Frontier Farm[/b][/size][/color]
[size=3]The recommended farm map for Stardew Valley Expanded. it features an expansive plot of land bordering the 'Ferngill Republic Frontier'. Many different wild trees are rooted here, there's several new questlines, and there's secrets to discover. Players start with one pear tree.
[/size][spoiler]
[img]https://i.ibb.co/J5wg2M8/Farm-map-with-debris.png[/img]﻿
[/spoiler][/center]

[center][color=#e69138][size=4][b]Grandpa's Farm[/b][/size][/color]
[size=3]A humble farm map. [b]Replaces the standard farm layout[/b]. Modestly sized farmland, tillable grass, multiple questlines, different landmark locations, shortcuts to surrounding areas, and secrets to discover. Grandpas Farm comes with a sandbox layout configuration for players wanting full control on map design.[/size]

[spoiler]
[b]Farm map with debris[/b]
[spoiler]
[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1303/images/7608/7608-1610385791-2014750724.png[/img]﻿
[/spoiler]

[b]Farm map with no debris[/b]
[spoiler]
﻿[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1303/images/7608/7608-1610385795-1040711272.png[/img]﻿
[/spoiler][/center]
[/spoiler]

[center][color=#e69138][size=4][b]Immersive Farm 2 Remastered[/b][/size][/color]
[size=3]A huge plot of land optimized for multiplayer or solo players looking for a challenge. Features tillable grass, a questline, and secrets. Comes with many configurations![/size][/center][center]
[spoiler]
[size=3][b]Farm map with debris[/b][/size]
[spoiler]
[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1303/images/7608/7608-1610385556-344242399.png[/img]﻿﻿
[/spoiler]

[size=3][b]Farm map with no debris[/b][/size]
[spoiler]
[size=3]﻿[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1303/images/7608/7608-1610385560-1043636426.png[/img]﻿[/size]﻿
[/spoiler]
[size=3][/size][/center]
[/spoiler]


[center][size=3][img]https://i.postimg.cc/k4tVNRcg/New-Locations.png[/img]﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿[/size]
[size=3]SVE adds 58 new locations. Some areas must be unlocked through progression and quests. The [url=https://stardewvalleyexpanded.wiki.gg/]Stardew Valley Expanded Wiki[/url]﻿ [/size][size=3]has more information on some of the new areas.[/size][/center][size=3]
[/size]


[center][size=3][img]https://i.postimg.cc/yNyMhc32/Location-Messages.png[/img]﻿﻿﻿[/size]
[size=3]All maps - vanilla and new, interior and exterior - are now filled with location messages for you to discover and experience. There are over 900 throughout the world![/size][/center]


[center][size=3][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1303/images/7608/7608-1610385446-999942014.png[/img]﻿﻿[/size]
[size=3]A configuration file is generated within the [CP] Stardew Valley Expanded, [CP] Immersive Farm 2 Remastered, and [CP] Grandpa's Farm folders when the game is run at least once. Configurations can be toggled at will on already existing saves. The spoiler tab has detailed information on all possible configurations. Players can change the way they wish to experience their SVE play throughs![/size][/center][center]
[spoiler]
[color=#e69138][size=4][b]STARDEW VALLEY EXPANDED CONFIGURATIONS[/b][/size][/color]

[b][size=3]Seasonal Edits[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default =“True”[/size]
[size=3]Adds leaves to all building roofs during fall and snow to many objects during winter (fences, stop signs, plants, etc). Automatically adjusts colors if players have Starblue Valley or Eemie recolors installed.[/size]
[size=3] [/size]
[b][size=3]Replace Default/Hardwood Fences[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default =“True”[/size]
[size=3]Replaces the standard and hardwood fences with the matching static fences found throughout exterior locations. Automatically adjusts colors if players have Starblue Valley or Eemie recolors installed.[/size]
[size=3] [/size]
[b][size=3]Immersive Shadows[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default = "True"[/size]
[size=3]Adds shadows to buildings and large objects in the world.

[b][size=3]Mist Effects[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default = "True"[/size]
[size=3]Adds mist during rainy days to many maps. This does impact performance, so disable this if you're experiencing lag on rainy days.[/size][/size]

[b][size=3]Original Mines Entrance[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default = "False"[/size]
[size=3]Adds back the original mine entrance at the mountain, cutting down time it takes players to walk to the mines.

[b][size=3]Disable SVE Joja Shop[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default = "False"[/size]
[size=3]Removes SVE Joja Shop patches, giving other mods editing the vanilla shop priority.[/size][/size]

[b][size=3]Remove Objects From Grandpas Shed[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default = "False"[/size]
[size=3]Once players complete the quest, they can opt in to remove all static objects from the interior.

[b][size=3]Remove Southern Cactuses From Desert[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default = "False"[/size]
[size=3]Removes a number of catuses in Calico Desert, allowing easier placement of objects such as kegs.[/size][/size]
[b]
[/b][size=3][b]Alternative Craftables[/b]
Default = "False"
Changes the design of the furnace, kiln, beehives, kegs, seed machine, cheese machine, and mayonnaise machine.[/size]

[b][size=3]Balanced Crafting[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default =“False”[/size]
[size=3]Crafting requires more/different resources. Some craftables are easier to make. Recommended if players are using the “Automation” mod and/or IF2R to balance resource intake.[/size]
[size=3] [/size]
[b][size=3]Harder Building Construction[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default =“False”[/size]
[size=3]Constructing buildings cost more resources and money.[/size]
[size=3] [/size]
[b][size=3]Stronger Monsters[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default =“False”[/size]
[size=3]Monsters receive a significant buff in damage dealt to players (double), with slight increases in HP. Shadow Brutes and Mummies have large HP and damage buffs.

[/size][b][size=3]SVE Community Bundles[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default =“True”[/size]
[size=3]Items from SVE are not automatically included in the Community Center bundles.

[b][size=3]Hard SVE Community Bundles[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default =“False”[/size]
[size=3]Increases the difficulty of Community Center bundles with SVE items.

[/size][b][size=3]Reduce Crimson Badlands Monster Spawns[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default =“False”[/size]
Lowers the number of monsters in the badlands, making the area easier to explore and helps minimize lag.

[b][size=3]Retextured Farmhouse Kitchen[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default =“True”[/size]
[size=3]The farmhouse kitchen graphics are given a cleaner texture.

[/size][b][size=3]Remove Winery Decorations[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default =“False”[/size]
[size=3]The bar and seating area in the buildable Winery are removed.[/size][/size]
[size=3] [/size]
[b][size=3]Older Sophia Portraits[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default =“False”[/size]
[size=3]Makes Sophia's portraits look older!

[b]Shorter Path To Blue Moon Vineyard[/b]
Default = "False"
Pushes the warp point from Cindersap Forest > Blue Moon Vineyard closer to Fairhaven Farm, cutting down on travel time.[/size]

[b][size=3]Mature Events[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default =“False”[/size]
[size=3]Adds an additional 10 events that contain extremely serious topics, dark undertones, and moral ambiguity. If players wish to play SVE with these events, they must consent via toggling the configuration to “true”.

[b][size=3]Use Galdoran Theme All Times[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default =“False”[/size]
[size=3]Changes the Stardew Valley region UI to the Galdoran region UI in all locations.

[b]Disable Galdoran Theme[/b]
[size=3]Default =“False”[/size]
[size=3]Disables the Galdoran UI in all locations. Useful if players have a mod already editing the UI.[/size][/size][/size]
[size=3] [/size]
[color=#e69138][size=4][b]GRANDPA'S FARM CONFIGURATIONS

[/b][/size][/color][size=3][b]Sandbox Layout[/b]
Default = “False”
Turns the farmland into a huge dirt field, similar to the standard farm. Perfect for players who want full control over map design.

[b]Remove Fences[/b]
Default = "False"
Removes pre-built fences on the farm.

[b]Remove Grass Stretch[/b]
Default = "False"
Removes the long bit of grass adjacent to the dirt crop field.

[b]Grass Crop Field[/b]
Default = "False"
Changes the large dirt field to grass.

[b]Heavy Debris[/b]
Default = "False"
Fills the farm landscape with a plethora of resources.
[b]
Remove Land Expansion Pond
[/b]Default = "False"
Removes the south east pond on the farmland accessible after buying the property deed.

[b]Larger Bridge To Grandpas Shed[/b]
Default = "False"
Extends the patched bridge height by 1 tile, making it connect better with player-laid paths that are 2 tiles in height.

[b]Larger Greenhouse[/b]
Default = "False"
Makes the greenhouse slightly bigger, with designated fruit tree spots.

[b]Remove Greenhouse Objects[/b]
Default = "False"
Removes static objects, such as plants and barrels, from the greenhouse interior.[/size]

[b][color=#e69138][size=4]IMMERSIVE FARM 2 REMASTERED CONFIGURATIONS[/size][/color][/b]

[b][size=3]Farm Sand Box Layout[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default = “False”[/size]
[size=3]Transforms IF2R into a more standard farm map, excluding predetermined crop fields, shed building, and static fences. There’s an image of this version in the IF2R section. The configuration file is located within the [CP] IF2R Folder. Players may set this configuration to “true” on existing SVE saves using IF2R.[/size]

[b][size=3]Farm-Light Debris[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default = “False”[/size]
[size=3]Balances resource intake and makes the farmland more manageable. Must be toggled to true before save file creation to take effect.[/size]

[b][size=3]DirtCropField1, DirtCropField2, DirtCropField3…[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default = “False”[/size]
[size=3]Crop fields can be toggled between grass and dirt variants.[/size]

[b][size=3]Remove Fences In Front Of Shed[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default = “False”[/size]
[size=3]Removes the fences in-between the shed and crop field 6.[/size]

[b][size=3]Remove Farm Animal Area Fences[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default = “False”[/size]
[size=3]Removes the fencing in the middle of IF2R.[/size]

[b][size=3]Remove Fences Left Of Crop Field 1[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default = “False”[/size]
[size=3]Removes the fences beside the river.[/size]

[b][size=3]Remove Central Cherry Blossom Tree And Water Trough[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default = “False”[/size]
[size=3]Removes the large pink tree and water trough below the fenced animal area[/size]

[b][size=3]Remove Fences From Backyard[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default = “False”[/size]
[size=3]Removes all fences around the farmhouse.[/size]

[b][size=3]Remove Table From Backyard[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default = “False”[/size]
[size=3]Removes the table and chair under the blossom tree.[/size]

[b][size=3]Remove Pet House From Backyard[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default = “False”[/size]
[size=3]Removes the pet house. Useful if players are using a recolor that doesn’t match the color palette/design.[/size]

[b][size=3]Remove Flowerbed From Backyard[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default = “False”[/size]
[size=3]Removes the flowerbeds above the farmhouse.[/size]

[b][size=3]Remove Minecart System[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default = “False”[/size]
[size=3]Removes the minecarts around the farmland.[/size]

[b][size=3]Remove Objects From Greenhouse[/size][/b]
[size=3]Default = “False”[/size]
[size=3]Removes static plants, boxes, flowers, and barrels from the IF2R greenhouse interior.[/size]

[size=3] [/size]
[size=3][/spoiler][/size][/center]


[center][size=3][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1303/images/7608/7608-1610385423-147396219.png[/img]﻿﻿﻿﻿[/size]
[size=3]43 new fish are introduced in SVE! Details on where to catch them, difficulty, sale price, and more can be found on the [url=https://stardewvalleyexpanded.wiki.gg/]Stardew Valley Expanded Wiki[/url]﻿[/size][/center]
[center][/center]
[center][size=3]﻿﻿[/size][/center]

[center][size=3][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1303/images/7608/7608-1610385454-991174073.png[/img]﻿﻿[/size]
[size=3]Immersive custom forage spawn locations throughout the world, in every map. Some forage/objects are one-time spawns and others spawn in specific areas. After playing for 2+ years, many spawns are buffed and new ones will appear. [/size][/center]




[center][size=3]﻿[img width=425,height=250]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1303/images/7608/7608-1610385402-235940300.png[/img]﻿[/size]
[size=3]The future of SVE is bright! Here is what players can expect in future updates:[/size]
[size=3] [/size]
[spoiler]

[size=3]New villages, towns, and cities[/size]
[size=3]New fully-fledged NPCs[/size]
[size=3]New maps[/size]
[size=3]New festivals[/size]
[size=3]New music[/size]
[size=3]Dungeons[/size]
[size=3]More character events[/size]
[size=3]Lore and character arcs[/size]
[size=3]New desktop wallpapers[/size]
[size=3][/size][/center]
[/spoiler]

[center][size=3][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1303/images/7608/7608-1610385467-608615976.png[/img]﻿﻿[/size]
[size=3]Have a question? Check these frequently asked questions first! Your question may have been answered![/size]
[size=3][url=https://github.com/FlashShifter/StardewValleyExpanded/wiki/FAQs]Click Here![/url][/size][/center][size=3][url=https://github.com/FlashShifter/StardewValleyExpanded/wiki/FAQs]




[/url][/size][center][size=3][url=https://github.com/FlashShifter/StardewValleyExpanded/wiki/FAQs][img]https://i.postimg.cc/HnXxhFGM/Compatibility-And-Modder-Resources.png[/img][/url][/size]
[size=3]Stardew Valley Expanded is compatible with other expansions such as Ridgeside Village, East Scarp, Downtown Zuzu City, Boarding House, and Mineral Town! Generally speaking, SVE is compatible with the vast majority of other mods. There's also many resources available to new aspiring modders and guides to make their mods compatible with Stardew Valley Expanded and other expansion mods. The link below has more information:

[/size][url=https://linktr.ee/sdvmodding][size=3]Stardew Valley Expanded Compatibility Form and Modder Resources[/size][/url][/center]﻿


[center][/center]

[center][size=3][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1303/images/7608/7608-1610385450-1648113139.png[/img]﻿﻿
[/size][spoiler]
[size=5][url=https://twitter.com/ConcernedApe]ConcernedApe[/url]﻿[/size]
[size=4][b]Creator of Stardew Valley

[/b][/size]- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -

[b][size=4]Stardew Valley Expanded Co-Developers:[/size][/b]

[url=https://forums.nexusmods.com/index.php?/user/2679113-poltergeister/][size=4]Poltergeister[/size][/url][size=3]﻿[/size]
[size=3]Portrait Artist[/size]

[url=https://twitter.com/iKeychain][size=4]iKeychain[/size][/url][size=3]﻿[/size]
[size=3]Object Artist[/size]

author: FlashShifter

name: Farm Type Manager (FTM)
summary: Farm Type Manager (FTM) is a framework that lets other mods spawn objects and monsters anywhere in the game, with various controls and customization options.
description:
Despite the name, [b]Farm Type Manager (FTM)[/b] is a framework that allows other mods to spawn objects and monsters anywhere in the game. It can also customize objects and monsters in unique ways. Modders can configure or randomize the number of objects spawned per day, where and when they spawn, and more.

FTM supports content packs, allowing other mods to configure spawning automatically. If any of your other mods come with "[FTM]" folders, they will automatically use FTM to spawn custom content.

? In other languages: [url=https://github.com/Esca-MMC/FarmTypeManager/blob/master/docs/readme_zh.md]zh (中文)[/url]
[line]
[b][size=4]Installation[/size][/b]

1. [b]Install the latest version of [url=https://smapi.io/]SMAPI[/url].[/b]
2. [b]Download FarmTypeManager[/b] from the Files tab, the [url=https://github.com/Esca-MMC/FarmTypeManager/releases]GitHub releases page[/url], or [url=https://www.moddrop.com/stardew-valley/mods/598755-farm-type-manager]ModDrop[/url].
3. [b]Unzip FarmTypeManager[/b] into the [i]Stardew Valley/Mods[/i] folder.

If you have other mods that require FTM, then you're all set!

[b]Notes for multiplayer[/b]: [b]All players[/b] should have this mod installed. If only the host has this mod, other players might encounter SMAPI errors or be unable to see/interact with some objects and monsters.

Only the host's spawn settings will actually be used in multiplayer. Console commands should generally work for all players.
[line]
[size=4][b]Options[/b][/size]

FTM has a few basic "global" options for players, such as a limit to the number of monsters allowed in each area. To edit these options:

1. [b]Run the game[/b] using SMAPI.
2. [b]Open the config.json file[/b] in the [i]Stardew Valley/Mods/FarmTypeManager[/i] folder and edit its settings. Refer to the [url=https://github.com/Esca-MMC/FarmTypeManager#mod-settings]Mod Settings[/url] section on Github for more info.

Alternatively, you can install [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/stardewvalley/mods/5098]Generic Mod Config Menu (GMCM)[/url] and edit these settings in Stardew's main menu (look for a gear icon). The menu should display tooltips to explain each setting.
[line]
[b][size=4]Translation[/size][/b]

FTM supports translation of its GMCM setting names and descriptions.

The mod will load a file from the [i]FarmTypeManager/i18n[/i] folder that matches the current language code. If no matching translation exists, it will use [i]default.json[/i].

See the Stardew Valley Wiki's [url=https://stardewvalleywiki.com/Modding:Translations]Modding:Translations[/url] page for more information. Please feel free to submit translation files by linking them in a comment, submitting a GitHub pull request, or any other method.

The mod currently includes these translations:

[url=https://github.com/Esca-MMC/FarmTypeManager/blob/master/FarmTypeManager/i18n/default.json]English[/url]
[url=https://github.com/Esca-MMC/FarmTypeManager/blob/master/FarmTypeManager/i18n/zh.json]Chinese (Simplified)[/url] (by [url=https://github.com/SummerFleur2997]SummerFleur[/url])
[url=https://github.com/Esca-MMC/FarmTypeManager/blob/master/FarmTypeManager/i18n/nl.json]Dutch[/url] (by [url=https://github.com/UnrealThingTriesCode]UnrealThingTriesCode[/url])
[url=https://github.com/Esca-MMC/FarmTypeManager/blob/master/FarmTypeManager/i18n/fr.json]French[/url] (by [url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/Fsecho7]Fsecho7[/url])
[url=https://github.com/Esca-MMC/FarmTypeManager/blob/master/FarmTypeManager/i18n/de.json]German[/url] (by [url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/777PamPam777]777PamPam777[/url])
[url=https://github.com/Esca-MMC/FarmTypeManager/blob/master/FarmTypeManager/i18n/pt.json]Portuguese (Brazilian)[/url] (by [url=https://github.com/roanrobersson]roanrobersson[/url])
[url=https://github.com/Esca-MMC/FarmTypeManager/blob/master/FarmTypeManager/i18n/tr.json]Turkish[/url] (by [url=https://github.com/Rupurudu]Rupurudu[/url])
[url=https://github.com/Esca-MMC/FarmTypeManager/blob/master/FarmTypeManager/i18n/uk.json]Ukrainian[/url] (by [url=https://github.com/burunduk]burunduk[/url])
[line]
[size=4][b]Customization[/b][/size]

FTM is mainly used by content pack mods, but it can be also customized for personal use. To change settings for a specific character:

1. [b]Run the game[/b] using SMAPI.
2. [b]Load the farm[/b] you want to customize or create a new one. This will create files for your character in the [i]Stardew Valley/Mods/FarmTypeManager/data[/i] folder.
3. [b]Find the .json file[/b] with your character's name on it. Load it with the [b]ConfigEditor.html[/b] file in the [i]FarmTypeManager[/i] folder, or edit manually with any text editor.
4. [b]Customize the file's settings.[/b] For more info, see the Config Editor's tooltips or the [url=https://github.com/Esca-MMC/FarmTypeManager]GitHub readme[/url].
5. [b]Exit your farm and reload it[/b], or progress to the next in-game day. The mod's settings are updated every morning.

[b]Modders:[/b] To learn about creating FTM content packs, see the [url=https://github.com/Esca-MMC/FarmTypeManager]GitHub readme[/url]. The [url=https://github.com/Esca-MMC/FarmTypeManager#content-packs]Content Packs[/url] section explains how to start a new content pack.
[line]
[size=4][b]Commands[/b][/size]

This mod adds the following commands to SMAPI's console:

The [b]whereami[/b] command. Enter it to display information about your current location, including:

[list]
[*]The map's name (e.g. "Farm" or "BusStop")
[*]Your current tile's X/Y coordinates
[*]The tile's terrain type (e.g. "Dirt" or "Stone")
[*]Whether the tile is "Diggable" with tools
[*]The tile's spritesheet index number (used to identify "Quarry" tiles or set up custom tile-matching)
[/list]
The [b]list_monsters[/b] command. Enter it to display a list of available monster names to use with the MonsterName configuration setting.

The command will list the primary name of each monster from Stardew Valley itself, and then scan other mods for custom monster classes.

The [b]remove_items[/b] command. Enter it to delete an item or object directly in front of the player. This can be useful for certain objects that players can't remove normally, e.g. "permanent" objects spawned by mods.

name: CJB Cheats Menu
summary: A simple in-game cheats menu with lots of cheats.
description:
[b]CJB Cheats Menu[/b] lets you press [font=Courier New]P[/font] (configurable) to show a full cheats menu:

[img]https://raw.githubusercontent.com/CJBok/SDV-Mods/master/CJBCheatsMenu/docs/screenshot.gif[/img]

[b][color=#6aa84f]For non-English players, translations are included in the base mod. [url=https://github.com/CJBok/SDV-Mods#translating-the-mods]Contributions are welcome[/url]![/color][/b]


[size=6][b]Install[/b][/size][list=1]
[*][url=https://smapi.io]Install the latest version of SMAPI[/url].
[*]Download this mod and unzip it into [font=Courier New]Stardew Valley/Mods[/font].
[*]Run the game using SMAPI.
[/list]

[size=6][b]How to use[/b][/size]
Load your save, then [b]press [font=Courier New]P[/font] on your keyboard[/b] to show the cheats menu. Press the button [b]when no in-game menu is shown[/b].

To change the button:
[list]
[*]On Linux/macOS/Windows, open the menu and then use the "Controls" tab to choose a different controller/keyboard/mouse button. (You can also [url=https://stardewvalleywiki.com/Modding:Player_Guide/Key_Bindings]change [font=Courier New]config.json[/font] directly[/url] if needed.)
[*]On Android, see [url=https://stardewvalleywiki.com/Modding:Player_Guide/Key_Bindings#Use_key_bindings_on_Android][i]use key bindings on Android[/i][/url].
[/list]

[size=6][b]Troubleshooting[/b][/size]
If the menu doesn't open, see the pinned comment on [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/stardewvalley/mods/4/?tab=posts]the Posts tab[/url].


[size=6][b]Common questions[/b][/size]
[b][size=3]Is the mod still compatible?[/size][/b][color=#444444][list][*][color=#FFFFFF]The mod is compatible with Stardew Valley 1.6 or later on Linux/macOS/Windows, both single-player and multiplayer.[/color]
[/list][/color]
[b][size=3]Does the mod work in multiplayer?[/size][/b][color=#444444][list][*][color=#FFFFFF]Yep, all the cheats should work in multiplayer (except changing the time if you're a farmhand). The mod is local, so it's fine if only some players have it (whether they're the host or farmhands).[/color]
[color=#FFFFFF]
These cheats will affect all players: changes to the world (like changing the date or completing the community center), instant buildings, fast machines, harvest with sickle, adding money (if money is shared), durable fences, auto-feed, auto-pet, infinite hay, setting tomorrow's weather, and time. These cheats will affect players in the same location as you: one-hit kill and one-hit break. Otherwise cheats will mostly only affect you.

Other players usually won't see that you're using cheats (except indirectly, like seeing you zoom across the screen with the speed cheat). However, they might install mods that specifically detect your installed mods.[/color][/list][/color]
[b][size=3]Does the mod conflict with any others?[/size][/b][color=#444444][list][*][color=#FFFFFF]There are no known mod conflicts.[/color]
[/list][/color]
[b][size=3]Can you remove the limit on daily NPC dialogue?[/size][/b][color=#444444][list][*][color=#FFFFFF]Nope. Due to the way dialogue works in the game, NPCs would just repeat the same lines anyway. (The mod does remove the gift limit though.)[/color]
[/list][/color]
[b][size=3]Can you add custom skills/professions to the list?[/size][/b][color=#444444][list][*][color=#FFFFFF]Not currently. Custom skills are hard to support due to how they work in the game. It's something I want to look into for future versions, though it's low-priority since it'll probably require changes to all the skill mods too.[/color]
[/list][/color]
[b][size=3]Can the mod be used in split-screen mode?[/size][/b][color=#444444][list][*][color=#FFFFFF]Yep. You can [url=https://stardewvalleywiki.com/Modding:Player_Guide/Key_Bindings]edit the key binding[/url] to add a controller keybind. For example, if you do this:[/color]
[color=#FFFFFF][code]"OpenMenuKey": "P, LeftShoulder + ControllerB",
[/code]Then the main player can press [font=Courier New]P[/font] to open the menu on their screen, and the other players can press left shoulder + B on their controller to open it on theirs.[/color][/list][/color]

[size=6][b]See also[/b][/size]
Main links:
[list]
[*][url=https://github.com/CJBok/SDV-Mods]Source code[/url]
[*][url=https://github.com/CJBok/SDV-Mods/blob/master/CJBCheatsMenu/docs/release-notes.md#release-notes]Release notes[/url]
[*]Originally created by [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/stardewvalley/users/3108607]CJBok[/url] (maintained by Pathoschild since 2017)
[/list]
Other mods that might be useful:
[list]
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/stardewvalley/mods/14000]Change Farm Caves[/url]
[/list]
author: CJBok and Pathoschild

name: Lookup Anything
summary: See live info about whatever's under your cursor when you press F1. Learn a villager's favourite gifts, when a crop will be ready to harvest, how long a fence will last, why your farm animals are unhappy, and more.
description:
See live info about whatever's under your cursor when you press [font=Courier New]F1[/font]. Learn a villager's favourite gifts, when a crop will be ready to harvest, how long a fence will last, why your farm animals are unhappy, and more ([url=https://github.com/Pathoschild/StardewMods/tree/stable/LookupAnything#showcase]see showcase[/url]). Works on things in the world, your inventory, the calendar, game menus, shops, and more.

[img]https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Pathoschild/StardewMods/stable/LookupAnything/docs/screenshots/animated.gif[/img]

[b][color=#6aa84f][b][color=#6aa84f][b][color=#6aa84f][b][color=#6aa84f]For non-English players, translations are included in the base mod. [url=https://github.com/Pathoschild/StardewMods#translating-the-mods]Contributions are welcome[/url]![/color][/b][/color][/b][/color][/b][/color][/b]


[img]https://i.imgur.com/suHzq2E.png[/img]
[list=1]
[*][url=https://smapi.io]Install the latest version of SMAPI[/url].
[*]Unzip the mod folder into [font=Courier New]Stardew Valley/Mods[/font].
[*]Run the game using SMAPI.
[/list]

[img]https://i.imgur.com/j6fpZ3A.png[/img]
Just press [font=Courier New]F1[/font] to view info about something under your cursor, or press [font=Courier New]left shift[/font] + [font=Courier New]F1[/font] to search. (On laptops or macOS, you may need to press [font=Courier New]FN[/font] + [font=Courier New]F1[/font] instead. You can also set it to a different button using [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/stardewvalley/mods/5098]Generic Mod Config Menu[/url].)

If there's no cursor (e.g. when playing with a controller or on mobile), the most relevant subject is shown instead. That may be something in front of the player, the player on the skills menu, the NPC on their profile page, etc.

[url=https://github.com/Pathoschild/StardewMods/tree/stable/LookupAnything#showcase]See the showcase page[/url] for many examples and explanations of what the mod does. You can optionally [url=https://github.com/Pathoschild/StardewMods/tree/stable/LookupAnything#configure]customise the mod settings[/url] (including button mappings and optional data mining fields).


[img]https://i.imgur.com/M7KlO44.png[/img]
[list]
[*]Compatible with...[list]
[*]Stardew Valley 1.6.15 or later;
[*]Linux, macOS, and Windows;
[*]single-player and multiplayer (even if other players don't have it). In multiplayer mode, NPC schedules aren't visible to farmhands due to game limitations.
[/list]
[*]Mod conflicts:[list]
[*]Custom Ore Nodes: both mods will work fine, but you can't look up custom ore nodes yet.
[/list]
[/list]Other useful mods that complement Lookup Anything:[list]
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/stardewvalley/mods/8505]Event Lookup[/url];
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/stardewvalley/mods/8970]Fish Info Overlays[/url].
[/list]

[img]https://i.imgur.com/ccJ4SPZ.png[/img][list][*][url=https://github.com/Pathoschild/StardewMods/blob/stable/LookupAnything/docs/release-notes.md#release-notes]Release notes[/url]
[*][url=https://github.com/Pathoschild/StardewMods/tree/stable/LookupAnything]Source code[/url]
[*][url=https://github.com/Pathoschild/StardewMods#readme]My other mods[/url]
[/list]
[size=1]Tiny reminder: endorse the mod if you like it. It means more people will see it, and it makes the author happy. :)[/size]
author: Pathoschild

name: Skull Cavern Elevator
summary: Adds an elevator to the Skull Cavern
description:
[color=#6aa84f]This Mod is based on SMAPI 3.0+, for installing SMAPI refer to https://smapi.io/.
For installation unpack the zip-File in the Mods Folder, just like any other SMAPI Mod.[/color]

An elevator like in the normal mine is added to the Skull Cavern, like in the normal mine the elevator is updated every 5th Floor by default.
Note: The elevator is always placed to the right of the ladder.

If you want to support us, feel free to leave us a tip at: [url=https://www.buymeacoffee.com/Bifibi]Buy me a Coffee
[/url]Thanks

[size=4]Update 1.1.2[/size]

In the folder SkullCavernElevator there will be a file called config.json after starting the game with the mod, in this file you can change how frequent the elevators appear. You just need to change the value behind the "elevatorStep" : (the 5). 

[size=4]Update 1.2.1
[/size]
A new setting called "difficulty" has been added to the config.json file. It changes the number of elevator levels appearing in the menu. The value is calculated as follows: deepestMineLevel/elevatorSteps/difficulty.

[size=4]Update 1.3.0[/size]
A new setting called "FirstLevelElevatorActive": has been added to the config.json file. It is used as a workaround if the elevator is not shown in the entrance area, but if set to "true" the elevator will appear in the first floor.
[size=4]
Update 1.4.0[/size]
The settings in the config.json were renamed to start with a Capital letter, difficulty -> Difficulty, elevatorStep -> ElevatorStep, firstLevelElevatorActive -> FirstLevelElevatorActive.

A new setting called "ElevatorCostPerStep": with default value 0 has been added to the config.json file. If this value is set to > 0, a cost will be calculated and displayed in the elevator menu. The formula is (Position of Elevator displayed) * ElevatorCostPerStep. This cost will be subtracted from your current available money. If you do not have enough, the elevator will not be used.

[size=4]Difficulty and elevator setting

[/size]A few examples to illustrate how difficulty and elevatorStep work. In all examples it is assumed that the lowest mine level reached is 40.
First elevatorStep, this changes step width between elevators appearing.
elevatorStep 5 (and default difficulty 1) means when you reach level 40 of the SkullCavern, you will see the elevators: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35 and 40
elevatorStep 10 (and default difficulty 1) means when you reach level 40 of the SkullCavern, you will see the elevators: 10, 20, 30 and 40

Difficulty setting changes how difficult it is to travel further down with the elevator.
Difficulty 2 (and default elevatorStep 5) means when you reach level 40 of the SkullCavern, you will only see elevators up to level 20 (My Lowest Level[40]/Difficulty[2]). Means elevators: 5, 10, 15 and 20
Difficulty 4 (and default elevatorStep 5) means when you reach level 40 of the SkullCavern, you will only see elevators up to level 10 (My Lowest Level[40]/Difficulty[4]). Means elevators: 5 and 10
As you can see, a higher value in Difficulty means that less elevators appear and you need to get further down to unlock new elevators.

A combination of elevatorStep and Difficulty can look like this:
Difficulty 1.5 and elevatorStep 10 means when you reach level 40 of the SkullCavern, you will only see elevators up to level (Lowest Level[40]/Difficulty[1.5]) 26. Means elevators: 10 and 20. (due to the elevatorStep of 10).


[url=http://community.playstarbound.com/threads/smapi-skull-cavern-elevator.130637/]Official Discussion Thread[/url]

name: Mill Quality Mod
summary:
Makes mill products inherit the quality of their input items.
让磨坊产物继承输入材料的品质。
description:
In vanilla Stardew Valley, the mill always produces normal-quality items, regardless of the quality of the ingredients used. This mod changes that behavior to make processing more consistent and rewarding.

✨ Features[list]
[*]Each processed item retains the same quality as its input
[*]One-to-one quality mapping (not averaged or highest-based)
[*]Works with all vanilla mill recipes:
[list]
[*]Wheat → Flour
[*]Beet → Sugar
[*]Unmilled Rice → Rice
[/list][*]Supports all quality levels:
[list]
[*]Normal
[*]Silver
[*]Gold
[*]Iridium
[/list][*]Fully compatible with existing saves
[*]No configuration required (plug & play)
[/list][line]? Example[list]
[*]5 Gold Wheat → 5 Gold Flour
[*]3 Iridium Beet → 9 Iridium Sugar
[*]Mixed inputs:
[list]
[*]2 Gold Wheat + 3 Normal Wheat →
2 Gold Flour + 3 Normal Flour[/list][/list][line]⚙️ Requirements[list]
[*]SMAPI 4.0+
[*]Stardew Valley 1.6+
[/list]在原版星露谷物语中，无论你使用什么品质的原料，磨坊产物始终都是普通品质。本模组对此进行了优化，使加工结果更加合理且具有收益价值。

✨ 功能特点[list]
[*]每个产物与对应原料品质一一对应
[*]不是取最高品质，也不是平均品质
[*]支持所有原版磨坊配方：
[list]
[*]小麦 → 面粉
[*]甜菜 → 糖
[*]未碾米 → 大米
[/list][*]支持全部品质等级：
[list]
[*]普通
[*]银星
[*]金星
[*]铱星
[/list][*]可兼容已有存档
[*]无需配置，安装即用
[/list][line]? 示例[list]
[*]5个金星小麦 → 5个金星面粉
[*]3个铱星甜菜 → 9个铱星糖
[*]混合情况：
[list]
[*]2个金星小麦 + 3个普通小麦 →
2个金星面粉 + 3个普通面粉[/list][/list][line]⚙️ 前置需求[list]
[*]SMAPI 4.0 或更高版本
[*]星露谷物语 1.6+
[/list][list]
[/list]
author: yszl2159

name: The Melting Pot - ZH
summary:
The Melting Pot 的汉化.

《星露谷物语》的大炖不讳是一个美食料理扩充模组，加入了来自世界各地多元的料理！甚至是你不知道的地方，你喜爱的料理起源地！
description:
[size=4]欢迎来到“大炖不讳”模组-

一场美食冒险，让来自各大洲的风味在这里和谐汇聚！这个模组不仅通过你可以在沙漠商人处购买的6本食谱书为你的菜单增添风味，还引入了使用 ItemExtension 框架的全新功能！别忘了去 格斯那里拿一箱 可乐娜啤酒，里面有六瓶清爽的 可乐娜啤酒-庆祝的绝佳选择！也别错过在 皮埃尔 那里的包装番石榴！这个美味物品会赠送你一块实心番石榴糖，与所附食谱分开！去沙漠商人那儿，用日常物品换取一小口来自 Stardew Valley 丘陵之外世界的风味，直接送到你温馨的小厨房！每种食物还配有独特的幽默描述，如果你喜欢一点小搞笑甚至是黑色幽默的话! :P

作者：[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/stardewvalley/users/143352938]Unicornkiitty[/url]

原模组：[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/stardewvalley/mods/28734?tab=description]The Melting Pot at Stardew Valley Nexus - Mods and community

[/url][/size][size=3]此汉化只做了大部份机翻跟少部份能翻译成原话的幽默段子,原作很有趣但要让它本地化的有趣我不如闪边去

所以如果有哪些食物让你想起哪些著名电影桥段or你个人所知的笑话欢迎投稿( 我要验牌！

ex: mod里的肉干我就用了这段[/size]

[youtube]4tuQVvvJsLw[/youtube]

name: Find a Familiar - Ferngill Familiar Association
summary: An expansion mod which currently includes 4 new characters! Still a huge WIP, but come meet the gang! :)
description:
Hi friends! This will be my submission for the 2026 Stardew Modding Festival ([url=https://www.nexusmods.com/news/15436]A Decade in the Valley - Modfest 2026 at Nexus mods and community[/url])
THE SUBMISSION DATE STARTS ON 02/26/2026 AND ENDS ON 03/26/2026- it's not too late to join, so please do! I would love to see many wonderful new mods crop up! :)

Oh yeah, and I guess I'm supposed to mention my chosen team/theme:
Team Bog, led by [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/profile/BogWyytch]BogWyytch[/url] and [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/profile/Abagaianye]abagaianye[/url] - A theme inspired by boggy, witchy ideas. Great for anything with a mossy, mysterious, or eerie feel.

[b][size=5]TW: Themes of mental illness, war
[/size]I do not make it my aim to, in any way, shape, or form- belittle the struggles of the mentally ill or neurodivergent. Often times, the feelings expressed by my characters are the artistic expression of my own feelings. That is to say, it might get a little trauma dumpy. But these things are portrayed as problems, issues that the character struggles with- NOT a glorification.
Most of my characters are at least morally pacifistic. That is to say, they are often anti-war and anti-violence.
You can often times avoid these more emotional moments by being short during events and not asking questions. Of course, you won't always get the most satisfying responses- but generally you won't HURT your relationship with Salem unless you choose the obviously mean option.
[/b]
[b][size=5]Salem the (not) Cat! (He/She/They/It)
[/size][/b][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1303/images/thumbnails/42872/42872-1772950518-872228578.png[/img]
[size=3][b]Includes:[/b][/size]
﻿-Salem Portrait and Sprite (Thank you, Fellowclown!)
-An introductory event sequence comprised of 5 mini-events.
﻿-Two heart event sequence composed of 7 mini-events.
﻿-Four heart event
﻿-Optional five heart event sequence (reliant on completing two heart event sequence in its entirety)
-Six heart event
-Eight heart event
-Ten heart event
﻿-More than 40 total lines of dialogue (not including events)
﻿-Trinket Tinker Companion!
-Decide between having Salem as an indoor cat or outside cat!
-Use GMCM to edit Salem's spawn co-ordinates for custom maps!


[b][size=5]Luna the Shewolf! (She/They)[/size]
[/b][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1303/images/thumbnails/42872/42872-1772950518-803043992.png[/img]
[size=3][b]Includes:
[/b][/size]﻿-Luna Portrait and Sprite (Fellowclown!!!)
-An introductory event sequence comprised of 3 mini-events.
-Two heart event
﻿﻿-Invite Luna to live in your farm cave!
﻿-Trinket Tinker Companion!
﻿-More than 10 total lines of dialogue (not including events)
﻿-Use GMCM to edit Luna's spawn co-ordinates for custom maps!


[size=5][b]Lechuga the Frog! (She/Her)[/b][/size]
[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1303/images/42872/42872-1772950519-1677913703.png[/img]
[size=3][b]Includes:
[/b][/size]-Lechuga Portrait and Sprite (OF COURSE IT WAS FELLOWCLOWN!)
-At two hearts, ask Lechuga to become your familiar (assuming you have what she wants!)!
-Trinket Tinker Companion!
-Two whole lines of dialogue! (Not including events)
﻿-Use GMCM to edit Lechuga's spawn co-ordinates for custom maps!


[b][size=5](OPTIONAL) Eddie Munson (He/Him)[/size]
[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1303/images/thumbnails/42872/42872-1772950519-856688163.png[/img]
[size=3]Includes:[/size]
[/b]﻿-This IS Eddie Munson from Stranger Things, and is meant to be integrated into the Stardew Universe in the most "immersive" way possible a la isekai-style fan fiction. Don't want Eddie in your game? That's fine! He's disabled by default, and can only be enabled by enabling him in the GMCM!
﻿-Eddie Portrait and Sprite (Fellowclown)
-2 Event intro sequence
-2 Heart event
-4 Heart event
-Optional event with Sam and Sebastian depending on your choices in 2 heart event
-More than 15 total lines of dialogue (not including events)
-Use GMCM to enable Eddie! Note that once he is enabled, you cannot disable him!
-[b]PLEASE NOTE THAT EDDIE HAS LIKE, THE ONLY EXAMPLE OF CURSING IN THE MOD? I THINK? HE SAYS SHIT ONCE. I THINK THAT'S IT IN THE ENTIRE MOD? SORRY IF THIS LOSES MY G RATING :([/b]

[size=5][b](OPTIONAL) Clover Field (She/Her)[/b]
[/size][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1303/images/42872/42872-1773448306-1938814629.png[/img]
[b]Includes:[/b]
[size=2]-Intro Event
[/size][size=2]-3 lines of dialogue!! WhoAAA!
[/size]-She can live in your house or at the saloon!
-Use GMCM to enable Eddie! Note that once he is enabled, you cannot disable him!

[b]OTHER FEATURES:
[/b][size=2]-Enable the early trinket setting in GMCM to equip your contracts without having to unlock combat mastery first!
[/size]-The FFA Novice License can be used to have up to 2 familiars equipped simultaneously.
-Advance within the FFA to receive the FFA Associate License, allowing 3 equipped contracts.
-Honestly check the most recent changelog! TOO MUCH STUFF FOR ME TO UPDATE THIS RN, I'M GOING TO BED!!!

[size=4][b]What comes next? NOT IMPLEMENTED
[/b][/size]Well, honestly, this mod is probably going to grow exponentially as my skill increases (time permitting). So, what do I have planned for the future? Check it out:
-Full heart events for [b]every NPC in the mod[/b]. I'm talking a minimum of 5 heart events (2 hearts, 4 hearts, 6 hearts, 8 hearts, 10 hearts) for every NPC!
-Full daily dialogue for [b]every NPC in the mod[/b]. I'm talking a minimum of 40 dialogue lines per NPC!
-A location for the Ferngill Familiar Association Headquarters
-Quests!
﻿-Advance within the FFA!
﻿-Recruit Eddie and Clover to join the FFA!
-Maximum compatibility! I'm a mod fiend!
-Crossovers and references to friends! I'M A MOD FIEND!
-Working translation files for easy translation!
-Conversation Topics for reactive dialogue!

-Open to suggestions!

[size=4][b]A new challenger approaches! NOT IMPLEMENTED
[/b][/size]A LIST OF MY PLANNED NPCs, REFERENCES TO MY FRIENDS!
 
soon... Ares the Hawk [img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1303/images/42872/42872-1773448305-305740210.png[/img]
soon... Abbi the Red Panda [img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1303/images/thumbnails/42872/42872-1773485701-988864701.png[/img]
soon... Peachi the Junimo [img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1303/images/thumbnails/42872/42872-1773485669-548383823.png[/img]
soon... Reece the Tuxedo Cat [img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1303/images/42872/42872-1773611298-1591044799.png[/img]
soon... Nafanio the Nightwing
eventually... Corvus the Grandmaster
eventually... Abbot the Phoenix
eventually... Gus the Goose
eventually... Kali the Kitty
eventually... Payton the Panda
...and that's just as of right now!

If anyone is interested in collaborating, please, let me know!

[b]KNOWN BUGS[/b]: AFAIK there are currently none! That being said, [b]please, please make me aware of bugs that need fixes[/b]! I am currently NOT interested in reports about incompatibilities (aside from specifically SVE-related incompatibility), but feel free to make the bug reports regardless. Just know that I will not get to them for a long, long time.

[b]Compatibility[/b]:
-Should be compatible with most things! At this time, I make no changes to any vanilla data. Salem is currently set to only be in your Farmhouse/on your Farm.
-[b]Event Limiter [/b]is an excellent mod with an incredible vision. I use it frequently because I often have hundreds of NPCs in my game. Unfortunately, several of the "mini-events" in my mod operate under the assumption they are taking place on the same day. Naturally, Event Limiter has potential to break up the timing and therefore change the way you experience the mod. I would recommend disabling it or at least making it so that there is no limit to the amount of events you can see in a single day.
-[b]SVE and Expansions[/b]: Should be mostly compatible to the best of my knowledge! I'm using the vanilla version of the Wizard for some events, so there might be some consistency issues I need to iron out. But it seems to be working, and I believe I have fixed any event changes that were broken due to map edits.
-[b]Custom Farm Maps compatible!:[/b] Use the GMCM to change Salem's Outside X/Y co-ordinates. Preferably, you should do this BEFORE starting the questline to meet Salem. If Salem has already spawned, you can use [b]debug killNPC alexanderthedead.faf_Salem [/b]to remove Salem and respawn him the next day.
-[b]Custom Farmhouse Maps compatible![/b]: Use the GMCM to change Salem's Inside X/Y co-ordinates. Preferably, you should do this BEFORE starting the questline to meet Salem. If Salem has already spawned, you can use [b]debug killNPC alexanderthedead.faf_Salem[/b] to remove Salem and respawn him the next day.
-[b]Custom FarmCave Maps (mostly?*) compatible![/b]: Use the GMCM to change Luna's X/Y co-ordinates. Preferably, you should do this BEFORE starting the questline to summon Luna. If Luna has already spawned, you can use [b]debug killNPC alexanderthedead.faf_Luna[/b] to remove Luna and respawn her the next day.
[b]-Mod rights are completely open, feel free to upload compatibility patches or edited versions of the mod.[/b]

**It occurs to me as I make some minor changes on this page that, with some of Luna's events playing in the FarmCave map, it's possible that a custom FarmCave map could break these events. Worst case scenario, you should be able to use the NoClip mod ([url=https://www.nexusmods.com/stardewvalley/mods/3900]Noclip Mode at Stardew Valley Nexus - Mods and community[/url]) and activating NoClip during the event should at least allow the event to continue? Might be a little wonky! Sorry! :)

[b]Language[/b]: Sorry for my lazy, English-centric brain. I started the mod before I understood how translation files worked. Translation, unfortunately, is currently the last thing on my to-do list. Mod rights are completely open, feel free to upload translation patches or edited version of the mod.

[b]To start the demo, head to the community center. From there, you should be able to figure out the rest.

[/b]Big thank you to Fellowclown for providing all the (good) art in the mod! There are still some assets leftover that I drew myself, but if you see something that looks good... You can bet it was Fellowclown! [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/profile/PossiblyClown]PossiblyClown's Profile - Nexus Mods[/url]

Thank you to the Stardew Valley and Stardew Modding wiki contributors and specifically tiakall ([url=https://www.nexusmods.com/profile/tiakall/mods]tiakall's Mods - Nexus Mods[/url]), whose guide for custom NPCs and templates ([url=https://stardewmodding.wiki.gg/wiki/Tutorial:_Making_a_Custom_NPC]Tutorial: Making a Custom NPC - Stardew Modding Wiki[/url]) I have been using. 

Thank you to mushymato ([url=https://www.nexusmods.com/profile/mushymato/mods]mushymato's Mods - Nexus Mods[/url]) for their work on TrinketTinker and several of its associated content packs. Specifically, thank you to mushymato for allowing me to steal their code to implement early trinket slots via mail flag manipulation and "critter boxes" (which I will be turning into "Contract Books" to hold multiple contracts!)

Additional thanks to the innumerable people who have helped me at one point or another from the Official Stardew Valley Discord! I'll list who I remember below- if you helped me out, feel free to remind you and I'll be more than happy to add you to my credits!
-Claire: [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/profile/ClairesStoryofSeasons/mods]ClairesStoryofSeasons's Mods - Nexus Mods[/url]

author: AlexanderTheDead

name: Better Frog Trinket
summary: Trinket drop chance, choose frog color, tongue range, fullness time, which monster to eat, if monster drop loot or coin, if frog can heal and as basilisk paw.
description:
This mod overhauls Hungry Frog Companion, giving you full control over how the frog behaves, what it eats, and what rewards it provides.

Features:

[list]
[*]Force frog variant - choose specific frog color instead random roll one
[*]Tongue reach - control how far the frog can eat monster
[*]Fullness time - control how long the frog stays full to eat again
[*]Trinket drop chance - adjust trinket drop chance
[*]Loot drop - toggle if monster eaten drop the normal loot
[*]Coin drop - toggle if monster eaten drop coin like parrot egg, drop chance and count
[*]Healing - toggle auto healing like fairy box, healing interval and power
[*]Debuff - toggle if frog grant debuff immunity like basilisk paw
[*]Monster control - whitelist or blacklist monster type to eat
[/list]Perfect for player who want either a balanced vanilla-friendly tweak or totally overpowered trinket frog. All features are fully configurable via [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/stardewvalley/mods/5098]Generic Mod Config Menu (GMCM)[/url]

Checkout my other QoL mods on
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/profile/po0pfart/mods]po0pfart's Mods[/url]

author: AlleyBalley and ZoeyHoshi

name: Script Merger
summary: Detects conflicts between mods & helps you resolve them, either by creating merged files that override the conflicting ones, or by configuring a custom load order for your mods. Merges are easy to undo because they don't change the original mod or game files. Depending on your mods, some merges may require basic understanding of code.
description:
[center][color=#ffff00][size=5]Why It's Useful[/size][/color][/center]
When you have multiple mods that modify the same game file, TW3 only loads 1 of the modded versions of that file, overriding all over versions.  This kind of conflict is common & can break mods.  Script Merger tries to make the problem less painful.

[color=#ffff00][size=4][center][size=5]What It Does[/size][/center][/size][/color]
Despite the name, Script Merger detects mod conflicts in .bundle packages as well as .ws scripts.  Then, it helps you resolve them by creating merged versions of conflicting text files. It leaves the original files untouched, but overrides them. [b]It can't merge non-text conflicts[/b], but for these it can help you manage your custom load order (mods.settings file) to make certain mods override others.

Uses these 3rd-party external tools:
- [url=http://kdiff3.sourceforge.net/]KDiff3[/url] by Joachim Eibl, for merging text files.
- [url=http://aluigi.altervista.org/quickbms.htm]QuickBMS[/url] by Luigi Auriemma, for scanning & unpacking .bundle files.
- [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/news/12625/?]wcc_lite[/url] by CD Projekt RED, for packing merged text files into .bundle files.

Also contains code from [url=https://github.com/wilhelmliao/xxHash.NET]xxHash.NET[/url] by Wilhelm Liao, to detect when mod updates have changed files that you merged.

[color=#ffff00][size=4][center]NOTES[/center][/size][/color]
When merging, KDiff3 simply does a [color=#ffff00][i][b]text merge[/b][/i][/color].  In many cases, that works fine.  But because the tool doesn't actually understand the code, sometimes it can merge things in the wrong order. Auto-resolved conflicts aren't guaranteed to be correct.  In these cases, it needs human help.

Also, script mods can have [color=#ffff00][i][b]logic conflicts[/b][/i][/color] that the tool can't detect.  In these cases, even if the text can merge without direct conflicts, the resulting script won't work properly.  For example, if Mod A tries to [color=#ffff00][i][b]hide [/b][/i][/color]the HUD, but then Mod B [i][b][color=#ffff00]displays[/color][/b][/i] the HUD, Mod A won't work.  Resolving this also requires human help.

NMM users: Currently, Script Merger doesn't support [b][color=#ff0000]NMM's multi-HD mode[/color][/b] (or symlinks in general).





[color=#ffff00][size=5][center]Instructions[/center][/size][/color]
1)  Download, extract, & launch Script Merger.

2)  Enter your game's installation path in the textbox at the top.

3)  Click "Refresh" button above the "Conflicts" list.

4)  If mergeable conflicts are detected, repeat these steps until the conflicts are resolved:
     > Choose 2 or more versions of any file(s) to merge.
     > Click "Create Selected Merge" button.
     > If the mods have differences that don't overlap, KDiff3 can merge them automatically.
     > If the mods have differences that overlap, KDiff3 will appear so you can merge them manually.  See below for tutorial videos.
     > If Script Merger can't find the vanilla version of the modded file, you must merge all differences manually when KDiff3 appears.

5)  If non-text conflicts are detected, Script Merger displays them but can't merge them.  So, one of the mods in each conflict will "win" & override the others.  Here's how the winner is decided:
     > By default, the game loads mods in order of folder names — numbers first, then underscores, then letters (not case-sensitive).
     > Or, you can right-click one of the mods to set its priority (1 to 9999, lower number loads first).  Here's an excellent [url=http://www.moddb.com/mods/the-witcher-3-wild-hunt-launcher/downloads/using-the-modssettings-file]detailed explanation[/url] by Kad_Venku.

6)  Play!

7)  When you uninstall or update one of the mods included in a merge, Script Merger will detect the change & prompt you to delete affected merges.  If you update a mod & Script Merger doesn't prompt you delete any of that mod's merges, then they weren't affected by the update.



[center][color=#ffff00][size=5]Tutorial Videos[/size][/color]
[size=4]
General Usage
(thanks to [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/users/38219/?]Gopher[/url] & [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/users/905332/?]wghost81[/url])
[color=#f1c232]Note: Gopher's tutorial, though quite helpful overall, says to edit the Script Merger config file manually.  Typically, this is unnecessary & increases the chance of misconfiguration.[/color]
[youtube]YolaeWk5Dm4?start=574[/youtube] [youtube]STQRXwaqzvY?start=47[/youtube]

Using KDiff3[/size]
[size=2][url=https://www.youtube.com/v/3kyAbHiUqZQ?start=139&end=193&autoplay=1]This video[/url] shows a couple KDiff3 basics. [url=https://youtu.be/-CkqiIPAzgQ?t=6m6s]This video[/url] goes a bit more in-depth.[/size][/center]


[center][color=#ffff00][size=5]Example Scenario[/size][/color][/center][quote][size=2][b]
Mod 1 - modFOVTweak8070[/b][/size] ([url=http://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/321/?]link[/url])
[list]
[*][color=#ffd966][size=2]content\scripts\game\player\[b]r4Player.ws[/b][/size][/color]
[/list]
[b][size=2]Mod 2 - modManualCam[/size][/b] ([url=http://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/326/?]link[/url])
[list]
[*][size=2]content\scripts\game\cameras\combat\customCamera.ws[/size]
[*][size=2]content\scripts\game\player\playerInput.ws[/size]
[*][size=2][color=#ffd966]content\scripts\game\player\[b]r4player.ws[/b][/color][/size]
[/list]
[size=2][b]Mod 3 - modNoWitcherSense[/b][/size] ([url=http://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/351/?]link[/url])
[list]
[*][size=2][color=#ffd966]content\scripts\game\player\[b]r4Player.ws[/b][/color][/size]
[/list][/quote]If we successfully merge all 3 versions of [b]r4Player.ws[/b], then Script Merger will put the merged script here by default:
[quote][b]
New Mod - mod0000_MergedFiles
[/b][list]
[*][color=#1e84cc][size=2]content\scripts\game\player\[b]r4Player.ws[/b][/size][/color]
[/list][/quote]The new mod folder starts with "0000_" to ensure that it loads before any other mod folder.  (TW3 loads in ASCII order.)  Because the merged version of r4Player.ws loads first, the non-merged versions will simply be ignored by the game.

[center][color=#ffff00][size=5]
Uninstallation[/size][/color][/center][size=2]
[/size][list]
[*][size=2]To undo all merges & return your mods to their original state, delete the merged mod folder (mod0000_MergedFiles in v0.5, mod__MergedScripts in v0.4.3).[/size]
[*][size=2]To completely remove Script Merger from your computer, delete the files that were in the .zip file you downloaded, as well as the generated MergeInventory.xml file if you have it.[/size]
[/list]
