[youtube]2oOrRNhiF5Q[/youtube][youtube]k33430KHQpA[/youtube]


[size=1]Guide by [/size][size=1][color=#6d9eeb][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/users/905332/?tb=about&pUp=1][color=#6d9eeb]Wasteland Ghost aka wghost81[/color][/url] [/color]can be found here:[/size]
[url=https://wghost81.wordpress.com/2017/11/13/the-witcher-3-mod-manager-0-4-1beta/][color=#6d9eeb][size=6]Guide to The Witcher 3 Mod Manager[/size][/color][/url][size=6][/size]

[size=1]More detailed explanation of each feature can be found on forum page:[/size]
[url=https://forums.cdprojektred.com/forum/en/the-witcher-series/the-witcher-3-wild-hunt/mod-discussions/9668201-tool-the-witcher-3-mod-manager][color=#6d9eeb][size=6]The Witcher 3 Mod Manager - CDPR Forum[/size][/color][/url]




[color=#6d9eeb]________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
[/color]
[color=#6d9eeb][size=5]What is it?[/size]
[size=2][/size][/color][color=#6d9eeb]________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________[/color]

It is a tool that allows you to install and uninstall Witcher 3 mods in one click with a many more features!

[color=#6d9eeb][size=4][u]FEATURES:[/u][/size][/color]

[color=#a4c2f4]1) installation:[/color]

- Detects and installs Main mod files and folders
- Adds DLC files and folders if found
- Adds Mod menus if found
- Adds custom keys to input.xml if found
- Adds custom keys to input.settings if found
- Adds custom settings to user.settings if found
- Remembers mod state for later uninstallation
- Overwrites or first removes old files if found
[size=3]- Can install multiple mods at the same time
[/size]- Can install mods from zip, rar or 7z archives

[color=#a4c2f4]2) Uninstallation:[/color]

- Deletes main mod files and folders
- Deletes added keys to input.xml
- Deletes added mod menus
- Deletes added DLC files
[size=3]- Can uninstall multiple mods at the same time[/size]

[color=#a4c2f4]3) Disabling mods:[/color]

- Does not remove anything just makes it so the mod is ignored by the game which includes:
- Renames the modXXX to ~modXXX
- Renames all dlc files XXX to XXX.disabled
- Renames all mod menus XXX.xml to XXX.xml.disabled
- Removes all input.xml keys added by the mod

[color=#a4c2f4]4) Enabling mods:[/color]

- Revertes everything disabling did
- Restores the keys back to input.xml

[color=#a4c2f4]5) Settings:[/color]

- Configures path and some options

[color=#a4c2f4]6) Other features:[/color]

- Custom toolbar actions - run anything directly from the manager
- Load order display
- Editing mod priorities on the fly
- Displaying everything the mod installed
- Mod Renaming
- Installation by Drag and Drop
- Search currently installed mods
- . . .



[color=#6d9eeb]________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
[/color]
[color=#6d9eeb][size=5]How to use it?[/size]
[size=2]___________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________[/size][/color]

On first run select it's going to prompt you to select witcher3.exe file if you already didn't

[color=#a4c2f4]installation:[/color]

1) Either click button install Mod, go to Mods/Install Mod or press Ctrl+E
2) Select* folder(s) which contain mod files or archive(s) file ( .zip, .rar or .7z) or both at the same time
3) It's done and you can read how many of each files it installed
[color=#a4c2f4]
Uninstallation:

[/color]1) Select one or more mods in the list and you can either click on Uninstall Mods, go to Mods/Uninstall Mods, right click on them and select Uninstall Selected Mods or press Delete button on the keyboard.
2) Confirm, and it's done.

[color=#a4c2f4]Enabling/Disabling mods:[/color]

To disable one or more mods, simply select them in the list and you can either click on Enable/Disable Mods, go to Mods/Enable/Disable Mods, right click on them and select Enable/Disable Selected Mods, press Ctrl+Q on the keyboard or you can simply double click on the mod.

[color=#a4c2f4]Installation of merged scripts:[/color]

Select 2 or more mods that the merged scripts are for. Then you can either go to Mods/Install Merged Scripts or press Ctrl+M on the keyboard. You will be prompted to select folder containing merger scripts, most likely it's going to mod0000_MergedFiles folder

[color=#a4c2f4]Configuring settings:[/color]

Go to Settings\Configure Settings ... and change available options

[color=#6d9eeb]________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
[/color]
[color=#6d9eeb][size=5]How does it actually work?[/size][/color]
[color=#6d9eeb]________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
[/color]

[color=#a4c2f4]Detection:[/color]

If the selected file is archive it extracts it first and then does the same things as for regular folder. The installer searches through all files and folders within selected folder trying to find files it needs. Searches mod* folders for actual mod data, dlc folder for dlc data, .xmls for mod menus, .txt files and input.xml file for keys. Therefore inner structure doesn't matter much, if it's there the installer is going to find it. Also when reading txt files it will ignore any text that is not in a structure of input.xml or input.settings, and when it finds correct lines it will extract them and use them later.

[color=#a4c2f4]Installation:[/color]

After it detects all files and folders, it copies all mod* folders to Game\Mods folder, DLC folders to Game\DLC folder, mod menu xmls to Game\bin\config\r4game\user_config_matrix\pc folder. After that if it found and key lines, first it opens input.xml and looks for them, if not found it adds lines above <!-- [BASE_CharacterMovement] --> line. If input.xml file is provided by mod author and not only new Vars, installer will extract all lines from it and will try to add them. Since only new lines are different only they should be added. Also if mod author defined a new xml group in input.xml, the whole group will be put in the correct position and not above base_characterMovement. Same thing happnes to input.settings except it searches for them based on the context they are in and adds them to their correct context. As for user.settings it just adds lines on the beginning of the file, as the game merges those automatically and uses last written value for the setting.

[color=#a4c2f4]Installation of multiple mods:[/color]

It does basically the same thing as regular installation of one mod, only it repeats the process for each mod it found

[color=#a4c2f4]Uninstallation:
[/color]
The Installer deletes selected mod* folder, searches to see if that mod added any mod dlc files, menus or lines to input.xml, if it did it deletes them too. Note that only the mods installed using this installer will have their input.xml lines, dlc files and mod menus deleted. For the ones you already have you have do it manually or reinstall them using the installer. 

[color=#a4c2f4]Enabling/Disabling mods:[/color]

When you choose to disable selected mod ( or mods ) it renames mod* folder to ~mod* folder, making the game ignore it. It also disables all other files added by the mod, as it renames all dlc files (blobs, metadatas, textures etc) and adds them .disabled extention, renames all mod menu *.xml to *.xml.disabled and removes all keys from input.xml. When you select to enable it back it basically reverts everything disabling process did and restores the mod back to normal.

[color=#6d9eeb]________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
[/color]
[color=#6d9eeb][size=5]NOTES[/size][/color]
[color=#6d9eeb][size=2]___________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________[/size]
[/color]

[color=#e06666][size=3]- This is still the BETA version of the program so some bugs are possible but not expected[/size][/color]
- Since there are really no rules how the mod authors structure and present their mods it's quite difficult to cover all cases, but t
his program tries to cover as much as possible
- The installer will prompt you to Run the script merger after each install/uninstall. Can be turned off in the 
- Output is mostly used for showing any errors that occured
- The Mod Manager supports only 64bit windows
- 0.4 version is not backwards compatible
- Only mods installed with 0.4 version and onward will be displayed in the installed mods section


[color=#6d9eeb]________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
[/color]
[color=#6d9eeb][size=5]Plans for future versions?[/size][/color]
[color=#6d9eeb]________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
[/color]
[color=#93c47d][s]- Add support for installing many mods at the same time instead of only one at the time  [/s] [size=4](ADDED)[/size][/color]
[color=#93c47d][s]- Add support for installing mods from .zip, .rar and .7z archives[/s][size=4] (ADDED)[/size][/color]

[color=#93c47d][s]- integrate script merger[/s] [size=4](ADDED)[/size][/color][/color]
[color=#93c47d][s]- Support installing hidden.xml data as well [/s][size=4](ADDED)[/size][/color]
[color=#93c47d][s]- Show actual mod names in the list on the right, instead of all mod* folders [/s][size=4](ADDED)[/size][/color]
[color=#93c47d][s]- Add automatic updates [/s][size=4](ADDED)[/size][/color][size=4]
[/size]- Add feature to automatically download and install a mod when providing URL
- Add Profiles
- Suggestions?

[color=#6d9eeb]________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
[/color]
[color=#6d9eeb][size=6]THANKS[/size][/color]
[color=#6d9eeb]________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
[/color]

[size=6][b]Install with Vortex or manually:

* Halk Ultra HD config is highly recommended to achieve best quality and game look. Thanks to this configuration general quality of textures will be better (better sharpness, no flickering/shimmering, better smoothness) and game will can render 4K textures (instead of 2K). Also in some cases game can work smoother and faster by increased textures budget.
[/size][size=5]* If you have ##preset_value_Halk Ultra HD or blank area instead of Halk Ultra HD in graphic settings - install [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/897]Custom Localization Fix[/url]﻿ (It's not an important matter, but if it bothers you, it's easy to fix it).[/size]
[color=#980000]
[/color][img]https://i.imgur.com/aktAi94.png[/img]
[size=5] It mainly depends on your video card, but generally modification doesn't hit performance too much. Modification requires minimum a 3GB VRAM for smooth gameplay in 1080p. For best results 4GB or more is recommended.
 Possible decrease in fps are 0-5 which depend on pc spec, game settings and ingame location.
 On GTX 970/AMD R9 290X and higher there should be no fps drop.

author: Halk Hogan PL

name: All Quest Objectives On Map
summary: Shows on map all objectives for all player's active quests. Like tracking multiple quests in the same time. You can also change the currently tracked objective without going to the Quests menu. Optionally can cache the location of discovered merchants.
description:
Shows on map all objectives for all player's active quests. See the screenshots for details. See wmkMapMenu.ws file for some settings.

[color=#e69138]This mod doesn't show the location of quests the player doesn't have in journal.[/color]

[color=#00ff00]HOW TO INSTALL[/color]

Install it [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/videos/122]like any other Witcher 3 mod[/url]. Compatible with [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/games/mods/modmanager]Nexus Mod Manager[/url]. Use [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/484]Script Merger[/url] to detect and solve any conflicts with other installed mods.

See the downloads page for a fix for some generic merchants: sometimes is impossible to view their inventory and they don't have a pin on map, even if the player is close. Also a wrong model may be used during the dialog scene. Is a must have if you use the full version of All Quest Objectives on Map mod, otherwise it won't save the map pins for bugged merchants. See the README.txt file included in the archive for more details.

If the scripts fail to compile with errors about [i]AddSessionTag[/i], [i]LogWithName[/i], [i]LogWithValue[/i], [i]LogWithLabel[/i], [i]LogWithLabelAndValue[/i] etc... functions then you have an old telemetryKeyword.ws script file. Or you don't have the file. See the MISCELLANEOUS section from downloads page for a fix.

[color=#ffff00]LITE VERSION[/color]

Shows the location for all player's active (not finished, not failed) quests.

I don't recommend this version and instead you should use the standard one. Install it only if you also use a mod that changes panel_worldmap.redswf file, but currently there are very few. For more details see the COMPATIBILITY section below.

This version doesn't support custom icons for quest pins and instead uses an white exclamation mark as icon!

Adds only few new lines in mapMenu.ws file. Easy to merge if there's a conflict with another mod.

[color=#ffff00]STANDARD VERSION

[/color]Supports custom icons for quest pins. Click on a quest pin to change the currently tracked quest (or objective) directly from map. Compatible with pin filtering mechanism (the pins can be hidden, they appear in Quests category etc...).

Adds more changes to mapMenu.ws file than the lite version and also changes the panel_worldmap.redswf file.

[color=#ffff00]FULL VERSION[/color]

By default some merchants are visible on map only when the player is close to them. This version saves their location and adds the missing icons when the map is opened. Works only for merchants discovered after the mod is installed. You can click on a cached / saved pin to remove it. The list with discovered merchants is not lost when the game is restarted. By default the wandering merchants are ignored and their location is not saved, but this is configurable.

Note that the solution is not perfect and in some cases the mod may add a pin for a merchant that already has one. This usually happens for the merchants that are involved in quests, like Keira Metz or The Pellar, when they change their location. Cannot fix this, but happens rarely and you can remove the duplicated pins without problems.

Changes few more files than the lite and standard versions. Also changes panel_worldmap.redswf file.

[color=#00ff00]COMPATIBILITY[/color]

If you use other mods that provide compatibility patches with this one, verify that were also updated for the Next-Gen version. Same for translation mods. Don't mix mods created for the Classic game version with mods created for Next-Gen game version.

The standard and full versions [color=#ff0000]are incompatible[/color] with any other mod that changes the [color=#e69138]panel_worldmap.redswf[/color] file because merging is not possible. The only exception are the mods that provide a compatible version, like [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/938]Colored Map Markers[/url] by DJ_Kovrik. Read their description for more details.

[color=#00ff00]COLORED MAP MARKERS[/color]

The lite version is fully compatible with the main file from [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/938]Colored Map Markers[/url] by DJ_Kovrik.

For standard and full versions you must install the compatible file from Colored Map Markers, instead the main one. If you use mod.settings file to manually set mod priorities, make sure that Colored Map Markers has higher priority (as it is by default). If you use Script Merger and want to have a clean conflicts panel you may remove the blob0.content and metadata.store files from this mod (not recommended).

Don't install the compatible file from Colored Map Markers with the lite version! Use the main file, otherwise you won't be able to fast travel.

[color=#00ff00]PERFORMANCE[/color]

By default the coordinates for pins are always retrieved when the map is opened. For slower computers I've implemented a caching mechanism that is disabled by default. Actually I don't know if caching makes any difference. To enable it change the CACHE_PIN_POSITIONS setting to true. Do this only if the map loads slower for you, otherwise leave it disabled.

[color=#00ff00]OTHER MODS[/color]

You may also like [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/934]Action Log[/url] and [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/1221]More Quick Slots[/url] mods.

name: Fast Travel from Anywhere
summary: You can fast travel without using a signpost!
description:
[center][img]http://static-6.nexusmods.com/15/mods/952/images/324-0-1440265759.png[/img][/center]
[size=4]   No need to be near a signpost anymore![/size]
[color=#1e84cc] [/color][color=#6d9eeb][size=5][u]Description[/u][/size][/color]
 [size=3]To fast travel in vanilla you must be near a signpost. This mod allows you to fast travel to a signpost no matter where you are. [/size][size=3]It works if you are in the middle of the wilderness and don't feel like traveling to a signpost a billion lightyears away.[/size]


  [size=3]Thanks [b]Gopher [/b]for including my mod in his video (Fast Travel from Anywhere begins at [b]7:13[/b]):[/size][center][youtube]yexVHgIrFhY[/youtube][/center]
[color=#6d9eeb][u] [/u][/color][color=#6d9eeb][size=5][u]Installation Instructions[/u][/size][/color][color=orange]
[/color]  [size=4]Compatible with patch 1.31! [/size]
[size=3] [b]Install with NMM: [/b]just click [b]Download (NMM);
 Install Manually: [/b]extract [b]modFastTravelAnywhere [/b]to your [b]The Witcher 3 Wild Hunt[/b]\[b]Mods [/b]folder (create it if you do not have it).

[color=#980000] [/color][b][color=#ea9999]Notice: if you fast travel during quests chances are you will break the game. You shouldn't fast travel when the game forbids you to.[/color][/b]

[/size] [size=5][u][color=#6d9eeb]Uninstallation Instructions[/color][/u]
[/size] [b]NMM: [/b]delete it from NMM.
[b] Manual install: [/b]delete the folder [b]modFastTravelAnywhere.[/b]

name: Auto Apply Oils (Next Gen Compatible) NO LONGER REQUIRED
summary: A mod which automatically applies the most effective oil at the start of combat.
description:
[size=6][b]Auto Apply Oils[/b][/size]

Tired of opening your inventory to apply your oils at the start of combat? Then this mod is for you! When you draw your sword the mod scans the enemies around you, and applies the oil that will be effective most against them.

[size=5]SINCE PATCH 4.0.3 YOU DON'T NEED THIS MOD ANYMORE[/size]

[size=6]Installation Instructions[/size]

Witcher Mod Manager (recommended)
[list]
[*]Install the file in the normal way. After WMM version 0.9, WMM can handle all configuration required.
[/list]Vortex (do not use)[list]
[*]Vortex cannot handle all the configuration required, as far as I know. I highly recommend using Witcher Mod Manager instead. If you must use Vortex, make sure you go through the “manual” install instructions to configure the mod. 
[/list]
Manual
[list]
[*]Download the file manually and extract the contents of the folder to your Witcher 3 install folder. If you've installed the mod before you'll be asked to overwrite some files. It's safe to overwrite everything
[*]Copy the contents of the user.settings.part.txt file to the bottom of the user.settings file in the My Documents\The Witcher 3\. Do the same for the dx12user.settings.part.txt file.
[*]Edit the DX11 and DX12 file lists text files to include the text "modAutoApplyOils.xml;" at the bottom. These files are found in [The Witcher 3 install location]\bin\config\r4game\user_config_matrix\pc.
[/list]
[size=6]Configuration[/size]

Config options can be found in the mod menu. If the mod menu doesn't show up, make sure you changed the DX11 and DX12 files mentioned above properly (WMM shuold do this for you now).

[size=6]Bug reporting[/size]

If notice any situations where this mod is not running as intended, please use the bug report feature. I need the following information:

[list]
[*]How you installed the mod (manual or WMM)?
[*]What other mods are you using?
[*]Does the problem still happen when you have no other mods installed?
[*]Have you uninstalled the mod, verified your game files, and re-installed the mod?
[/list]
Thanks :)

[size=6]Credits[/size]

CDPR for the game and mod tools!
AnotherSymbiote for creating the Script Merger tool.
JupiterTheGod (I think) for the city detection code
Tenzian for maintaining this mod.
AzarGiN for adding the mod menu entry
HermesZum for the batch script idea

[list]

[*]RU - DarkEviLx17
[*]ZH - r901042004
[*]FR - SkweeZ & qtaim76
[*]PL - pizdadupa
[*]DE - Neko14789
[/list]
author: SheepDarklord

name: Always Full Exp (Next-Gen)
summary: get always full exp for quests, alternative variants available: Always Full Exp, Always Difficulty Exp, Always Mixed Exp, Always DLC Exp, Always Neovalen Exp
description:
Always Full Exp
===============
get always 100% exp for quests

Always Difficulty Exp
=====================
takes difficulty setting into account (100% exp on Easy/Normal, 90% exp on Hard, 80% exp on Deathmarch)

Always Mixed Exp
================
gives 100% exp for quests, only grey quests give 50%

Always DLC Exp
==============
uses a slightly altered version of the exp system for DLC quests for all quests - gives more exp for quests above your level and less exp for quests below your level (max: 150% exp, min: 50% exp)

Always Neovalen Exp
==================
takes difficulty setting into account, halves exp for grey quests, credits to Neovalen

You can only use one of the variants! All Mods are for Witcher 3 Next-Gen patch (4.00).

Older versions for Witcher 3 Patch 1.3x and Witcher 3 GOTY Patch 1.3x are still available under "old files".


Installation
============
extract contant of the archive into the folder ...\The Witcher 3 Wild Hunt\mods


Clarification on Experience Rewards
===============================
The mod "Always Full Exp" only alters the modifier for exp rewards, not individual exp rewards.

If you do not get any exp, then there is no exp-reward defined for the completed quest stage. Witcher 3 contains quests, where you only get exp on earlier quest stages and no exp reward on the final completion of the quest.

Also keep in mind, that exp rewards for minor quests are rather small. Discoverable short quests on the map often only give 10, 25 or 50 exp.

Here the decison tree for all factors that influence quest experience rewards:

1) Do you use "Always Full Exp"?
- [yes] Congratulation, you get 100% exp.
- [no] (proceed with next question)

2) Is your current quest from Heart of Stone or Blood and Wine DLC Expansion?
- [yes] For each level difference you get -/+16% exp (min 5% exp, max 150% exp)
- [no] (proceed with next question)

3) Have you completed the main story of Witcher 3?
- [yes] you get 50% exp
- [no] (proceed with next question)

4) Is your current quest a grey quest? (level difference of 6 or higher)
- [yes] you get 5% exp
- [no] (proceed with next question)

5) On what difficulty setting are you playing?
- [Easy] you get 100% exp
- [Normal] you get 100% exp
- [Hard] you get 90% exp
- [Deathmarch] you get 80% exp

Example: Level 25 Non-DLC-Quest that gives 500 exp
- If you do this quest with level 30 on Normal, you get 500 exp (100%)
- If you do this quest with level 30 on Deathmarch, you get 400 exp (80%)
- If you do this quest with level 31 on Normal, you get 25 exp (5%)
- If you do this quest with level 31 on Deathmarch, you get 25 exp (5%)
- If you do this quest with level 30 on Normal after completing the main story, you get 250 exp (50%)
- If you do this quest with level 30 on Deathmarch after completing the main story, you get 250 exp (50%)
- If you do this quest with level 31 on Normal after completing the main story, you get 250 exp (50%)
- If you do this quest with level 31 on Deathmarch after completing the main story, you get 250 exp (50%)
- If you do this quest with "Always Full Exp" active, you get 500 exp (100%)
author: Tommy1984

name: Indestructible Items - No More Weapon Degradation (Next-Gen Update Compatible)
summary: Removes weapon degradation so Geralt's weapons and armor will always be at 100% durability. Updated for the Next-Gen Update (v4.00)! Works with both Steam and GOG versions.
description:
***Updated for The Witcher 3 Next-Gen Update***

Removes weapon degradation so Geralt's weapons and armor will always be at 100% durability. If you already have a broken weapon/armor and you install this mod, you just have to use the weapon or get hit while wearing the armor and it will be set to 100% durability.

Works with both Steam and GOG versions.

Installation:
- Just unzip the download file and drop the modUnlimitedDurability folder into your Witcher 3/mods folder. If you don't have a mods folder, create one.
author: SunBroDave

name: Dodgy Basterds - Fight by the Book.
summary: Dodgy Basterds – Fight by the Book reimagines The Witcher 3 to reflect Geralt and the brutal world of Sapkowski’s books. Combat relies on real weapon collisions and armor interaction, while enemies fight to survive rather than die heroically.
description:
[size=6]Overview[/size]

[b]Dodgy Basterds – Fight by the Book[/b] reimagines [i]The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt[/i] to better reflect Geralt and the brutal world portrayed in Andrzej Sapkowski’s books.
Combat is rebuilt around real weapon collisions and the physical interaction between blades and armor.
Enemies are driven by a new AI that values survival above all — they want to live, not die heroically.
The result is a darker, more grounded gameplay experience where every fight is dangerous and unpredictable.
[size=6]
Books vs Game[/size]

One of the main goals of this project is to bring the gameplay experience closer to the tone and logic of the books.
[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/952/images/11971/11971-1773689478-1436915047.png[/img]

[size=6]Core Features[/size]

[b][size=4]Collision-Based Combat System [/size][/b]

The combat system is rebuilt around [b]real weapon collisions and geometry[/b]. Strikes are evaluated based on the physical interaction between weapon, armor and character models. Positioning, distance and blade alignment matter far more than raw statistics.
This creates combat where:
[list]
[*]a precise strike can end a fight quickly
[*]a poorly positioned attack can miss or glance off armor
[*]movement and spacing become critical
[/list]
[size=4][b]Layered Armor and Hit Zones[/b][/size]

Armor has been redesigned with [b]hit zones and layered protection[/b].
Different parts of the body provide different levels of protection depending on armor coverage and materials.
This means:
[list]
[*]some strikes will be absorbed by armor
[*]others may penetrate weak points
[*]correct targeting becomes important
[/list][list]
[/list][b][size=4]Reworked Loot System[/size][/b]

The loot system has been redesigned so equipment is primarily earned in combat. Weapons and armor can be taken from defeated enemies, but there is no need to collect flowers, nails, boards or other random junk from huts and barrels across the world.  No Grind. No Junk Loot. This project removes many mechanics that exist only to artificially extend gameplay.

The mod focuses on:
[list]
[*]meaningful combat encounters
[*]gameplay systems that interact with each other
[/list]There is [b]no grind, no junk scavenging, and no filler mechanics[/b].


[size=6]Installation
[/size]
Download archives: [b]modDodgyBasterds_FightByTheBook.zip[/b] and [b]Settings.zip

[/b]Extract [b]modDodgyBasterds_FightByTheBook.zip[/b] into your [b]The Witcher 3 installation folder (where witcher3.exe is located).[/b].

Copy files from settings.zip into the following folders:

﻿input.settings -> Documents\The Witcher 3

hidden.xml -> SteamLibrary\steamapps\common\The Witcher 3\bin\config\r4game\user_config_matrix\pc

[color=red][b]⚠ IMPORTANT:[/b] Make a backup copy of your existing files input.settings and hidden.xml before installing the mod.[/color]

[size=6]Visit Patreon to support the project or for additional materials:[/size]

[url=https://www.patreon.com/cw/DodgyBasterds]https://www.patreon.com/cw/DodgyBasterds[/url]

name: Survival Mode
summary: Adds hidden survival stats mainly hunger and sleep deprivation
description:
Note: The mod counts in game hours (meaning cut scenes and fast travel will drain stats accordingly to keep it more immersive)

You need to meditate or sleep in a bed 6 hrs for every 36 in game hours.

You need to eat 100 units of food every 12hrs (each food item gives about 30-40).

Debuffs:
- Sleep deprivation: You get blurry vision and at max there is a 5% chance you will be knocked out and looted.
- Hunger: At max hunger you slowly lose health all the down to 0%.

Todo:
- Add support for companions (MCM Enhanced)
- Add UI for stats (may ruin the immersion though)

name: Dynamic Ragdolls (Fixed Drowners and Ghouls)
summary:
Dynamic Ragdolls aims to recreate the feel of the ragdolls seen in the 2014 demo. It removes the stiff joints and awkward floating behavior of the current ragdoll system, replacing it with more natural and dynamic physics.

This early test version currently affects Drowners and Ghouls, with plans to expand to more creatures over time.
description:
Dynamic Ragdolls is a physics overhaul mod focused on restoring the heavier, more natural ragdoll behavior shown in the 2014 gameplay demo. The goal is to remove the current stiff and sometimes floating-looking ragdolls that can break immersion during combat.
In the vanilla game, enemies often fall in unnatural ways, with rigid joints and bodies that appear to hover or slide unnaturally after death. This mod adjusts ragdoll parameters to produce more weighty, reactive, and believable physics when enemies collapse or are knocked down.
The intention is to make combat feel more impactful by allowing bodies to react more dynamically to the environment and momentum, closer to how they behaved in early promotional footage.

[b]Current Features[/b]
[list]
[*]Reworked ragdoll physics for [b]Drowners[/b]
[*]Reworked ragdoll physics for [b]Ghouls[/b]
[*]Reduced stiff joint behavior
[*]Reduced floating/hovering ragdoll effects
[*]More dynamic and weighty body reactions
[/list][b]Planned[/b]
[list]
[*]Support for additional enemy types
[*]Further tuning and balancing of physics parameters
[*]Improvements based on community feedback
[/list][b]Important[/b]
This is an [b]early test version[/b]. Bugs, odd physics behavior, or unexpected interactions may occur. The mod will continue to evolve as more creatures are supported and the physics are refined.
Feedback is welcome and will help shape future updates.

name: Community Patch - Mods Menu Category (Classic n Next Gen) - French Translation
summary:
Traduction Française du mod Community Patch - Mods Menu Category (Classic n Next Gen)
Chaîne localisée préconfigurée permettant d’organiser les paramètres des mods en différentes catégories.
Conçue pour Menu Organizer.
description:
Traduction française du mod mod Community Patch - Mods Menu Category (Classic n Next Gen)

Le mod original est disponible pour la version next gen 4.04 et la version classic 1.32.
La traduction fonctionne pour les deux versions next gen 4.04 et la version classic 1.32.

[u][size=5][b]Description en français du mod :[/b][/size][/u]

Quel est le but ? Avec l’autorisation de [b]wghost81[/b], ce mod est conçu pour être utilisé comme [b]ressource commune de modding[/b], afin de créer une [b]meilleure structure de menus[/b] et de [b]résoudre le problème du nombre limité d’entrées[/b]. Il peut également être utilisé par les joueurs pour corriger les problèmes liés aux mods existants.
Dans le jeu vanilla, lorsque les paramètres d’un mod contiennent [b]plus de 10 entrées de menu[/b], certaines d’entre elles [b]ne s’affichent pas[/b], car la liste n’a pas assez d’espace et le menu ne propose pas de fonction de défilement apparente. De plus, si vous faites défiler et cliquez sur un menu, il peut ouvrir [b]la mauvaise option[/b].
Le problème des entrées de menu est que [b]vous ne pouvez pas simplement les ajouter en éditant des fichiers Bin[/b]. Il faut soit utiliser [b]Menu Organizer[/b], soit disposer de [b]chaînes localisées avec des IDs correspondants[/b] pour que le jeu les affiche correctement.
Ce mod est prévu comme [b]alternative à l’ancien Community Patch – Menu Strings de wghost81[/b]. Vous pouvez les combiner, mais gardez à l’esprit que vous [b]ne pouvez placer que 9 catégories par page[/b] si vous utilisez Menu Organizer.

[color=#ffff00][size=5]Les [u]NOUVELLES[/u] Catégories :[/size][/color]

Mods d’animation
Mods de caméra
Mods de contrôle
Mods d’immersion
Mods de menus
Mods utilitaires
Mods d’interface utilisateur
Mods visuels
Mods divers

[u][size=5][b]Installation :[/b][/size][/u]

Extraire le contenu de l'archive dans votre dossier The Witcher 3 Wild Hunt/Mods

[size=5][u][b]Remarques :

[/b][/u][/size][list]
[*]Je sais que le mod Menu Category fonctionne également avec Mod Menu Organiser et je prévois de le traduire prochainement
[*]Si vous aimez la traduction, j'apprécierais que vous cliquiez sur les boutons Track, Endorse et Vote en haut de cette page et pour le mod que j'ai inscrit comme requirements.
[*][url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/Veanes]N'hésitez pas à consulter les autres mods que j'ai créés.[/url]﻿﻿
[*]Si vous avez des questions ou des commentaires, n'hésitez pas à les poster sur la page Posts. Je ne promets pas que je répondrai à toutes les questions mais j'essaierai de le faire. Cependant, d'autres personnes pourront le faire pour vous.
[/list]

[size=3][b][i]Misc Changes:[/i][/b]
Every hit now applies a brief slow-motion effect (configurable).
[/size][size=3]Weapon Damage is no longer random, now it's static instead of ranging from 0.9x to 1.1x.
Crossbows have been buffed slightly, additionally they deal 0.5% of Enemy HP as Damage, Bolts gain 1 Damage per Level until Level 25.
Enemies Heal 0.2% +5 HP per second, they gain 2 HP/s Regen for Every Level until Level 25. Wraith Bosses only get % Heal, Elementals and Golems don't Heal, it doesn't apply during Fist Fights.
Mutations are available from the start, but they cost 5 Skill Points and 5 Mutagens, Multi-Path Mutagen Cost is 4 for Each Color. Mutations no longer have pre-requisites, you can research any mutation at any time.

[size=3][b][i]Removed Stats on Level Up, increased the Base Stats.[/i][/b]
Geralt's Speed is increased by 5%.[/size]
[size=3]Max Adrenaline 3 -> 5[/size]
[size=3]Vitality 3500 -> 5000[/size]
[size=3]Vitality Regen 1/s -> 5/s[/size]
[size=3]Weight Limit 60 -> 100[/size]
[size=3]Crit Change 5% -> 10%[/size]
[size=3]Crit Damage 25% -> 50%[/size]

[size=3][b][i]All actions now cost Stamina:[/i][/b][/size]
[size=3]Fast Attack - 6[/size]
[size=3]Strong Attack - 12[/size]
[size=3]Dodge - 5[/size]
[size=3]Roll - 8[/size]
[size=3][i][b]All of those have a 1 second delay before regen starts.[/b][/i][/size][/size]

[size=3][b][i]New Damage Formula: [/i][/b][/size]
[size=3]previously you could spam Fast Attacks against any Enemy and win easily and Armor had no effect on your DPS, now you have to penetrate through Armor first, which means Armor Piercing is now an actually useful stat.[/size]
[b][i][size=3]Previous Damage Formula: Weapon DMG * AP% * RES - Armor = Final Damage.[/size]
[size=3]New Damage Formula: ((Weapon DMG - Armor) * AP%) * RES = Final Damage.[/size][/i]

[size=3][i]Enemy Level Scaling:[/i][/size][/b]
[size=3]Enemies now gain Armor and Damage Resistance per Level. At Level 1 they have 30% of Base Armor and normal Resistances, Every Level increases Armor by 2.5% until level 25, at which they have 100% Armor, after that they gain 1.5% Armor for Every Level. Additionally Every Level increases Damage Resistances by 1.5% (Max 40% Extra Res, Max 75% Total Physical Res).[/size]

[size=3][b][i]Reworked Adrenaline:[/i][/b]
Attack Power per Adrenaline Point 10% -> 5%
At Max Adrenaline press the Sheer Force key-bind to activate it. Upon activation time slows down by 30%, the player retains their speed, Adrenaline drains by 0.5 points per second and cannot be generated.

[b][i]Reworked Status Effects:[/i][/b]
Bleed, Poison, Burn, Freeze are no longer Chance Based, instead you need to apply a Total of 100% Chance to proc said effect. So with 20% Bleed Chance you need to hit 5 times to apply Bleed.
Bleed Duration 10s -> 8s
Poison Duration 5s -> 8s
Bleed Damage 0.8% HP/s -> 1% HP/s
Poison Damage 1.6% HP/s -> 1.3% HP/s
Freeze now deals 5% of Enemy HP as Damage

[b][i]Reworked Basic Signs: [/i][/b]
All Signs Cost 50 Stamina.
All Signs have an independent 5 Second Cooldown.

[b][i]Aard: [/i][/b]Deals 1% of Enemy HP and 90 Damage, every Player Level increases the Damage by 15 Points scaling to Level 25 (max 450). Alt Aard deals only 1% of Enemy HP, % Damage scales with Sign Intensity - 0.01% HP for Each % of Sign Intensity.
[b][i]Yrden: [/i][/b]Upon entering the Yrden Circle Stuns the Enemy for 2.5 Seconds with an 8 Second Cooldown.
[b][i]Igni: [/i][/b]Burn Damage lowered from 2.7%/s to 0.3%/s. Increased Burn Duration to 6 Seconds. Base Damage at 40 and every Player Level increases Damage by 5 Points, Scaling to Level 25 (Max 160 + Sign Intensity Scaling).
[b][i]Quen: [/i][/b]No longer a one-hit Shield, now it's HP based. Every Hit breaks the Shield but if the Damage is Higher the rest goes to the Player, It starts at 400HP and every Player Level increases Shield HP by 10, Scaling to Level 30 (Max 700HP).
[b][i]Axii: [/i][/b]Disorients the Enemy and makes them Lower their Guard, Lowers Armor by 50 and Resistances by 5% for 7 Seconds.

[b][i]Red, Green, Blue Mutagens: 
[/i][/b][i][b]Red: [/b][/i]5/7/10% Attack Power -> 3/4/5% Attack Power.
[i][b]Green: [/b][/i]50/100/150 Vitality -> 2/3/4% Vitality.
[i][b]Blue: [/b][/i]5/7/10% Sign Intensity (Unchanged).[/size]

[size=3][b][i]Potion Changes: [/i][/b]
All Potions have 90/120/150 seconds Duration, except Blizzard 90/120/120 seconds, Cat 180 seconds, Swallow 60/90/120 seconds, and White Raffard 60/90/120 seconds.
All Changed Potions now have a 25/30/40 Toxicity Cost, except Cat 20/25/30.
All Potions now have 2/3/4 Charges.
[/size]
[size=3][b][i]Black Blood: [/i][/b]
Before: Returns 15/20/30% Damage Taken to Necrophages and Vampires.
After: Returns 25/35/50% Damage Taken to Necrophages and Vampires.
[b][i]Blizzard: [/i][/b]
Before: Slows Time by 20/30/40% when in Combat.
After: Slows Time by 10/15/20% when in Combat.
[b][i]Cat: [/i][/b]
Before: Increases Crit Chance by 5/7/10%.
After: Increases Crit Chance by 5/10/15%.
[b][i]Full Moon: [/i][/b]
Before: Increases Vitality by 300/650/1000.
After: Increases Vitality by 10/15/20%.
[b][i]Maribor Forest: [/i][/b]
Before: Increases Adrenaline Gain by 0.15, Level 2 longer duration, Level 3 grants 1 Adrenaline Point upon drinking.
After: Increases Adrenaline Gain by 0.15/0.20/0.25, Level 2 grants 0.5 Adrenaline Points, Level 3 grants 1 Adrenaline Point upon drinking.
[b][i]Petri's Filter: [/i][/b]
Before: Increases Sign Intensity by 15/20/25%.
After: Increases Sign Intensity 25/35/45%, Level 3 grants increased Stamina Regen by 4/s.
[b][i]Swallow: [/i][/b]
Before: Heals Vitality by 40/65/80 per second.
After: Heals Vitality by 1/1.5/2% per second.
[b][i]Tawny Owl: [/i][/b]
Before: Increases Stamina Regen by 5/8/10 per second.
After: Increases Stamina Regen by 4/5/6 per second, Level 3 grants 10% Attack Power and 20% Sign Intensity.
[b][i]Thunderbolt: [/i][/b]
Before: Increases Attack Power by 30/30/35%.
After: Increases Attack Power by 20/25/30% and Armor Piercing by 20/30/40.
[b][i]White Raffard Decoction: [/i][/b]
Before: Instantly Heals 35/60/100% Vitality, Level 3 grants Invulnerability for 3 seconds.
After: Grants 5/10/15% all Damage Resistance, Heals 0.75% Vitality per pecond.

[b][i]Oil Changes: [/i][/b]
All Oils now have 30/50/70 uses instead of 20/40/60

[b][i]Beast Oil:[/i][/b]
+10/15/20% Attack power versus Beasts, 10/15/20% Chance to Apply Bleed.
[b][i]Cursed Oil:[/i][/b]
+10/15/20% Attack power versus Cursed Ones, 10/15/20% Chance to Apply Burn.
[b][i]Draconid Oil:
[/i][/b]+10/15/20% Attack power versus Draconids, 10/15/20% Chance to Stagger.
[b][i]Elementa Oil:[/i][/b]
+10/15/20% Attack power versus Elementals, 10/15/20% Chance to Stun +20/30/40 Armor Piercing.
[b][i]Hanged Man Venom (Human Oil):[/i][/b]
+10/15/20% Attack power versus Humans, 10/15/20% Chance to Apply Poison.
[b][i]Hybrid Oil:[/i][/b]
+10/15/20% Attack power versus Hybrids, 5/15/20% Chance to Stagger.
[b][i]Necrophage Oil:[/i][/b]
+10/15/20% Attack power versus Necrophages, 10/15/20% Chance to Apply Bleed.
[b][i]Relict Oil:[/i][/b]
+10/15/20% Attack power versus Relicts, 10/15/20% Chance to Apply Burn.
[b][i]Specter Oil:[/i][/b]
+10/15/20% Attack power versus Specters, + 30/40/50 Silver Damage, +10/15/20% Chance for 20% Slowdown for 7 Seconds.
[b][i]Vampire Oil:[/i][/b]
+10/15/20% Attack power versus Vampires, Each Hit Heals 50/100/150 HP.
[b][i]Ogroid Oil:[/i][/b]
+10/15/20% Attack power versus Ogroids, +10/15/20% Chance to Freeze for 7 Seconds.
[b][i]Insectoid Oil:[/i][/b]
+15/20/25% Attack power versus Insectoids.

[b][i]Skill Changes: [/i][/b]
[b][i]Combat Skills: 
Muscle Memory:[/i][/b]
[size=3]Before: Fast Attack Damage Increased by 10/20/30%.
After: Fast Attack Damage Increased by 5/10/15%.[/size]
[b][i]Precise Blows:[/i][/b]
[size=3]Before: Increases Chance of landing a Fast Attack Critical Hit by 4/8/12% and Fast Attack Critical Hit Damage by 25/50/75%.
After: [/size][size=3]Increases Chance of landing a Fast Attack Critical Hit by 5/10/15% and Fast Attack Critical Hit Damage by 15/30/45%.[/size]
[b][i]Crippling Strikes:[/i][/b]
[size=3]Before: Fast Attacks now also Apply a Bleeding Effect. Affected Enemies lose 50/100/150 Vitality or Essence points per second. Duration: 5 Seconds.
After: [/size][size=3]Fast Attacks now also Apply a Bleeding Effect. Affected Enemies lose 50/100/150 and 1/2/3% Vitality or Essence points per second. Duration: 3 Seconds.[/size]
[b][i]Strong Attack:[/i][/b]
[size=3]Before: Allows you to perform Strong Attacks which Ignore Enemy Armor.
After: Allows you to perform Strong Attacks which Ignore 150 Armor Points of the Enemy.[/size]
[b][i]Strength Training:[size=3] [/size][/i][/b]
[size=3]Before: Strong Attack Damage Increased by 10/20/30%.
After: Strong Attack Damage Increased by 5/10/15%.[/size]
[b][i]Crushing Blows:[/i][/b]
[size=3]Before: Increases Strong Attack Critical Hit Chance by 4/8/12% and Strong Attack Critical Hit Damage by 25/50/75%.
After: [/size][size=3]Increases Strong Attack Critical Hit Chance by 5/10/15% and Strong Attack Critical Hit Damage by 15/30/45%.
[b][i]Sunder Armor: [/i][/b]
Before: Reduces enemy damage resistance by 10/20/30%. (Misleading Description)
After: Strong Attacks Ignore 4/8/12% of Enemy Resistance.
[/size][b][i]Counterattack: 
[/i][/b][size=3]Before: [/size][size=3]After a Successful Counterattack, the Next Attack deals +30/60/90% Damage, Level 2 has a Chance for Knockdown based on Adrenaline Points, Level 3 is guaranteed to crit.[/size]
[size=3]After: After a Successful Counterattack, the Next Attack deals +25/50/75% Damage, Level 2 has a Chance for Knockdown based on Adrenaline Points, Level 3 has +10% crit chance.[/size]
[b][i]Deadly Precision:[/i][/b]
[size=3]Before: Each Adrenaline Point adds 1/2% to the chance of killing an opponent instantly. 15 second cooldown.[/size]
[size=3]After: After a Successful Counterattack You Ignore 10/20% of Enemy Resistances For 5 Seconds.[/size]
[b][i]Resolve:[/i][/b]
[size=3]Before: Adrenaline Point loss upon taking damage is lowered by 33/67/100%.
After: [/size][size=3]Adrenaline Point loss upon taking damage is lowered by 25/50/75%.

[/size][b][i]Sign Skills: 
Changed Positions: [/i][/b]
Row 1: Alt Skills [i](Passive once learned, can be slotted for Mutagen Bonuses)[/i]
Row 2: Sign Intensity [i](Passive once learned, can be slotted for Mutagen Bonuses)[/i]
Row 3: Former First Skills
Row 4: Unchanged
Small exception for [i]Melt Armor[/i] and [i]Pyromaniac[/i]. [i]Melt Armor[/i] is Row 4 and [i]Pyromaniac[/i] is Row 3.
[/size][size=3][b][i]Passive:[/i][/b]
Before: Stamina Regeneration in Combat: +0.5/1/1.5/s
After: Stamina Regeneration in Combat: +0.25/0.5/0.75/s
[b][i]All Sign Intensity Skills:[/i][/b]
Before: Increases Sign Intensity by 10/20/30%.
After: Increases Sign Intensity by 15/30/45%.
[b][i]Far-Reaching Aard (Now Called Frostbite): [/i][/b]
Before: Increases Aard's Range by 1-3 Yards.
After: Aard now has 10/20/30% Chance to Freeze.
[b][i]Melt Armor: [/i][/b]

After: Damage dealt by Igni also permanently Weakens Enemy Armor by 20/40/60%.
[b][i]Firestream (Alt Igni):[/i][/b]

Lowered Stamina Cost per Second from 50 to 30.
[b][i]Pyromaniac:[/i][/b]
Before: Increases the Chance to Apply Burning by 33/67/100%.
After: Increases the Chance to Apply Burning by 20/40/60%.

Increased Sign Intensity Scaling.

Before: Adds 5/10/15% protection against attacks from the monster type the oil targets.
After: Now Only 2 Levels. Adds 5/10% protection against attacks from the monster type the oil targets.

Before: Adds 33/66/infinite% Oil Charges. Level 2 Can Apply 2 Oils, Level 3 Can Apply 3 Oils.
After: Now Only 2 Levels. Adds 30/60% Oils Charges. Level 2 Can Apply 2 Oils.

After: Each Point of Adrenaline Increases Critical Hit Damage Against The Targeted Enemy Type by 10/20/30%.

[size=3]After: Each piece of Light Armor Increases Critical Hit Damage by 15% and Critical Hit Chance by 5%.[/size]
[b][i]Griffin School Techniques:[/i][/b]

[b][i]Bear School Techniques:[/i][/b]
[size=3]Before: Each piece of Heavy Armor Increases Maximum Vitality by 5% and Strong Attack Damage by 5%.
After: [/size][size=3]Each piece of Heavy Armor Increases [size=3]Maximum Vitality by 10%[/size], Strong Attack Damage by 15% and [size=3]Critical Hit Chance by 2.5%.[/size][/size]

[size=3]Before: Instead of its previous effect, Battle Trance now increases Critical Hit Chance by 8% per Adrenaline Point.

[size=3]Buffed Crossbow Damage to 75%.
[/size][b][i]Strong Back:[/i][/b]
[size=3]Buffed Inventory Weight to 100.

name: Random Encounters Reworked Russian Translation
summary:
Russian Translation for Random Encounters Reworked. This is the most up-to-date Russian localization for Random Encounters Reworked by Aeltoth.
The previous translation hasn't been updated for several versions. I have taken over to bring the localization up to standard, ensuring it matches the current build of the mod.
description:
Актуальный русификатор [b]для версии 3.41 с GitHub[/b]. Это наиболее свежий и полный перевод мода [b][url=http://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/5018]Random Encounters Reworked[/url][/b] от [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/profile/Aeltoth?gameId=952]Aeltoth[/url]. Предыдущий автор(ы) перестал(и) поддерживать локализацию, из-за чего накопилось много непереведенных строк . Я обновил его, чтобы он полностью соответствовал текущей версии.

Хотя я и старался максимально сохранить оригинальные названия и терминологию официальной русской версии игры, текста много, поэтому если кто заметит ошибки или неточности — буду рад фидбеку. Пишите в комментарии, и я оперативно всё поправлю.

[list]
[*][b]Полный перевод:[/b] Переведены все диалоги, заказы и меню настроек, а также элементы добавленные в последних патчах.
[*][b]Работа над ошибками:[/b] Исправлены неточности и ошибки старых версий локализации.
[*][b]Стиль:[/b] Текст адаптирован под общий стиль "Ведьмака".
[/list]Инструкция по установке:

[list=1]
[*]Скачайте архив.
[*]Переместите папку mods из архива в корневую папку игры (The Witcher 3\Mods).
[*][b]Обязательно[/b] подтвердите замену файлов.
[*]Играйте!
[/list][b]Обратная связь:[/b] Если заметите опечатку, ошибку в контексте или нелогичную фразу — пишите на странице мода или мне в личку. Давайте сделаем локализацию качественной.[line][b]
Обо мне:

[/b]Я аудиопродюсер (саунд-дизайн, озвучка, имплементация). За плечами опыт работы над крупными игровыми релизами и сложными продакшн-проектами.
Всегда открыт к сотрудничеству, особенно если это касается звука в геймдеве и саунд-дизайна.

I am Audio Producer (Sound Design, VO, Implementation). I have experience working on major game releases and handling complex audio productions. Always open to professional collaboration, especially regarding game audio and sound design.

[list]
[*]Portfolio: [url=https://chroniclesprod.wixsite.com/chronicles]Chronicles[/url]
[*]LinkedIn: [url=https://www.linkedin.com/in/michael-sound/]Michael[/url]
[/list]
author: chronomic

name: Tapetum Lucidum A modular catsight
summary: Eyes of course. Compatible with modular eyes. Light reflections purely dependant on enviroment conditions. Capable of burning like candles, yet not emitting any light by themselves. Attempt  of how could eyes of a monster slayer behave, with detailed new textures: thin, rounded and extra wide pupils.
description:
Eyes, eyes,... So many to choose from. Well, this is another attempt. 

[size=1][i][youtube]g029NOZNIC4[/youtube]
Showcase of how the mod Tapetum Lucidum: A modular catsight, version 1.2. works.
With the Quen [i]light-casting[/i] mod (latest version of [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/8449]Eternal Hunt[/url], or [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/8710]E3 Quen (NEXT GEN)[/url][/i][/size]

[color=#f1c232][size=3][b]What to expect: 
[/b][/size][/color]
Modular, light-reflecting eyes that depend purely on environmental conditions.
They are capable of burning like candles, yet they do not emit any light themselves.
The behaviour is very variable from subtle, moderate to strong, depending on the surrounding light and Geralts' head movements.
An attempt to show how the eyes of a monster slayer could behave, with new, detailed textures.
Main version with thin, rounded and  extra-wide pupils, plus alternative versions for regular eyes or swapped alt-regular modular eyes.

A picture speaks a thousand words — check out the videos showing how they behave in changing light and environments!

[color=#f1c232][size=3][b]Goals: 
[/b][/size][/color]
1. Firstly, the light-reflecting behaviour of the eyes, which is common to many mammals and is caused by the[i] tapetum lucidum[/i] (or 'bright tapestry'), was the focus of attention. This tissue multiplies the strength of incoming light, resulting in better night vision and a haunting, illuminated gaze.
2. It was crucial to make the pupils glow in the right dim conditions, but not the irises.
3. Conversely, the pupils should be as dark as possible in direct light, with the irises appearing shiny.
In short, the pupil and iris exchange roles in terms of brightness and light absorption during the day and night.   

[b][color=#f1c232][size=3]How: 
[/size][/color][/b]
Of course it's a trick. Its all down to the normal maps. No glowing effects were used. I spent more than half a year learning, balancing and going back and forth to achieve this result (well, of course, it was mainly because I didn't have enough time, as everyone here). The key was to make the normal map behave as an impenetrable barrier when needed. The pupil reflection is forced to react to side light, only a bit to upper light, and not at all to direct light. I believe it's a close-to-right result for now. 

[b][color=#f1c232]Versions[/color]
[color=#f4f4f4][size=2]
ALL VERSIONS HAVE THE SAME LIGHT REFLECTING BEHAVIOR[/size][/color][/b]

[b]Full: [/b]Modular (thin for regular, wide for alt and extra wide for cat potion and high toxicity effect) 
[b]Full reversed:[/b] (wide for regular, thin for alt and extra wide for cat potion and high toxicity effect)
[b]Only regular:[/b] thin pupils
[b]Only regular:[/b] rounded (wide) pupils

OTHER WITCHERS: Please note that for now it's about the main character, Geralt. You can give this mod priority over other mods that cover other witchers without any problem. Alternatively, you can leave it to the Modular Eyes add-on, which covers them too.  
[color=#f1c232]
[b][size=3]Strongly recommended:
[/size][/b][/color] ﻿
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/7240]No Artificial Player Light - Next Gen[/url] Not just because of this mod but it is a visual game changer a must have for every immersive playtrough. Use also with cutscenes lights turn off, you‘ll see. 
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/3890]Modular Eyes[/url] For the full version of my mod. Nothing complicated, just go and download it. It is the best.  
And from a perspective of visuals:
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/7434]Darker Nights - Next Gen[/url] To see the light one needs to get darker. 
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/8781]Quest Cutscene Lighting Edits by OFG[/url] If you‘re still, or again, in the playtrough
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/10311]Neutral Face Mimic[/url] Another must have for no silly face Geralt.

[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/8710]E3 Quen (NEXT GEN)[/url] For Quen that emits real light and casts shadows (but I don‘t like the E3 color), OR:
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/8449]Eternal Hunt[/url] the latest version of this overhaul also includes Quen that emits real light and casts shadows, on top of that in vanilla orange color of this sign.
Both of this mods work wonderull with Tapetum Lucidum, check video on top of this page.  

[color=#f1c232][size=3][b]Installation

[/b][/size][/color]Drag and drop to mods folder, or use W3Manager. Put higher priority over other mods containing Geralt eye textures.

[color=#f1c232][size=3][b]Deepest respect to: 
[/b][/size][/color]

author: RPCockien

name: Witcher School Set Rework and Balance
summary: Complete rework of all Witcher Set through stats and bonus, to make them more unique, balanced yet powerful.
description:
[color=#6aa84f][size=5][b]What's the purpose?[/b][/size][/color]
This [b][color=#ff0000]Next-Gen [u]4.04[/u][/color][/b] and [color=#00ff00][b]Brothers In Arms compatible[/b][/color] mod rebalance all the Witcher School Set, in attempt to make them more unique and distinguee each other. They are still best and valuable to craft, but now no longer we have Bear > Cat.
Keep in mind the main goal is making the set more balanced yet powerful each other. Your feedback are important and may help for my big project.

[u]General Improvement[/u]
- All Set Bonus is reworked. The First Set Bonus depend on the equipped set number, while Second Set Bonus have two: passive effect (no condition) and active effect (with condition or increase effect).
- Incorporate progressive [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/7182]Minor Set Bonus[/url] by [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/users/98471183]FreakVIp[/url]. The First Set Bonus is now active before you reach Grandmaster level.
- On Normal New Game, you start with maximum 3 equipped count for the first bonus, then this equipped count increase when upgrade to the next tier until Mastercrafted level.
- On New Game Plus, you start with maximum 3 equipped count for the first bonus, then it goes straight to 6 equipped count when crafted Enhanced level.
- You unlock the Second Set Bonus when crafted Grandmaster level. Or Mastercrafted level if you are on New Game Plus.
- Importing normal armour when starting New Game Plus no longer have Set Bonus. Given you more reason to scavenge their "Legendary" version.
- Each tier has the same base level requirement, in the order progression of:
Level = 15 -> 25 -> 30 -> 35 -> 40 (May vary on NG+)
- Their total Damage/Armour are slightly different though.
- Each tier has the same number of stats list but with distinct progression (armour gain less and swords gain more). In the order progression of:
Armour = 5 -> 6 -> 6 -> 6 -> 6
Sword = 3 -> 4 -> 5 -> 6 -> 6
- Steel and Silver have the exact same amount of stats and level requirement.
- Total glyphs progression: 1 -> 3 -> 5 -> 7 -> 7
- Total runes progression: 0 -> 1 -> 2 -> 3 -> 3
- Weight and price adjusted.
- Aerondight now apply to any witcher set, with global stats and being indestructible.


[color=#00ffff][size=5][b][u]Cat School Set[/u][/b][/size][/color]
In vanilla, is meant for fast killing, mostly against humans, with the trade-off of poor resistance. Good set just because it makes fast attack very powerful.
The mod readjusts the resistance balance, with bleeding resistance, so it is as effective against both monster and humans.
The set bonus is nerfed but provide a new effect that give high critical damage. With the skill" Cat School Technique", this should be the deadlier set of all.

[i]Vanilla: Light Armour[/i] ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿[u]M[/u][u]od: Light Armour[/u]
Resistance to slashing = 35%/50% ﻿ ﻿ Resistance to slashing = 30%/45%
Resistance to piercing = 27%/40% ﻿ ﻿  Resistance to piercing = 30%/45%
Resistance to monster = 28%/42% ﻿ ﻿ Resistance to monster = 30%/45%
Resistance to elements = 57%/64% ﻿ ﻿Resistance to elements = 55%
Attack power = 55%/85% ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ Resistance to bleeding = 75%
﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿   Attack power = 50%/60%

[i]Vanilla: Swords[/i] ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿[u]M[/u][u]od: Swords[/u]
Critical Hit Chance = 10%/15% ﻿ ﻿      Critical Damage Bonus = 75%
Bleeding Effect = 15 ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿Bleeding Effect = 10%/15%
Aard Intensity = 15%/<20% ﻿ ﻿ ﻿    Axii Intensity = 25%
Dismember Chance = 10% ﻿ ﻿ ﻿     Dismember Chance = 10%
Exp = <20% ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿      Attack Power = 10%/15%
﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿  Exp = 20%

[i]Vanilla OLD Bonus[/i]
- Strong Attack increase Fast Attack damage for 5 seconds by 10% for each piece of the set. (Up to 60% Fast Attack damage)
- Rear attacks deal 50% more damage, and also stun the opponent at the cost of 1 Adrenaline Point(s).
[u]Mod NEW Bonus[/u]
- Strong Attack increase Fast Attack damage for 5 seconds by 5% for each piece of the set. (Up to 30% Fast Attack damage)
- Rear attacks deal 40% more damage. Successfully cast Axii increases critical damage bonus by 25% for 30 seconds.

Other change:
- The buff of the "Cat First Set Bonus" will display the current bonus value.
- The buff of the "Cat Second Set Bonus" uses the same icon as the vanilla "Gryphon Second Set Bonus" buff.
- Teigr (Gaetan's Sword) is part of the Cat set and have vanilla Grandmaster stats.
- Partially include [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/8078]Crossbow Rework[/url] for the Feline Crossbow. Greatly buffed but heavier, require mastercraft smith and additional Silver Ore.
- If using [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/10076]More Reasonable Damage[/url]. The second set bonus will be nerfed to "Critical Damage Bonus by 15%".


[color=#8e7cc3][size=5][b][u]Griffin School Set[/u][/b][/size][/color]
In vanilla, it make Geralt a wizard where Sign are overpowerful, especially Yrden. Almost like we don't need a sword.
The mod nerf the Yrden bonus, remove the broken "Two Sign Cast" but increases instead the Total Stamina (not regeneration) and Alternative Sign abilities.
With "Gryphon School Technique", I keep the combat style where magic is strong, in exchange to less resistance and improve the swords with “Stun Effect”.

[i]Vanilla: Medium Armour[/i] ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ [u]M[/u][u]od: Medium Armour[/u]
Resistance to slashing = 45%/60% ﻿ ﻿Resistance to slashing = 40%/55%
Resistance to piercing = 35%/50% ﻿ ﻿Resistance to piercing = 40%/55%
Resistance to bludgeoning = 22%/27% ﻿ Resistance to bludgeoning = 30%

Resistance to elements = 45%/65% ﻿      Resistance to elements = 35%
Spell power = 55%/85% ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿  Spell power = 50%/60%

[i]Vanilla: Swords[/i] ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿[u]M[/u][u]od: Swords[/u]

Critical Damage Bonus = 25%/50% ﻿       Yrden Intensity = 25%

Exp = <20% ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿      Stamina = 25%
﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿  Exp = 20%

[i]Vanilla OLD Bonus[/i]
- After using Stamina to cast a Sign in its standard mode, the next Sign cast within 3 seconds will be cast in standard mode without using Stamina.
- The size of Yrden traps is increased by 40%. While you are within an Yrden trap, Stamina regeneration is increased by 5/s and Sign Intensity by 100%, and damage received is reduced by 20%.
[u]Mod NEW Bonus[/u]
- While you are within an Yrden trap, Spell Intensity is increased by 10% for each piece of the set. (Up to 60% Yrden trap size)
- Increase the abilities effect of Alternative Sign. The size of Yrden scales with Yrden Sign Intensity up to maximum of 50%.

Other change:
- The buff of the "Gryphon First Set Bonus" will display the current bonus value.
- Here the detailed effects of the Second Set Bonus: Aard Sweep knockdown chance. Puppet duration. Firestream burning damage. Active Shield defense. Magic Trap damage. 
- For information, to reach +50% Yrden size, you need 300% Yrden Intensity.
- Include a UI tweak where the Sign icon on the stamina bar light up when there is enough stamina to cast. Made by [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/users/122150253]Tinnaib [/url]from [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/6298]Unofficial HUD and UI Additions for Ghost Mode[/url].
- Include the "Stamina Regen Decoupled from Total Stamina" fix by [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/users/60589726]AleksXAA[/url], from [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/7250]Yellow Mutagens[/url]. Meaning the increasing Total Stamina doesn’t increase Stamina Regeneration. 
- If you're using [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/10076]More Reasonable Damage[/url], you need to install optional file "Compatible Patch - Yellow Mutagens" from this More Reasonable Damage mod page.


[b][color=#ff7700][size=5][u]Bear School Set[/u][/size][/color][/b]
In vanilla, it's the most defensive, make Quen overpowerful and high Adrenaline Bonus which... it's not like "Bear School Technique" skill.
Quen now deals less damage but without any abilities, add "Berserk" effect where Geralt is stronger when he is about to die. And add defence against heavy weapon.
Replace the Adrenaline Bonus with Vitality Bonus and the sword are almost like heavy weapon. Now it feels like being a "tank" as "Bear School Technique" should be.

Resistance to slashing = 55%/70% ﻿ ﻿   Resistance to slashing = 50%/65%

Resistance to bludgeoning = 30%/40% ﻿    Resistance to bludgeoning = 50%
Resistance to monster = 50%/65% ﻿ ﻿   Resistance to monster = 50%/65%
Resistance to elements = 31%/50% ﻿ ﻿  Resistance to elements = 25%
Adrenaline Bonus = 55%/85% ﻿ ﻿ ﻿    Vitality = 2000/2500

Critical Hit Chance = 5% ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿    Armour Reduction = 250

[i]Vanilla OLD Bonus[/i]
- When a Quen shield shatters, there is a chance a new one will be cast at no Stamina cost. The bonus increases by 5% for each piece of the set. (Up to 30% chance)
- Damage dealt by Abilities involving the Quen Sign is increased by 200%.
[u]Mod NEW Bonus[/u]
- Quen deals additional damage by 15% for each piece of the set. (Up to 90% Quen damage)
- Can parry strong and heavy weapon attacks from the opponent at the cost of 70 Stamina. Strong Attack damage is increased by 0.5% proportionally to lost Vitality.

Other change:
- In case someone ask. The vanilla "Damage dealt by Abilities involving the Quen Sign" is disabled. The bonus set damage is added to the skill damage.
- Partially include [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/8078]Crossbow Rework[/url] for the Ursine Crossbow. Greatly buffed but heavier, require mastercraft smith and additional Meteorite Ore.


[color=#ff0000][size=5][b][u]Wolf School Set[/u][/b][/size][/color]
In vanilla, it's the “Mario” of the game. Average stats sound good on paper, but it's not good enough for all situations.
The mod greatly buffs to feel like playing Mario from Super Smash Bros. More stats diversify that make the perfect average set and making useful in all situations.
The reworked bonus was made in complement to [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/10076]More Reasonable Damage[/url] by [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/users/114315413]Teamy88[/url] to bring the infamous Diablo Barbarian build.

[i]Vanilla OLD Bonus[/i]
- Each instance of Bleeding applied to enemies increases sword damage by 1% for each piece of the set. (Up to 6% sword damage... for each Bleeding instance)
- Each Adrenaline Point increases the possible number of Bleeding effects that can be applied to a single enemy.
[u]Mod NEW Bonus[/u]
- When foe is bleeding, sword deals 5% more damage for each piece of the set. (Up to 30% sword damage)
- Increase Whirl duration by 1 second and Rend damage by 10%. When Adrenaline Points are at their maximum, parry an attack will always trigger a counterattack.

Other change:
- The First Set Bonus applies on Fast Attack, Strong Attack, Whirl and Rend.
- Tor'haerne (Berengar's Sword) is part of the Wolf set, witcher quality and have vanilla Grandmaster stats.
- Include [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/11260]Brothers in Arms[/url] fix of Wolf wrong crafting level and floating scabbard (for compatibility merge).


[color=#00ff00][size=5][b][u]Viper School Set[/u][/b][/size][/color]
In vanilla, it's the worst set without bonus... poorly made against poison monster while in lore-aspect it's meant against Wild Hunt (!)
The mod add an original set bonus that focus on Poison, Oil and Damage Received. Deadlier against humans and monsters but mainly against Wild Hunt.
It's now Heavy armour, provide high Adrenaline Bonus and resistance. Greatly better when being Light Armour with Glyphwork.

[i]Vanilla: Medium Armour[/i] ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ [u]M[/u][u]od: Heavy Armour[/u]
Resistance to slashing = 50% ﻿ ﻿ ﻿Resistance to slashing = 55%/70%
Resistance to piercing = 50% ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ Resistance to piercing = 40%/55%
Resistance to monster = 44% ﻿ ﻿ ﻿Resistance to bludgeoning = 40%
Resistance to elements = 60% ﻿ ﻿       Resistance to monster = 55%/70%
Resistance to poison = 100% ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ Resistance to elements = 75%
﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿  Adrenaline Bonus = 50%/60%

[i]Vanilla: Swords[/i] ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿[u]M[/u][u]od: Swords[/u]
Critical Hit Chance = 10% ﻿ ﻿ ﻿       Armour Reduction = 200
Critical Damage Bonus = 75% ﻿ ﻿ ﻿Critical Hit Chance = 10%
Poison effect = 15% ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿Critical Damage Bonus = 25%
Aard Intensity = 25% ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿Poison Effect = 20%/25%
Exp = <20% ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿       Adrenaline Bonus = 10%/15%
﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿  Exp = 20%

[i]Vanilla OLD Bonus[/i]
- NOTHING!
- AND NOTHING!!
[u]Mod NEW Bonus[/u]
- If foe is poisoned or a member of the Wild Hunt, increases critical chance by 2.5% for each piece of the set. (Up to 15% critical hit chance)
- Damage dealt by enemies is reflected back on the attacker by 10%. Oil applied to blades now causes slowdown to the target on each hit.

Other changes:
- The First Set Bonus applies on Fast Attack, Strong Attack, Whirl and Rend.
- They now have better stats in NG+ than the normal game.
- There is now three type of schematic: Basic, Mastercrafted and Grandmaster.
- The vanilla "Viper Set" become Basic, are level 5 and can be crafted by "Journeyman" smith. Their schematic can be found in White Orchard during the Viper Scavenger quest.
- The Mastercrafted schematic is sellable by Countess Mignole, included Swords. She still sell the Basic Viper at very cheap price.
- The Grandmaster schematic can be loot in the Auction House, in the same chest as the vanilla "Venomous Steel Viper" (which is removed from there). Or... sell by Larfargue in Toussaint.
- The crafted "Venomous Silver Sword" is replace by crafted "Sarrim" relic sword.
- With these, that also means Mastercrafted require the Basic Viper to craft, and the Grandmaster require the Mastercrafted tier to craft. Also they're no longer named "Venomous Viper".
- If using [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/11545]Immersive Gears Rewards[/url], you can bought a map in White Orchard to start the scavenger viper; Saving Letho will reward you the Mastercrafted set (armour and swords), which in that case, Mignolle will sell the Grandmaster schematic; Lastly, beating Hearts of Stones will always reward the Grandmaster.
- Grandmaster Viper will have belt and flask If using [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/5911]Professional Witcher Belt and Items[/url], while Mastercrafted Viper won't. That way you'll tell if it's the Mastercrafted or the Grandmaster armour.
- And Mastercrafted Viper Swords will have the appearance of "Venomous Viper", while the Grandmaster Viper Swords will have the appearance of Belhaven/Gesheft (aka Serpent Steel/Silver Sword 3).
- Include [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/11260]Brothers in Arms[/url] fix of the Basic Viper Swords appearance (for compatibility merge).


[color=#bf9000][size=5][b][u]Manticore School Set[/u][/b][/size][/color]
In vanilla, I don't get it... Was it meant for using more potions and decoction, or for using more bombs efficiently? 
The mod chooses the bomb efficiently way. Stronger bombs from the set bonus, bring burning effect for swords and burning resistance for armour.
It's a Light Armour by default that gives high critical stats and Igni boost. This is meant so fire is more destructive than before.

[i]Vanilla OLD Bonus[/i]
- Critical hit chance and critical hit damage also apply to bombs. Bombs are thrown without any delay.
- The maximum number of charges for each alchemy item is increased by 1.
[u]Mod NEW Bonus [/u]
- Bombs deal 50 fire base damages for each piece of the set. The damage scales to the enemy remaining health. (Up to 300 fire damages)
- Critical hit chance and critical hit damage also apply to bombs. For each foe and instance of Burning applied, Igni deals additional damage.

Other changes:
- The "Bombs are thrown without any delay" bonus is integrated to the Perk skill "Advanced Pyrotechnics".
- The common Alchemy skills "bomb damage +5%/10%/15%" also apply to the bonus set.
- Add an "already crafted" and Mastercrafted version, only available in Lafargue shop.
- Additionally, add the title "Grandmaster" to the vanilla set and require the Mastercrafted set in the recipe.
- And the Mastercrafted set will have the normal appearance, while the Grandmaster will have the New Game Plus appearance.


[size=5][b][u][color=#000000]Forgotten Wolf Set[/color] [color=#616161]+[/color] [color=#d8d8d8]Kaer Morhen Set[/color][/u][/b][/size]
In vanilla, Forgotten Wolf is a poor attempt of making the "Luigi" of the Wolf set. And Kaer Morhen is the default armour no longer useable.
The mod rework Forgotten Wolf by taking the vanilla Manticore stats, so its purpose is mainly about using potion/decoction, while still being a Medium Armour.
Kaer Morhen is improved and long-term useful until you equip to another Witcher set.

[i]Vanilla: Medium Armour[/i] ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ ﻿ [u]M[/u][u]od: Medium Armour [/u]

[i]Vanilla OLD Bonus[/i]
- Increases the duration of potions. The bonus increases by 7% for each piece of the set. (Up to 42% potions duration)
- Aard deals additional damage to enemies affected by Yrden.
[u]Mod NEW Bonus[/u]
- Increases potion charge by half of the piece of the set. (Up to 3 potion charges)
- Aard deal additional damage to enemies affected by an Yrden trap. While Toxicity exceed 40% of the threshold, parry an attack will always trigger a counterattack.

Other change:
- They now have better stats in NG+ than the normal game.
- Add Schematics of Kaer Morhen Set in the beginning. They have lesser-"Enhanced" stats and Forgotten Wolf Set Bonus, with lower level requirement, and can be crafted with "Journeyman" smith.
- The Kaer Morhen set has Resistance to Bludgeoning and Armour Reduction instead of Poison Resistance and Poison Effect.
- The Starting Swords have the appearance of the Vesemir's Sword and they now level up when Geralt levels up too. Also the crafted swords will have the E3 Swords appearance (Top Notch swords).
- The original Top Notch swords become the original Starting Swords, with the name "Top Notch". Not Witcher quality, nor with Forgotten Wolf set, but provide "Mastercrafted" version of Kaer Morhen stats.
- If starting new game for "Hearts of Stone" or "Blood & Wines", the starting armour will have lesser-"Mastercrafted" stats.
- The "Basic" Forgotten Wolf now become "Superior", with higher stats but higher level requirement. No change to the name and doesn't require Kaer Morhen set! Also remove the "NoNotification" tag to Forgotten Wolf.
- Crafted Kaer Morhen will have belt and flask If using [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/5911]Professional Witcher Belt and Items[/url], while the standard one won't. That way you'll tell if it's the Enhanced or the Basic armour.
- Include [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/11260]Brothers in Arms[/url] fix where Forgotten Wolf got some stamina bonus… And include the mod [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/7926]Netflix Season 2 Strap Restored Cut-Content[/url], by [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/users/74758253]Bjorn18 [/url](for compatibility merge).
- When using [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/8081]Alchemy[/url] compatible patch, the first bonus is replaced by "Decreases Toxicity damage by 2.5% for each piece of the set. (Up to 15% Toxicity damage reduction)"


[color=#6aa84f][size=5][b]Installation[/b][/size][/color]
For newcomer/beginner: I invite you reading  [url=https://docs.google.com/document/d/13dCXx0fnaI7xeo2bZBwCOALqIKOJSF-CJea-0-ZBHy4/edit?pli=1]The Tome of Witcher 3 Modding[/url]

[b]0 [i]DON'T USE VORTEX!![/i][/b]
1. Use [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/2678]Witcher 3 Mod Manager[/url], or extract the files in your game's directory Witcher 3 Wild Hunt GOTY Edition.
2. Launch [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/8405]Script Merger[/url] and merge the script if it conflicts.

[color=#6aa84f][b]How to uninstall?[/b][/color]
Remove the folder "mod7WitcherSetBalanced" from Mods and "dlcwitchersetbalanced" from DLC. (There is a chance Geralt will be nude).

[color=#6aa84f][size=5][b]Compatibility and Conflict[/b][/size][/color]
[size=3]Reminder:[color=#ff0000] [b]Next-Gen compatible![/b][/color][/size]
So far the bigger mod I made before I could release my overhaul project.
It change so much in the script and XML that may hardly conflict and easily cause merged conflict.

At the moment, the main files is compatible with:
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/11339]Advanced Icons for Witcher Gear[/url]
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/11260]Brothers in Arms[/url] 
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/7185]Grammar of the Path[/url] 
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/8078]Crossbow Rework[/url] 
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/11253]Dynamic Scabbards[/url] 
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/3703]Improved Fist Fights[/url] 
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/8883]Path Of The Tough[/url] (Only the "Lite" version)
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/5911]Professional Witcher Belt and Items[/url] (for only Crafted KM and Grandmaster Viper)
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/3333]Simple Alchemy Refill[/url]
- ANY Texture Mods for armour/sword

Compatible patch available for:
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/8081]Alchemy[/url] 
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/7464]Balanced Damage and Scaling[/url] 
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/7466]Balanced Item Level Scaling[/url] 
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/5104]Essential Weapon Rework[/url] 
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/11545]Immersive Gears Rewards[/url] 
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/10076]More Reasonable Damage[/url] (Install "2. Compatibility Patch - Yellow Mutagens (SRDfTS)" from the misc files)
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/6555]Standardised Naming Convention - Witcher Gear[/url] 

NOT compatible with:
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/10547]Combat Resource Overhaul[/url] 
- [url=https://modspot.dev/u/aeltoth/tw3-crow]CROW[/url] 
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/3605]No levels[/url] 
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/9857]Progressive Scavenger Hunt[/url] [color=#00ffff](Temporality incompatible until I rebuild with REDKit)[/color]
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/9657]Spectre[/url] 
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/6404]Swap Witcher Armor Appearances[/url] 
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/7323]Swap Witcher Swords Appearances[/url] 
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/5802]W3EE[/url] (whoever ask will be block!)

And you NO LONGER need:
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/7182]Minor Set Bonus[/url] 
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/7926]Netflix Season 2 Strap Restored Cut-Content[/url] 
- Stamina Regen Decoupled from Total Stamina (From [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/7250]Yellow Mutagens[/url])

[b][color=#6aa84f][size=5]Recommended Mod[/size][/color][/b]
Below are the list of mod I recommend with mine:
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/9857]Progressive Scavenger Hunt[/url]: Most Recommended. Reorder all schematic location in a progressive way.
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/10076]More Reasonable Damage[/url]: Most Recommended. Balance skills and damage for a good meta build of Witcher set.
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/8883]Path Of The Tough[/url] : Bring various fixes and balance of the core gameplay.
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/5104]The Essential Weapon Rework[/url]: Statistics rework of all Relic and Witcher swords to make them unique and powerful.
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/8078]Crossbow Rework[/url]: Buff and rework all crossbow to a more unique.
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/6568]Progress on the Path[/url]: Track everything for completion.
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/8005]Lore-Friendly Scabbards[/url]: Change the scabbard texture into natural and consistent colour.
