name: ImpUI (ImprovedUI)
summary: NOT MAINTAINED - Changes existing UI to enhance the current modding scene by removing intrusive in-game warning and enhancing character creation options. Also provides optional files that replace UI elements based on player feedback
description:
[b]Disclaimer - This mod is not maintained.[/b] There are no plans to actively maintain the project as of this time. Feel free to reach out if you want to work on ImpUI or any other UI mods.

[b]22/4/25 patch 8 update 3[/b] - Some CC features have been re-added by bibsan, which were removed in the patch 8 slimmed-down version (which was released to reduce issues with patch 7 and conflicts with patch 8).

[url=https://github.com/TheRealDjmr/BG3ImprovedUI/blob/main/README.md]Updated Readme[/url]

[b]Use only if other mods utilise features of this mod, otherwise can be removed.
[/b]
Known UI issues with patch 8:
[list]
[*]KB&M spellbook appears duplicated - [b]fixed 17/4/25[/b]
[*]Controller action resources are buggy and do not show in inventory - [b]fixed 17/4/25[/b]
[*]Sub-race spells and racial passives selection not available at level up/Withers in the base game, available in ImpUI patch 7 - [b]fixed 22/4/25 in bibsan version[/b]
[/list]
[b][u]Patch 8 CC fixes[/u]
[/b]
Features:
[list]
[*]CC/Level up Layout tweaks: Passive and Spell selection to Races
[*]Respec tweaks: Race and Background tabs, including racial passives that can be unselected then changed, and racial spells that can be switched
[*]CC/Respec tweaks: Icons for modded backgrounds
[/list][u]
Installing[/u]

Place ImpUI.pak under "%LocalAppData%\Larian Studios\Baldur's Gate 3\Mods" - enable via the in-game mod manager[b]

DONATIONS[/b]

Donations - ImpUI contains amendments from many authors and includes amended versions of .xaml files originally from Larian Studios (this is Larian Intellectual Property, licenced by their terms). We do not accept direct donations for the project - we would instead recommend supporting any charities of your choice.[url=https://www.womensaid.org.uk/give/donate/donate-now/]

[/url]Donations Made:

30/12/2023 - Thanks! Received donation confirmation of [url=https://i.imgur.com/2Pgf9k5.png]$100[/url] to Doctors Without Borders

name: Baldur's Gate 3 Mod Fixer
summary:
This pak forces the story to recompile, allowing pak mods to work with the Full Release of Baldur's Gate 3.
All credit for this technique goes to Norbyte.

This mod is no longer required when playing Baldur's Gate Patch 7 or newer.
description:
[color=#ff7700][size=5][b]Nexus Mods Staff note:[/b] This mod is no longer required when playing Baldur's Gate Patch 7 or newer. [/size]
[/color]

[line]
[size=6]Mod Fixer[/size]

This pak forces the story to recompile, allowing pak mods to work with the full release of Baldur's Gate 3.

Just load this mod along side others and it will let you start a new game with mods enabled.
[i][size=3]
 [/size][size=5]All credit for this technique goes to Norbyte![/size][/i]

[size=5][b]Installation[/b][/size]
Just drop the .pak file into your mods folder and it will work. No need to edit modsettings.lsx file.
author: Chance Santana-Wees

name: Mod Configuration Menu (MCM)
summary: Provides an in-game graphical interface, accessible via main and ESC menus, allowing players to easily manage mod settings, keybindings, and custom IMGUI widgets.
description:
[size=3][b][img]https://i.imgur.com/kbiG11u.png[/img]
Baldur's Gate 3 [color=#6d9eeb]Mod Configuration Menu[/color] ([color=#6d9eeb]MCM[/color]) provides an in-game menu through which mod users can configure mod settings and hotkeys.[/b]
It is a central location for mod configuration and can be accessed via the main and ESC menus, or pressing the [i]Insert[/i] key (by default).[/size][size=3]

[size=2]Are you a mod author? Check the [url=https://wiki.bg3.community/Tutorials/Mod-Frameworks/mod-configuration-menu]documentation for integrating mods with the MCM[/url]; it's a simple process and there are a lot of author-facing features you definitely want to hear about![/size]
[/size]
[center][size=3]Here's [b][color=#6d9eeb]MCM 1.33[/color][/b] in action with several mods, showcasing profiles, keybindings, and mod pages:[/size]
[size=3][b][img]https://i.imgur.com/PGuMLG3.gif[/img]

[size=3][b]If you'd like to support MCM and my other mods, consider supporting me on Patreon; every bit helps!
[/b][/size][/b][size=3][size=3]Consider politely asking authors of mods that use Script Extender to integrate with MCM![/size][/size][b][url=https://patreon.com/Volitio?utm_source=join_link&utm_campaign=creatorshare_creator]
[/url][url=http://www.patreon.com/Volitio?utm_source=join_link&utm_campaign=creatorshare_creator][img]https://i.imgur.com/MBOhOZl.png[/img][/url]﻿ [url=https://discord.gg/PUx3vJgapM][img]https://i.ibb.co/Tx68gFCt/join-my-discord.png[/img]﻿[/url]
[/b][/size][/center][img]https://i.imgur.com/zFbSiOA.png[/img]
[list]
[*][b]Easily configure mods[/b]: MCM provides a simple and intuitive way to change mods' settings. No need to manually edit JSON files - although still allowed, just like in the old days!
[*] [b]Instant setting saving and loading[/b]: Unlike the traditional way of handling settings, MCM-integrated settings update in real-time as they are changed, without requiring save reloads or SE commands;
[*][b]Load order health checking[/b]: MCM also checks your load order for missing/outdated dependencies and mods with invalid UUIDs.
[*][b]Keybinding management[/b]: easily assign keyboard bindings directly from the MCM window, with built-in conflict handling.
[*][b]UI agnostic[/b]: MCM's core configuration code is designed to work independently of IMGUI. This means that even if IMGUI support were to be completely removed, MCM would still work. [b]Users who can't see the current UI will still be able to use MCM's under-the-hood benefits to manage their settings, and also edit them through JSON files[/b].
[*] [b]Save-safe[/b]: MCM does not write anything to your save files - all modifications are done in-memory and then saved to JSON files. MCM can also be safely uninstalled without affecting your save files at any time, provided any mods that use it are disabled or uninstalled as well;
[*] [b]Multiple profiles[/b]: MCM allows you to manage multiple configuration profiles, so you can have different settings for different playthroughs/characters;
[*] [b]Reduced user error[/b]: MCM performs several settings validations, reducing the risk of user error when configuring mods, even if manually editing JSON files;
[*] [b]Export and import settings[/b]: Mod settings are stored per-profile and per-mod JSON files under [font=Courier New]Script Extender\BG3MCM[/font], making it easy to create backups and restore settings.
[*] [b]Uncluttered UI[/b]: MCM consolidates all mod settings into one interface, reducing screen clutter.
[*] [b]Robust[/b]: MCM has dozens of automated tests to ensure it works as expected, without halting;
[*] [b]Localization support[/b]: MCM allows you to view settings in your preferred language, if translations are provided by mod authors/translators;
[/list][line]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/sxXwnGA.png[/img]
[b][size=3]Installation is the same as any other pak mod[/size][/b] and can be performed with new and existing saves:
[list=1]
[*]In [url=https://github.com/LaughingLeader/BG3ModManager]Baldur's Gate 3 Mod Manager[/url], (or use Vortex at your own discretion), install/enable the Script Extender (SE) by clicking on the 'Tools' tab at the top left of the program and selecting 'Download and Extract the Script Extender'. [b]Alternatively,[size=2][size=2] press CTRL+SHIFT+ALT+T while BG3MM's window is focused;[/size][/size][/b]
[*]Import this mod's .zip file into the Mod Manager, then drag the mod from the right panel of the Mod Manager to the left panel; make sure to [b]drag it to the top of your load order[/b], as it needs to be placed above any mods that rely on this framework;
[*]Save and export your load order. MCM will now automatically pick up and be available to any mods that may use it.
[/list]
[size=3]You should then see a button for MCM in the ESC menu in-game. It can also be opened with the INSERT key by default.[b]
[/b]You may also update MCM at any point[/size][size=4], unless stated otherwise. [/size][b]
[line]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/aF63bEO.png[/img]
UI/IMGUI support in SE has been significantly improved since launch, but you may still face some issues that are beyond my control.[/b] If you don't see the MCM window, you can try these things:
[list=1]
[*]restart your game ([color=#e06666][b]don't alt-tab before reaching the main menu[/b][/color]);
[*]temporarily disable overlays (Discord/RTSS/etc);
[*][color=#efefef]disable third-party frame generation (NVIDIA Smooth Motion, AMD Fluid Motion, FG mods, etc)[/color]

[*]toggle the rendering API to Vulkan/DX11.
[*]verify that the problem persists with no other DLL mods, such as WASD movement;
[*]﻿delete the [b]imgui.ini[/b] file at [i]AppData\Local\Larian Studios\Baldur's Gate 3\imgui.ini[/i]
[*]if you disabled SE autoupdates or are using the Devel channel and having problems, please switch back.
[/list]
[color=#6aa84f][size=4][b]Even if you have issues with IMGUI, MCM will continue working as a config/JSON manager in the background if its window does not appear.
[/b][/size]
[/color][line]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/DpLvnqC.png[/img]
[size=3]MCM 1.30+ greatly improved multiplayer compatibility. If you still face problems, however, let the host manage settings.
Players joining a multiplayer session don't need to match the host's MCM settings unless stated by the respective mod author.
[/size]
Please report issues using the [b]Bugs[/b] tab.
Suggestions are welcome in the [b]Forum[/b] or [b]Posts[/b] tab.

[size=4][b]Files location[/b][/size]
Individual user settings can be located in the [font=Courier New]BG3MCM\Profiles\<PROFILE_NAME>\<MOD_NAME>\[/font] folder. If you're feeling adventurous, here's a spoiler with an [i]example[/i] of the full file structure:
[spoiler]You can find the root folder on Windows by pressing WIN+R and entering:[quote][code]explorer %LocalAppData%\Larian Studios\Baldur's Gate 3\Script Extender\BG3MCM[/code][/quote]BG3MCM/
│ mcm_params.json (Stores profile data, needed to be able to load profiles)
│ mod_configuration_menu_config.json (Manages a few MCM settings before MCM can load its own settings)
└───Profiles
├───Default
│ ├───AutoSendFoodToCamp
│ │ settings.json (Stores current values for mod Auto Send Food To Camp)
│ ├───PreemptivelyLabelContainers
│ │ settings.json
│ └───WaypointInsideEmeraldGrove
│ settings.json
├───Profile 1
│ ├───AutoSendFoodToCamp
│ │ settings.json
│ └───WaypointInsideEmeraldGrove
│ settings.json[/spoiler][b][size=4][line]
[/size][/b][img]https://i.imgur.com/hDj8F0X.png[/img]
Similar to my experience with [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/8295]ISF[/url], I'd like to thank the foundational work of [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/users/21094599]Focus[/url]﻿ and [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/users/244952?tab=user+files]Nells[/url]﻿/[url=https://github.com/BG3-Community-Library-Team/]Community Library team[/url]. While applied to a lesser extent in this context, their insights throughout ISF have made this endeavor a much less daunting task. I will be forever grateful for their open-source contributions to the Baldur's Gate 3 modding scene. Of course, none of this would be possible without the work put into Script Extender & LSLib by [url=https://github.com/Norbyte/]Norbyte[/url]﻿ and his responsiveness to feedback and troubleshooting. It was a pleasure working asynchronously with you, gentlemen ??.
Also, a shoutout to [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/users/64167336?tab=user+files]Fallen[/url] for alpha testing this mod, to [url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/skiz/mods?gameId=3474]Skiz[/url] for being in the IMGUI trenches with me, to [url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/Aahz07/about-me?gameId=3474]Aahz[/url] for some help with sidebar design and implementation (also [url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/osirisofinternet/mods?gameId=3474]osirisofinternet[/url]) with detaching windows, state management involving client-side UI (adapting ReactiveX for SE, etc), and porting vanilla icons for usage inside IMGUI, to the [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/fallout4/mods/21497]F4MCM[/url] team for inspiration, to [url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/MattifusP/]MattifusP[/url]﻿ and [url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/Valeriede]Valeriede[/url] for the contributions on GitHub and to many many others in the modding community that shared support and/or donated!
[size=4][b]
Source Code
[/b][/size]The source code is available on [url=https://github.com/AtilioA/BG3-MCM/]GitHub[/url] or by unpacking the .pak file. Endorse on Nexus and give it a star on GitHub if you liked it![line]

name: Native Mod Loader
summary: This is NativeModLoader, used to load any native dll plugins within bin\NativeMods\ folder.
description:
I added very large titles for each section. Hope it helps![font=Lucida Sans Unicode][size=3][img]https://i.postimg.cc/j5QDwTyM/banner-brief.png[/img]
[/size][size=3]
Native Mod Loader is used for loading any native dll plugins within game's [color=#00ff00]bin[/color][color=#f4f4f4]\NativeMods\[/color] folder.
[/size][size=3][color=#00ffff]This is just the[b] loader! [/b]It doesn't bundle any native mods. If you are not redirected here from other native mods, you don't need to install this.
[/color][/size]
dll插件加载器。



[img]https://i.postimg.cc/WzNh0ynT/banner-install.png[/img]
[size=3][color=#ffff00]
[/color][font=Lucida Sans Unicode][size=4] [u][b][color=#ff7700]Manual Install[/color][/b][/u]:[/size][/font][size=4]
[/size][list=1]
[*][size=4][font=Lucida Sans Unicode][font=Lucida Sans Unicode] [/font]Extract [b]both [/b][color=#dd7e6b]bink2w64.dll[/color] and [color=#93c47d]bink2w64_original.dll[/color] from archive file into your game's [color=#00ff00]bin[/color] folder e[/font].g steam path (this may vary):  "D:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Baldurs Gate 3\[color=#00ff00]bin[/color]"[/size]
[*][font=Lucida Sans Unicode][size=4] ([color=#ff0000]Important![/color]) Confirm replacement/overwrite when prompted.[/size][/font]
[/list][size=4]
[font=Lucida Sans Unicode] [u][b][color=#ff7700]Vortex Install (This mod cannot be installed via BG3MM)[/color][/b][/u]:
[list=1]
[*]Download and Install this mod with Vortex.
[*]Click 'Install as Replacer', [color=#ff0000]ignore error and continue.[/color]
[*]Double click this mod
[*][u]Select '[color=#ffff00]Engine Injector[/color]' in 'Mod Type' [/u][u]dropdown[/u] !! [color=#ff0000]Important[/color]
[*]Click the '[color=#00ffff]Deploy Mods[/color]' button, or '[color=#00ffff]Deploy[/color]' in the notification
[/list][/font][/size][/size][size=4]
将 [color=#dd7e6b]bink2w64.dll[/color] 和 [color=#93c47d]bink2w64_original.dll[/color] 两个文件[b]都[/b]解压缩到游戏安装目录下[color=#00ff00]bin[/color]文件夹内。
示例路径(并非绝对):
 "D:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Baldurs Gate 3\[color=#00ff00]bin[/color]"
([color=#ff0000]重要![/color]) 提示覆盖时确认。[/size]



[/font][center][font=Lucida Sans Unicode]﻿Thanks to [url=https://www.youtube.com/@WitherFoxOfficial]WitherFox[/url]'s awesome tutorial video![/font][/center][center][font=Lucida Sans Unicode][youtube]aHtxhh8J1pE[/youtube][/font][/center][font=Lucida Sans Unicode]


[color=#d5a6bd]
[/color][img]https://i.postimg.cc/nr3M21R7/banner-uninstall.png[/img]
[size=3][color=#00ffff]The loader comes with BG3's original bink2w64.dll file. If you carefully follow the steps below, you don't need to re-verify your game files. ( you can, of course )[/color]

- Within your game's [color=#00ff00]bin [/color]folder: 
  - Delete `[color=#ea9999]NativeMods[/color]` folder .
  - Delete `[color=#b4a7d6]bink2w64.dll[/color]`.
  - Rename `bink2w64_original.dll` to `[color=#b4a7d6]bink2w64.dll[/color]`.

- 在游戏安装目录下[color=#00ff00]bin[/color]文件夹内: 
  - 删除 `[color=#ea9999]NativeMods[/color]` 文件夹 .
  - 删除 `[color=#b4a7d6]bink2w64.dll[/color]`.
  - 将 `bink2w64_original.dll` 重命名为 `[color=#b4a7d6]bink2w64.dll[/color][/size][size=3]`.
[/size]


[img]https://i.postimg.cc/v8h1GQYB/banner-faq.png[/img]
[size=3]
+ Mod no work. Halp!
- This is not to be installed with [i][b]any [/b][/i]Mod Manager. Manual install only.

+ I don't have `[color=#93c47d]NativeMods[/color]` folder!
- This is just the loader. If the native mod you are installing doesn't come with a proper folder structure, you can create `bin\[color=#93c47d]NativeMods[/color]` folder yourself.

+ Failed to load `[color=#b4a7d6]bink2w64_original.dll`[/color]
- Must put both files into the [color=#00ff00]bin[/color].

+ [color=#ff0000]Data Mismatch error.[/color]
- Because the loader replaces a file, it's normal and can be ignored.

+ 加载失败
- 需要将两个文件都放入[color=#00ff00]bin[/color]文件夹。

+ 我没有 `[color=#93c47d]NativeMods[/color]` 文件夹!
- 此mod仅是插件加载器，如果你正在安装的原生插件没有自带该文件夹，你可以在bin文件夹中新建一个 `[color=#93c47d]NativeMods[/color]` 文件夹。

+ [color=#ff0000]数据完整性错误[/color]
- 加载器替换了游戏文件，可以忽略此错误。


[img]https://i.postimg.cc/433425Qf/banner-modder.png[/img]
[color=#dd7e6b][size=3]
NativeModLoader [/size][/color][color=#f4f4f4][size=3]loads all native dll mods within [/size][/color][color=#00ff00][size=3]bin[/size][/color][color=#f4f4f4][size=3]\[/size][/color][color=#b6d7a8][size=3]NativeMods[/size][/color][color=#f4f4f4][size=3]\ folder. To get started developing a native dll plugin, see referencec to [url=https://github.com/gottyduke/BG3_PluginTemplate]BG3 Plugin Template[/url]. [/size][/color]


[color=#ffd966][size=3]
[img]https://i.postimg.cc/SNKnswRG/banner-credits.png[/img]
[/size][/color][size=2]
The source code inherits and sustains the MIT License。

name: Tav's Hair Salon
summary: Collection of hairstyles. Mostly for elf/half-elf/drow/human/tiefling bodytypes 1&2
description:
[center][size=6][b]Tav's Hair Salon
✂️ ✂️ ✂️
[/b][/size]
[b][size=4]Do not reupload my mods or any of the files included in them, not to other sites, not to discord servers.[/size][/b][i][size=4]
[/size][size=3]
Tavs Hairpack: Mostly for human/elf/half-elf/drow/tiefling bodytype 1 and for bodytype 2. 2 Hairstyles for gith bodytype 1. No vanilla hair is replaced so these are for player character only, you will not stumble into any NPC with the same hairstyle. Some hairs are just small edits of vanilla hairs that originally had clipping in ears for some elf heads I wished to use. Some hair do no support highlights.

Autosnap Hairpack: For all bodytypes. Some hairstyles removed since EA version because of issues, NO physics

All hair can be loaded at once! Use at your own risk, I have tested hairs till getting to the beach. Hatstyles most likely won't be visible with these hairs.[/size]

[/i][size=4]
[b]HOW TO INSTALL[/b][/size]
[i][size=3]With mod manager of your choice.[/size][/i][b][s]
[/s][/b]
[i][size=3]If you wish to see icons for each hair in CC, use this [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/3346]patch[/url] made by perseidipity!
Multiple patches for different race mods are also hosted there! ?[/size][/i]
[i][size=3]
[/size][/i][size=4][b]HOW TO UNINSTALL[/b][/size]
[size=3][i]Just disable in your mod manager, things will revert back to vanilla[/i][/size]

[size=4][b]OTHER MODS IN SCREENSHOTS[/b][/size]
[size=3][i][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/2067]Trips' Accessory Collection[/url]﻿ by Tripsadin with template from [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/2001]Piercing Edits[/url]﻿ by jerinski that I used to make my own "set" for personal use.
[/i][/size]
[size=3][i]All the presets, edited clothes etc are my own mods. The clothes in early access era pictures are not available anymore cause my mod got broken so badly at launch. Many presets are also not available anymore or I have never even shared them to begin with.[/i][/size][size=4]

[b]THANK YOU[/b][/size]
[size=3][i][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/users/2651530]Padme4000[/url] for hair resource template and mod name!
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/76]Shinyhobo [/url]for modders multitool
[url=https://github.com/Norbyte/lslib/releases]Norbyte [/url]for Export Tool
Larian Studios for amazing EA game and assets
labotor & Aloija for I peaked through your files to know how to make icons show up in CC
[/i][/size][/center]

name: BG3SX - Sex Framework
summary: Custom sex animations & Framework for BG3
description:
[size=4][b][u][center][img]https://i.imgur.com/znimKVr.png[/img][/center][/u][/b][/size][size=4]
[/size][center][size=4][u][url=https://discord.gg/ChndmEPUpt]﻿Discord[/url][/u] - [/size][size=4][u][url=https://www.patreon.com/cw/BG3AF_BG3SX]Patreon[/url][/u][/size][size=2]
(Patreon gives you no benefits, its optional in case you want to support us!)[/size][/center]

[u][size=5][b]v2.0 Update:[/b][/size][/u]

[list]
[*]No more spells
[*]Now uses [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/9162]MCM[/url]﻿ as a HUB for a new UI
[*]Individual Tabs for specific things that spells previously did:
[/list]

Scene Tab:
[list]
[*]This tab will let you create SFW or NSFW scenes (in the background, animations made by animators can now be tagged, if you don't have any installed there are none to display)
[*]A new scene will open a small window that lets you control that scene (SceneControl)
[*]The buttons you see behave the same way as the spells did previously but you also have a drop down menu which lets you select animations (Filtering by type or mod will be added later).
[*]If you close the window (not end scene), you can still re-open it via the Scene Tab. The name of the Scene will always be a combination of those involved.
[/list]

The Appearance Tab:
[list]
[*]When you switch characters it auto-updates
[*]It allows you to set character specific stripping-blocks and genital behavior
[*]You can assign an out-of-sex and during-sex genital that will get auto-applied when entering/leaving scenes
[*]Genitals are sorted by Modded, MrFunsize Erections (come with the mod), Vanilla Penis, Vanilla Vulva (base-game genitals)
[*]Allows hiding entities with a checkbox for potential make-shift threesomes
[/list]

NPC Tab:
[list]
[*]Allows selecting a range and clicking "Scan" to get a list of NPC's around you to "Add" them to a separate NPC interface above the tabs
[*]"Adding" NPC's allows you to start scenes with them as the initiator, as well as editing the same features in the appearance tab like with regular party members
[*]NPC-on-NPC scenes are possible this way
[/list]

Settings Tab:
[list]
[*]Barebones hiding of individual tabs without storing these as permanent settings (for now)
[*]Checkbox to auto-scan for NPC's at the set range in the NPC tab (can be quite demanding so disabled by default)
[/list]

Whitelist Tab:
[list]
[*]To be enabled in the Settings tab
[*]Shows you a list of Tags the selected entity has
[*]Shows you a list of all relevant tags
[/list]

FAQ Tab:
[list]
[*]With the help of baeator we compiled a list of Frequently Asked Questions that may help you
[/list]

+ A LOT of (partially self-inflicted) bug-fixes and changes under the hood.[/list]

[b][u]For Modders[/u][/b]: - [b]More information about each of these in our [/b][url=https://discord.gg/kDmq7TXME3][b]Discord[/b][/url]
[list]
[*]ModEvents - Modders can now hook into events to gather data from several stages of our mod
[*]Whitelist/Blacklist - Going forward, custom race modders need to whitelist their race before you are allowed to use them for our mod (Reason: Complaints)
[*]Heightmatching Animations - This is a system to make adding your own animation sets easier
[/list]
[u][size=5][b]Features:[/b][/size][/u]
[list]
[*]Fully custom animations
[*]Solo & paired animations
[*]NPC Stripping
[*]Character matched voices for companions
[*]Automatic stripping & redressing
[*]Automatic Erections via bundled [url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/MrFunSize/mods]MrFunSize's[/url]﻿﻿ Erections Mod
[*]Easily switch between animations during a scene
[*]New animation IDs, so no overriding of existing animations
[*]ModEvents
[*]Whitelist/Blacklist
[*]Heightmatching animation
[/list]
[b]
Thank you [url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/Kikyo99?gameId=3474]Kikyo[/url] and [url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/Bagarete?gameId=3474]Bagarete[/url] for the amazing screenshots!
Some animations you see in the images are still WIP from addons!
So keep an eye out on updates.[u]

Additional info since some people don't read the load order section:[/u][/b]
If you have custom races installed, list them under our mod in your load order to actually be able to access our whitelist tables on mod load.
[u][size=5][b]Addons:[/b][/size][/u]
Check out various Animation Addons by searching Nexus again for "BG3SX"

[center]NPC Stripping:
[img]https://i.imgur.com/YWgsP8l.gif[/img]﻿[/center][center]
Cycling through available Genitals, even modded ones: (Testing still had ? icon, ingame its a Salami Icon)
[img]https://i.imgur.com/UtF4xWh.gif[/img]﻿[/center]﻿
[center]Automatic Erections: (Settings for these are available in a cucumber Icon Spell)
[img]https://i.imgur.com/kzUITGP.gif[/img]﻿
If you want to use another erect penis, go into cucumber spell and disable "Auto-Erection" and select the erect penis as your default. (Otherwise they'll get overwritten by our bundled ones)[/center]
[u]
[b][size=5]Load order:[/size][/b][/u]

High > Low
﻿MCM > BG3AF > BG3SX > Animation or other Addons of BG3SX > Custom Races or Visual mods (for example those from [url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/Sindae/about-me?gameId=3474]Sindae[/url]﻿ or [url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/MsHellspawn/about-me?gameId=3474]MsHellspawn[/url]﻿ , or [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/10180]Brush your Teeth[/url]﻿ by [url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/Kaz/about-me?gameId=3474]Kaz[/url]﻿)

[u][b][size=5]Fresh Installation Instructions:
[/size][/b][/u][list=1]
[*]Download & Install Norbyte's Baldur's Gate 3 Script Extender from here: https://github.com/Norbyte/bg3se/releases. If you are using the ModManager you can install it from there, but its better to always grab the newest version from github and add the DWrite.dll to your "Baldurs Gate 3/bin" folder.
[*]Install BG3 Mod Manager (BG3MM): https://github.com/LaughingLeader/BG3ModManager
[*]Download ﻿BG3 Sex Framework PAK.zip:
[*]Install the BG3SX.pak file with BG3MM:
[/list] ﻿ ﻿- Drag ﻿"LunesBG3SX.zip" into BG3MM. It will appear in the panel on the right hand side.
﻿ ﻿- Drag 'BG3SX Sex Framework' from the right-hand side to the left-hand side of BG3MM. (1) ﻿
﻿ ﻿﻿- Press the 'Save Load Order to File' button to save your mod load order, or just press 'ctrl + s' (2)
﻿ ﻿- Now press the 'Export Order to Game' button to deploy your mod load order to your game, or just press 'ctrl + e' (3)

[b]NOTE: The mod has been renamed to BG3SX - new images following soon[/b]

[img]https://i.imgur.com/kZlFXPz.png[/img]﻿

[u][b]Detailed Fresh Installation Instructions:[/b][/u][b]
[/b]You need Norbytes Script Extender for this mod to function.
If you are using the ModManager you can install it from there, but its better to always grab the newest version from github and add the DWrite.dll to your Baldurs Gate 3/bin folder. There is also a version without an updater for those who experience problems with it saying that you have no internet:[url=https://github.com/Norbyte/bg3se/issues/53]https://github.com/Norbyte/bg3se/issues/53[/url]

Drag the .pak into your ModManager.
In ModManager, drag the downloaded .pak of the mod to the left side to activate it.
If you have other mods, decide on your load order between them (Future sex mods you might want to load after the framework)
Now, let the ModManager actually create the load order file, called modsettings.lsx, by clicking "Export Order to Game"
Everything should now be installed correctly.

When you are in game, you should see a pink heart (Like in the Name). This action is called "Sex". It has melee range and you can choose between "Ask for Sex" and "Masturbation". Click "Ask for Sex" and then on another character you want to have sex with. After a quick black screen fadeout the scene will begin. After a second or two your action bar will be available again and you can choose between different positions or stop having sex entirely.

author: Lune - Skiz - Satan

name: Appearance Edit Enhanced
summary: Enables changing ANY character's body type and race, as well as editing Origin characters in the Mirror.
description:
[center][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/7294][img]https://i.imgur.com/ofxhnc9.png[/img][/url][/center][left][color=#6fa8dc][b][size=5][size=3][b][color=#6fa8dc][b][size=5]
[/size][/b][/color][url=https://github.com/Norbyte/bg3se/releases/]This mod [u][color=#ff0000]requires[/color][/u] Norbyte's Script Extender﻿﻿﻿.[/url]﻿[/b][/size]﻿[size=3][b] [/b][/size]﻿[size=3][b]You install it by dropping it into the bin folder in your game directory. Additionally, if you're using the BG3 Mod Manager, you can go to "Tools"-> "Download & Extract Script Extender"[/b] [b]to have the manager install it for you.[/b][/size][/size][/b][/color]
[color=#cc4125][b][size=5][/size][/b][/color][color=#6fa8dc][b][size=5]
Mod Instructions:[/size][/b][/color][u]

[/u][i][b][/b][/i][i]Save your game first because you may come across problems that require loading your save.[/i][i]

[/i][i][b]Make sure you have the newest version of Script Extender.[/b][/i]

When you launch the game for the first time, you will receive a spell on your hotbar automatically. The spell looks like Seaming (blue with 3 masks on top of each other). If you remove this spell from your Hotbar, you won't get it back. You'll have to check your spellbook for the character to see the spell again and re-add it to your Hotbar.

The spell you get is a container for 4 spells.

[b][u]Resculpt[/u]

[color=#ff0000]Resculpt is not perfect since Patch 6. You will have issues in the mirror/level-up/respec screens on characters whose body size you changed dramatically.[/color]
[/b]
This spell lets you change the body type or race of [b]ANY[/b] character that casts it. Please follow these instructions step-by-step if you wish to use this feature:

[list=1]
[*]Before you begin, make sure you're on the[b] newest version of the mod[/b] and your [b]script extender version is at minimum 8[/b].

[*]Select the character that you wish to edit. The character that casts the Resculpt spell will be the one that is edited, even in a multiplayer lobby.

[*]Casting the Resculpt spell will open the Character Creation Screen for [b]EVERY [/b]player in the lobby. If you are in a multiplayer lobby, please tell the players that [b]DID NOT CAST THE SPELL [/b]to select Dark Urge and hit confirm, confirming on the guardian screen as well, to exit out. Picking [b]Dark Urge[/b] in this menu is how you [b]exit the menu[/b] if you change your mind in either solo or multiplayer play.

[*]If you are the one who cast the spell, select the [b]Custom Origin[/b] at the very top of the screen. [b]DO NOT SELECT ANYTHING ELSE[/b]

[*]Modify the character's race, subrace, body type, and appearance however you want. [b]ONLY THESE THINGS [/b]will be copied over. Voice is copied for Tavs but not Hirelings or Origins.

[*]Hit confirm (ignore the naming part, it doesn't matter) and hit confirm again on the Guardian screen. Nothing you do on the guardian screen matters so skip it by confirming forward.

[*]Your character should now change to the new appearance. If you see an extra character, or your appearance isn't changing, or any other issues, make sure you have followed the steps properly. If you have, make a post or bug report [b]WITH A SCREENSHOT OF THE CONSOLE AND EXACT STEPS LISTED[/b].

[*]There are some things you have to do after.
[list]
[*][b]Your portrait won't change until you enter the Mirror, change something minor (like hair color), and confirm.[/b]
[*]You won't get the new[b] racial passives/spells[/b] [b]UNTIL [/b]you[b] save and reload[/b] the game.
[*]Your [b]background changes[/b] won't change until you [b]save and reload[/b] the game.
[/list]

[/list]Current "issues" and solutions:
[list]
[*]Uninstalling the mod from this point on may cause issues with how the character looks in the Mirror, Respec, and Level-Up screens. Be aware of this. There shouldn't be any other issues other than visual from what I've seen. I recommend using the Restore spell before uninstalling.
[/list][list]
[*]If you remove transforms or have another mod that removes transforms from your character, it may mess with the look of the character. A common occurrence will be if Wyll gets turned into the devil and you wish to use the command I list to remove him from it. If this happens, just Resculpt again.
[/list][list]
[*]If you have any issues where characters are changed even after loading a previous save just restart your game.
[/list][list]
[*]There is no way to edit the appearance of a character before they join your party. I may add this feature.
[*]The "Restore" spell can fix many issues associated with origin characters. Try it out.
[*]If playing multiplayer, you need to save and reload before players join your lobby if you've recently resculpted.
[/list]
[b][u]Mirror[/u]
[/b]
This spell opens the mirror on any character to let you edit your character. If you are trying to edit an Origins character, you [b]MUST [/b]install the optional [b]Appearance Edit Origins[/b] file to be able to change what you see in the mirror.


[b][u]Respec

[/u][/b]This spell lets you respec as if you were talking to Withers. It does nothing else.[b]


[b][u]Restore

[/u][/b][/b]This spell lets you restore the appearance of an ORIGIN character back to their normal state. This can also be used to try to fix any issues associated with "Confirm" button not working. [b]This spell only works on Origin characters. You will need to save/load to see racial changes.
[/b][b][color=#b6d7a8][size=5][size=5][size=5]
Known "Issues[/size][/size][/size][/color][color=#b6d7a8][size=5][size=5]":
[/size][/size][/color][/b]
[list]
[*][b]Wyll is not editable after transformation. Run this command. (Be aware, that this will break any race/body type changing you have done and remove his devil form permanently)[/b]
[b]
[/b][code]Osi.RemoveTransforms("S_Player_Wyll_c774d764-4a17-48dc-b470-32ace9ce447d")
[/code][/list][list]
[*][b]Confirmation button is not working[/b]
[list]
[*]Make sure you've updated ImprovedUI if you use it
[*]Make sure you've selected voice
[*]Make sure you've got all options in CC selected (check every tab), even if the option is no option
[*]Maybe move the position of the mod around in the load order
[*]Try using the Restore spell
[*]Try hitting randomize
[/list][/list][list]
[*][b]Body/Race swap issues[/b]
[/list] ﻿Most issues related to body/race swap can be fixed by resculpting again. Additionally, make sure you have Script Extender updated to a minimum of v14

[list]
[*][b]Resculpting Helsin makes him lose his unique Cave Bear wildshape. You can get it back by running this command[/b]
[b]
[/b][b][code]AddSpell("S_GLO_Halsin_7628bc0e-52b8-42a7-856a-13a6fd413323", "Shout_DEN_Halsin_WildShape_Bear")[/code]
[/b][/list][b][color=#b6d7a8][size=5][size=5][size=5]
Compatibility[/size][/size][/size][/color][color=#b6d7a8][size=5][size=5]:
[/size][/size][/color][/b]
[list]
[*]If you are having issues with CC option compatibility, make sure you're on the latest version of Appearance Edit Origins. There might be compatibility issues with other mods that change this same file and the same characters.
[/list]
[color=#b6d7a8][size=5][b]Installation:[/b][/size][/color][u]
[/u]
Use either the Vortex Mod Manager or the BG3 Mod Manager and install this mod. Make sure you deploy your mods if using Vortex or export the mod list if using BG3 Mod Manager. Editing characters requires Norbyte's [url=https://github.com/Norbyte/bg3se/releases/]Script Extender[/url]﻿﻿.

You should put this mod and the Origins optional file at the bottom of your load order.

[color=#b6d7a8][size=5][b]Uninstallation:[/b][/size][/color][u]
[/u]
Remove the mod like you would any other. [b]Be aware, if you resculpted to a different Race/Body Type, you may see issues with your character in the Respec, Mirror, and Level-up screens after you uninstall. Re-installing should fix these. [/b]Make sure to export your mod load order and delete the modsettings.lsx file entirely.

If you installed the Script Extender and wish to remove it, remove [b]DWrite.dll [/b]and [b]ScriptExtenderSettings.json[/b] from your [b][i]C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Baldurs Gate 3\bin [/i][/b]or wherever your game install location is. As long as you removed these properly, there shouldn't be any issues on your save. If you do have problems, verify the integrity of the game cache on Steam.

[b][size=5][color=#b4a7d6]Support Me:[/color][u]
[/u][/size][/b][/left][url=https://ko-fi.com/eralyne][img]https://github.com/doodlum/nexusmods-widgets/blob/main/Ko-fi_40px_60fps.png?raw=true[/img][/url]
author: Eralyne

name: Party Limit Begone
summary: Increases the party limit to 16 members and multiplayer limit to 8 players!
description:
[b][size=3]Installation (Standalone version, recommended):
[/size][/b][list=1]
[*]Download and unzip the standalone version.
[*]Drag and drop the entire mods folder in to your baldurs gate 3 data folder. (example: \steamapps\common\Baldurs Gate 3\Data)
[*]That's it, enjoy the game! [b]Note:[/b] Dismiss one party member if you already have 4, this will reset the party limit and set it to 16.
Not working? Delete everything but PlayerProfiles in C:\Users\%username%\AppData\Local\Larian Studios\Baldur's Gate 3

[/list][b][size=3]Installation (Script Extender version):[/size][/b]
[list=1]
[*]Download and setup [url=https://github.com/LaughingLeader/BG3ModManager]Baldurs Gate 3 Mod Manager.[/url] (Vortex should work aswell but be sure to have script extender installed)﻿
[*]In the mod manager go to tools and select: Download & Install the script extender and hit yes.
[*]Download Party Limit Begone and drop the zip file in to your mod manager window.
[*]Now go to file and select: Reload all. Then drag and drop Party Limit Begone mod in your list from the right side to the left, to active mods.
[*]Go to file again and select: Save Order. That's it! Enjoy the game.
Not working? Delete everything but PlayerProfiles in C:\Users\%username%\AppData\Local\Larian Studios\Baldur's Gate 3

[/list][b][size=3]Multiplayer Patch Installation:[/size][/b]
[list=1]
[*]First follow one of the mod installations up above. Then download the multiplayer patch and unzip it.
[*]Drag and drop either your bg3.exe or bg3_dx11.exe file onto the PartyLimitBegonePatcher.bat file. It will create a backup for you.
[*]That's it! Enjoy the game.
[b]Note:[/b] [b]Hotfix 36 broke steam matchmaking for some users, workaround is to close steam and launch bg3.exe directly. This bypasses steam matchmaking.[/b] bg3.exe is used if vulkan is selected in the larian launcher and bg_dx11.exe if directx is selected instead. Windows defender might flag it on some systems but it's safe to use, nexusmods scans every upload. I changed it multiple times but after a while defender starts acting up again. So just allow it and move on.

[/list][size=3][b]Known Bugs (READ ME):[/b][/size][list]
[*][b]Grymforge Map transition:[/b] Reduce your party size to 4 members before sailing with the boat. If you already used it and your characters are stuck, dismiss the ones that are with you and sail back and forth to unstuck the rest. [b]Note:[/b] After using the boat a timed quest is triggered and going to camp will progess it.
[*][b]Some quest or important items aren't registered correctly if they are carried by a fifth or higher party member.[/b] So make sure your first 4 party members carry any important items.
[*][b]Camp sleeping bug:[/b] Use one of the awake companions and talk to Lae'zel, use a camp chest or reduce your party size to 4 before resting.
[*][b]Some ingame events are simply hardcoded for 4 party members and will glitch out.[/b] This is usually solved by either dismissing the additional members/players or by teleporting back and forth.
[/list][b][size=3]Linux and Mac support:[/size][/b]
[list]
[*]PLB is supported by both linux and mac but the MP patch is tricky to install, the easiest solution would be for a friend to send you their patched exe file. Otherwise you must edit the PartyLimitBegonePatcher.bat file with any text editor and change this line: [i]Set "GetFileName=%~n1.exe"[/i] to something like [i]Set "GetFileName=bg3.exe"[/i] then place your bg3.exe file next to it - in to the same folder and run the PartyLimitBegonePatcher.bat file with [url=https://www.winehq.org/]wine[/url]﻿.
[/list][b][size=3]Console/Crossplay support (BROKEN as of Patch 8 Hotfix 36, every hotfix since patch 8 broke it more and more):[/size][/b]
[list]
[*]PLB standalone works with console players if the hosting player is on PC.
Only 3 console players are supported and they must join first because only PC players can join after 4 slots are already taken. (host + 3 console players)[*]Player 5+ can only join via lan mode or if the host opens port 23253 they can type the host IP in to the direct code box to join. For lan mode install something like hamachi and have the hosting players and player 5+ in the same hamachi network. The hosting player must also enable "lan connection" in options -> gameplay.
[*]To continue an existing crossplay save with more than 4 players you have to create a new fresh lobby have everyone join and start a new game. Once ingame you can load your old main save. This will create new saves so the hosting player should probably clear them out every now and then.
[*][b]Latest hotfix broke steam matchmaking for some users, workaround is to close steam and launch bg3.exe directly. This bypasses steam matchmaking.[/b]
[/list][b][size=3]Quick installation video guide[b][size=3] (Standalone version):[/size][/b]
[youtube]ueBgTiq8rFI[/youtube][/size][/b]

[size=3][b]Uninstall Standalone version:[/b][/size][list][*]Delete the Mods folder inside your Data folder, that's it.
[/list][b][size=3]Uninstall [b][size=3]Script Extender version[/size][/b]:[/size][/b]
[list][*]Use your mod manager to uninstall the mod.
[*]Enable your SE console either manually or in your mod manager.
[*]Load up your save and run the following commands in the SE console to reset your party limit:﻿
[*]Osi.SetMaxPartySizeOverride(4)
Osi.PROC_CheckPartyFull()[/list][b][size=3]Uninstall MP Patch:[/size][/b]
[list][*]Delete the patched exe file and restore your backup. The patcher creates a bg3.exe.backup (or bg3_dx11.exe.backup) file in your game folder, simply rename it back to bg3.exe. (or bg3_dx11.exe)
[/list]
Credits to [b]amlw [/b]from the BG3 discord for finding the hardcoded multiplayer bits and helping with it in general!

[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/users/2875770?tab=user+files]All Nexusmods[/url]

author: Sildur

name: Tutorial Chest Summoning
summary: Grants an ability allowing you to summon the tutorial potion chest. Useful for easy access to mod-added items.
description:
[i]Several mods add new items to the Cartilaginous Chest containing potions in the tutorial area. That's fine if you only need a single copy of an item at character start, but doesn't help when adding or updating a mod in an existing playthrough, or if you want multiple copies.

This mod adds an ability that summons a fresh copy of the tutorial chest, containing a new set of any items it's been modded to contain.[/i]


[size=3][b]How to Use[/b][/size]
[url=https://github.com/Norbyte/bg3se/releases]BG3 Script Extender[/url] is recommended but not required. When using Script Extender your characters will be granted the ability to summon the chest automatically on loading or starting a new game.

Otherwise [b][color=#00ffff]the summoning ability is granted when using the Hide action for the first time[/color][/b]. (Regular Hide, not Cunning Action: Hide or other variants.)

The ability will be placed in the Common section of the action bar. The icon looks like the chest.
If you've removed it from the bar, it can be found in the Class Actions section of the Common tab of the Spellbook window. Drag it from there to the action bar.

Cast the summoning ability to get a temporary chest added to your inventory.
[b][color=#00ffff]Enter turn-based mode to give yourself time to browse[/color][/b] - the chest and all its contents will be destroyed after 5 turns.
Any AP spent while the chest is summoned will be refunded, so you can try on or dye equipment without unpausing.

For mod installation instructions, see the [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Guide:Installing_Mods]wiki[/url]﻿.
Script Extender can be installed via [url=https://github.com/LaughingLeader/BG3ModManager/releases/]BG3 Mod Manager[/url], or by following the instructions on its [url=https://github.com/Norbyte/bg3se/releases]download page[/url].


[b][size=3]Optional Files[/size][/b]
There are 6 different versions of the mod.
The optional files are alternate versions to be used [i]instead of[/i] the main file, not at the same time. It's safe to switch to a different version at any point.

[b]Default[/b]: Grants the ability on Hide, or via Script Extender if installed.
[b]
No Hide[/b]: [color=#00ffff][b]Recommended for Script Extender users.[/b] [/color]Does not override Hide. Grants the ability via Script Extender if installed. Otherwise the only way to learn the ability is via a potion inside the chest or by first using the default version of the mod and then switching to No Hide.

[b]Scriptless[/b][b]:[/b] Grants the ability on Hide. Does not contain Script Extender support. Avoids Mod Manager warnings for users who can't or won't use Script Extender.

[b]Scriptless No Hide[/b]: Ability can only be gained via potion inside the chest or by first using another version of the mod and then switching to this one.

[b]Temporary[/b]: Temporarily grants the ability on Hide, and then removes it. Attempts to remove the ability via Script Extender if installed. This is the version to use if you just want to install, summon once, then uninstall.

[b]Uninstaller[/b]: Does not override Hide. Attempts to remove the ability - most reliably via Script Extender, but under normal circumstances should work without it too. Best version to use if you tried and failed to uninstall as it will avoid crashing, keeps trying to remove the ability so you may be able to uninstall later, and will not add the ability back when hiding.


[center]﻿[url=https://ko-fi.com/wesslen][img]https://i.imgur.com/QEzQ86X.png[/img][/url][/center]

[b][size=3]Compatibility[/size][/b]
The Hide ability is overridden to grant the summoning ability in the Default, Scriptless, and Temporary versions of the mod. If you are using another mod that also changes the Hide ability then the mod listed last (lower) in the load order will win.

If you are using Script Extender then the No Hide version of the mod is recommended and has no risk of conflicting with other mods.

If you are not using Script Extender but want to avoid risk of conflicts on Hide, you can use the Default version to give yourself the ability, save, then switch to the No Hide version.


[b][size=3]Uninstalling[/size][/b]
[color=#00ffff][b]Uninstalling mods can break your save!
[/b][/color]
If you insist on uninstalling anyway, the best way is to use the Uninstaller version of the mod. It should be enough to load your game with the Uninstaller mod active, then save the game.

If the Uninstaller doesn't help then instead use the Temporary version of the mod, load your save, Hide, and then stand up again to remove the summoning ability. [b]You must do this for all characters who ever learned the summoning ability, even if they are not currently in your party.[/b] When no character can summon, save the game and disable the mod. It [i]may[/i] now be safe to continue without the mod installed. [b]Absolutely no guarantees!

[/b]If you crash later, install the Uninstaller version to attempt to fix it (or it crashed for an unrelated reason.)


[b][size=3]Credits[/size][/b]
Thanks to [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/users/3493726]Yerbaster[/url] for contributing the Polish localization.
Thanks to [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/users/2356484]TheArmageddonJoker[/url] for contributing the German localization.
Thanks to [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/users/46779888]swissfred[/url] for pointing me in the right direction on hirelings.

Inspired by the summoning amulet added by [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/87]Basket Full of Equipment[/url]﻿.
Infinite AP inspired by [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/17974]Cost of Equipping and Dropping Items Rebalanced[/url].
author: wesslen

name: 5e Spells
summary: This mod adds a number of unimplemented spells into the game, some of which require Norbyte's Script Extender and can be partially configured to gain access to some spells rather than all at once.
description:
[center][/center][center][size=4]﻿﻿
[u]Update 2.2.0.0[/u][/size]

With this update, the mod has been incorporated into the [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/9162]Mod Configuration Menu (MCM)[/url]﻿ mod. Please refer to its page for instructions on how to navigate and use it for this mod's configurable features if you aren't already using the mod and the Configuration section to see the option. Additionally, with 2.4.0.0, the ritual spells implementation in the mod has been added as a configurable option in MCM.[/center][center][size=4]﻿[u]Overview[/u][/size][/center]
[center]This mod is one of the many that serve to add an additional selection of spells from 1st level spells up to 9th* level (including cantrips) for all classes and their spell list. Additional lists include the Spell Sniper and Ritual Caster spell lists for spells made in this mod. My purpose in creating this mod was to be implemented in a number of other mods (most  especially my own) to enable additional options, though it is entirely capable of being used alone for the 1st to 6th level spells for the base game. Some specifically require the script extender to work, which should be noted below in the list of spells but for those unable to use the script extender, there are the optional 5e Spells Spell List and 5e Spells Cantrip List mods.

Additionally of note, the spells that would serve as ritual spells added by this mod operate in a similar fashion by only those considered ritual casters, being Bard, Cleric, Druid, Wizard and Warlocks with the Book of Ancient Secrets invocation. Wizards in particular gain a separate ritual spell under the Common tab of the hotbar that allows them to cast any of the applicable ritual spells without needing to prepare them (includes Find Familiar as well for the time being). These work based off the DnD source material works rather than Larian's method for those using the script extender. There might be some hiccups that occur, however, so let me know if you run into issues with those ritual spells (Detect Magic, Unseen Servant, Water Walk and Commune With Nature).[/center]
[center]* To gain access to spells above 6th level, you must use a mod that includes the spell lists from this mod. Otherwise, they will be unavailable.[/center]
[u][size=4][center]Configuration[/center][/size][/u]
[center]While the mod is setup through MCM now, you can still opt to modify the .json that is created MCM at the file location: [u][u][/u]AppData\Local\Larian Studios\Baldur's Gate 3\Script Extender\BG3MCM\Profiles\Default\5eSpells.[/u] You can modify the file using any text editor like Notepad, Notepad++ or Visual Studio Code. In order for the options to actually take after you've selected them, you must restart the game. Below is a description of each file:

[u]Remove Spells[/u] - This section is separated into spells that originate from 5e Spells and from spells that were added to applicable class spell lists from the Tasha's Cauldron of Everything* 5e sourcebook. Each of deal with removing entire levels of spells.

Additionally, you can add exceptions for removal in the Spell Exemptions setting specifically for new spells added by this mod and isn't applicable to TCoE spells. Please refer to the Articles for a list of spell IDs that you can add as additional elements in MCM.

* These are a number of spells that were optionally opened up to other classes that were originally limited when the aforementioned book was released some time following Dungeon and Dragon's 5e edition first Player Handbook and the list of such can be found by googling a number of websites. [u]These are specifically limited to the base game spells.[/u] Additionally, enabling any of this section's 3rd, 5th, 7th or 9th level spells will result in every TCoE spell being removed from the Bard's Magical Secrets (same applies to the "5e" section for this mod's spells).

[u]Rules as Written (RAW) Adjustments[/u] - This section deals with modifying certain game mechanics to operate closer to how they do in DnD 5e tabletop.

[list]
[*]Spare the Dying. If enabled, the Help action should be modified so it requires a DC 10 Medicine check to successfully stabilize a downed creature (also without granting the 1 hit point).
[*] Raise Dead. If enabled, the Revivify spell and its respective scrolls should only be allowed to resurrect a creature that has been dead for 10 turns. The Gentle Repose spell can freeze the turns in place.
[*]Ritual Spells. I've separated the inherent changes for ritual spells in the base game to this setting (which I realized I never explained previously but oh well). When enabled, it adds a "RitualCasting" type of passive to the Artificer, Bard, Cleric, Druid, Warlock and Wizard classes. For the first four, while outside of combat, certain ritual spells will gain the "Ritual" green star-shaped resource similar to how it works in the base game and when entering combat, it gets removed. All non-ritual spells (based on DnD 5E) that also have that "resource" will be removed so that basically only the aforementioned classes will be able to cast ritual spells (as in, without expending a spell slot when using the base level of a spell while outside of combat). For Wizards and Warlocks (if they have Pact of the Tome and Book of Ancient Secrets), they gain a separate, ritual-specific version of a ritual spell they possess. This setting also removes the base game's version of Find Familiar and Speak with Animals from the Ritual Caster feat's spell list and replaces them with the aforementioned ritual-specific versions.
[/list][/center][center][u]Miscellaneous [/u]- This section deals with any other changes that don't quite fit the other categories.[/center][list]
[*]Booming Blade Change - If enabled, it will remove Larian's version of Booming Blade and replace it with his mod's version from before the former was added.
[/list]
[center][size=4][u]List of Commonly Asked for Mods[/u][/size][/center]
[list]
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/279]Expansion[/url] - Created by myself, contains level 13-20 progressions for all base game classes and some of their subclasses and includes the Wizard Bladesinger subclass. It is also configurable for some features.
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/377]Unlock Level Curve[/url] - Another level 13-20 mod﻿﻿.
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/1329]Zerd's Rules as Written (RAW)[/url] - This mod changes spells to be closer to DnD 5e and is one I've contributed most of the spell changes from the 5e Spells Adjust optional mod to. FYI, you can select which features of the mod to enable so if you don't want everything it provides, you don't have to.
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/473]Homebrew Spells[/url][s]﻿[/s] - Created by me, this mod adds additional spells not found in 5e at all as they are created by third party individuals.
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/5822]War Caster (2014 and UA2)[/url]﻿ - Useful for those wanting the feat to work with more than just Shocking Grasp. 
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/1590?tab=posts]5e Spells - Larian Durations[/url] - Adjusts durations for various spells to match that of base game spells.﻿
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/4426]5e Spells References: Valdacil's Spell Adjustments[/url]﻿ - A mod that makes quite a number of balance changes to spells in this mod and the base game.﻿﻿
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/2145?tab=posts]Secrets Scrolls for 5e Spells[/url] - Adds scrolls with spells contained in this mod.﻿
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/2641?tab=description]Actual Green Flame Blade[/url] - Name says it all.﻿ I've added a version of green to the cantrip but it offers alternatives.
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/9608]Spell List Sorter[/url]﻿ - Helps in arranging the spells in alphabetical order and by level if that's desired since my method of adding the spells through the script extender goes against it (and have no idea where to start even attempt that on my own).﻿
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/3719]Minthra's Configurable Enemies[/url] - Adds spells from this mod to enemies.﻿
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/5207]Combat Extender[/url]﻿ - Another mod that add spells from this mod to enemies (personally in a better way).
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/8692]Summon Initiative Fixer[/url]﻿ - If you would like to allow the applicable summons in this mod to share their turn with their summoner like they do in DnD 5e, this mod enables that. Applicable summons are Unseen Servant, Dust Devil, Healing Spirit, Summon Beast, Summon Shadowspawn, Summon Aberration and Summon Elemental.
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/13542]True Darkness[/url]﻿ - Implements the Darkness spell as it operates in DnD 5e.
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/14429?tab=description]Advanced Tabletop Spells[/url]﻿ - Implements additional 7th+ level spells.
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/13458]Summon Beast and Conjure Animals[/url]﻿ - Adds the two named spells. The former is also in this mod but only two creatures models for the animals that you can summon with the linked mod giving more options.
[/list]
[center][size=4][u]Miscellaneous Features and Changes[/u][/size][/center]
[center]﻿[/center][center]There are a number of additional changes and fixes along the lines of quality of life this mod offers which includes the following:[/center]
[list]
[*]Booming Blade and Green Flame Blade have been adjusted to account for the Damage Sources and Damage Riders mechanics of certain features and items in the game.
[*]Booming Blade and Green Flame Blade also grant Sneak Attack and Divine Strike variants to allow you to combine their use with interrupts that would otherwise conflict, such as Sneak Attack and Divine Strike. Will also introduce Smite as well at some point and potentially add even more combinations such as BB/GFB + SA + DS to be used with Smite or BB/GFB + DS + Smite to be used with Sneak Attack, etc.
[*]Added Toll the Dead to the Potent Spellcasting feature to add Wisdom modifier to its damage through script extender.
[*]Changed many of the base game conditions that check specifically for the Frightened condition to a general Frightened status group which would include things like the Feared effect, etc.
[/list][center][/center]

[center]With all of that said, the spells added in this mod are:[/center]

[center][u][size=3]﻿[/size][size=3][b]Cantrips[/b][/size][/u][/center]
[list]
[*]Booming Blade - Though added by the base game, this mod still maintains its original version that differs from Larian's in that it doesn't proc Extra Attack as it shouldn't per DnD 5e rules.
[*]Control Flames
[*]Create Bonfire
[*]Druidcraft - This cantrip can summon leaves to distract hostile enemies and remove/apply fire to items like torches.
[*]Frost Bite
[*]Green-Flame Blade - Finally has a green VFX thanks to [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/users/68892633]VladmirXII[/url]﻿. Check out a lot of his other awesome, VFX related mods!
[*]Gust - This cantrip can push creatures or objects and can create a gust of wind that briefly distracts some creatures in an area.
[*]Infestation
[*]Lightning Lure
[*]Magic Stone - Converted to Osiris from script extender.
[*]Mind Sliver - Credit to [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/users/77184368]havsglimt[/url]﻿ for the tooltip icon.
[*]Mold Earth - This cantrip creates mud surfaces or removes them.
[*]Prestidigitation - This cantrip can clean dirt, blood and sweat off creatures, can remove some surfaces and can remove/apply fire to items like torches.
[*]Primal Savagery
[*]Shape Water - This cantrip can freeze water surfaces, can create an ice block to stand on and can teleport a water surface from one location to another.
[*]Spare the Dying - This spell operates largely the same way the Help action does. Enable the Spare the Dying MCM configurable option for 5e Spells to configure the Help action to operate like it does in DnD, wherein it requires a DC 10 Medicine ability check, so it gives Spare the Dying a use case for those interested in such.
[*]Sword Burst
[*]Thunderclap

[/list]

name: Choose Your Camp
summary: Choose what camp you want to go to when hitting the Go To Camp button. Have you ever been in a certain area of an Act and wanted to go to a different camp? Now you can! *Limited to the camps available in the current Act.
description:
[size=5][b]Choose Your Camp[/b][/size]

Ever found yourself deep in the Underdark but longing for the open sky of the wilderness camp? Stuck at Moonrise Towers when you'd rather rest in the shadow-cursed wilds? Now you have the choice.

[b]Choose Your Camp[/b] adds a spell that lets you pick exactly which camp you want to travel to when you hit "Go To Camp". No more being forced into whatever camp the game decides for you.

[line]

[size=4][b]Features[/b][/size]

[list]
[*]Select your preferred camp from a simple spell menu
[*]Works with all camp variants across all three acts
[*]Persists until you change it or travel to a new act
[*]"Default Camp" option to return to normal game behavior at any time
[*]Spell automatically granted to all player characters
[/list]

[line]

[size=4][b]Available Camps[/b][/size]

[b]Act 1 — Wilderness[/b]
[list]
[*]Wilderness Camp (main outdoor camp)
[*]Basement Camp
[*]Granite Cave Camp
[*]Sandstone Cave Camp
[*]Abbey Ruins Camp
[*]Shar Temple Camp
[*]Underdark Camp
[/list]

[b]Act 2 — Shadow-Cursed Lands[/b]
[list]
[*]Mountain Pass Camp
[*]Shadow-Cursed Lands Camp
[*]Moonrise Towers Camp
[/list]

[b]Act 3 — Baldur's Gate[/b]
[list]
[*]Elfsong Tavern Camp
[*]Farm Camp
[/list]

[line]

[size=4][b]How To Use[/b][/size]

[*]Find the [b]"Camp Selection"[/b] spell (camp sack icon) on your hotbar
[*]Select your desired camp
[*]Hit "Go To Camp" as normal. You'll travel to your selected camp.

To return to default behavior, simply cast [b]"Default Camp"[/b] from the same menu.


[line]

[size=4][b]Compatibility[/b][/size]

[list]
[*][color=#00ff00]✓[/color] Compatible with most (if not all) other mods
[*][color=#00ff00]✓[/color] Safe to add mid-playthrough
[*][color=#00ff00]✓[/color] Works in multiplayer
[*][color=#ffff00]?[/color] May conflict with other mods that modify camp selection logic, although I'm not aware of any of those currently existing
[*][color=#00ff00]✓[/color] NO Script Extender required
[/list]

[line]

[size=4][b]FAQ[/b][/size]

[b]Q: Why can't I select certain camps?[/b]
A: Camps are restricted by act. You can only choose camps that exist in your current act. If you try to select an invalid camp, you'll get a message explaining it's not available.

[b]Q: What happens when I travel to a new act?[/b]
A: Your camp selection automatically clears if it's not valid in the new area. You'll return to default behavior until you make a new selection.

[b]Q: Does this affect companions at camp?[/b]
A: No. Your companions will be at whatever camp you travel to, just like normal.

[b]Q: I don't see the spell in my spellbook![/b]
A: Try saving and reloading. The spell is granted on level load, so a quick save/load should trigger it.

[b]Q: Are there any negative or side effects to going to the same camp throughout a whole act?[/b]
A: I honestly don't know. If you use this to intentionally avoid a place that has a cutscene or related quest, I'm not clear what effect that has. Feel free to test and report if you have a specific instance in mind.

[b]Q: Will this come to consoles?[/b]
A: Hopefully! It was made appropriately and I have submitted it to Larian. The rest is up to them.

[line]

[size=4][b]Known Issues[/b][/size]

[list]
[*]None currently. Please report any bugs in the comments or on my Discord.
[/list]
[line]

[url=https://discord.gg/AgsPzTwfjR]Visit my Discord![/url][/b][/size]
[line]

[url=https://ko-fi.com/for_kiramay]Buy me a coffee![/url][/b][/size]

author: For_Kiramay

name: masa's Pose Pack
summary: BT2 solo poses and BT2 x BT2 pair poses.
description:
[u]The English text was translated using a translation tool.[/u]
The original Japanese text follows the English text.

Holding hands, asking for kisses, being pampered like a pet...
Includes a variety of poses, from everyday scenes to more intense ones.

I made this for my Tav, but I thought I'd share it in case there are others who are also looking for BT2 x BT2 poses.
For pair poses, the two characters need to be perfectly positioned and facing the same direction. Use [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/14364]Lighty Lights[/url] to align the characters.
If searching in [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/17737]QSAT[/url], search for 'MSPO' after the list is updated.
The pair poses are designed with the assumption that Tav will use the one whose name ends in 'b'.

[u][b]All poses are designed to match my Tav, and the following issues arise with other characters.[/b][/u]
[list]
[*]Not suitable for body types other than BT2.
[*]Poses that touch the head may look unnatural depending on the race and head type.
[*]Poses created for use with nude or specific clothing may result in clipping in certain areas depending on the clothing being worn.
[/list]
This was created by a Japanese person who can barely read or write English. The pose names may be inappropriate or incorrect.
If the legs or lower body look unnatural, or if the pair pose is misaligned even after aligning the two characters with Lighty Lights, you can fix it by raising or lowering the character's Y-axis in the Photo Mode menu.
There are no plans to adjust this for other body types.

―――――――――――――――――――――――――――――

手をつないだり、キスを求めたり、ペットとして可愛がられたり…。
日常的なものから刺激的なものまでさまざまなポーズを収録。

私のTavのために作ったものですが、BT2×BT2ポーズを求めている人は他にもいるかもしれないので共有することにしました。
ペアポーズは2人の立ち位置と向きを完璧に同じにする必要があります。キャラの位置合わせには[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/14364]Lighty Lights[/url]を使ってください。
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/17737]QSAT[/url]で探す場合はリスト更新後『MSPO』で検索してください。
ペアポーズの名前末尾『b』の方をTavが使う想定で作成しています。

[u][b]ポーズはすべて私のTavに合わせて作られており、他のキャラクターでは以下の問題があります。[/b][/u]
[list]
[*]BT2以外のボディタイプには適していません。
[*]頭部に触れるポーズは、種族やヘッドの種類によっては不自然になります。
[*]裸や特定の衣装で使うことを前提として作成されたポーズは、着ている衣装によっては接触部位がめり込む場合があります。
[/list]
英語の読み書きがほぼできない日本人が作っています。ポーズ名が適切でなかったり、間違っている場合があるかもしれません。
足や下半身が不自然になっていたり、Lighty Lightsで2人の位置を揃えてもペアポーズがズレる場合は、フォトモードのメニューでキャラのY軸を上げ下げすると直ります。
他の体型向けに調整する予定はありません。

―――――――――――――――――――――――――――――

[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/7430]Dark Consort Fangs[/url] | [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/213]Tav's Hair Salon[/url] | [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/21610]ZillionZ of Expressions by Echo[/url]
