[/url][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/8301]﻿Precious Pet Collars[/url] | [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/14366]Affinity Rings[/url] | [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/14584]Mouse's Manacles[/url]
author: masa

name: Absolute Gear
summary: Adds gear themed around the absolute, filling gaps left by the default game set.
description:
[b][u][size=6](You may also like my other mod: [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/17215]Healer Gear[/url])[/size][/u][/b]

The Absolute Gear set is unfortunately one of the more stunted sets in the game. All of the Absolute Gear is found in Act 1 with the exception of [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Absolute%27s_Protector]Absolute's Protector[/url] which is found in Act 2.

This mod implements a few changes to the existing Absolute items and adds 14 new pieces of gear around the Absolute.

The vanilla changes are the following:

[url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Absolute%27s_Protector][img width=30,height=30]https://bg3.wiki/w/images/thumb/7/79/Safeguard_Shield_Unfaded_Icon.png/30px-Safeguard_Shield_Unfaded_Icon.png[/img][/url][url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Absolute%27s_Protector]Absolute's Protector[/url]:
- now reduces all damage by 1 (not only from spells) if the wearer bears the absolute's mark.

[url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Absolute%27s_Talisman][img width=30,height=30]https://bg3.wiki/w/images/thumb/d/d6/Absolute%27s_Talisman_Unfaded_Icon.png/30px-Absolute%27s_Talisman_Unfaded_Icon.png[/img][/url][url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Absolute%27s_Talisman]Absolute's Talisman[/url]:
- "[url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Absolute%27s_Talisman:_Aid]Absolute's Talisman: Aid[/url]" now works just as the normal Aid spell and is cast on the entire party as a level 2 spell. Cooldown remains the same (once per Long Rest).

[url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Absolute%27s_Warboard][img width=30,height=30]https://bg3.wiki/w/images/thumb/5/58/Absolute%27s_Warboard_Unfaded_Icon.png/30px-Absolute%27s_Warboard_Unfaded_Icon.png[/img][/url][url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Absolute%27s_Warboard]Absolute's Warboard[/url]:
- now also grants +1 Intimidation.

[url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Gloves_of_Power][img width=30,height=30]https://bg3.wiki/w/images/thumb/c/cb/Gloves_Leather_1_Unfaded_Icon.png/30px-Gloves_Leather_1_Unfaded_Icon.png[/img][/url][url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Gloves_of_Power]Gloves of Power[/url]:
- now unmistakingly states that the wearer needs to be branded for its effect to work. This was a little confusing in the vanilla game.

[url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Ring_of_Absolute_Force][img width=30,height=30]https://bg3.wiki/w/images/thumb/a/a3/Ring_of_Absolute_Force_Unfaded_Icon.png/30px-Ring_of_Absolute_Force_Unfaded_Icon.png[/img][/url][url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Ring_of_Absolute_Force]Ring of Absolute Force[/url]:
- "Thunderwave" is now cast as a level 2 spell.

[url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Vision_of_the_Absolute][img width=30,height=30]https://bg3.wiki/w/images/thumb/d/d3/Vision_of_the_Absolute_Unfaded_Icon.png/30px-Vision_of_the_Absolute_Unfaded_Icon.png[/img][/url][url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Vision_of_the_Absolute]Vision of the Absolute[/url]:
- added Weapon Enchantment +1.
- added "Absolute's Smite": This Weapon deals 1 Thunder damage when you successfully strike an enemy with it. Requires the wearer to carry the Absolute's Brand.


This mod adds 14 new items themed around the Absolute. These items can be found throughout the game.
The default Absolute items in the game give us the sense, that the set seems to focus on:
1. The Absolute's Brand as a requirement
2. Life & Death
3. Thunder damage
4. Melee weapons
5. Harmful effects
6. Damage bonuses
7. Damage reduction


[b][u][size=6]ACT 1:[/size][/u][/b]

5 out of the 6 Absolute gear items in the game are found in this act, yet there is still room to add some more here. This specifically done to fill empty gear slots that have no dedicated item.

[b]Absolute's Warpath (Uncommon | Boots):

[img]https://i.ibb.co/q3DhZJZV/Terroroftheabsolute.png[/img]
[/b]
- Terror of the Absolute: If the wielder bears the Absolute's Brand, hitting an enemy with a melee weapon Frightens them for 2 turns unless they succeed a Wisdom Saving Throw (DC12).
- Relentless March: You gain a +1 bonus to Initiative Rolls.

[i]* Lore: Her army strikes fear into the very ground it walks on.[/i]

Can be purchased from [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Roah_Moonglow]Roah Moonglow[/url].


[b]Eyes of Scrutiny (Uncommon | Light Armour | Helmet):
[img]https://i.ibb.co/Z6sjwDBc/Eyesof-Scrutiny.png[/img]
[/b]
- Perfect Scrutiny: Enemies with less than 50% of their hitpoints remaining in a radius of 6m around you receive "Absolute's Scrutiny".
[u][i]* Note: Entities with this status effect receive +1d4 more damage from melee attacks and sources of thunder damage from you and all allies within 6m.[/i][/u]

[i]* Lore: This helmet seeks out those who are too weak to stand in the light of the Absolute and marks them for death.[/i]

Can be purchased from [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Grat]Grat[/url].


[b][u][size=6]ACT 2:[/size][/u][/b]

6 new items are added in this act.

[b]Servant's Cloak (Rare | Cloak):

[img]https://i.ibb.co/LDQgfkjn/servantscloak.png[/img]
[/b]
- Wall of Faith: After you take damage, any other instances of damage are lowered by 1 until the start of your next turn.
- Mark of Faith: After you deal damage, the target has all following instances of damage they receive increased by 1 until the start of their next turn.
- Religion +1.

[i]* Lore: Putting this on gives you the pressing urge to serve and to shed blood in Her name.[/i]

Can be purchased from [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Talli]Quartermaster Talli[/url].


[b]Absolute's Fleshwall (Rare | Medium Armour | Body):
[img]https://i.ibb.co/jkcPB05j/absolutesfleshwall.png[/img]
[/b]
- Flesh for Flesh: Any damage you take or deal is increased by 1.
- Disadvantage on Stealth Checks.
- Grants the spell "Mass Bane" (Once per Long Rest): All enemies within 9m around you take 4d8 Necrotic damage and are [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Bane_(Condition)]Baned [/url]for 3 turns. If they succeed a Charisma Saving Throw, they take half damage.
[i][u]* Note: [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Bane_(Condition)]Bane [/url]is always applied, even if the Saving Throw succeeds![/u][/i]
- Grants Resistance to Thunder damage.

[i]* Lore: "Weaklings have no place at the Seat of the Absolute. Any who desire to prove themselves to the Absolute must be able to withstand and to punish equally."
- Disciple Z'rell[/i]

Can be purchased from [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Roah_Moonglow]Roah Moonglow[/url].


[b]Eternal Devotion (Rare | Ring):
[img]https://i.ibb.co/tTrjbYzs/eternaldevotion.png[/img][/b]
- Brutal Pleasure: When you hit an enemy with a melee weapon, you heal for 1d4 hit points.
- Quick Vengeance: When you are hit by an enemy with a melee weapon, they take 1d4 thunder damage.
- Religion +1.

[i]* Lore: This ring carries a fine, pink gemstone of highest quality in it. If you look at it for too long, the colour seems to shift to a deep blood red - and your thirst for destruction awakens.
[/i]
Can be purchased from [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Talli]Quartermaster Talli[/url].


[b]Fingers of Absolute Destruction (Rare | Gloves):
[img]https://i.ibb.co/XkvNjgyL/fingersofabsolutedestruction.png[/img][/b]
- Destructive Fingers: When you hit a target affected by [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Bane_(Condition)]Bane [/url]with a melee weapon, they take 1d4 thunder damage and the duration of [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Bane_(Condition)]Bane [/url]is extended by 1 turn.
- Melee Attack +1

[i]* Lore: These gloves have pushed an innumerable amount of weaker beings to the ground. As long as their wearer stands tall in the Absolute's light, these gloves will continue to subjugate the weak.[/i]

Can be purchased from [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Lann_Tarv]Lann Tarv[/url].


[b]Boots of Subjugation (Rare | Boots):
[img]https://i.ibb.co/Txgb29T6/bootsofsubjugation.png[/img]
[/b]
- Subjugation: Enemies within melee range of you have their Armour Class reduced by 1 and can't take reactions.
- Dexterity Saving Throws +2

[i]* Lore: Your presence strikes uncertainty into the very souls of your enemies. This is the power that the Absolute promises.[/i]

Can be purchased from [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Lann_Tarv]Lann Tarv[/url].

[b]Heart of the Absolute (Rare | Amulet):
[img]https://i.ibb.co/vxnpmYy0/heartoftheabsolute.png[/img]
[/b]
- Explosive Heart: After dealing thunder damage with a spell or after casting an illithid spell on an enemy, you trigger an explosion on all targets, dealing 1d6 thunder damage in a 3m radius.
[i]
* Lore: This necklace once belonged to a drow woman who was a devout follower of Lolth. It is unknown how and when this necklace fell into the hands of Absolute worshippers but once it changed hands, it was infused with Her thunderous fury. Now, it unleashes wrathful explosions on the enemeis of Her design.[/i]

Can be purchased from [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Roah_Moonglow]Roah Moonglow[/url].
[size=5][b][u]
[/u][/b][/size][size=6][b][u]ACT 3:[/u][/b][/size]

6 new items are added in this act.

[b]Glory of the Absolutely Faithful (Legendary | Greataxe):
[img]https://i.ibb.co/67mQfXrq/gloryoftheabsolutelyfaithful.png[/img]
[/b]
- Her Punishment: If you carry the Absolute's Brand, this weapon deals an additional 1d4 Thunder damage.
- Absolute Destruction: The number you need to roll a Critical Hit while attacking is reduced by 1. This effect can stack. When you critically hit an enemy, you deal an additional 1d4 Thunder damage.
- Hold the Weak: When you deal damage with this weapon and your target fails a Dexterity Saving Throw (DC 15), they are [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Constricted_(Condition)]Constricted[/url].
- Weapon Enchantment +2

[i]* Lore: The sharp edges of this axe can cut any enemy of the Absolute down with ease. As you grip the axe, your blood hunger awakens. The thunderous might of this weapon is enhanced for those that have been marked by Her.[/i]

Can be purchased from [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Entharl_Danthelon]Entharl Danthelon[/url].

[b]Absolute Devotion (Legendary | Heavy Armour| Body):

[img]https://i.ibb.co/Zpm9j73v/absolutedevotion.png[/img]
[/b]
- Her Hatred: Any incoming damage is lowered by 3. If you have the Absolute's Brand, attacker's take 2d6 thunder damage after hitting you.
- Truest Soul: You may toggle this feature on in order to cast Illithid spells without expending Actions or Bonus Actions. Whenever you cast an Illithid spell this way, you take 5d6 necrotic damage. If you are [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Awakened]Awakened [/url]from using a Zaith'isk, you take no damage. You can only use this feature once per turn.
- Disadvantage on Stealth Checks.
- Grants the spell "Absolute Resurrection" (Once per Long Rest): Resurrects a dead ally with full hit Points and restores their Action and Bonus Action.

[i]* Lore: This armor once belonged to an order of Paladins, worshipping a heretic god. When the Cult of the Absolute slaughtered the Paladins and plundered their remains, they stumbled upon this magnificent plate armor. Now, it lies desecrated and whichever god it was once devoted to is lost to time. It serves only the Absolute now.[/i]

Can be purchased from [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Mystic_Carrion]Mystic Carrion[/url].

[b]Grasp of the Absolute (Legendary | Medium Armor | Gloves):
[img]https://i.ibb.co/62yzpwr/graspoftheabsolute.png[/img][/b]
- Her Benevolence: You are proficient with all weapon types and any attack and damage rolls you make receive a +2 bonus. If you have the Absolute's Brand, the bonus increases to +4 instead.
- Decaying Touch: Melee attacks you make apply "Decay" to their targets for 1 turn. If you apply Decay to a target, that is already affected by it, they immediately take 2d4 necrotic damage. 
[i][u]* Note: Decay deals 2d4 necrotic damage to the affected entity when it expires.

[/u]* Lore: It is as if the Absolute Herself moves your hands inside these plate gloves. Every strike is willed by Her, any weapon practically floats in your hands and any aggression is blessed with success. You are one with Her.[/i]
[i]
[/i]Can be purchased from [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Exxvikyap]Exxvikyap[/url].

[b]Steps of Absolute Torment (Legendary | Medium Armor | Boots):
[img]https://i.ibb.co/4wXNkg7Z/stepsofabsolutetorment.png[/img]
[/b]
- Her Brutality: If you carry the Absolute's Brand, stepping into melee range of enemies applies "Slay the Weak" for 1 turn. This can only occur once per combat per enemy.
[i][u]* Note: "Slay the Weak" makes the affected entity vulnerable to Bludgeoning, Slashing and Piercing damage. This effect overwrites and is overwritten by the vanilla [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Bludgeon_the_Weak_(Condition)]Bludgeon the Weak[/url], [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Slash_the_Weak_(Condition)]Slash the Weak[/url] and [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Pierce_the_Weak_(Condition)]Pierce the Weak[/url] status effects. Contrary to these three effects, "Slay the Weak" is not immediately removed upon dealing damage.[/u][/i]
- Exploit the Weak: You ignore enemy resistances to Bludgeoning, Slashing and Piercing damage.
- Grants the spell "Swift Advance" (Once per Short Rest): Grants all allies in a 18m radius a 5m Movement Speed Bonus and the ability to
ignore Difficult Terrain. Lasts 3 turns.

[i]* Lore: Any steps taken with these boots seem to shake the very ground they walk on, like a multitude of explosions at once - or psyonic earthquakes from below.[/i]

Can be purchased from [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Sorcerous_Sundries]Sourcerous Sundries[/url].

[b]Absolute Adjudication (Legendary | Medium Armor | Helmet):

[/b][img]https://i.ibb.co/nqdDMDw0/absoluteadjudication.png[/img]

- Her Voice: If you have the Absolute's Brand, you gain [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Leadership_Aura_(Condition)]Leadership Aura[/url] at the start of Combat.
- Truest Strike: The first attack roll you make in a battle will always be a critical hit. You cannot be Blinded or Charmed.
- Wisdom Saving Throws +2.
- Deception +2

[i]* Lore: Putting this helmet on seems to make your tadpole quiver in fear. Whoever wore this before you, they must have had tremendous power.
[/i]
Can be purchased from [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Sorcerous_Sundries]Sourcerous Sundries[/url].

[b]Absolute's Fortress (Legendary | Shield):
[img]https://i.ibb.co/9k66bLPB/absolutesfortress.png[/img]
[/b]
- Her Bulwark: If you carry the Absolute's Brand, your Armour Class is increased by 2.
- Spell Resistance: You have Advantage on Saving Throws against spells.
- Shaky Ground: When an enemy hits you with a melee attack, they take 1d6 thunder damage and must succeed a Constitution Saving Throw (DC 15) or fall [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Prone_(Condition)]Prone[/url].
- Advantage on Strength Saving Throws Checks.
- Advantage on Constitution Saving Throws Checks.

[i]* Lore: Holding this shield feels as though the Absolute's watchful gaze intensifies on you. You can tell, that whoever magically imbued this shield, was a faithful follower of Her. Whatever happened to them, their magic is potent and easily further enhanced if a devotee of the Absolute carries it.[/i]

Can be purchased from [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Bareki]Bareki[/url].
author: Maxwell61

name: Containers Extended Compact for Japanese

description:
[size=6]Containers Extended - コンパクト[/size]

Containers Extended Modのより意見を主張した単一のMODファイルバリアントで、プレイヤーや仲間のインベントリにコンテナの自動ソートと積み重ね機能を追加します。コンテナはキャンプチェストの仕分け用にウィザーズから購入可能です。
パフォーマンス上の理由から、自動積み重ねはコンテナが開封された時のみトリガーされます。

ポーションや手榴弾を作るための材料を分類します。ゲーム内のコンテナはアイテムを自動積み重ねるよう強化されています。

author: Eola

name: Potent Sluts
summary:
This mods implements a unique "SLUT" class with 5 different subclasses for all the girl companions + player character.

Many new class actions for your dirtiest runs providing you a fresh combat expirience, while not being too harsh and hardcore.

The weirdest mod from a weirdest man on Earth(me)!
description:
BG3 provides a variety of different roleplay moments, but in NSFW part there is next to nothing except succubus mods.
One morning I tried to gather some mods for a new run in my favorite game, but noticed, that there are no features I was seeking. So instead I decided to make a mod myself. So, here we go:


[size=6][b][u][i]New update![/i][/u][/b][/size]
[size=3][i]It's mostly some optimizations but there's also some rebalances.[/i][/size]
[list]
[*]You can't target yourself with the spells, unless it's intended.
[*]Each pregnancy stage now takes﻿ a long rest instead of 10 turns.
[*]After some testing I forsake the idea of a "permanent" [b]Sanctuary[/b]. You can still have it but [b]Foreplay [/b]will be blocked during the combat. Now when you use any of slut spells, you will gain a 2 turn [b]Sanctuary[/b]. It will overwrite the permanent one, so you will have to use your skills at least every other turn to keep it.
[*]All sluts will receive a [b]Sanctuary [/b]after a long rest saving your time for precasting.
[*][b]Sharite Orgy[/b] won't restrain allies anymore. Worth mentioning, that with the addition of a [b]Sanctuary [/b]overwrite, you won't lose your protection after casting [b]Cervix Penetration[/b] or taking damage from [b]Reverberation[/b]. Previously, losing concentration made you too vulnerable, basically punishing for playing the subclass the intended way.
[*]Some other little bug fixes I can't remember.
[*]Compatibility with a [b]DLC [/b]I am planning on releasing.
[/list]


All the features revolve around a new class "Potent Slut", that provides a unique gameplay.

[b][i][size=5]Potent Slut general kit.[/size][/i][/b]
Level 1 features:

[b][i][size=5]Pregnancy:
[/size][/i][/b]To spice the things up I implemented "Pregnancy" in Baldur's Gate. It provides you additional Tadpoles and allows to increase some stats.

When a character succeeds in Cervix Penetration action, they receive status White Flood. It has Constitution DC of 1 and if you fail it, you will get pregnant.
You will face 3 stages, each lasting until the long rest, each providing various penalties.
If you don't end pregnancy with Illithid Smite, you will produce a tadpole, and get a number of statuses:
[list]
[*]Overjoy - 10 turns, advantage on checks and saves. Temporarily plus 4 Charisma
[*]Mind Break - 1 stack
[*]Number of Children - 1 stack. For each stack, increase Charisma by 1
[/list]
If you are using Avernus Dickgirl, then steps are a bit different. If you fail an athletics check, against a creature tagged "FEMALE", she can get pregnant by failing a Constitution DC of 5(10 with a level 9 passive). After that you experience a set of stages, similar to Cervix version. In the end you receive a tadpole, as well as:
[list]
[*]Reunion - 10 turns, advantage on checks and saves. Temporarily plus 2 Charisma
[*]Mind Break - 1 stack
[*]Number of Children - 1 stack. For each stack, increase Charisma by 1
[/list]

[i][b][size=4]Miscellaneous stuff:[/size][/b][/i]
[list]
[*]Becoming half-illithid applies one of a two special statuses, disadvantage on Constitution Saving throw and disadvantage on Strength checks
[*]Usually, when one of the Ability stats reaches 0, you die of atrophy. That was the reason behind the Mind Break status, but, as I tested, it works inconsistently
[*]All the icons are from the game, except Class icons, which was drawn by yours truly
[*]It is recommended to use some body altering modifications
[*][s]While working on this mod I realized, I either have dyslexia or I'm just bad at typing in English. Look out for typos. [/s]Though there may be typos in the mod itself still, the typos in this description were mostly fixed
[*]Don't be harsh, that's my first time modding any game :3
[/list]

[size=3]This is a gameplay mod. It does NOT provide any visual changes to the character models. The image in the heading here is just because it's pretty. 
[/size]Sindae's mods are a major part of the look - https://www.nexusmods.com/profile/Sindae/mods?gameId=3474. Check them out.
The other visual mods you can notice there are:
[size=3][list]
[*]https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/18745 - Shadowheart's face
[*]https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/4444 - Lae'zel's face
[*]https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/6971 - Karlach's private part
[*]https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/899 - To be able to re-sculpt companions
[/list]And couple of other mods I downloaded from in-game mod manager from the first pages[/size]

name: Doom plate armor (from Lineage 2)
summary: Add the Doom plate armor from Lineage 2 For BT1 & BT3
description:
[size=6][center][b][font=Lucida Sans Unicode]COMPATIBLE BODY[/font][/b][/center][/size][line]
[size=3][center]For now, only [b]body type 1 & 3[/b] work for :[/center][/size]
[size=3][center][b]Elf
Half-Elf
Drow
Human
Tiefling
Githyanki
Half-Orc
Dragonborn[/b][/center][/size]

[size=3][center]The 2.0 version of the mod is not compatible yet with all old body mod addons, but will be in the futur !

Compatible Body mods for 2.0 :

[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/16384?tab=description]Clawstep - Dragonborn body - Digitigrade legs[/url]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/20548]Clawstep - Dragonborn body - Breast CC Selector[/url]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/4864]SBBF[/url]

Compatible Body mods for 1.5 ONLY :

[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/2351]BCB[/url]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/14746]Remastered Dragonborn Body[/url]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/14757]Strongwomen Of Faerun[/url]

You'll find the 1.5 Addons in the "Old files" categorie, same for the old Main file

Note: Body 2, 4 and small races use vanilla Githyanki armor.[/center][/size]

[size=6][center][b][font=Lucida Sans Unicode]DOOM PLATE ARMOR SET[/font][/b][/center][/size][line]
[center][size=3]The armor exists in 5 versions, each including body armor, gloves, boots, Wings (Cloak) and a shield.
There is an additionnal clothing and underwear with the set.[/size][/center]

[center][size=4][b]Old Doom Set[/b][/size]
Found in the tutorial chest (in the Nautiloid). It’s a basic armor with very low stats and [b][u]cannot[/u][/b] be upgraded.

[size=4][b]Doom Set ([color=#6aa84f]Uncommon[/color])[/b][/size]
Can be purchased at the Druid Grove. It has improved stats and passive effects, and can be upgraded.

[size=4][b]Doom Set ([color=#3c78d8]Rare[/color])[/b][/size]
Can be purchased during Act 2 at the Last Light Inn. Better stats, passives, and still upgradable.

[size=4][b]Doom Set ([color=#9900ff]Very Rare[/color])[/b][/size]
Can be purchased during Act 3 from the kobold merchant at the circus. Even better stats and still upgradable.

[size=4][b]Ultimate Doom Set ([color=#ffd966]Legendary[/color])[/b][/size]
Cannot be purchased. It is only obtainable by upgrading a previous set.[/center]


[center][size=3]Note : For 1.5 users, you can remove or add the wings on the back using the “Doom Wings Toolkit”. You can buy it from a vendor or find one in the tutorial chest. In the 2.0, the wings are a separate item using the cloak slot.

The body armor grants “Heavy Armor Proficiency” and the shield grants the corresponding proficiency.[/size][/center]

[b][center][size=6][font=Lucida Sans Unicode]UPGRADING[/font][/size][/center][/b][line]
[center]Starting from the Doom Set (Uncommon), you can upgrade each piece of equipment using “Shillien’s Stones”, up to the Ultimate Doom Set!
The stones drop from various bosses (see the list below). You need one of the same color as the piece of equipment you want to upgrade.[/center]

[b][center][size=6][font=Lucida Sans Unicode]WHERE TO DROP ?[/font][/size][/center][/b][line]
[center]Act 1 : “Green” Shillien’s stone
[url=https://gamerant.com/bg3-how-beat-spider-matriarch-baldurs-gate-3/][b]Phase Spider Matriarch[/b][/url]
[url=https://gamerant.com/baldurs-gate-3-the-adamantine-forge-grym-boss-guide/][b]Grym[/b][/url]
[url=https://gamerant.com/baldurs-gate-3-how-to-beat-the-spectator/][b]Spectator[/b][/url]
[b]Bulette[/b]
[b]Lava elemental[/b]

Act 2 : “Blue” Shillien’s stone
[b][url=https://gamerant.com/baldurs-gate-3-gerringothe-thorm-boss-guide-bg3/]Gerringothe Thorm[/url][/b]
[b][url=https://gamerant.com/baldurs-gate-3-bg3-how-beat-malus-thorm/]Malus Thorm[/url][/b]
[b][url=https://gamerant.com/baldurs-gate-3-bg3-how-beat-thisobald-thorm/]Thisobald Thorm[/url][/b]
[b][url=https://gamerant.com/how-to-kill-balthazar-in-baldurs-gate-3/]Balthazar[/url] (Golem)[/b]
[b]Dark Shambling Mound[/b]

Act 3 : “Violet” Shillien’s stone
[b][url=https://gamerant.com/bg3-baldurs-gate-3-how-beat-steel-watch-titan-boss-guide/]Steel Watch Titan[/url][/b]
[b]Raphael[/b]
[b][url=https://gamerant.com/baldurs-gate-3-ansur-dragon-wyrmway-location-boss-guide-bg3/]Ansur[/url][/b]
[b][url=https://gamerant.com/how-to-defeat-sarevok-anchev-baldurs-gate-3-bg3/]Sarevok Anchev[/url][/b]
[b]Lorroakan[/b][/center]

[b][center][size=6][font=Lucida Sans Unicode]ADDONS[/font][/size][/center][/b][line]
[center]These add-ons are only compatible with the Main File 2.0.

[size=3][b]Tutorial chest Addon[/b]
For those who want everything from the beginning, you can download this Addon, it adds all armor sets + Cloths + Shillien’s Stones to the tutorial chest.

[b]Light Armor Addon[/b]
Switch the doom set from Heavy to Light ! the AC is reduced, but you get your dexterity modifier :)

[b]Cloth Armor Addon[/b]
Switch the doom set from Heavy to Cloth ! the AC is set to 10, but you get your dexterity modifier and a +1 to AC after upgrading it.

[b]No Heels Addon[/b]
Remove the heels from all armor and camp cloth !

[b]No Pants Addon[/b]
Remove the lower part of the armor (and camp cloth). If using the 'No Heels Addon', you must put the 'No Pants Addon' lower in your mod order ![/size][/center][size=3]

[b][center][size=6][font=Lucida Sans Unicode]OLD ADDONS[/font][/size][/center][/b][line]
[/size][center][size=3]These add-ons were for version 1.5 of the main file only; they can be found in the “Old Files” section of the downloads.
For now, all body add-ons are not supported in version 2.0, but I'm working on it.

[b]Camp cloth Armor Addon[/b]
Want a camp cloth version of the armor ? with this addon you can get it in the tutorial chest and at some vendors.
(Note: the regular armor and the basic cloths remains available in the game.)

[b]No panties Addon[/b]
This addon remove the integrated underwear of the Doom armor, allowing you to wear any other underwear you like !

[b]Beautiful Curvy Body (BCB) Addon[/b]
For [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/2351]BCB[/url] users.

[b]Stylized Beautiful Body for Female (SBBF) Addon (1.5)[/b]
For [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/4864]SBBF[/url] users.

[b]Clawstep - Dragonborn Body Addon[/b]
You'll need this if you're using [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/16384?tab=description]Clawstep - Dragonborn body - Digitigrade legs[/url]

[b]Clawstep - Dragonborn Body Addon (with breast)[/b]
To use with [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/20548]Clawstep - Dragonborn body - Breast CC Selector[/url] addon.

[b]Remastered Dragonborn Body Addon[/b]
For the [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/14746]Remastered Dragonborn Body[/url] User !

name: Divinity - Paths to Ascension
summary: Divinity adds a new Ascension Path selection framework, new spells and status effects, and path-specific progression hooks that grant and manage abilities cleanly during play. Expect new cantrips and leveled spells, custom combat behaviors, and ongoing support for mid-playthrough updates as additional paths and abilities are added.
description:
[quote]
[center]Tymora's Ascension Path is now complete. Helm Phase 2 is live. Expect frequent updates.[/center][/quote]

[center][size=6][b]D I V I N I T Y[/b][/size][/center]

[center][i]An Ascension Path Mod for Baldur's Gate 3[/i][/center]

[center]---[/center]

[center][i]The gods of Faerun have turned their gaze upon you.[/i][/center]

[center]You were not chosen at random. Something in the way you fight, the risks you take, the prayers you whisper between battles. It caught their attention. Now a covenant is offered. Accept it, and your journey through Baldur's Gate will never be the same.[/center]

[center]Divinity is a power fantasy progression mod built around [b]Ascension Paths[/b]: deity-driven covenants that rebuild your character from scratch. Choose a god. Receive their gifts. Ascend through six tiers of growing divine power, from blessed mortal to living demigod, as your patron's magic bleeds into your spells, your combat style, and the battlefield itself.[/center]

[center][b]This is not a subtle mod.[/b] This is what happens when a god decides you are worth investing in.[/center]

[center]---[/center]

[center][size=5][b]WHAT MAKES DIVINITY DIFFERENT[/b][/size][/center]

[center]---[/center]

[center]Each Ascension Path is a full progression arc built from scratch. You don't just get stronger spells. You get an identity. Each tier unlocks new abilities, upgrades old ones, and adds passives and synergies that make the path feel like a real covenant between you and your god.[/center]

[center][b]Tier 1 | THE CALLING[/b]
A cantrip and a core passive mark you as chosen. Your god's influence starts here, woven into every roll and every fight.[/center]

[center][b]Tier 2 | THE GIFT[/b]
Your first real spell arrives. The power is obvious now. Allies benefit. Enemies notice.[/center]

[center][b]Tier 3 | THE ARSENAL[/b]
A second spell, an upgraded kit, and an out-of-combat passive that extends your god's reach beyond the battlefield.[/center]

[center][b]Tier 4 | THE WEAPON[/b]
A third spell and a combat passive that changes how encounters play out. The battlefield starts bending to your will.[/center]

[center][b]Tier 5 | THE ULTIMATE[/b]
Your god's signature miracle. A once-per-rest ability that can end encounters, warp the battlefield, or just break the rules. This is the big one.[/center]

[center][b]Tier 6 | THE ASCENSION[/b]
Demigod. Permanent divine defenses, a transformative toggle that reshapes you into a living avatar of your god, and the knowledge that mortality is behind you.[/center]

[center]Progression runs from Level 1 to 20 with the Expansion module, or Level 1 to 12 with the Vanilla add-on. Both hit the same power ceiling.[/center]

[center]---[/center]

[center][size=5][b]MEET THE GODS[/b][/size][/center]

[center]---[/center]


[center][i][color=#e69138][size=6][b]Tymora - The Lady of Luck[/b][/size][/color][/i]
[b]COMPLETE - All Six Tiers Playable[/b][/center]

[center][img]https://i.imgur.com/L1zC1cM.png[/img][/center]

[center]The first complete Ascension Path belongs to [b]Tymora[/b], the Lady of Luck, goddess of fortune and the bold. Her path runs on chaos, chance, and the belief that fate rewards anyone reckless enough to grab it by the throat. All six tiers are fully implemented and playable from start to finish.[/center]

[center]Every ability in Tymora's kit rolls the dice differently. Some bless you. Some curse you. Some turn the entire battlefield into a carnival of impossible outcomes. Nothing is guaranteed. Everything is possible.[/center]

[quote]
[b]GLIMMERDART[/b] (Cantrip)
A volley of chaotic magic darts. Each dart hits with a randomly chosen damage type from all thirteen in the game. No two casts play the same way. Upgrades at higher tiers into a serious barrage, and every cast has a chance to trigger a Wild Magic Surge.

[b]FORTUNE'S FAVOR[/b] (Tier 2 Spell)
Bless an ally with Tymora's direct attention. The target heals each turn, gains refreshing temporary hit points, and receives one random boon per turn from a pool of twelve: combat advantage, bonus damage, elemental resistances, life steal, enhanced movement, and more. Gets stronger at higher tiers.

[b]GILDED PROVIDENCE[/b] (Tier 3 Spell)
A concentration aura that turns combat into a gold rush. Allies in range can generate gold for the party each turn, scaling with level. Enemies can be turned to solid gold for three turns. No saving throw.

[b]WEIGHTED DICE[/b] (Tier 4 Spell)
Self-cast. Tymora sizes up how badly things are going for you (your health, your downed allies) and responds accordingly. The worse your situation, the bigger her intervention. Good outcomes include Haste, full heals, and Death Ward. Bad outcomes include Bane, fear, and Tymora accidentally dropping the Carnival of Fortunes on you instead.

[b]CONTROLLED CHAOS[/b] (Tier 4 Passive)
A reactive interrupt that fires when nearby enemies cast spells, twisting their magic into one of thirty random chaotic effects: status afflictions, terrain hazards, and hostile creature spawns, including elemental myrmidons, dryads, devas, and cambions that fight everyone on the field.

[b]CARNIVAL OF FORTUNES[/b] (Tier 5 Ultimate)
The signature Tymora experience. A massive combat aura that hits every creature in range with a per-turn roulette of seventy-four random outcomes: buffs, debuffs, transformations, AoE spells erupting out of nowhere, item drops, Wild Magic surges, polymorph, and an independent 2% chance per turn of instant divine smiting. Once per Long Rest. Absolute chaos. Pure Tymora.

[b]ASCENDANT'S AEGIS[/b] (Tier 6 Passive)
The reward for reaching the end of Tymora's path. Permanent immunity to Poison, Stun, Sleep, Paralysis, Charm, Fear, and Petrification. Immunity to Acid, Cold, and Lightning damage. Resistance to Fire. +2 to Armor Class, Attack Rolls, Saving Throws, and Ability Checks. Always active. No toggle, no cost. You earned this.

[b]DIVINE MANIFESTATION[/b] (Tier 6 Toggle)
Activate your divine avatar state. While toggled on, you regenerate 4d10+20 HP each turn, cannot be killed (instant full restore at 0 HP), and project an aura that boosts nearby allies and weakens nearby enemies. Crystalline radiance, glowing eyes, and the casting presence of a demigod. Toggle it off when you want to walk among mortals again.

[b]SUPER LUCKY[/b] (Tier 1 Synergy)
Take the Tymora Ascension Path and the Lucky feat together, and Tymora takes personal notice. You get permanent advantage on all ability checks for free, two new triple-roll combat interrupts fueled by LuckPoints, and a once-per-rest death save that yanks you back from zero HP with invulnerability.

[b]CORE SCALING[/b]
A passive that grows every level, building up your LuckPoint pool as you progress. The backbone of any Tymora build.[/quote]


[center][size=6][b][i]Helm - The Vigilant Watcher, Unyielding Guardian of Duty and Protection[/i][/b][/size]
[b]PHASE 2 - NOW PLAYABLE[/b][/center]

[center][img]https://i.imgur.com/vPyKzA8.jpeg[/img][/center]

[center]The second Ascension Path belongs to [b]Helm[/b], the Vigilant One, god of guardians, protectors, and those who stand watch so others can sleep. Where Tymora thrived on chaos and chance, Helm rewards discipline, vigilance, and the willingness to stand between your allies and whatever is trying to kill them. His chosen become living shields.[/center]

[center]Phase 2 adds Watcher's Rebuke - Helm's AoE chain cantrip that binds enemies with divine chains, forcing them to fight on your terms or suffer the consequences. The remaining tiers are in active development.[/center]

[quote]
[b]WATCHER'S REBUKE[/b] (Tier 1 Cantrip - 6 Tiers)
An AoE shout that chains every enemy in range with divine tethers. Chained enemies have their movement halved, are compelled to attack you, and suffer disadvantage on attacks against anyone else. When the chains expire, they shatter for Force damage. Costs a Bonus Action with no cooldown. Enemies already chained are immune to new chains, so you can recast freely without disrupting active bindings. Scales across all six tiers: range grows from 6m to 30m, duration from 2 to 6 turns, and expiry damage from 1d8 to 12d8 Force. Visible chain beam tethers connect you to every bound enemy.

[b]GUARDIAN'S INTERPOSITION[/b] (Tier 2 Reaction)
When a nearby party member is about to take damage, you teleport to their side and shield them with Helm's divine ward. The protected ally becomes immune to all damage until your next turn. Each hit the ally absorbs costs you a percentage of your maximum hit points - The Watcher's Price. The cost decreases as your bond with Helm deepens, from 8% at Tier 2 down to 2% at Tier 5. At Tier 6, both you and the ally become Invulnerable at no cost. You also gain scaling Damage Reduction and bonus AC while the ward is active. One ally per round.

[b]ASCENDANT'S AEGIS[/b] (Tier 6 Passive)
Shared across all Ascension Paths. Permanent demigod-tier immunities, resistances, and stat bonuses. See Tymora section for details.

[b]DIVINE MANIFESTATION[/b] (Tier 6 Toggle)
Shared across all Ascension Paths. Divine avatar transformation with regeneration, death immunity, and ally/enemy aura. See Tymora section for details.

[b]PLANNED - COMING IN FUTURE UPDATES[/b]
- Vigilant Ward (Core Scaling) - a passive that grows every level, building up damage reduction as Helm's devotion deepens. The backbone of any Helm build.
- Enduring Presence (Tier 3 Passive) - Helm's vigilance extends beyond the battlefield. While exploring, a protective ward slowly builds temporary hit points on all nearby party members, scaling with tier.
- Praesidium Aeternum (Tier 3 Spell) - Eternal Fortress. A personal ward that grants Helm massive scaling temporary hit points renewed each turn, plus reflects melee and ranged damage back at attackers.
- Shield of the Faithful (Tier 4 Spell) - a protective ward placed on an ally that grants partial resistance to all damage types, temporary hit points, and healing each turn.
- Vigila et Protege in Aeternum (Tier 5 Ultimate) - Watch and Protect for Eternity. A divine combat sphere that grants invulnerability while preserving full ability to attack. Helm's signature miracle.[/quote]


[center][color=#38761d][size=6][b][i]Silvanus - The Treefather[/i][/b][/size][/color]
[b]SCHEDULED[/b][/center]

[center][img]https://i.imgur.com/cBIxqXj.jpeg[/img][/center]

[quote]Scheduled[/quote]


[center][color=#ffd966][size=6][b][i]Corellon - Maker of Songs[/i][/b][/size][/color]
[b]SCHEDULED[/b][/center]

[center][img]https://i.imgur.com/vPlFVq7.png[/img][/center]

[quote]Scheduled[/quote]


[center]---[/center]

[center][size=5][b]DEITY SELECTION[/b][/size][/center]

[center]---[/center]

[center]After taking the Path to Ascension feat, a deity selection spell appears on your hotbar. Cast it to choose your divine patron from the available paths. Each deity can only be claimed by one party member per playthrough. Respeccing frees your choice so another character can claim it.[/center]

[center]---[/center]

[center][size=5][b]REQUIREMENTS[/b][/size][/center]

[center]---[/center]

[center][b]!! IMPORTANT - READ BEFORE INSTALLING !![/b][/center]

[center]Divinity requires [b]TWO[/b] external dependencies for full functionality:[/center]

[center][b]1) [url=https://github.com/Norbyte/bg3se/releases]NORBYTE'S SCRIPT EXTENDER[/url][/b] (Required)[/center]
[center]Needed for creature spawning, Lua combat systems, diagnostic logging, and several core features. Without it, Controlled Chaos, Super Lucky, Guardian's Interposition, Watcher's Rebuke expiry damage, and Glimmerdart's damage dispatch will not work.[/center]

[center][b]2) [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/279?tab=description]EXPANSION: LEVEL 13-20 (CONFIGURABLE)[/url][/b] (Required unless using Vanilla add-on)[/center]
[center]Divinity's default progression goes from Level 1 to 20. You need this mod to reach the full six-tier arc. Without it, progression stops at the base game's level cap.[/center]

[center][b]VANILLA ALTERNATIVE:[/b] If you want to stay within the base game's Level 1-12 range, grab the Divinity Vanilla Level Scaling add-on instead. It compresses all six tiers into 1-12 and hits the same final power level. The Expansion mod is NOT needed if you use the Vanilla add-on.[/center]

[center]---[/center]

[center][size=5][b]UPDATING MID-PLAYTHROUGH[/b][/size][/center]

[center]---[/center]

[center]Divinity is built to be mid-save friendly.[/center]

[center]1) Replace the old .pak with the new version.
2) Load your save.
3) If spells or passives are missing, respec the character, long rest and pick Path to Ascension again.
4) If something still seems off, report bugs here.[/center]

[center]---[/center]

[center][size=5][b]COMPATIBILITY[/b][/size][/center]

[center]---[/center]

[center]Divinity should work fine alongside most mods. Potential conflicts with mods that heavily change the feat UI, feat tables, or Wild Magic surge behavior. If you run multiple feat-adding mods, load order may matter.[/center]

[center]Visual effects across all paths are an ongoing work in progress. Expect continued polish and updates to spell FX, auras, and casting visuals in future patches.[/center]

[center]---[/center]

[center][size=5][b]DISCLAIMER[/b][/size][/center]

[center]---[/center]

[center]Divinity is a fan-made mod. Baldur's Gate 3 is developed and published by Larian Studios. Forgotten Realms and Dungeons & Dragons are owned by Wizards of the Coast. This mod is not affiliated with or endorsed by either.[/center]

[center]Nexus Mods Page:
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/21422?tab=description]https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/21422?tab=description[/url][/center]

For my final treat, I offer you some alternative designs to replace the in-game clown face paint that you get in Act 3's Circus.

This was an incredible amount of fun, and I love the results. I'd love to do more, but I could only handle 6 for the time being. I'll update with some new designs!

Some of these were quite tricky, such as the 'mask' design. I hope you like these as much as I do.

Please have fun and stay safe this Halloween!

[u]included in this mod:[/u]

[b]VANILLA REPLACERS
[/b]Download the 'Vanilla Replacers' folder
Choose the design you like - use the mod images to find the right name
Copy the 'Generated' folder
Paste it into your Data folder

OR

[b]UNIQUE TAV FILES
[/b]Download the 'Unique Tav Files' folder
Choose the design you like - use the mod images to find the right name
Copy the 'Generated' folder
Paste it into your Data folder

Apply face paint in game and enjoy!

[ you can use EasyCheat to spawn the face paint item before reaching Act 3 ]

[b]FACE PAINT FILES[/b]
the alphas for each design, so that you can add them to your own atlas.

[b]HOW TO USE THE ALPHAS:
[/b]
Each design is in its own folder.
Ex. The first design is M30_CLOWN_01

Folders contain 3 images - these are the separated channels for the face paint.
Ex. Clown01_BLUE, Clown01_GREEN, Clown01_RED
The colors at the end are the channels that they go into.
Ex. Clown01_BLUE should be in the blue channel of your atlas.
Simply replace the vanilla clown face paint channels with your preferred design!

- a more comprehensive guide is coming soon -
