author: mouch30

name: Guilt-Free Slayer Form - BETA
summary: Claim the Slayer Form without murdering anyone.
description:
[i][i]Note: this description contains spoilers for The Dark Urge's storyline in both Act 2 and 3.
[/i][/i][i][i]
[/i][/i][size=3][b]Why is this in Beta?[/b][/size]
[size=3][list]
[*]The new dialogue lines, etc could possibly be improved.
[*]The changes to the flow of the cutscene are janky in places.
[*]I don't know if I've made this too difficult or not. Maybe it's just right?
[*]There might be bugs lurking somewhere.
[*]I'm looking for feedback on what works and what doesn't.
[/list][/size][i][i]

[center]﻿[i][i][img]https://i.ibb.co/Y4x3tFVG/overview.png[/img][/i][/i][/center]﻿
[/i][/i][size=3][b][img]https://bg3.wiki/w/images/thumb/5/51/Slayer_Form_Condition_Icon.webp/30px-Slayer_Form_Condition_Icon.webp.png[/img] Deceive Sceleritas Fel and gain the [url=https://bg3.wiki/wiki/Slayer]Slayer form[/url] without killing Isobel or your lover/favourite companion. [img]https://bg3.wiki/w/images/thumb/5/51/Slayer_Form_Condition_Icon.webp/30px-Slayer_Form_Condition_Icon.webp.png[/img][/b]

[b][img]https://bg3.wiki/w/images/thumb/c/c5/Slay_Icon.webp/25px-Slay_Icon.webp.png[/img] Before:[/b]
The Dark Urge can only gain the Slayer form in Act 2 by either:[/size][list]
[*][size=3]Killing Isobel or allowing her to die. [/size]
[*][size=3]Killing their lover or the companion with the highest approval.[/size]
[/list][size=3][size=3]If you don't kill Isobel after first meeting her, [size=3][size=3]Sceleritas Fel will visit your camp[/size][/size] and will compel you to kill your companion. You will need to pass a saving throw to resist the urge to kill them.[/size]

[b][img]https://bg3.wiki/w/images/thumb/6/68/Sumptuous_Blood_Bath_Icon.webp/25px-Sumptuous_Blood_Bath_Icon.webp.png[/img]﻿ After:[/b]
This mod allows the Dark Urge to deceive Sceleritas Fel into thinking they have killed their favourite person. To succeed you will need to choose the correct dialogue options, and pass two additional skill checks. Fel will then grant you the Slayer Form.
[size=3]
[b][img]https://bg3.wiki/w/images/thumb/3/37/Relentless_Lunge_Icon.webp/25px-Relentless_Lunge_Icon.webp.png[/img]﻿ How Does This Work?:[/b]
If you meet Isobel for the first time and do [b]not[/b] kill her, Sceleritas Fel will visit your camp that night. You will then have the opportunity to deceive him and convince your favourite person to help stage their own death.
This won’t be easy. You must [size=3][size=3]select [b]four specific dialogue options[/b][/size][/size], two of which require successful skill checks. Failure at any point will break the deception: Fel may see through the lie, or your companion may actually die in the process.[size=3]
[/size][/size]
[/size][i][i][img width=25,height=25]https://bg3.wiki/w/images/thumb/8/8c/Portrait_The_Dark_Urge.png/25px-Portrait_The_Dark_Urge.png[/img] [size=3] Please note: I’ve tried to make this as immersive as possible by having the entire sequence play out in a cutscene. It’s not
perfect, far from it. But I think it’s... [i]good enough[/i]. You will have to suspend a bit of disbelief.
I didn't want to make any changes to the vanilla flow of the dialogue/cutscene. This allows anyone who would still like to choose to kill their companion to do so.

[/size][center][img]https://i.ibb.co/V02JkvQj/about.png[/img]﻿﻿[/center]

[/i][/i][size=3][size=3][b]﻿ [img]https://bg3.wiki/w/images/thumb/d/de/Piercing_Growl_Icon.webp/25px-Piercing_Growl_Icon.webp.png[/img] The Details:
[/b][/size]To successfully deceive Sceleritas Fel, you’ll need to choose four specific dialogue options:

[/size][list=1]
[*][size=3]When Fel says [i]“You could kill this one deliberately…”[/i][i] [/i]choose: [b][i]"[Lie] Consider it done.[/i]"[/b][/size]
[center][img]https://i.ibb.co/PfzBgZM/D1-small.png[/img]﻿﻿[/center]

[*][size=3]After waking your companion, they will ask what’s wrong. Choose: [i][b]"You are in a lot of danger. We have only moments to pull this off."[/b][/i] Then succeed the active roll. Failure here will result in your companion’s death.[/size]
[center][img]https://i.ibb.co/WN3FZjMh/D2-small.png[/img]﻿﻿[/center]

[*][size=3]When the narrator says [i]“As you tell your story, fatigue fills your body…”[/i] choose:[/size]
[size=3][b]“I’m going to kill the person I care most about: you. Instead we will fake it. We need to move quickly if we’re going to make this look convincing.”[/b][i][/i][/size]
[center][img]https://i.ibb.co/XZVmtw8J/D3-small.png[/img]﻿﻿[/center]

[*][size=3]After your companion ties you up, the narrator says [i]“You are not yourself…”[/i] When your companion tries to reassure you, choose the saving throw: [/size][size=3][b]Explain your plan to deceive the Butler.[/b][/size]
[center][img]https://i.ibb.co/qYSK20xd/D4-small.png[/img]﻿﻿[/center]
[/list][size=3]Once all four steps are completed, a cutscene will play where it appears you kill your companion. [size=3]Do not be alarmed, this is part of the deception. [/size]
[center][img]https://i.ibb.co/Kcsdt5zM/Success-thumb.png[/img]﻿﻿[/center]
The next morning, they will be alive, and a journal update will confirm your success.
[center][img]https://i.ibb.co/pBsy3htt/Journal-thumb.png[/img]﻿﻿[/center]

Sceleritas Fel will visit again the following night. Believing the deed is done, he will grant you the Slayer Form.

[/size][i][i] [i][i][img width=25,height=25]https://bg3.wiki/w/images/thumb/8/8c/Portrait_The_Dark_Urge.png/25px-Portrait_The_Dark_Urge.png[/img][/i][/i]  [size=3]Note: Once you have convinced your companion to trust you and proceed with the deception, the scene will play out much the same as if you really did kill them. [i][i][size=3]Suspension of disbelief will be needed here.[/size][/i][/i][/size]
[/i][/i][i][i]

[size=3][size=3][b]﻿ [img]https://bg3.wiki/w/images/thumb/b/b5/Dismiss_Slayer_Icon.webp/25px-Dismiss_Slayer_Icon.webp.png[/img] [/b][/size][/size][/i][/i][size=3][size=3][b]Other Changes[/b][/size][/size][size=3][size=3][b]:[/b][/size][/size][i][i]
[/i][/i][list]
[*][size=3]The morning after the deception scene, Dark Urge and their companion will both be covered in blood.[/size]
[*][size=3]A new journal quest update has been added to the Dark Urge's personal quest.[/size]
[*][size=3]A line has been changed in the scene where Fel visits the following night. [i]Worry - was he fooled by your deception[/i][/size]
[/list][i][i]
[b]⚠️[size=3]Known Issues[/size][/b][/i][/i][list]
[*][size=3]The scene transition from being tied-up to then proceeding to kill (pretend) your companion is quite jarring. A possible fix would be to re-edit the cutscene entirely, but this would make the vanilla scene no longer available.[/size]
[/list][i][i]

[center][img]https://i.ibb.co/RTCMFMXH/compatibility.png[/img]﻿[/center]

[/i][/i]Not compatible with any mods that override the following files.
CAMP_DarkUrge_SparedIsobel_SD.lsj
CAMP_DarkUrge_KilledIsobel_SD.lsj
[i][i][center][img]https://i.ibb.co/S4nYVw2p/faq.png[/img]﻿﻿[/center]
[/i][/i][size=2]Do I need to start a new game?
[spoiler]No. But this needs to be installed before Sceleritas Fel visits you in camp after you've met Isobel.[/spoiler]
Can this be removed mid-playthrough?
[spoiler]Yes, this can be removed at any time.[/spoiler]
Can I still kill Isobel or my companion and get the Slayer form that way?
[size=2][spoiler]Yes, you can. The vanilla methods should still work. Please let me know if anything doesn't.[/spoiler]
Why would I want to be the Slayer, but not murder anyone for it?
[size=2][size=2][spoiler]I think having the Slayer form makes a resisting Durge story more dramatic, but I don't want characters I like to have to die. As a bonus, this will prevent Orin from becoming the Slayer.[size=2][size=2][/spoiler][/size][/size][/size][/size]
[/size]Well ackshually...
[size=2][size=2][spoiler]No.[/spoiler][/size][/size]
[/size]
[b]Mods used in screenshots:[/b]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/2235]Shadowheart Hair Options[/url]﻿ - Feywild Trickster
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/3608]More Prominent Scars for Shadowheart[/url] - Freckles and All Scars - Mixed
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/15421]Disguised Host
[/url][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/2413]Pretty Font Project[/url]﻿ - Google Sans

[b]Resources Used[/b][list]
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/12308]Moonglasses[/url] for dialogue editing.
[/list][b]Thank You To[/b][list]
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/12308][/url][url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/astralities]astralities[/url] for providing the CMTY Mod Page Assets.
[/list][b]My Other Story/Gameplay Mods:
[/b][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/18685]A Beautiful Webbing - Minthara's Romance Scene Unlocked[/url]﻿﻿﻿ - Experience an alternate version of Minthara's Romance scene.[b]
[/b][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/17344]Priestess Gut Gonna Knock You Out[/url] - Priestess Gut will knock elves/drow out when her sleeping potion doesn't work.
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/17485]Stomp that Tadpole[/url]﻿ - Allows you to stomp on the Astral-Touched Tadpole — even if you've already consumed tadpoles.
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/18079]Aylin Always Summons Moonlight Slivers[/url]﻿ - Aylin will always summon the Moonlight Slivers in the final confrontation with Lorroakan.
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/18227]Won't somebody think of the goblin children?[/url]﻿ - Goblin children are treated the same as tiefling children.
author: whatsontheflip

name: Star Citizen 4.1 Xbox GamePad Keybindings
summary: Star Citizen 4.1 Xbox GamePad Keybindings
description:
[list=1]
[*]Here are the keybinds I have been working on for Xbox gamepad and a searchable word doc as a reference, also there are now available quick reference guide images created by Syldoc.
[*]I have tried to ensure the controls are intuitive and easy to use, and that as many things are covered as i can, to hopefully allow you to have an enjoyable experience in Star Citizen with a regular Xbox Controller, no Elite Controller or 3rd party programs required.
[*]Please note that due to various bugs in the current version of the game, making selections in some screens is unreliable with a GamePad, so you may also want to use a mouse for these specific menus for now.
[*]If you are wanting to try out this keybind file for yourself, extract then move the Star Citizen gamepad mappings .xml file to your Mappings folder for Star Citizen, normally will be [C:\Program Files\Roberts Space Industries\StarCitizen\LIVE\USER\Client\0\Controls\Mappings]. Next import the gamepad mappings to Star Citizen in game via Advanced Controls Customization, Control Profiles.
[*]There are some quick reference guide images created by Syldoc for visual reference. I also suggest downloading the optional word document, which is a searchable reference of all the controls that I have currently mapped.
[*]This is a constant work in progress, and I will update as time goes on.
[*]I would appreciate any feedback and suggestions!
[*]Here is my referral code for new players: STAR-XHYR-7ZHS
[*]Credit goes to Syldoc for the visual reference guide images - Thank you o7

[/list]
author: BluWolve

name: PS5 Controller Touchpad as Mouse - Star Citizen 4.1.1
summary: Star Citizen gamepad bindings for the DualSense Playstation 5 Controller using the touchpad as a mouse.
description:
Star Citizen gamepad bindings for the DualSense Playstation 5 Controller (in Xbox compatibility mode) using the touchpad as a mouse with the DS4Windows software ([url=https://ds4-windows.com/]https://ds4-windows.com/[/url]).

The controller bindings should allow for everything needed during general gameplay in vehicles and on foot. Use the touchpad for the mobiGlas, personal inventory (this takes some getting used to), elevator buttons and ship MFDs. To improve dragging items in the inventory, you can also hold the mute button on the controller as a left mouse click. All standard controller buttons are bound directly in the Star Citizen settings as a gamepad and can be modified there. The touchpad, gyro, home button and mute button are bound via DS4Windows as keyboard and mouse controls and can be modified there. No keyboard or mouse controls were modified in game.

Notes:
- Most buttons are bound to multiple functions using the following modifiers: single tap, double tap, hold, and each with L1 modifier.
- The mute button is bound to left mouse click. With the L1 (left bumper) modifier, it is bound to right mouse click. Useful for inventory management.
- Interaction mode has a number of things to remember: activate primary action (R1), open interaction wheel (L1+R1) and activate from interaction wheel (A).
- I suggest setting interaction mode to toggle in the gameplay settings rather than hold.
- There is no way to rotate objects with the tractor beam, which significantly hurts cargo gameplay. [url=https://issue-council.robertsspaceindustries.com/projects/STAR-CITIZEN/issues/STARC-111574]https://issue-council.robertsspaceindustries.com/projects/STAR-CITIZEN/issues/STARC-111574[/url]
- Jumping can cause persistent vibration. Turn off vibration in the Star Citizen settings.
- Vehicle freelook can be done both by holding the keybind or by toggling it.
- The in-game mappings image only shows the default, so use my provided images.
- If you make changes to the bindings, you can update your diagrams on [url=https://draw.io/]draw.io[/url] using the source files included in the zip archive.

Things I wish CIG would improve:
- Some keybinds are just missing in the settings.
- "Hold" should be toggleable just like "double tap" in the keybind settings.
- Blocking between modifiers should be enabled by default, e.g. L1+tap should not also activate simple tap. Currently inconsistent.
- Warning message when assigning a key that is already assigned should include all modifiers for the existing keybind. If it is bound with double tap but not tap, display that there for convenience.
- Keybinds should be more contextual, e.g. I do not need to call ATC or lower landing gear while in mining mode. This would free bindings up a lot.

INSTALLATION (see video below)
- Install DS4Windows ([url=https://ds4-windows.com/]https://ds4-windows.com/[/url]) to run the PS5 controller in Xbox controller emulation mode on Windows. DS4Windows suggests to install HidHide in Step 4 and FakerInput in Step 5, which might be necessary to avoid problems with Star Citizen Anti-Cheat.
- Extract the two .xml files from the [font=Courier New]star_citizen_dualsense_ps5_controller.zip[/font] file available on this mod page.
- Move the DS4Windows .xml file to the profiles folder [font=Courier New][C:\Users\username\AppData\Roaming\DS4Windows\Profiles\][/font].
- Import the DS4Windows profile to the software and assign it to the PS5 controller. For this go to the tab "Profiles", click "Import" and select the .xml file. Then go to the tab "Controllers" and assign the profile to your controller. The LED light on the controller should turn orange and move towards red with low battery.
- Move the Star Citizen gamepad mappings [font=Courier New]layout_ps5_exported.xml[/font] file to [font=Courier New][C:\Program Files\Roberts Space Industries\StarCitizen\LIVE\USER\Client\0\Controls\Mappings\][/font].
- Import the gamepad mappings to Star Citizen in the game menu, under Advanced Controls Customization. Replace all entries for gamepad (and joystick to clear bindings that might interfere). It might say N/A for gamepad but select this anyways.
- If the light bars of the controller are not orange in-game you will need to follow the troubleshooting steps below to set up HidHide.

[youtube]fC5NUMcio7Q[/youtube]


TROUBLESHOOTING (see also image)
- Red flashing lights mean the Bluetooth latency is high.
- If the controller does not have orange light bars when in-game (e.g. blue or white) it means the DS4Windows profile is not active.
- The Anti-Cheat software now used in Star Citizen means the DualSense wireless controller needs to be hidden using HidHide, in order for the emulated XBOX controller to work.
- This is the cause for [b]missing L1 modifier functionality[/b] (Star Citizen doesn't seem to support that for directly connected Playstation controllers).
- Getting the correct HidHide settings seems to be dependent on the order of applying them. Try the steps below.
- In DS4Windows -> Settings -> Controller/Driver Setup make sure to [b]install HidHide and FakerInput[/b] (to deal with Star Citizen Anti-Cheat). Restart the computer as prompted.
- Open the Windows "Set up USB game controllers" panel (search for gamepad) to confirm which controllers are visible to the system when applying the below settings.
- Make sure DS4Windows is running (should say Stop on the bottom right button) and the controller is connected (blue light bars).
- In DS4Windows -> Settings untick the option "[b]Hide DS4 Controller[/b]".
- In DS4Windows -> Settings -> HidHide Configuration Client -> Devices you should see both the XBOX 360 and the DualSense controller. [b]Check the box next to the DualSense controller[/b] and the box at the bottom to "[b]Enable device hiding[/b]". Close the HidHide window.
- The Windows Game Controllers window (opened above) should now only show the XBOX controller.



The Aegis Inquisitor in the banner image and on my desktop is a ship concept I created in Unreal Engine!
[url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YPIgMyvQmFQ]https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YPIgMyvQmFQ
[/url][url=https://robertsspaceindustries.com/community-hub/post/aegis-inquisitor-made-by-the-community-EI3IqbESbaKp4]https://robertsspaceindustries.com/community-hub/post/aegis-inquisitor-made-by-the-community-EI3IqbESbaKp4[/url]


CHANGELOG

4.1.1
To accommodate vehicles with many operator modes, I removed all specific mode bindings and replaced it with cycle modes.
- Vehicles: Bound cycle operator mode to X (because e.g. the Golem has both missiles and mining)
- Vehicles: Unbound all direct operator mode bindings (scanning, missiles, salvage, mining)
- Vehicles: Unbound pin target from digi-left and unpin target from digi-left hold
- Vehicles: Bound cycle sub-target to digi-left and reset to main target to L1+digi-left
- Vehicles: Set jump drive request to R1
- Vehicles: Diagram removed Salvage Mode Left Module from X (left by mistake)
- Vehicles: Diagram removed speed limiter on L1+digi-up [2] (left by mistake)
- Vehicles: Rebind lights from L1+X [2] to L1+digi-up [2] (to align with change in On-Foot mappings)
- On Foot: Rebind flashlight from L1+X [2] to L1+digi-up [2] (to make space for Secondary Reload)
- On Foot: Rebind underbarrel action from L1+digi-up [2] to L1+digi-left [2] (to make space for flashlight)
- On Foot: Rebind Ammo Repool from L1+X to L1+X [2]
- On Foot: Bind Secondary Reload on L1+X (e.g. empty/full salvage canister; you need to hit mute/trigger once or L1+mute to switch between salvage and repair mode respectively -see icon on tool display for current mode- and then use secondary reload to switch between empty and full canisters; still a tricky implementation)
- On Foot: Bind ADS hold breath on left stick button
- On Foot: Remove rotate tractor on home button (locked the mouse in place until toggled again, also L1 modifier reduces tractor distance)
- On Foot: Diagram change Throw to Throw/Place on right trigger
- General: Diagram add Select Interaction Wheel on A
- Holding the PS5 button and Start for 5 seconds shuts the controller down

4.0.0b
Interaction mode primary action (R1) and interaction wheel (L1+R1) are now properly separated. This is a significant playability improvement.
- Separated keybinds for interaction mode to have primary interaction on R1 and interaction wheel on L1+R1
- Bound ship weapon group 2 on right shoulder button double tap
- Corrected vehicle mappings image should be M1 Lights [2], not M1 Lights (courtesy of user Orihem)
- Updated vehicle mappings image with mining mode jettison cargo L1+B (not tested)

4.0.0a
Overall a good patch for gamepad
- FPS weapon shortcuts are working again (digi-down [2] melee weapon, digi-right primary weapon, digi-right [2] sidearm, digi-left gadget)
- Changed vehicle ADS from tap to hold
- On-foot image replace melee on digi-down with medpen
- Mining mode bind first module to B and second module to B [2], bind jettison cargo to L1+B
- Tractor beam does not toggle, requires holding the trigger
- Tractor beam traversal mode has no keybinding (right mouse)
- The right stick moves the mouse in interaction mode, which sometimes doesn't work well with selection wheels
- Unbound playstation button in DS4Windows
- Added stick max zone 0.95 in DS4Windows


3.24.2a
Fixed some rebinds caused by the update.

3.24.0a
We are back with the usual complex mappings.
- On Foot: Added EVA controls from the 3.23 simple mappings
- On Foot: Added ammo repooling on LB+X
- On Foot: Added helmet equip on LB+digi-down
- Vehicles: Changed ping and scanning mode modifiers
- Vehicles: Changed speed limiter modifier since it lost importance
- Vehicles: Removed gimbal mode binding
- Vehicles: Added precision targeting on digi-up. This might not work too well.
- Vehicles: Updated salvage mode bindings to include support for the tractor beam, fracture and disintegrate
- I added QR codes to give you a way to tip me some sats!

3.23.1a
Due to the current keybind issues, I have remapped everything using no modifier button or double tap. This means a number of quality-of-life bindings are missing (select primary weapon, select gadget, ...), but generally I am surprised that this mostly works out ok.
- Many actions need to be done via Inner Thought (Mobiglass, Landing Gear, Lights, ...). It might take a bit of time to populate the Inner Thought wheel.
- Select weapons via the weapon wheel. This doesn't work in armistice zones, meaning the only way to select gadgets there is via Inner Thought or Inventory. Select in the weapon wheel does not work with thumbstick during EVA, use the touchpad.
- Ship flying basically only works in Cruise Mode. Look at the small target speed number left of the speedometer and adjust it with the digi-pad. For precision landing, you can disable Cruise mode and use the digi-pad. Landings are not as smooth as before.
- For ship combat use auto-targeting (toggle status displayed on the right side of the HUD).

3.21.1a
Ranged weapon melee/takedown now seems to be forced to the right stick button, without a keybind to change it. This means crouch/prone had to move to B, and the drop/place/throw bindings had to adjust. The customize weapon keybind no longer works with hold, and the scanning mode keybind seems to have changed behaviour. Some of these changes might have already happened in 3.21.0 or 3.20.X.
- Ship scanning mode from L1+X to X
- Ship radar ping from X double-tap to X hold (& release)
- Ship lights from L1+X double-tap hold to L1+X
- On foot flashlight from L1+X double tap to L1+X
- On foot melee/takedown from L1+R1 to right stick button (dev change, missing keybind)
- On foot crouch/prone from right stick button to B
- On foot throw overarm from B to L1+B double-tap
- On foot throw underarm unbind from L1+B
- On foot drop item from B double-tap to L1+B. Hold for place item does not work anymore.
- On foot weapon customization from digi-up hold to digi-up (dev change)
- On foot change firemode from digi-up to L1+digi-left

3.20.0a
- Minor fixes of keybind changes in the patch

3.19.1a
- Fixed ship salvage beam not working with R1, and fixed weapon firing during inner thought, by binding weapon groups 1 and 2 instead of "all".
- Moved Alt+R Nvidia GeForce Experience performance overlay to Modifier+Home button to avoid bringing the overlay up when turning off the controller.

3.19.0a
- Updated installation instructions for clarity
- Recreated all mappings from scratch in case of potential issues arising when not deleting the USER folder upon game update
- Removed all Joystick bindings because these interfere with the gamepad bindings for many users (especially movement and countermeasures; e.g. left shoulder button deploys decoys)
- Added underbarrel action (e.g. weapon light) to modifier + digi-up (double-tap)
- Added melee attack and takedown (modifier + right shoulder button) bindings to the diagram
- Changed PS5 button from Win+Alt+O (Shadow PC streaming) to Alt+R, which brings up the Nvidia GeForce Experience performance overlay

3.18.0a
- Vehicles: Change cycle gimbal mode from modifier + digi-up (double tap) to modifier + digi-left due to conflict with cruise control
- Vehicles: Seats and Operator Modes: Bind Salvage mode to Modifier + A, same as mining mode
- Vehicles: Salvage Model: Bind swap left module to X, swap right module to Circle
- Vehicles: Bind flight systems ready to digipad-up double-tap
- Vehicles: Diagram add [H] for hold to ship lights keybind (no hold needed on foot)
- General: Bind tilt left to open bracket (accept social) and tilt right to close bracket (decline social), replacing wipe helmet visor
- On foot: Bind wipe helmet visor to L1 + digi-up
- On foot: Bind change fire mode from L1 + digi-up (double tap) to simple digi-up

3.17.2b
- Unbind inspect from right shoulder button

3.17.2a
- Bind digipad down to cycle ship configuration (e.g. Hull-A).

3.17.1b
- Added draw.io diagram files

3.17.1a
- Bind left mouse click to mute button to use for inventory dragging.
- Bind left bumper (L1) + mute button to right mouse click (for FPS mining)
- Added Request Landing (ATC)
- Rebind vehicle weapon groups to right shoulder button (underlying structure changed)
- Change vehicle ping to double tap to avoid ping when entering scanning mode
- Add scroll up/down to diagrams
author: Cr4y7

name: Simple mapping for Xbox controller
summary: Simple mapping to play Star Citizen with your Xbox controller.
description:
Simple mapping to play Star Citizen with your Xbox controller.

This mapping is made to be intuitive and go to the essentials, for PvE combat or transport.

You can control almost everything, whether on foot, in your ship or in a
land vehicle without having to put down the controller. Only very can of
interaction requires the keyboard or the smile.

Once the downloaded file simply copy/paste it into your StarCitizen/Live/User/Client/0/Controls/Mappings folder.

Once in the game, go to the options/Keybording and load the preset.

___________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________

Mappage simple pour jouer a Star Citizen avec votre manette Xbox.

Ce mapping est fait pour être intuitif et aller a l'essentiel, pour le combat PvE ou le transport.

Vous pouvez presque tout contrôler, que ce soit a pied, dans votre vaisseaux
ou dans un véhicule terrestre sans devoir poser la manette. Seulement
très peut d'interaction nécessite le clavier ou la souri.

Une fois le fichier téléchargé copier/coller le simplement dans votre dossier StarCitizen/Live/User/Client/0/Controls/Mappings.

Une fois en jeu, rendez-vous dans les options/Keybording et charger le preset.
author: JakeCookSC

name: User Config Tweaker
summary:
This is a little Tweak Tool to Edit the user.cfg to get more performance.
The default values of this Tool are low end PC performance Tweak values - but you can also set your own values..
By the way - this tool is not required if you get enough fps with the in game settings.
description:
User Config Tweaker for Star Citizen (Alpha 3.0.0-Live)

This is a little Tweak Tool to Edit the user.cfg to get more performance.

The default values of this Tool are low end PC performance Tweak values inspirated
by a forum post by Mazer Rackham in the Modding Section.
But you can also set your own values (just select "Empty Config" at the lower left).

By the way - this tool is not required if you get enough fps with the in game settings.

Usage:
Start it, set the Path to the Star Citizen / "LIVE" folder by choosing the "Path" Button,
the low end value tweaks are already loaded, if you click on "Save" this values will be
saved to the specified location.

With the checkboxes you can enable / disable sections you wish to be in your config.

If you first time play the game with the new config you might notice that the loading time
and performance is not very well, just run around a little bit, close the game and restart it,
now it should be fast.

Note:
Currently the Tool do not load a existing user.cfg, the Tool will overwrite / create it.

View FPS:
If you dont like the FPS Display in Game with this tweaks go to "r_DisplayInfo" and set the value to 1

Remove:
To remove the tweaks go to your LIVE Folder inside your Star Citizen Folder and delete the user.cfg

The Tweaktool is written by triplex2011 / zeittresor.de
I have have nothing to do with RSI/Cloud Imperium Games.
This is a fun and freeware project by a fan for "Star Citizen by Cloud Imperium Games".
author: triplex2011

name: Star Citizen 3.16 - Steam Controller Configuration
summary: An exhaustive and potentially obsessive Star Citizen configuration for Steam Controllers. Requires my custom gamepad mappings xml and the Steam Controller configuration.
description:
[b][size=4]Star Citizen [Hybrid+] ?+?[/size][/b]
[size=2]an exhaustive and potentially obsessive Star Citizen configuration for Steam Controllers by RambleTan
*requires download of a custom .xml mappings file for in-game binding changes

[youtube]QfVqeM0s1oU[/youtube]
[/size]

[b][b]ADDING STAR CITIZEN TO STEAM (3 DIFFERENT METHODS)[/b]
[youtube]yYCNjg5Vkdw[/youtube]

ADDING STAR CITIZEN TO STEAM (3 DIFFERENT METHODS)[/b]
[list]
[*]Video Guide: [url=https://youtu.be/yYCNjg5Vkdw]https://youtu.be/yYCNjg5Vkdw[/url]
[*]The 3 methods outlined below all have pros and cons based on how much functionality you need from Steam Overlay and whether you need the game in Fullscreen mode or not. (I use 3rd method)
[*]Regardless of method make SURE you name your non-steam shortcut “Star Citizen” and in Steam, go to Settings / In-Game / CHECK “Use the Big Picture Overlay when using a Steam Input enabled controller from the desktop.” 
[/list]
[b] 1. ﻿RSI LAUNCHER Direct[/b]
[list]
[*]Easiest method and works with fullscreen, windowed, borderless but you won’t have full overlay functionality; OSK will double inputs, configurator will be laggy, and you have to use Shift + Tab (not SC button) to access overlay. Unfortunately this is where we are with Easy Anticheat. 
[*]Add RSI Launcher to Steam as non-steam shortcut. (Steam/Games/Add a Non-Steam Game to my Library…) Browse to: (default) ”C:\Program Files\Roberts Space Industries\RSI Launcher\RSI Launcher.exe” Rename it to “Star Citizen” (older configs are under “StarCitizen”)
[*]In Steam, go to Settings / In-Game / CHECK “Use the Big Picture Overlay when using a Steam Input enabled controller from the desktop.” 
[*]Do NOT use Steam Chord (Home) button to access Steam Overlay (now in Big Picture Mode BPM). Use Shift + Tab or bind Shift + Tab to your Guide Button Chord Configuration, otherwise the overlay gets stuck on the wrong window. Additionally you need to navigate BPM with mouse bindings, gamepad bindings won’t work.  (Guide Button Chord Configuration Tutorial) [url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xAZkWdO815c]https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xAZkWdO815c[/url]
[/list]
[b] ﻿2. GlosSI Empty Desktop Shortcut)[/b]
[list]
[*]Requires installing GlosSI and will not work in fullscreen mode. Provides full Steam Overlay functionality. 
[*][url=https://alia5.github.io/GlosSI/]https://alia5.github.io/GlosSI/[/url] Download latest release and fully install.
[*]Launch GlosSIConfig.exe and make a new shortcut. Leave Path empty and name the shortcut “Star Citizen”  (older configs are under “StarCitizen”) Add to Steam and restart Steam.
[*]In Steam, go to Settings / In-Game / CHECK “Use the Big Picture Overlay when using a Steam Input enabled controller from the desktop.” 
[*]Launch the GlosSI created shortcut “Star Citizen,” then run RSI Launcher and launch game outside of Steam. DON’T forget to CLOSE the non-steam shortcut when you’re done playing since it’s an empty shortcut. 
[/list][b]
﻿﻿3. GlosSI + RSI Launcher + .bat FILE (method I use)[/b]
[list]
[*]Requires installing GlosSI and making a .bat file. A pain in the butt but works with fullscreen mode (which I need for Nvidia Image Scaling), allows OBS to capture Steam Overlay on Game Capture method, and provides full Steam Overlay functionality. 
[*][url=https://alia5.github.io/GlosSI/]https://alia5.github.io/GlosSI/[/url] Download latest release and fully install.
[*]Launch GlosSIConfig.exe and make a new shortcut. WIN32 PROGRAM Browse to: (default) ”C:\Program Files\Roberts Space Industries\RSI Launcher\RSI Launcher.exe” Rename it to “Star Citizen”  (older configs are under “StarCitizen”)
[*]Check “Launch app,” “Close when launched app quits,” and “Hide (Real) Controllers.” Add to Steam and restart Steam.
[*]In Steam, go to Settings / In-Game / CHECK “Use the Big Picture Overlay when using a Steam Input enabled 
[*]Right Click on “Star Citizen” shortcut / Manage / Add Desktop Shortcut. On Desktop Right Click on the shortcut, go to Properties and copy the URL: (it’ll look like this) steam://rungameid/10022843949514752000
[*]Right Click on Desktop / New / Text Document
[*]In Text Document paste:
START steam://rungameid/10022843949514752000[/list] ﻿timeout /t 30 /nobreak
﻿taskkill /im GlosSITarget.exe /f
[list]
[*]Rename file to “Star Citizen.bat” and launch game FROM this bat file, NOT from Steam.
[/list]

[b]IMPORTING MY CONTROL PRESET (not Steam Profile, but the Game Bindings xml)[/b]
[list]
[*]Download from NEXUS "Steam Controller Hybrid Plus" 
[*][url=https://discord.gg/At3sX8b]ALTERNATE DOWNLOAD ON MY DIS﻿CORD[/url]﻿
[*]Copy or move the downloaded LIVE folder to Star Citizen Install: “C:\Program Files\Roberts Space Industries\StarCitizen”
[*]Verify that the downloaded xml file is in the “C:\Program Files\Roberts Space Industries\StarCitizen\LIVE\USER\Client/0/Controls\Mappings”
[*]In game go to Options / Keybindings / Advanced Controls Customization (bottom left), Control Profiles (bottom right), select “Clear All Device Bindings.” For Device to Load To select GamePad only. Repeat process and load “Steam Controller [Hybrid+]” and Load to GamePad ONLY.
[*]FYI, my XML file is only making changes to gamepad mappings, it makes no changes to mouse and keyboard bindings and is relying on the defaults for those.
[/list]
[b]USING MY STEAM CONTROLLER PROFILE[/b]
[list]
[*]From Desktop Steam right click on “Star Citizen” and select “Edit Steam Controller Configuration,” Press X to Browse Configs, Select “Community,” look for "Star Citizen (Current Version #) [Hybrid+] ?+?" in the list.
[*]For a Direct Link go to my Discord server
[/list]
[url=https://bit.ly/StarCitizenControlsGuide]
[/url][b]PERSONAL [b]IN-GAME [/b]PREFERENCES[/b]
GAME SETTINGS: Vehicles - Exiting Combat Seats Requires Hold (250ms): NO
Controls / Mouse: *Mouse Smoothing in Game: 0.04
Sensitivity 40
Controls / Gamepad: Deadzone Gamepad Thumb Left / Right: 0.05
Gamepad Sensitivity: 1.00


[b]PATCH NOTES[/b]
[code]CHANGES FOR 3.16
Added Menu (Mouse Cursor) Action Set that automatically switches when cursor is present (Menu, Mobi)
B button closes Mobi (see LS Left (Hold) note below)

“LP (Hold): Analog Roll + Flight Extras” has replaced “Analog Roll”
QoL improvements, adjusted Gamepad Curves in game. 
Acceleration Limiter +/- to LS. 
Mining to Y, Power to X, VTOL to B button
Mouse Cycle Aim Mode HOMAS to RB… 
Decouple RP Click
basically this layer is replacing the Flight functions from RB Hold in older versions.
Because of these changes I’ve also removed the “Analog Landing” layer. I didn’t use it much and it felt clunky plus it frees up some in-game bindings.

RB (Hold): 
Swapped Lights and Inspect Item / Target Zoom bindings due to a binding bug in the game’s XML system. Action hasn’t changed in config, still Swipe Down for lights, Up for Inspect Item / Target Auto Zoom (while holding RB)
Changed RP Flick UP to be Inventory, removed Inspect (can be accessed through interaction mode)

LS Left (Hold): removed Swipe Down to Close Mobi, was creating bugs with the Mouse Cursor Action Set.

Eliminated Gyro Menu due to Steam Overlay issues introduced with EasyAntiCheat
Select (2X) Press changed from Take Screenshot to Advanced Camera Layer, Screenshot moved to RB Hold + (2X) Select
(2X) Start: goto Ground Vehicles (or back to Main), (long) Start opens Keyboard

ADV CAM: Removed Touch Menu for Views, moved 1-4 on Left Stick + A

====================================================================================================

CHANGES FOR 3.14 (main ones my fried brain can remember)
MAIN
Changed LP to be Strafe instead of Roll. Roll is now on RP Edge Click (Q&E) OR LP middle click HOLD + RP for analog (see below).
Hold Start for Gyro Menu Layer Changes; switch to Ground Vehicles, Adv. Cam, Main, Keyboard
Changed RP Click Left / Right to Q / E so it will also work to switch Turret modes. 
TURRETS: Q = ESP, E = Relative Mode, LS Up / DN = Limiter, LP Edge Touch) = Limiter Mode
Full Pull Left Trigger Reg Press is PING, Long is EMPTY for Gyro Flicks
Full Pull Left Trigger + RP Clicks (for FPS): Left: Thumbs Up, Right: Thumbs Down, Down: Stop
2X Select changed from Adv Cam to Take Screenshot (Adv Cam now handled by Gyro Menu on Start Hold)
LS Reg. Press changed from Reset SCM to Scan Mode, Customize Weapon removed for FPS (anticipating FPS scanning)
Reset SCM added to 2X press of Dpad Down
Y changes to Missile Operator Mode
(2X) X changes Missile Quantity 
LB hold is now Capacitors and Countermeasures Layer (lots of extra functions)

FREELOOK
Added Dynamic Zoom to LT and RT
Added Y Offset (extra zoom): Hold (full) LT + LS up / down

ADV CAM
Added Y Offset to Left and Right Trigger
Cam Pan Up and Down moved to Grips
Added Gyro Menu on Hold A. Select Option with Right Trigger
Reset Cam added to middle RS Click

RB HOLD 
LS Left / Right: Changed from Accept / Reject to Increase / Decrease Ping Angle
RS Click: Up: Audio Channels, Down: Proximity VOIP, Left: Accept, Right: Reject, Center: VOIP
Power Triangle Management added to LP Clicks: Left: Weapons, Right: Shields, Down: Engines, Middle: Reset

GROUND VEHICLES
Added Brake to Full Pull Left Trigger for power slides.
A button is now Idle Brake Toggle. (similar to a parking brake though it doesn’t seem to work that well in game)

CM / CAPACITORS (LB Hold)
LS Directions = Shield Raise Levels. LS Click: Reg = Top, Long = Bottom
Y = Decoy, X = Increase Decoy Burst, B = Decrease Decoy Burst, A = Noise
RP Directional Swipes = Capacitor Power Triangle Management. Left = Weapons, Right = Shields, Down = Engine, Middle Click = Reset Priority.

ANALOG ROLL (LP Middle Click Hold)
RP is Joystick Camera for Analog Roll and Strafe Up / Down
[/code]
author: RambleTan

name: Star Citizen - Couch Citizen Config v3.22 WiP
summary: Play Star Citizen 100% with a gamepad! Designed to be as intuitive and simple as possible while incorporating ALL vital commands, as if you were playing Star Citizen on console. Use the Xbox Elite controller and the ReWASD remapping app to control ALL functions in Star Citizen.
description:
[size=5]Couch Citizen Configuration v3.22[/size]

[font=Comic Sans MS]
[center][youtube]aUq9q1f0qVY[/youtube]
[youtube]9gPdLI2DewE[/youtube]﻿[/center]
[/font]These are custom ReWASD configs and Star Citizen profiles to be used with an Xbox Elite controller and the ReWASD remapping software to play Star Citizen 100% with a gamepad*
The layout is an All-In-One config meant to be used for ALL major actions and controls in Star Citizen with no major compromises (i.e. you won't have to toggle into a different control input or gamepad layout for flying, etc). It was designed to be as intuitive and simple as possible by making use of the Elite's 4 underside paddles AND the Xbox button to combine gampad and mouse/keyboard keybinds into one comprehensive layout.

[b][color=#00ffff][b]- IDEAL MOVEMENT/ANALOG INPUTS:[/b] [/color]
[b][u]*ALL* movement axis (for flight and FPS) are all accessible SIMULTANEOUSLY!! Full 6DoF while flying![/u][/b] [/b]Other configs make you manually shift to a different layout to access some movement controls which can be cumbersome and inhibit control...NOT WITH THIS CONFIG!
You'll be circle-strafing like a pro and able to compete with players using flight sticks! Jump, crouch, lean and aim simultaneously for maximum player control!

[color=#00ffff][b]- ALL-IN-ONE:[/b][/color] NO necessary toggling to different controller layouts, NO swapping to different devices! Run to your ship after a gunfight, fly in battles, do some mining, space walk, hop in a land vehicle, control your mobi-glass, even text chat* and accept invites/missions, all while taking pretty screenshots and clips, and all done intuitively and fluidly using your controller. Layout changes are done by holding modifier/shift buttons on the controller and it reverts back to the base layout upon release.
This is "pick up n go" gaming without having to make many sacrifices or endure inconveniences!

[b][color=#00ffff]- UNIFORMITY/COMFORT[/color][/b] across different action sets (i.e. STRAFE UP/DOWN in ships = JUMP/CROUCH on foot, ROLL LEFT = LEAN LEFT, etc)
With the proper setup you can literally play SC from the comfort of your couch, as if you're playing it on console. 

[b][color=#00ffff]- KEEPING THUMBS ON THE ANALOG STICKS:[/color][/b] The combination of this controller and the remapping software gives access to actions like JUMP, CROUCH, LEAN, STRAFING, BRAKING etc., all while keeping your thumbs on the analog sticks to reinforce control of the player character and your vehicles within the universe. Now you can also do things like LOOK BEHIND, ADJUST SHIELDS, ADJUST CAPACITORS, and perform your favorite [b]16 EMOTES[/b]

[b][color=#00ffff]- ADVANCED CAMERA CONTROL:[/color][/b] Many SC configs focus on actions and ignore Advanced Cam controls, forcing the player to use their keyboard for it, but 3rd person camera access is [u][b]key[/b][/u] to playing SC optimally!
This config gives you great access to [u]ALL[/u] necessary Advanced Camera actions with analog control of all camera movements, easily. Even save and load 4 custom views on a whim!
Are you a screen-archer? With this you can get those perfect shots with altered DOF, FOV, smooth camera movement and more, without having to swap to another device or make sacrifices. Take your beautiful screenshots and/or video clips, and get right back into the action without pause!

[b][color=#00ffff]- CREATE YOUR OWN CONFIG:
[/color][/b]Highly customizable. Are you a lefty? Do you like your analog sticks inverted? With ReWASD and in-game options you can use my config as a base and adjust it to fit your own personal style

[b][color=#00ffff]- FULL KEYBINDS LEGEND:[/color][/b][color=#00ff00] [url=https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1aFz_twuGIXP9cgK8-hVp5xYqiL5yDL6B/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=116358060656388673215&rtpof=true&sd=true]https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1aFz_twuGIXP9cgK8-hVp5xYqiL5yDL6B/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=116358060656388673215&rtpof=true&sd=true[/url][/color]

[color=#ff0000][size=4][b][u]PLEASE NOTE!!
ReWASD remapping software is required for this mod!![/u][/b][/size][/color]: [url=https://www.rewasd.com/]https://www.rewasd.com/
[/url]THIS IS A LICENSED APPLICATION! THEY HAVE A 14-DAY TRIAL BUT YOU MUST PURCHASE REWASD IN ORDER TO MAKE FULL USE OF THIS CONFIG!
[img]data:image/png;base64,[/img]

[url=https://www.rewasd.com/map-xbox-elite]https://www.rewasd.com/map-xbox-elite[/url]
Once installed, simply download my ReWASD config as well as my Star Citizen XML file to be imported into the game's settings, and you should be set!

[b][u][color=#ff0000]REQUIREMENTS:[/color][/u][/b]
1. XBOX ELITE CONTROLLER V1 OR V2
2. LATEST VERSION OF REWASD
3. STAR CITIZEN


[b][size=3][u]
[/u][/size][/b][b][u][color=#00ff00]COUCH CITIZEN CONFIGURATION INSTALLATION GUIDE:[/color][/u][/b]

1. Download/install ReWASD and follow the setup instructions so that your Xbox Elite controller is detected and has default profiles in the slots:
https://www.rewasd.com/ or https://www.rewasd.com/download
Verify your PC detects your gamepad and keyboard
FREE RESOURCES TO TEST YOUR INPUTS
- https://gamepad-tester.com/
- https://www.keyboardtester.com/

2. Download the latest Couch Citizen Configuration from the following sources if you haven't already. You will have to extract the contents once downloaded: 
*NEXUS MOD PAGE HERE: https://www.nexusmods.com/starcitizen/mods/4
*MEDIAFIRE LINK HERE: https://www.mediafire.com/folder/5jrpr367vj7k2/COUCH_CITIZEN_CONFIG
*DROPBOX LINK HERE: https://www.dropbox.com/sh/qclnqdz0hayqzqa/AABk1AFmv_Vi3NQlcblXN17Na?dl=0

3. Extract the contents of the file to a directory of your choice. 
Open/Import the ReWASD profile[s] (.rewasd files) by launching ReWASD and clicking Options menu at the top, then choose the "Open file location" option, then browse to the .rewasd profile[s] you downloaded. You can import all profiles into ReWASD and choose the one you want to apply at any time

Choose CCC 3.22WIP.rewasd for the normal config (no Auto-Walk/Run/Drive)
Choose CCC 3.22WIP_AUTORUN.rewasd for the alt config (with Auto-Walk/Run/Drive)
Choose MySC.rewasd for the alt config (with Auto-Walk/Run/Drive) that includes custom analog stick settings

Make sure the configuration is showing in ReWASD, then click APPLY to Slot X at the bottom left to apply it to your designated controller slot (you can load the same or different configs to other slots). You can see which config is loaded to each slot near the bottom right of the ReWASD window.
Once it's loaded, test the config on your desktop to ensure it's functioning:

FREE RESOURCES TO TEST YOUR INPUTS
- https://gamepad-tester.com/
- https://www.keyboardtester.com/

4. Copy/Paste the downloaded "layout_x_exported.xml" files into your Star Citizen game directory, into the following folder location that corresponds to the version you're playing (LIVE, PTU, or EPTU):
LIVE = C:\Program Files\Roberts Space Industries\StarCitizen\LIVE\USER\Client\0\Controls\Mappings
 
PTU = C:\Program Files\Roberts Space Industries\StarCitizen\PTU\USER\Client\0\Controls\Mappings

EPTU = C:\Program Files\Roberts Space Industries\StarCitizen\EPTU\USER\Client\0\Controls\Mappings

5. Launch Star Citizen. From the main screen or in-game menu, go to:
OPTIONS > KEYBINDINGS > ADVANCED CONTROLS CUSTOMIZATION > CONTROL PROFILES
Choose the downloaded .xml file from step 4, which should now be showing up in the dropdown list; it will have the same name as the .xml file from the download (if they're not showing in the list then you may have put it in the wrong folder during step 4).

**NOTE** I advise loading the layout_Totally-Blank_exported.xml config first, then load one of the other configs afterwards

A window should pop up. For each of the devices on the left, select the corresponding device on the right (just choose the same devices):
Keyboard -> Keyboard
Mouse -> Mouse
Gamepad -> N/A

**NOTE** Without this step your controls will not work as intended!

6. Click BACK to exit the menu with changes saved, launch the game and enjoy! 
I recommend configuring all other settings for optimal performance (I've included pics of my personal settings)
author: julsnice

name: LC's Star Citizen Reshade
summary: A set of useful and visual-enhancing Reshade presets for Star Citizen.
description:
[size=5][center][i][font=Arial][b]Get 375% more immersified with the Lord Critish's Reshade for Star Citizen[/b][/font][/i][/center][/size]

For Reshade 5.3.0

[b][size=4]FEATURES[/size][/b]

[list]
[*]Six different presets, switchable for any situation.
[*]Improves the rather bleak default look of the game considerably with a warmer, cinematic look by modifying color saturation, contrast, sharpness, bloom, and a slight bit of film grain and an even tinier bit of chromatic aberration.
[*]Improves antialiasing.
[*]Sharper images.
[*]Adds a crosshair for FPS - and Night Vision presets for shooting from the hip.
[*]Adds a thin border around the screen to eliminate a visual glitch that  sometimes results in the game having bright borders at the screen edges.*
[*]And more.

[/list]

[b][size=4]INCLUDED PRESETS[/size][/b]

[list]
[*][b]01 Default[/b] - The default preset. (Previously named "Space") Makes everything more colorful and contrastier, useful for general game play,
[/list]space, etc.[b]
[/b][list]
[*][b]02 FPS[/b] - Intended for FPS gameplay. Less distracting effects and color modifications, adds a crosshair for easier shooting
[/list]from the hip.[b]
[/b][list]
[*][b]03 Cinematic[/b] - more generous with effects and adds more bloom, intended for screen archery, mostly looks great in interiors
[/list] and darker places.[b]
[/b][list]
[*][b]04 Night Vision[/b] - Provides a green-tinted night vision effect resembling phosphor-based night vision goggles that
[/list]enhance low light environments. Intended for very dark spaces like caves, wreck interiors, and everywhere where it's too dark at night.[b]
[/b][list]
[*][b]05 Infrared[/b] - Intended for low light areas while it somewhat resembles an infrared spectrum filter. Brighter than Night Vision preset. Useful for
[/list]all sorts of dark areas, flying planetside during darkness, and  approaching space stations that are covered in shadow.[b]
[/b][list]
[*][b]06 Darken[/b] - A darkening filter similar to wearing shades. Mostly useful for mining when the mining laser can blow out the HUD visuals. Can also look great  for screen archery in very bright places.
[/list]
[b][size=4]INSTRUCTIONS[/size][/b]

1. Uninstall any prior Reshade from Star Citizen.
2. install Reshade 5.3.0 for StarCitizen.exe (newer versions might work but are not officially supported by SC at the time of this writing).
   NOTE: Chose DirectX 10/11/12 during install.
   NOTE: Selected shaders during install don't matter.
3. Optional but recommended: Remove shaders and textures from the installed version.
4. Copy the contents of this archive to the SC exe folder (...\Roberts Space Industries\StarCitizen\LIVE\Bin64).
5. Start the game. A Reshade message should appear on the top of the screen.
   NOTE: In case game doesn't move forward to the menu screen, switch to desktop once and back.
6. If you use the included ReShade.ini you can toggle Reshade config window with [CAPSLOCK] key, Reshade effect on/off with [NUMPAD *], and cycle through Reshade presets with [NUMPAD -]. You can change key codes in the ini file or Reshade in-game menu.


[b][size=4]IN-GAME GRAPHIC SETTINGS[/size][/b]

1. Leave Gamma, Brightness, and Contrast at default 50.
2. Set Sharpening, Chromatic Aberration to 0 and Film Grain to No.
3. Recommended: Turn Motion Blur and VSync off.
4. TIP: Don't use max. quality for volumetric clouds. It's very unstable on some systems.

[b][size=4]*NOTE ON BORDER EFFECT[/size][/b]

For 16:9 or other-than-21:9 screen aspect ratios you might have to adjust the Border effect values to make it look right. There should only be a thin 2 pixel border around the whole screen.

--
Created by Lord Critish 2022 - Enjoy!
https://www.youtube.com/c/LordCritish
author: Lord Critish

This creates a more colorful, vivid landscape that feels alive and beautiful, while making smaller details "pop."

author: AspenZero

name: Nurseman's Reshade Presets
summary: Reshade Presets for 2 types of NVG and 2 Types of Sunglasses
description:
These reshade presets are designed to give a relatively immersive NVG capability, in two forms as in some environments one might not work as well compared to the other.

The NVGs are designed more for immersive function, rather than PVP, hence light sources will appear extremely bright so there are situations where they will actually be a hinderance, and other places where they are near essential. E.g. approaching ghost hollow they work great, but once you enter areas with ambient light, things get bright very fast. This is an approximation of my understanding of real life NVGs with adjustments after discussing with friends from the US military (e.g. monochrome Night vision is pretty old tech now according to them)

The Sunglasses are actually more useful in star citizen, making washed out bright areas much more visible. I'm pretty pleased with the result and they remind me of the difference sunglasses make when I used to go skiing in the Italian/French Alps.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Y21B1t6AGHU
I highly recommend using Fist and Jawa's video as a guide on reshade for Star Citizen, and they also have presets you might wish to try.
(but essentially once you have reshade running, pop these files in "\Roberts Space Industries\StarCitizen\LIVE\Bin64")

Please feel free to feed back in comments

I give permission for anyone to modify and redistribute my presets if they wish, if you can improve them I'll use em!

All the best to the community!

DISCLAIMER:

RESHADE IS APPROVED FOR USE IN STAR CITIZEN AND IS ON THE GREENLIST FOR EAC
THIS WILL PROBABLY CHANGE IN THE FUTURE WHEN STAR CITIZEN HAS IT'S OWN AUGMENTED VISION MODES
author: Nurseman

name: Vibrant ReShade
summary: A vibrant ReShade preset for Star Citizen. Enhanced colors, contrast, and depth in visuals.
description:
[b][size=5]Enhance your immersion in Star Citizen with this vibrant preset for use with ReShade.[/size][/b]
[size=3]
Make your game look less washed-out without oversaturating anything.

[color=#ffe599]Please Endorse if you like this mod![/color]


What the preset does:
[/size][list]
[*][size=3]Enhance colors[/size]
[*][size=3]Enhance contrast[/size]
[*][size=3]Enhance depth[/size]
[/list][size=3]
What the preset doesn't do:
[/size][list]
[*][size=3]Boost FPS[/size]
[*][size=3]Provide more gameplay[/size]
[/list]

[b]INSTALLATION
[/b][size=3]1. Install the latest compatible version of ReShade.
﻿NOTE: Install with DX11
﻿[s]NOTE: Rename dxgi.dll to d3d11.dll[/s][/size]
[size=3]2. Unzip the Vibrant.ini file and drag into your Star Citizen installation folder, found in \Roberts Space Industries\StarCitizen\LIVE\Bin64. Or create a presets folder in the same location and drag the Vibrant.ini file in here.
[/size][size=3]3. Start the game and profit.
﻿NOTE: Press HOME to open the ReShade settings in-game
﻿NOTE: Keep FakeHDR.fx, Technicolor2.fx, and Vibrance.fx checked[/size]

[b]UNINSTALLATION[/b]
[size=3]Remove the Vibrant.ini file from your Star Citizen installation folder to uninstall the preset. Or remove d3d11.dll or dxgi.dll , reshade.ini, and the reshade-shaders folder to uninstall ReShade completely.[/size]
[size=3]

[/size][size=3]If you enjoy my work, consider leaving a [url=https://ko-fi.com/glitchedinorbit]tip on Ko-fi[/url]﻿.[/size]

author: Denimos

name: AetherLens Reshade
summary:
AetherLens | Star Citizen Visuals
Transform Star Citizen's visuals! Diverse ReShade presets: cinematic, cel-shaded, retro, realistic. Enhance immersion, capture stunning shots. Install ReShade to SC\LIVE\Bin64, then copy AetherLens .inis. Open ReShade (Home key) in-game.
description:
[b]AetherLens | Star Citizen Visuals[/b][b]See the 'Verse Through a New Lens![/b]AetherLens is a meticulously crafted collection of ReShade presets designed to profoundly alter and enhance the visual landscape of Star Citizen. From the vibrant realism of "RealLife" and the iconic tones of "Kodachrome" to the striking artistry of "CelShaded" and the nostalgic warmth of "80s TV" and "CRTVHS," this pack offers an unparalleled visual spectrum to match every mood and preference.Whether you're capturing breathtaking screenshots, streaming your epic journeys, or simply craving a fresh and immersive perspective for your adventures in the Stanton system, AetherLens provides a suite of filters to redefine your visual experience.[b]Features:[/b][list]
[*][b]Diverse Visual Styles:[/b] Explore a vast array of looks, encompassing hyper-realistic, cinematic, stylized cel-shaded, comic book, and classic retro aesthetics.
[*][b]Enhanced Immersion:[/b] Deepen your connection to the 'Verse with finely tuned colors, contrast, sharpness, and atmospheric effects.
[*][b]Effortless Switching:[/b] Seamlessly toggle between presets in-game, allowing you to instantly adapt the visual mood to any moment or environment.
[*][b]Optimized Performance:[/b] Designed with efficiency in mind, aiming to deliver stunning visuals without crippling your framerate (individual results may vary based on your system and chosen preset's complexity).
[/list][b]Included Presets (Current Version):[/b][list]
[*][b]SC_80s TV.ini:[/b] Experience Star Citizen like it's being broadcast on a classic CRT television from the 1980s.
[*][b]SC_CelShaded High Filter.ini:[/b] A more aggressive cel-shading effect, adding strong outlines and flat colors for a distinct artistic feel.
[*][b]SC_CelShaded.ini:[/b] A lighter cel-shaded look, blending the game's visuals with a comic book aesthetic.
[*][b]SC_CineDirector.ini:[/b] Achieve a professional, film-like grade with rich colors and balanced tones, perfect for cinematic captures.
[*][b]SC_CinematicNatural.ini:[/b] A more subtle cinematic look, enhancing natural colors and depth without heavy grading.
[*][b]SC_Comic Book Cel Shaded.ini:[/b] Combine comic book stylization with cel-shading for a truly unique and illustrative appearance.
[*][b]SC_CRT90s.ini:[/b] A cleaner CRT look reminiscent of early 90s console gaming.
[*][b]SC_CRTVHS.ini:[/b] Dive into full retro glory with the authentic look of an old VHS tape played on a CRT TV.
[*][b]SC_JoeDirt.ini:[/b] (Likely a specific, perhaps humorous, film-inspired look - describe its style if possible!)
[*][b]SC_Kodachrome.ini:[/b] Recreate the iconic vibrant colors, sharp detail, and fine grain of classic Kodachrome film.
[*][b]SC_ProRealisticCinematic.ini:[/b] A professional-grade realistic cinematic look, focusing on lifelike colors and atmospheric depth.
[*][b]SC_RealLife.ini:[/b] A naturalistic enhancement aiming for a "real-life" visual fidelity, making the 'Verse feel more grounded.
[*][b]SC_StarWars1977.ini:[/b] Transport yourself to a galaxy far, far away with a visual style inspired by the original 1977 Star Wars film.
[/list][line][b]Installation Guide (ReShade & Presets):[/b][b]IMPORTANT:[/b] Always make a backup of your game folder before installing any mods, especially if you're unsure![b]Step 1: Download ReShade[/b][list=1]
[*]Go to the official ReShade website: https://reshade.me
[*]Click the "Download" button to get the latest ReShade_Setup.exe installer.
[/list][b]Step 2: Install ReShade to Star Citizen[/b][list=1]
[*]Run the downloaded ReShade_Setup.exe.
[*]Click "Click here to select a game and manage its ReShade installation."
[*][b]Crucial Step for Star Citizen:[/b] You need to point ReShade to the correct executable.[list]
[*]Navigate to your Star Citizen installation directory.
[*]Usually, this is C:\Program Files\Roberts Space Industries\StarCitizen\LIVE\Bin64\
[*]Select StarCitizen.exe within the Bin64 folder.
[*][b]Do NOT[/b] select the StarCitizen.exe in the main StarCitizen folder or any other executable.
[/list]
[*]ReShade will ask you to select the rendering API. For Star Citizen, select [b]"Direct3D 10/11/12."[/b]
[*]Now, ReShade will prompt you to select effect packages. To ensure compatibility with most presets (including ours), we recommend selecting the following:[list]
[*][b]Standard effects[/b]
[*][b]SweetFX by CeeJay.dk[/b]
[*][b]Legacy Standard effects[/b]
[*](Optional: If you plan to use presets that require specific effects like qUINT (MXAO, RTGI, ADOF), LumaSharpen, or others, select them now. More effects mean longer loading times for ReShade.)
[/list]
[*]Click "OK" or "Next" to begin the download and installation of the selected effects.
[*]Once finished, ReShade will confirm the installation. You can now close the ReShade installer.
[/list][b]Step 3: Install AetherLens Presets[/b][list=1]
[*]Download the "AetherLens | Star Citizen Visuals" mod from Nexus Mods (this page!).
[*]Unzip the downloaded archive. You will find a collection of .ini files (e.g., SC_80s TV.ini, SC_CelShaded.ini, etc.).
[*]Copy all of these .ini files.
[*]Paste them into the same directory where you installed ReShade for Star Citizen:[list]
[*]C:\Program Files\Roberts Space Industries\StarCitizen\LIVE\Bin64\
[*]Make sure they are directly in this Bin64 folder, alongside StarCitizen.exe and the d3d11.dll (or d3d12.dll) created by ReShade.
[/list]
[/list][line][b]In-Game Usage:[/b][list=1]
[*]Launch Star Citizen as you normally would.
[*]You should see a small ReShade banner at the top of your screen as the game loads, indicating it's active.
[*]Once in-game (either in the menu or flying in the 'Verse):[list]
[*]Press the [b]Home[/b] key (sometimes Pos1 on European keyboards) to open the ReShade UI.
[*]At the top of the ReShade window, you'll see a dropdown menu. Click it and select any of the "SC_" presets you installed (e.g., SC_CelShaded.ini).
[*]The game's visuals will instantly change!
[*]To toggle ReShade on/off, press the [b]Scroll Lock[/b] key (default binding).
[*]You can also click the "Reload" button in the ReShade UI if you make manual changes or want to ensure a preset is fully loaded.
[*]Feel free to explore the different effects within the ReShade UI. You can enable/disable individual shaders to customize a preset further, but remember to save your changes using the "Save current preset" button if you want them to persist.
[/list]
[/list][b]Uninstallation:[/b][list=1]
[*][b]To uninstall ReShade completely:[/b][list]
[*]Run the ReShade_Setup.exe again.
[*]Select StarCitizen.exe in the Bin64 folder.
[*]Click "Direct3D 10/11/12" again.
[*]ReShade will now offer you an "Uninstall" button. Click it.
[/list]
[*][b]To remove only the AetherLens presets:[/b][list]
[*]Simply go to C:\Program Files\Roberts Space Industries\StarCitizen\LIVE\Bin64\
[*]Delete all the .ini files that start with "SC_" (e.g., SC_80s TV.ini, SC_CelShaded.ini, etc.).
[/list]
[/list][line][b]Compatibility & Troubleshooting:[/b][list]
[*][b]Compatibility:[/b] This mod is compatible with all versions of Star Citizen that use DirectX 11/12. It should work alongside most other game modifications as long as they don't interfere with the d3d11.dll (or d3d12.dll) injection or graphics pipeline.
[*][b]Performance:[/b] ReShade has a minimal performance impact, but complex shaders (like RTGI or advanced depth-based effects) can significantly reduce FPS, especially on older hardware. If you experience performance issues, try a less demanding preset or disable some effects within the ReShade UI.
[*][b]ReShade Not Appearing:[/b][list]
[*]Ensure you installed ReShade to the correct StarCitizen.exe within the LIVE\Bin64\ folder.
[*]Verify you selected "Direct3D 10/11/12" during ReShade setup.
[*]Check your antivirus software; sometimes, it can block ReShade. Add StarCitizen.exe and your Star Citizen folder to your antivirus exceptions.
[/list]
[*][b]Presets Not Showing in Dropdown:[/b][list]
[*]Make sure you copied the .ini files directly into the LIVE\Bin64\ folder, not into a subfolder.
[/list]
[*][b]Game Crashes:[/b][list]
[*]This is rare, but if it happens, try uninstalling ReShade and reinstalling, ensuring you select the correct DX version and perhaps fewer effects packages initially.
[*]Outdated graphics drivers can also cause issues.
[/list]
[*][b]"Home" Key Conflict:[/b] If another software or in-game keybind uses the Home key, you can change the ReShade overlay key in the ReShade settings tab (while the overlay is open).
[/list][line][b]Credits:[/b][list]
[*]The incredible ReShade team for developing this powerful post-processing injector.
[*]The Star Citizen community for their continuous support and inspiration.
[/list][b]Support:[/b]If you encounter any issues or have questions, please use the comments section on Nexus Mods, and I'll do my best to assist you![b]Enjoy your new view of the 'Verse with AetherLens![/b]
author: Dubdarel

name: Gamepad Control Overhaul 4.5
summary:
Jouez à Star Citizen à la manette sans compromis. mouvements analogiques à 6 axes, combat en vol et au sol, contrôle de la camera, gestion des vues, menu, interaction, inventaire, social, le tout réuni dans un seul et unique objectif : la jouabilité

Les notes de mise à jour sont listées en bas de la page principale
description:
[font=Verdana][size=6]⚠ CE PROFIL UTILISE DES MACROS COMPLEXES VISANT EXCLUSIVEMENT À CONTOURNER CERTAINS BUGS ET AMÉLIORER LE CONFORT DE JEU, SOYEZ AVERTI ⚠


[/size]
[u]Ce profil a été conçu afin de permettre une expérience de jeu complète sur Star Citizen sans avoir à lâcher la manette. Il reflète ma vision de l’essentiel pour une pratique accessible et fluide via ce support.
[/u]

[b][left]Révision du mode interaction[/left][/b]
Le système d’interaction a été entièrement repensé. Il repose désormais sur deux boutons :

[/font][list]
[*][font=Verdana][b]A[/b] pour les interactions rapides[/font]
[*][font=Verdana][b]Y[/b] pour les interactions nécessitant l’usage du pointeur de la souris[/font]
[/list][font=Verdana]La touche Y constitue l’élément central de ce profil. Elle permet de quitter absolument TOUT les systèmes du jeu. Il est donc [b]fortement déconseillé[/b] d’utiliser la croix en haut à droite ou n'importe quel autres porte de sortie pour quitter les menus. Préférez toujours la touche [b]Y[/b].
En cas de blocage sur une couche secondaire due à un modificateur nécessitant un appui prolongé, Y sert de touche de secours.


[b][left]Schéma de contrôle[/left][/b]
Le schéma de contrôle a été élaboré avec rigueur pour refléter fidèlement le fonctionnement réel de ce profil. Chaque élément a été pensé et intégré de manière stratégique — aucune fonction n’a été laissée au hasard.
La redondance volontaire de certaines touches vise à favoriser la fluidité et le confort de jeu.

[b]
[left]Mode tourelle et modificateur caché[/left][/b]
Le mode tourelle, associé au modificateur [b]M3 View[/b] et à la touche [b]Y[/b], constitue une couche cachée jouant le rôle de rustine. En effet, toutes les tourelles du jeu ne sont pas compatibles avec une utilisation à la manette. Ce modificateur permet de basculer la visée sur les deux joysticks en émulant les mouvements de la souris. [s]Lorsqu’il est actif, un [b]point bleu[/b] apparaît au centre de l’écran.[/s]
Ce système est temporaire et sera amené à disparaître une fois que [b]CIG[/b] aura rendu toutes les tourelles pleinement compatibles avec la manette.
Je recommande, dans un premier temps, d’entrer dans les tourelles téléopérées via la caméra libre et la touche [b]A[/b] avant d’envisager l’usage du modificateur caché.

[b]
[left]Fonctionnalités complémentaires[/left][/b]
Certaines commandes bénéficient de [b]retours haptiques[/b], pour un ressenti tactile plus immersif ou pour valider leurs utilisation.


[b][center][/center][left]Système de sécurité du mode interaction[/left][/b]
Plusieurs garde-fous ont été implémentés pour le mode interaction. Exemple : Si vous tentez d’accéder à une console ou à un conteneur d’objets via un [b]appui court sur A[/b], vous serez immédiatement rejeté, afin de vous contraindre à utiliser l’[b]appui long sur A[/b]. 

[/font][left][font=Verdana][b]
Cas particulier de conflit[/b][b][center][/center][/b][/font][/left][font=Verdana]
Il peut arriver que le garde-fou associé à [b]A (appui court)[/b] entre en conflit avec certaines interactions avec un délai court, comme le maniement d’un chariot. Dans ces situations, il est recommandé d’utiliser la [b]caméra libre[/b], suivie d’un appui sur [b]A[/b], pour interagir sans rejet. Cette méthode est à réserver uniquement à ce type de cas.


[b][left]Évolutions à venir[/left][/b]
Comme indiqué précédemment, ces mécanismes de sécurité seront supprimés dès lors que [b]CIG[/b] aura finalisé le support complet de la manette, tout en conservant l'esprit du schéma de contrôle actuel.


[b]Fonctionnalités spécifiques du profil  
[/b]
Ce profil permet une série d’actions avancées non encore prises en charge nativement par CIG :

[/font][list]
[*][font=Verdana]Accroupissement en sprint avant via un simple appui sur [b]LS[/b][/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Ouverture automatique de la section des attaches d’armes lors de l’accès au menu de fouille[/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Sélection et calcul de trajectoire dans la Starmap par simple pression longue sur [b]A[/b][/font]
[*][font=Verdana]En vaisseau et en véhicule terrestre : possibilité de regarder en arrière tout en conservant la même liberté de mouvements qu'en regardant devant[/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Contrôle du pointeur souris à l’aide des deux joysticks analogiques, grâce à la couche de [b]modificateur Y[/b] et [b]Xbox[/b][/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Sortie immédiate de tous les systèmes en jeu grâce à 1 seule touche[/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Sortir de la Mobiglass est instantané[/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Il est possible d'enlever complètement l'ATH[/font]
[/list][font=Verdana]
[b]Références aux fonctionnalités natives
[/b]
Pour les fonctionnalités déjà implémentées par CIG, veuillez vous référer au schéma de contrôle également disponible en fichier texte dans la catégorie [b]Divers,[/b] en suivant ces principes :

[/font][list]
[*][font=Verdana]Les fonctions sont classées [b]par type de gameplay[/b] (volet droit), puis [b]par ordre de modificateur croissant[/b] (volet gauche)[/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Les fonctions [b]sans catégorie de gameplay[/b] sont considérées comme communes à plusieurs types de gameplay[/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Les touches [b]Y[/b] et [b]Xbox[/b] sont des bascules : lorsqu’une fonction est associée à deux modificateurs, cela souligne ce caractère. Ce sont également les seules fonctions associées à leurs codes couleur[/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Les fonctions [b]liées à la vue arrière[/b] sont identifiables par une mise en forme [i]gras, italique et soulignée[/i][/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Lorsque le [b]boost[/b] est actif, les touches [b]A[/b], [b]B[/b], [b]X[/b], [b]Y, D-pad, LS Bump[/b] et [b]Menu [/b] sont transférées sur la couche [b]M2 (LB)[/b] – vigilance requise[/font]
[*][font=Verdana]La couche [b]LB[/b] est prioritaire sur [b]RB[/b]. Si vous maintenez [b]RB[/b], puis [b]LB[/b] sans relâcher [b]RB[/b], [b]LB[/b] l’emporte. Par exemple, en vol, cela déclenche le boost tout en désactivant les déplacements latéraux analogiques[/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Les touches [b]non assignées[/b] dans la couche [b]Menu[/b] sont inactives lorsque cet activateurs est utilisé[/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Les touches [b]non assignées[/b] dans les couches [b]LS Bump[/b], [b]View,[/b] [b]LB[/b] et [b]RB[/b] reprennent les fonctions de la couche principale[/font]
[/list][font=Verdana]
[b]




Guide d’installation du profil "Gamepad Control Overhaul" pour Star Citizen
[/b]
? [b]Étape 1 — Installation de [url=https://www.rewasd.com/]ReWASD[/url][/b] Téléchargez et installez l’application [b][url=https://www.rewasd.com/]ReWASD[/url][/b], qui établira le lien entre la manette et le jeu. Attention, il s’agit d’un logiciel payant proposant un abonnement ou une licence à vie. Une période d’essai gratuite de 7 jours est également disponible.

? [b]Étape 2 — Manette requise[/b] Utilisez une [b]manette Xbox Elite Series 1 ou 2[/b].

? [b]Étape 3 — Extraction du profil[/b] Extrayez le contenu du fichier [b]GCO 4.5 HOSAS.rar[/b].

? [b]Étape 4 — Activation de la manette[/b] Allumez votre manette Xbox Elite.

? [b]Étape 5 — Configuration dans ReWASD
[/b]
[/font][list=1]
[*][font=Verdana]Ouvrez ReWASD[/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Cliquez sur le menu déroulant en haut à gauche[/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Sélectionnez [b]"Ajouter un profil de jeu"[/b] → nommez-le, par exemple : [i]Star Citizen[/i][/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Cliquez sur [b]"Ajouter"[/b][/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Sélectionnez [b]Config 1[/b] → [b]Importer[/b][/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Choisissez le fichier récemment extrait [b]GCO 4.5 HOSAS.rewasd[/b][/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Cliquez sur [b]"Appliquer à l’emplacement 1"[/b] (en bas à gauche)[/font]
[font=Verdana]
[/font][/list][font=Verdana]? [b]Étape 6 — Installation du layout XML[/b] Copiez le fichier [b]layout_GCO-4-5-HOSAS[/b] dans le répertoire suivant : [/font][code][font=Verdana]C:\Program Files\Roberts Space Industries\StarCitizen\LIVE\USER\Client\0\Controls\Mappings[/font]
[/code][font=Verdana]

? [b]Étape 7 — Activation du profil dans le jeu
[/b]
[/font][list=1]
[*][font=Verdana]Lancez [i]Star Citizen[/i][/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Allez dans [b]Options > Raccourcis clavier > Personnalisation commandes avancées > Profils de contrôle[/b][/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Sélectionnez le profil [b]GCO-4-5-HOSAS[/b][/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Définissez :[/font]
[/list][list=1]
[list]
[*][font=Verdana]Clavier → [b]Clavier[/b][/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Souris → [b]Souris[/b][/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Manette → [b]N/A[/b][/font]
[/list][/list][font=Verdana][b]
[/b]   5. Validez[b]
[/b]
? [b]Étape 8 — Paramétrage du gameplay[/b] Dans le menu [b]Paramètres[/b], configurez les options suivantes selon la traduction FR du Cirque Lisoir :     
     
     → Vibration = Non
     → Mode Interaction (Basculer) = Non
     → Sprint (Basculer) = Oui
     → Ramper (Basculer) = Oui
     → S'accroupir (Basculer) = Oui

[b]Recommandation :[/b]

     → Astuces de Raccourcis = Non
     → Invite d'interaction : Affichage icones d'entrée = Non
     → Mode Surbrillance = Toujours
[/font][list]
[/list][font=Verdana]? [b]Étape 9 — Sensibilité de la manette[/b] Dans [b]Contrôles[/b], sélectionnez la manette en bas à droite de l’écran, puis ajustez la sensibilité selon la [b]deuxième image[/b] fournie sur la page du mod.

? [b]Étape 10 — Configuration des raccourcis clavier[/b] Configurez les raccourcis selon la [b]troisième image[/b] figurant sur la page du mod.

?[b] Étape 11 — Nvidia App [/b]Pour les possesseurs de carte Nvidia

[/font][list=1]
[*][font=Verdana]Ouvrez l'Overlay avec Alt + Z[i][/i][/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Cliquez sur l'engrenage en haut à droite de la fenêtre[/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Ouvrez le menu "raccourcis"[/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Définissez les raccourcis comme sur la [b]quatrième image[/b] de ce mod[/font]
[/list][font=Verdana]
? [b]Étape 12 — Test de fonctionnement[/b] Dans le jeu, ouvrez la [b]Mobiglass[/b] avec le bouton [b]Xbox[/b], puis fermez-la en appuyant sur [b]"Y"[/b].

? [b]Étape 13 — Vérification[/b] Si cette dernière action fonctionne, le profil est correctement installé. Dans le cas contraire, recommencez à partir de [b]l’étape 3[/b].
