[*][font=Verdana]Allumer/Éteindre l'alimentation (L) sur [b]A [/b]dans la c[b]ouche d'interaction Y[/b][/font]
[*][font=Verdana]OUVRIR LA CARTE (1) sur [b]XBOX[/b] avec [b]LB[/b][/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Sortir du Menu Principal sur [b]Y[/b][/font]
[*][font=Verdana]Fermer le Tableau Des Scores (1) sur [b]Y[/b][/font]
[*][font=Verdana]V - autodestruction (L) sur [b]MENU[/b] avec [b]VIEW[/b][/font]
[*][font=Verdana]FPS - suicide (L) sur [b]MENU[/b] avec [b]VIEW[/b][/font]
[font=Verdana]
[/font][/list]
author: Navakyss

name: ContractTracker V2
summary: This tool is built for haulers in Star Citizen. Whether you're juggling multiple delivery contracts or just want a cleaner overview of your routes and rewards, this tracker keeps your missions organized, visible, and efficient and all in real-time.
description:
[b]V2 (New) 

I’m happy to introduce the V2 tracker, fully rewritten in C# to move beyond the limitations of the original python based version.

? ONE-BUTTON CONTRACT CAPTURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  • Press F3 (customizable) to instantly scan your mission panel
  • Automatically detects and parses:
    - Mission name and type
    - Pickup locations
    - Delivery destinations (supports multiple objectives!) (NEW?)
    - Cargo quantities (SCU)
    - Reward amounts (aUEC)
  • Smart OCR with Windows 10/11 built-in recognition + Tesseract fallback(NEW?)
?️ FLOATING OVERLAY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  • Always-on-top display that works while gaming
  • Shows all active contracts at a glance
  • Total reward counter
  • Fully customizable:
    - Drag to reposition anywhere on screen
    - Resize by dragging corners/edges(NEW?)
    - Adjustable transparency (20% - 100%)
    - Adjustable font size
    - Lock position to prevent accidental moves
  • Click-through mode - overlay becomes non-interactive so you can click 
    through it to your game(NEW?)
⌨️ GLOBAL HOTKEYS (Work In-Game!)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  All hotkeys work even when Star Citizen is focused:
  
  Default Hotkeys:
    F3  - Capture contracts from screen
    F4  - Retry capture (re-scan if OCR made mistakes)
    F5  - Reset session (clear all contracts)
    F6  - Open calibration overlay
    F9  - Toggle click-through mode
    F10 - Toggle overlay visibility
    -   - Make overlay more transparent(NEW?)
    =   - Make overlay more solid(NEW?)
  
  All hotkeys are fully rebindable!(NEW?)
   
? SMART LOCATION MAPPING(NEW?)
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  • Learns from your corrections! If OCR misreads a location name:(NEW?)
    1. Right-click the contract → Edit Location(NEW?)
    2. Enter the correct name(NEW?)
    3. The tool remembers this for future captures(NEW?)
  • Partial matching - add "Bueno Ravine" once and it matches variations like
    "Bueno Ravine on Pyro III" automatically(NEW?)
  • Built-in database of Star Citizen locations for fuzzy matching
? SCREEN CALIBRATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  • Visual overlay to select exactly where your mission panel appears
  • Draw a box around your contracts area for precise scanning
  • Saves your calibration between sessions
  • Supports any resolution and UI scaling(NEW?)
? RETRY FEATURE(NEW?)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  • OCR got "SAL-2" instead of "SAL-5"? Press F4 to retry!(NEW?)
  • Clears the last capture and re-scans(NEW?)
  • Keep retrying until you get accurate results(NEW?)
? DEBUG TOOLS(NEW?)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  • View raw OCR text output(NEW?)
  • Test parser with custom text(NEW?)
  • Export debug logs for troubleshooting(NEW?)
  • Load test images for offline testing(NEW?)
? PERSISTENT SETTINGS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  Everything is saved automatically:
    • Hotkey bindings
    • Overlay position and size
    • Transparency and font settings
    • Click-through state
    • Screen calibration region
    • Location mappings database
================================================================================
                           REQUIREMENTS
================================================================================
  • Windows 10 or Windows 11 (64-bit)
  • .NET 8.0 Runtime (Desktop)
  • Star Citizen (any version with mission panel)
  • Recommended: Borderless Windowed mode for best hotkey compatibility
================================================================================
                          INSTALLATION
================================================================================
  1. Download and extract to any folder
  2. Run "SC Contract Tracker.exe"
  3. (First time) Press F6 to calibrate - draw a box around your mission panel
  4. Open your contracts in Star Citizen
  5. Press F3 to capture!
================================================================================
                            QUICK START
================================================================================
  1. Launch Star Citizen in Borderless Windowed mode
  2. Start SC Contract Tracker
  3. Open your mobiglas (F1) → Contracts tab
  4. Select a hauling mission to view details
  5. Press F3 - your contract appears in the overlay!
  6. Repeat for all active contracts
  7. Use the overlay while flying to track your deliveries
================================================================================
                              TIPS
================================================================================
  • For best OCR accuracy, make sure the mission text is fully visible or you are not starving(Blur Effect)
  • If a location is wrong, right-click → Edit to add a correction
  • Use F4 (Retry) if numbers like 2/5 are misread
  • Lock the overlay (? button) once positioned to prevent accidental moves
  • Enable click-through (F9) when you need to interact with UI behind overlay
  • Adjust transparency with - and = keys while gaming
================================================================================
                           TROUBLESHOOTING
================================================================================
  "No contracts found"
    → Make sure you've calibrated (F6) to the correct screen region
    → Ensure the mission panel text is clearly visible
    → Try adjusting your in-game UI scale
  Hotkeys not working in-game
    → Run the tracker as Administrator
    → Make sure no other app is using the same hotkeys
  Wrong location names
    → Use the Locations tab to add corrections
    → The tool learns and improves over time
  OCR reads numbers wrong (2 vs 5, etc.)
    → Press F4 to retry - OCR can vary between captures
    → Add a location mapping if it's consistently wrong
================================================================================
                             CREDITS : Yuiki
================================================================================
  Developed for the Star Citizen community
  
  Technologies used:
    • Windows.Media.Ocr (Windows 10/11 built-in OCR)
    • Tesseract OCR (fallback engine)
    • WPF (.NET 8.0)
    • SQLite (local database)
    • FuzzySharp (location matching)
================================================================================
  Questions or feedback? Feel free to leave a comment!
  
  o7 Citizens - See you in the 'verse!
================================================================================







V1.0.2(OLD)
Works best on a single monitor setup.
Tested on standard 16:9 flat screen monitor at 1080p and 4K (non-HDR).

SUPPORTED LANGUAGES : English, German

Core Features[/b][list]
[*][b]Mission Tracking[/b]
Automatically captures and parses delivery contracts using OCR(Optical character recognition).[*][b]Reward Calculation[/b]
Instantly totals your earnings in aUEC.[*][b]Smart Organization[/b]
Groups missions by pickup and dropoff locations. Prevents duplicate entries.[/list][line][b]User Interface[/b][list]
[*]Sleek, modern UI with multiple themes:
[list]
[*]Default
[*]Star Citizen Dark
[*]Cyberpunk Neon
[*]Professional Dark
[*]Space Black
[/list][list]
[*]Optional borderless mode for a clean look
[/list][list]
[/list][*]Two display modes:
[list]
[*][b]Missions View[/b] – See detailed info for each contract
[*][b]Compiled View[/b] – View grouped cargo totals by route
[/list][/list][line][b]Floating Overlay[/b][list]
[*]Stays on top of the game window
[*]Adjustable transparency & font size
[*]Lockable position or free-drag mode
[*]Displays total rewards and route summaries
[/list][line][b]Hotkey Support[/b][list]
[*]Global hotkeys for:
[list]
[*]Toggling the overlay (Default: F5)
[*]Adding contracts (Default: F3)
[*]Resetting tracker data (Default: F10)
[/list][*]Fully customizable key bindings
[/list][line][b]Smart Features[/b][list]
[*]Fuzzy matching for inconsistent mission names
[*]Auto-normalization of location codes
[*]Duplicate detection
[*]Automatic cargo calculations
[/list][line][b]Technical Details[/b][list]
[*]OCR-powered contract capture
[*]Background hotkey listener
[*]Configurable screen/capture resolution
[*]Saves settings between sessions
[/list][line][b]Quality of Life[/b][list]
[*]Always-on-top option
[*]Clear, readable summaries and cargo visuals
[*]Position presets for overlay placement
[*]Built-in UI customization panel
[/list][line][b]Perfect For Star Citizen Players Who Want To:[/b][list]
[*]Manage multiple deliveries at once
[*]See real-time contract info while flying
[*]Optimize delivery routes to save time
[*]Track earnings with zero manual entry
[*]Stay organized in chaotic cargo runs
[size=5]Installation[/size][list]
[*]Extract the ContractTracker folder from the downloaded .rar file
[*]Place the folder anywhere you like on your system
[*]Run ContractTracker.exe and wait for the software to start
[*]Click Or Scroll to the Calibration Tab and click Start Calibration
[*]Drag Green Box over your Reward and Contract objective ( Right side of your screen basically)
[*][img]https://i.imgur.com/V5wsIRC.png[/img]﻿
[size=6]FAQ[/size][size=5] – Is it safe to use? Yes, the software is safe to use. Any security warnings related to python.exe are false positives, which are common when bundling Python scripts into executable files.[/size][size=5]Some antivirus programs may flag the application for keylogger behavior. This is usually triggered by the hotkey listener and the configuration file that updates when you change settings. The tracker does not log or transmit any personal data.
[/size][size=5]You can review the VirusTotal scan of the main script here:[/size][url=https://www.virustotal.com/gui/file/ebf669237019b05d51e6c16c8e9ff773231ca71c61661b6a165eb7fa78cb1288]https://www.virustotal.com/gui/file/ebf669237019b05d51e6c16c8e9ff773231ca71c61661b6a165eb7fa78cb1288[/url][size=5]If you're still unsure, you're welcome to scan the executable yourself.[/size][/list][b][size=5]Location or item name not showing correctly?[/size][/b]If a location or item name isn't showing up properly, follow these steps:[list=1]
[*]Close the software

[*]Open the locations.txt file in the same folder

[*]Add the missing name using the same format as the rest: - NAMEHERE

[*]Save the file and restart the software

[/list][/list]This method avoids issues caused by differences in game font rendering or monitor settings. Using a predefined list ensures more consistent and accurate parsing.

author: CitizenGattoMatto

name: SC Controls Mapper (Joystick Gremlin)
summary: SC Controls Mapper enables mapping Star Citizen controls directly within Joystick Gremlin. No more spending hours mapping your joysticks and other devices over and over after each patch. It allows you to map all your devices once within Joystick Gremlin and avoid using the in-game keybinding mechanism.
description:
[size=4][b]Overview[/b][/size]

SC Controls Mapper is extension of Joystick Gremlin EX and adds the ability to map your devices directly to Star Citizen controls. The advantages of mapping inside Joystick Gremlin include:

[list]
[*]Map everything one time without worrying about the keybinding settings in Star Citizen
[*]Quickly remap controls while in-game without entering the Star Citizen menus
[*]Any device available in Joystick Gremlin can be mapped to a Star Citizen control
[*]All the features of Joystick Gremlin including modes, response curves, etc. are available as part of your mappings
[*]Create your own profiles and share them with the community if you want
[*]vJoy interfaces are always available so you can add or remove devices on-the-fly without impacting your game
[*]Keep your keybindings after each patch without having to remap (hopefully :)
[/list]
In the Files section, you will find the following:

[list]
[*][u]Control Profile for Star Citizen[/u] - this is the profile you import into Star Citizen
[*][u]VjoyCheck Profile[/u] - a profile used to identify the vJoy order in Star Citizen
[/list]
[size=4][b]Getting Started[/b][/size]
To leverage the SC Controls Mapper, you need to install the following:

[list]
[*][url=https://github.com/jc43081/JoystickGremlinSC/releases/latest]Joystick Gremlin SC[/url] [size=1](download and install the MSI)[/size]﻿
[*][url=https://github.com/jshafer817/vJoy/releases]vJoy[/url]﻿
[*][url=https://github.com/nefarius/HidHide/releases]HidHide[/url]
[/list]
To understand how these applications work together and for great instructions on installing them, please watch the following video. You only need to watch through the configuration of HidHide, but his basic example can be helpful if you have never used Joystick Gremlin. Follow the instructions below to leverage the new "Map to Star Citizen" action include in the SC version.
[youtube]W6CFPW4go0M[/youtube]

[size=4][b]Setting up SC Controls Mapper[/b][/size]

Once you have all of these installed, follow the instructions below to get SC Controls Mapper working:

[size=3]1. [i]vJoy Setup[/i][/size]

Star Citizen supports 128 buttons per device. In order to support mapping all the controls, five (5) vJoy devices are required to be configured. Follow the instructions from the above video to setup the first vJoy to have 128 buttons, the second vJoy to have 127, the third vJoy to have 126, the fourth vJoy to have 125, and the fifth vJoy to have 124. No hats are required. 

[size=3]2. [i]Star Citizen Setup[/i]
[/size]
For the mapper to work with Star Citizen, you must import the Control Profile included on the Files tab. NOTE: You may want to save your current Star Citizen mappings as a backup in case you want to go back to them. See [url=https://support.robertsspaceindustries.com/hc/en-us/articles/360000183328-Create-export-and-import-custom-profiles]this article﻿ from Star Citizen[/url] if you need more guidance.

[i][b]IMPORTANT: Star Citizen will reorder your joysticks every time you reboot your machine (or whenever it likes). You'll need to repeat steps 3 through 17 each time this happens. Once everything is setup, it takes less than 2 minutes to perform this update and can be done while in-game.[/b][/i]

Here are the instructions:

[list=1]
[*]RECOMMENDED: Hide your joysticks from Star Citizen using HidHide. If you don't and have more than 3 hardware devices, Star Citizen will not recognize all your devices (it only recognizes up to 8).
[*]Download the "layout_JG_StarCitizenMapping_X.X.X_exported.zip" and the "vJoyCheck.xml" files to your computer. Both are found in the [i]Files[/i] tab above. Unzip both into your Joystick Gremlin Ex directory (c:\users\<your user>\Joystick Gremlin Ex).
[*]Open Joystick Gremlin SC and then open the Profile "vJoyCheck.xml". Activate the profile by clicking the controller icon (it turns green).
[*]Start up Star Citizen and go to the Options menu. 
[*]Choose Keybindings from top menu and choose Advanced Controls Customization on the bottom left. Then change the device to Joystick/HOTAS (bottom right corner). 
[*]Click on + next to "Vehicle - Seats and Operator Modes" and then double click on "Emergency Seat Exit". Once it says waiting, press 1 on your keyboard. Write done the Input Number. (If there is no number, that is vJoy #1) [i](NOTE: If nothing happens, make sure you activated the VJoyCheck profile in JG)[/i]
[*]Do the same thing four more times using the keyboard 2 through 5 keys. Write down each Input Number in order.
[*]Go back out to the Main Menu in Star Citizen (this is required a step).
[*]In Joystick Gremlin SC, deactivate the VjoyCheck profile and then choose "SC vJoy Mapper" from the Tools menu. 
[*]In the vJoy Order box, enter the numbers you wrote down in the same order capture (separate each number by a comma - ex. 1,3,4,2,5)
[*]Click "Select Control Profile XML" and then select "layout_JG_StarCitizenMapping_X-X-X_exported.xml" (this is the file you downloaded) for Base Control Profile
[*]Click "Select your SC Folder" and select the appropriate version (ex. LIVE, PTU, EPTU, etc.)
[*]Click on bottom button titled "6. Click HERE to run Mapper".  A success message should appear.
[*]In Star Citizen, go back to the Options menu (you must start from the main menu to refresh the Control Profiles list)
[*]Choose Keybindings from top menu and choose Advanced Controls Customization
[*]Then on the bottom right, click the "Control Profiles" button and then select "JG_StarCitizenMapping_X.X.X" from the list. (match X.X.X to the file you downloaded)
[*]Finally, map the five Joysticks to vJoy #1 through vJoy #5. (ex. first Joystick to vJoy #1, second joystick to vJoy #2, etc.). Click Load to load the bindings.
[/list]
NOTE: 4.x and above has a bug when importing MFD mappings. It is a known issue. All other mappings work fine so you only have to do the following if you are using MFD mappings. To work around this, an alternative way to import the Control Profile into Star Citizen is to open the command window (use ~) and then type following commands:
[i]pp_rebindkeys layout_JG_StarCitizenMapping_4-x-x_exported
pp_rebindkeys joystick 2 3
pp_rebindkeys joystick 4 5
[/i]Close the command window (use ~ again). If this doesn't work, you can reimport the mapping the normal way but MFD mappings won't work.

[size=3]3. [/size][i][size=3]Joystick Gremlin Profile[/size]

[/i]The final step is to create or load a Joystick Gremlin Profile. The profile can be stored wherever you like on your computer. It is recommended to put your profiles in the Joystick Gremlin folder automatically created on initial launch (C:\Users\<your user name>\Joystick Gremlin EX).  

To create a new profile, open up Joystick Gremlin and select "File" and the "New Profile" from the menu. When creating a new profile, you need to populate the Controls Mapping field on the Settings tab for the "Mapping to Star Citizen" action to work. Set Controls Mapping to "controls_mappings\StarCitizenControlsMappings-x.x.x.json" where X.X.X matches the Control Profile file you downloaded).

If you are using a profile created for joysticks you don't have, that profile can still be used. Instead of "New Profile" or "Open Profile, use the "Modify Profile" option in the "File" menu to load up the profile. You will see a list of functions that have been mapped within the profile. From there, go through and map your devices to each option. Once completed, click Save to create your new profile. You can then load it into Joystick Gremlin and tweak it from there. A really big time saver!!

[b][size=4]Mapping to Star Citizen Controls in Joystick Gremlin

[/size][/b]If you are not familiar with Joystick Gremlin, please check out [url=https://whitemagic.github.io/JoystickGremlin/interface/]this webpage[/url] for how to configure it's standard features. Additionally, the SC version extends Ex so also check out it's [url=https://github.com/jc43081/JoystickGremlinSC#joystick-gremlin-ex---134014ex-m22]Readme[/url] for the additional features added by it (remote control, osc support).

Before beginning your mappings, it is recommended you make "Map to Star Citizen" the default action. To do this, choose "Tools" and then "Options" from the menu. Change the default action to "Map to Star Citizen". Close the window.

To map your device to the Star Citizen controls, you'll need to connect your device so it is recognized by Joystick Gremlin. To identify a button, click the button or move the axis. Joystick Gremlin will highlight the associated control within the interface (this can be turned off in Tools... Options and then uncheck "Highlight currently used...").

Once you click on the button or axis you want to map, select [i]Add[/i] next "Map to Star Citizen". You can select the Category and then the desired Control. Categories and Controls are taken directly from the Star Citizen keybinding menu in order to make it as easy as possible. 

[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/2246/images/23/23-1712799809-1551331352.png[/img]

[i]Mapping Tips:[/i]
[list]
[*]In order to better understand your mappings, the Action Description is automatically updated for you. However, if multiple mappings are created for a single control (one example of this is when mapping a hat), the default description will be "Multiple Actions Defined...". You can override this by adding a "Description" action and entering in your own description in the corresponding textbox. This will override any automatic updates.
[/list]
[list]
[*]Multiple mappings can be added to the same control. When the button or axis is used, all mappings will be fired. This can be useful when a control is used in various game modes and the action taken varies based on which game mode is currently being applied (mining, salvage, scanning, etc.)
[/list]
[list]
[*]One great feature of JoyStick Gremlin is the ability to define modes within the tool. These modes are specific to Gremlin and are enabled through one of your buttons (either toggle temporarily or switch between modes). See the Joystick Gremlin documentation for more info. There is also a great video explaining how they work - [url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Jy53sR7UjWY]Joystick Gremlin | Modes & Switching (youtube.com)[/url]
[/list]
[i]NOTE: While you can still use the Remap and vJoyRemap actions, it is recommended that you NOT use these to map controls to the first 5 vJoys. If you do, it will likely conflict with other SC actions and create unpredictable results.[/i]
 
[size=4][b]Updating for Star Citizen Patches[/b][/size]
When Star Citizen updates to a new patch, there are often changes to the keybindings. Bindings can be moved between categories, removed, added, etc. When a new patch comes out, new files for the patch will be provided as quickly as possible (hopefully while still in the PTU). Here the steps needed to upgrade:

[list=1]
[*]Download and install the version of [url=https://github.com/jc43081/JoystickGremlinSC/releases/latest]Joystick Gremlin SC[/url]﻿ that supports the patch. A listing of impacted bindings should be included in the version release notes.  Take note of what has changed and whether it will impact your JG profile. 
[*]Open Joystick Gremlin SC and load your profile (likely will happen automatically). Go to the Settings tab and update the Controls Mapping textbox to match the latest control profile version. (ex. change 4.x.x to 4.5.x)
[*]Click the "Update Profile" button. You will be prompted to save your profile. You must save for the new Controls Mappings to be applied. Your profile will be reloaded and it is now ready for use.
[*]From the Files section, download the latest Star Citizen Control Profile and run the [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/starcitizen/articles/2]SC vJoy Mapper[/url]﻿﻿ tool to update your controls within Star Citizen itself. 
[*]You will want to double check your JG Profile against the list of binding changes (found on the Release page in Github) to ensure all your binding will still work appropriately. The description of any remapped controls is updated to reflect the change, so you may want to click through your bindings to verify them.
[/list]
[i]Credits:[/i]
WhiteMagic for the initial Joystick Gremlin version
Muchimi for the Joystick Gremlin EX version
author: jc43081

name: Mod Config Menu (UI)
summary: A menu for configuring mods <3
description:
[b]Features:[/b]
[list]
[*][b]In Game UI for configuring mod settings[/b]
[*][b][b]Controls mod config json files from in game[/b][/b]
[*][b][b][b]Easy to implement for mod developers[/b][/b][/b]
[*][b]Easy to use for players / users[/b]
[*][b]Designed after default game UI[/b]
[*][b]Thats All.. Only mod menu ting.[/b]
[/list][b]
Installation / How To Setup:[/b]
[list]
[*][b]Download & Extract THIS MODS ZIP FILE[/b]
[*][b]Download & Extract [url=https://github.com/UE4SS-RE/RE-UE4SS/releases]RE-UE4SS[/url]﻿ [/b]v3+[b]. [color=#ff0000]DO NOT USE UE4SS V2.5.2 - IT WONT FIND YOUR MOD CONFIG JSON FILES. [/color][/b]
[*]Note: Use a program such as winrar, winzip, 7zip, etc - to unzip the downloaded zip archive to copy the actual files. do not copy the zip/rar files.
[*][b]Copy Contents of UE4SS into your games binaries/win64 folder [/b]("\Palword\Pal\Binaries\Win64") (\WinGDK instead of \Win64 for gamepass users)
[*][b]MERGE the files (the Pal folder) from THIS MOD into your games "Pal" folder [/b]("\Palword\Pal")
[*](if using gamepass, move the files copied during the previous merge step from Win64 winto WinGDK)
[/list]
[b][color=#ff7700]NOTE: DO NOT TRY TO INSTALL THIS MOD ON YOUR SERVER. IT AFFECTS GAME CLIENTS ONLY. INSTALL IT TO YOUR LOCAL GAME FILES. [/color][/b][b][b]
[/b][/b][b]

For Mod Developers:
[/b]The [url=https://github.com/dekita/palworld-modconfig-devhelp]first draft of the help guide for developers[/url]﻿ who wish to use this interface for managing their own mod configuration options. Let me know if there are any missing features you might need or want! <3
[b]
Known Issues:

name: MapUnlocker
summary: Unlock the entire map / Works in multiplayer!
description:
The mod only shows the full map (removes the fog of war from unexplored areas). The mod does [b]NOT[/b] activate travel points; they must be explored independently.

Tested on Steam [b]version 0.1.4.1[/b] on a local server, community servers and official servers.
Game Pass version has [b]NOT[/b] been tested.

[b]Works in multiplayer![/b]


[b]Installation[/b]

[b]Steam[/b]
[list]
[*]Optionally backup you save file.
[*]Delete xinput1_3.dll in \Palword\Pal\Binaries\Win64 if you installed the xinput version of REUE4SS.
[*]Download REUE4SS: [url=https://github.com/UE4SS-RE/RE-UE4SS/releases]Releases · UE4SS-RE/RE-UE4SS (github.com)[/url]. Latest version is 3, direct link to 3: [url=https://github.com/UE4SS-RE/RE-UE4SS/releases/tag/v3.0.0]Release v3.0.0 · UE4SS-RE/RE-UE4SS (github.com)[/url]. File name: "UE4SS_v3.0.0.zip".
[*]Install REUE4SS in \steamapps\common\Palworld\Pal\Binaries\Win64\.
[*]Download the mod.
[*]Install the mod in \steamapps\common\Palworld\Pal\Binaries\Win64\Mods.
[/list]
[b]Game Pass[/b]
Thanks to users [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/palworld/users/66404356]Crazyon69[/url] and [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/palworld/users/9157639]AirborneMuffins[/url] for finding this solution and sharing it in the [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/palworld/mods/13?tab=posts]comments section of the Remove flying stamina cost mod[/url].
[list]
[*]Delete xinput1_4.dll in \Palword\Pal\Binaries\Win64 if you installed the xinput version of REUE4SS.
[*]Download REUE4SS: [url=https://github.com/UE4SS-RE/RE-UE4SS/releases]Releases · UE4SS-RE/RE-UE4SS (github.com)[/url]. Latest version is 3, direct link to 3: [url=https://github.com/UE4SS-RE/RE-UE4SS/releases/tag/v3.0.0]Release v3.0.0 · UE4SS-RE/RE-UE4SS (github.com)[/url]. File name: "UE4SS_v3.0.0.zip"
[*]Extract to C:\XboxGames\Palworld\Content\Pal\Binaries\WinGDK\
Alternatively, some users are reporting UE4SS should be installed in the following location: (C:\Users\"UserName"\AppData\Local\Pal\Saved\Config\WinGDK\)[*]In the UE4SS-settings.ini file, set bUseUObjectArrayCache to false.
[*]Download the mod.
[*]Install the mod in C:\XboxGames\Palworld\Content\Pal\Binaries\WinGDK\Mods.
[*]Need to enable mod in mods.txt manually, add [i]MapUnlocker [/i][i]: 1[/i] line for that.
[/list]

author: W1ns

name: AlwaysFastTravel
summary:
Now you can teleport anywhere by pressing M to open the map (You still need to unlock them manually)
Plus, you can literally move faster (increase movement speed)
description:
----------------
Regarding the lag when opening maps: this is because 0.2.x added a lot of extra tests to avoid bugs, and if you're sure you don't need them, 0.1.0 actually works perfectly fine and is more efficient.

Regarding 0.1.4: It runs fine on my local steam version, haven't tested it in multiplayer.

If you encounter problems [b]please attach the UE4SS.log [/b]from the game directory this will make it easier to figure out what's wrong.

----------------

You need to install https://github.com/UE4SS-RE/RE-UE4SS/ 

Normally, you should install this [url=https://github.com/UE4SS-RE/RE-UE4SS/releases/download/v2.5.2/UE4SS_Xinput_v2.5.2.zip]UE4SS_Xinput_v2.5.2.zip[/url] version and put it inside "X:\SteamLibrary\steamapps\common\Palworld\Pal\Binaries\Win64" if you're using steam. Or, double check if there is a Palworld-Win64-Shipping.exe when you install the UE4SS and the mod.

UE4SS was recently updated to 3.0, This mod is working under 3.0. But, if you have recently upgraded UE4SS, please remember to remove the old xinput_whatever.dll to avoid conflict.


Then extract the mod into the Mods directory, which should look like

- Mods
  - AlwaysFastTravel
    - enabled.txt
    - Scripts
      - main.lua

It overloads "Can Fast Travel" in WBP_Map_Base and "CanFastTravel" in WBP_Map_Body.


Thanks @Shredu2bits for clarifying, this mod will allow you to use fast travel everywhere in the game, but it will not unlock the travel points for you for now.

Also, it seems like a lot of people are experiencing crashes when turned on, you can try turning off bUseUObjectArrayCache in UE4SS-settings.ini, and/or try using this version below:
https://github.com/UE4SS-RE/RE-UE4SS/issues/309#issuecomment-1911975127

----- Game Tweak ------
A number of people have responded that this mod is not compatible with game tweaks. Unfortunately I can't figure out what exactly is causing this.
Because at least in my local game they work together so I cannot reproduce. Also, in its default configuration this mod doesn't change anything that game tweaks changes, except that they both register callback functions in /Script/Engine.PlayerController:ClientRestart.
But that shouldn't cause problems, at least as far as I understand it.
-----------

About the version: 
0.1 is most simple with no extra checks. As many mentioned, it causes bug if you travel inside dungeon or flying. But that can be solved by restart the game.
If you don't need those features, you may stay with v0.1.
The added checks will run checklist when opening the map, so that slows down the map opening.


----- Features & Key bingdings ----- 

Features can be turn on and off in the beginning of the main.lua for this mod, incuding the added safe/verysafe mode and fastmove, which I change the default setting to disable in this version.
Also if you encounter keys collision with other mods, you can also change that settings.

ALT+S Apply the map hooking on safe or verysafe mode.

ALT+J Esacpe from dungeon

ALT+Z Turn on Fast Move mode, to set everything back, you can go back to title and join again

ALT+U Activate all fast travel point, but this action does **NOT** add them to the map, so you still need to move close to them to use it. 


----- Changelogs ----- 
-----
Changelog v0.2.6
* :: Wrong version was uploaded yesterday

-----
Changelog v0.2.5
* :: Optimized the PalUtility logic
Hopefully it works

-----
Changelog v0.2.4
* :: Removed move.lua
It seems that it causes problem when loading this file, I moved its content to the main.lua

-----
Changelog v0.2.3
* :: Fast Movement on Riding
Now Alt+Z changes the speed of the current controlled character, instead of the player.

-----
Changelog v0.2.2
* :: Fixed Dungeon Judgement
Use IsInStage to determine if currently in any dungeon, instead of existance of a dungeon exit.

-----
Changelog v0.2.1
* :: Disabled Fast movement by default
In case you're using game  tweak, you can enable it by turning fast mode to true

* :: Limited Fast Travel
Now you can only fast travel if you movement mode is walking, this prevents you from 
fast travel when you're flying, which many reports cause bug in the bug.
You can change this setting by chaning allowtravelmodes

-----
Changelog v0.2
* :: Added safemode and verysafemode
Both modes were added due to complaints received about the game crashing. I'm not quite sure if they help, but my understanding is that the UE4SS hook is causing these issues.
In safemode, the mod only registers the hook after pressing Alt+S for the first time, meaning that the mod doesn't work while reading the file.
In verysafemode the mod never registers the hook, which means that every time you open the map you need to manually press Alt+S to unlock the teleport point, which I think should provide better stability.

* :: Disable fast travel in dungeons
Fast traveling in dungeons can cause problems, add this switch to disable it in dungeons

* :: Fast movement
Due to the limitations of the game and UE4SS, it may be easier to edit the archive if you want to unlock teleportation points.
This feature can drastically increase your machine power so you can run the map yourself to unlock teleportation points.
Please note that jumping higher means you are more likely to die from the fall!

* :: Dungeon Runaway
This function opens a dialog box to leave the dungeon, please note that this function has not been tested a lot, because I was unlucky not to encounter so many dungeons.
What I know must be a problem is that if you're already dead, don't use this shortcut, it'll get stuck respawning.

* :: Unlock all fast travel
Unlock all fast travel. But it's only unlocked, you can't use them until you actually pass them in the world. This is because unlocking icons on the map and unlocking fast travel (by pressing F) itself are two separate actions in this game; the former requires you to actually pass through these fast teleports for them to be recorded on your map. This feature will only do the latter.
author: yangff

name: PalEdit
summary: PalEdit is a simple user interface used for the editing and generation of Pals into save file data. Edit traits onto Pals so you don't have to deal with the whole breeding process trying to get perfect skills.
description:
PalEdit is a simple user interface used for the editing and generation of Pals into save file data. Edit traits onto Pals so you don't have to deal with the whole breeding process trying to get perfect skills. 

To add Pals to your saves, clone any pal that you like using the button under their picture and then change their species, stats, or whatever. Deleting of pals is in the works.

! ALWAYS MAKE SURE TO BACKUP YOUR SAVE FILES !

Join our [url=https://discord.gg/3FBpDKbs5R]Discord Server[/url] to get faster and more indepth support and help. If you find any huge bugs, please join the discord so that we can diagnose it and fix it for everyone using PalEdit.﻿

Alternatively to NexusMods, you can download it from the Github releases page [url=https://github.com/EternalWraith/PalEdit/releases]here[/url] or download the source.

Want to give a tip? [url=https://ko-fi.com/eternalwraith]I have a Ko-fi account[/url]; you don't have to but if you do, it's much appreciated :D

[url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=a66-Sgm24EU&ab_channel=T%C3%A0iNguy%E1%BB%85nC%C3%B4ngKh%C3%A1nh]Thank you to this user for uploading a how-to video, much appreciated.

[/url]Getting a False Positive from Windows Defender? [url=https://www.virustotal.com/gui/file/cefbb2aa0b6b3f343a51f003da466dc8b7bbd601a60fdbfd4723ebe52e175538?nocache=1]See the virus report here[/url]. It is very common for unsigned executables to get flagged with a False Positive. If you're worried about it, download the source code from GitHub and compile it for yourself.
author: Eternal Wraith

name: Creative Menu
summary: Lets you spawn Pals/NPCs and edit their Stats/IVs before spawning them in and it also has a tab for giving yourself any item in the game!
description:
[size=3]Hello! This is a more 'advanced' mod that lets you spawn in Pals or you may also give them to yourself directly. Now here's the fun part, you may customize their stats/IVs/abilities/passives to your liking before spawning them in which means you can give Lucky/Legend passive to non shiny or legendary Pals! You may also give yourself any item in the game.

Spawning items/pals/npcs works in multiplayer, however you will need to either be an admin or must be permitted by the host through Options > Permission Management and both the host and client must have this mod.[/size][size=3]
[/size]

[size=5][b]INSTALLATION
[/b][/size]
[b]For Steam:[/b] Install into steamapps\common\Palworld\Pal\Content\Paks\~mods and make sure to delete CreativeMenu.pak inside LogicMods folder if you had the old version at some point.

[size=5][b]HOW TO USE
[/b][/size]
You can open the menu with F1 and if you're using the mod for the first time, you'll get a popup asking you to change the keybind. By default it will be set to F1 and if you don't want to change the keybind, just hit OK. If you missed the first time popup, you can open the menu by holding the crouch button for 3 seconds.

name: Pal Analyzer
summary: Displays details and stats about wild pals just by hovering your cursor over them. You'll unlock more stats as you go, making the discovery feel balanced and fair (configuration options available). The goal of Pal Analyzer is to feel like a vanilla game system, so it uses as much of the existing UI elements as possible to seamlessly blend in!
description:
[center][color=#e69138][size=6][b][size=4][b][u][size=6][b][size=5][b][size=6][b]Please Read[/b][/size][/b][/size][/b][/size][/u][/b][/size][/b][/size][/color][/center][size=3]Please read the full installation instructions for your platform as well as the [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/palworld/articles/67]Troubleshooting[/url] article before seeking additional help.  If you're still having trouble or have suggestions please join me on [url=https://discord.gg/W6Y4ps699z]Discord[/url]﻿.
Thank you for the support![/size][size=3]

[/size][color=#ff0000][u][b][size=4]This mod does not need to be and should not be installed on your dedicated server.  This is a client side only mod and it works in both single and multiplayer[/size][/b][/u][/color][size=3][color=#ff0000][u][b].[/b][/u][/color]

By default this mod will show you the pals information above their head [b][color=#ffff00]ONLY while holding the LeftAlt key (for keyboard) or the Right Bumper (for gamepad)[/color].  [/b]However, [/size][size=3]This can be changed via [/size][size=3]in-game configuration menu ([size=3]Opened by pressing 'Shift + O' by default)[/size].[/size][color=#ffd966][size=5][b]

[/b][/size][/color][size=3]By default the stats you can see within the analyze tooltip are progressively unlocked.  If you haven't captured the species of pal you are trying to analyze most of it's stats will show up as '[b]???[/b]'.  [b][color=#ffff00]You will not be able to see all stats until you have captured at least 10 of that species of pal unless configured otherwise.[/color][/b]  This is to make the mod feel balanced and fair.

The default unlocks are:

[b]0 Captures[/b] - You can see stats like Attack and Defense
[b]5 Captures[/b] - You unlock the ability to see active abilities
[b]10 Captures[/b] - [size=3]You unlock the ability to[/size] see passive abilities

All of these settings are configurable via the in-game configuration menu ([size=3][size=3]Opened by pressing 'Shift + O' by default[/size][/size]).[/size][size=3]  If you enjoy this mod please consider hitting the endorse button, or even the vote button to vote this mod for mod of the month[size=3].[/size][/size][color=#ffd966][size=5][b]

[/b][/size][/color][color=#e69138][size=4][size=2][size=5][size=5][size=3][size=5][size=6][b][u][center]Installation[/center][/u][left][color=#e69138][size=4][b]Automatic Installation[/b][/size][/color][color=#f6b26b][size=4][size=2][color=#ffffff][size=2](via [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/about/vortex/]Vortex Mod Manager[/url])[/size][/color][/size][/size][/color][/left][/b][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/color][color=#f6b26b][size=4][size=2][list=1]
[*][color=#ffffff][size=3]If you don't already have [b]UE4SS[/b] installed, please install[/size][/color][color=#ff0000][size=3] [/size][/color][color=#ff0000][size=3][b]version 3.0 or higher[/b][/size][/color][color=#ffffff][size=3]. See '[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/palworld/articles/69]Installing UE4SS'[/url]﻿[/size][/color]
[*][color=#ffffff][size=3]Go to the files tab (located at the top of the mod page)[/size][/color]
[*][color=#ffffff][size=3]Click '[b]Mod Manager Download[/b]' for the latest version of [b]Pal Analyzer[/b][/size][/color]
[*][color=#ffffff][size=3]That's it!  Press '[b]Shift + O[/b]' while in game to open the options menu.[/size][/color]

[/list][color=#ffffff][size=3][size=2][size=2][size=3][size=3][size=2][size=3]If you have issues please check [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/palworld/articles/67]here[/url]﻿ for possible solutions.  If you're still having issues find me on [url=https://discord.gg/W6Y4ps699z]Discord[/url]﻿.[/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/color][/size][/size][/color]
[color=#f6b26b][size=4][size=2][list=1]
[color=#ffffff]
[/color][/list][/size][/size][/color][size=4][b][color=#e69138]Manual Installation[/color][/b][/size][color=#f6b26b][size=4][size=2][size=2][size=4][b][color=#e69138]
[/color][/b][/size][list=1]
[*][color=#ffffff][size=3][size=2][size=3]If you don't already have [b]UE4SS[/b] installed, please install it. See '[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/palworld/articles/69]Installing UE4SS'[/url]﻿[/size][/size][/size][/color]
[*][color=#ffffff][size=3]Navigate to your PalWorld game directory[/size][/color]
[color=#ffffff][size=3][size=2][size=3][b] ﻿Steam[/b]: Right click on game in Steam -> Manage -> Browse Local Files
﻿[b]Xbox App[/b]: Select Palworld, click the three dot button ([b] ...[/b] ) and click Manage from the dropdown.  Then select the FILES tab ﻿and click BROWSE...[/size][/size][/size][/color][color=#ffffff]
[/color][*][color=#ffffff][size=3]Navigate to the LogicMods folder in your PalWorld game directory:[/size][/color]
[quote][color=#ffffff][size=3][b]...\Pal\Content\Paks\LogicMods\[/b][/size][/color][/quote][color=#ffffff]
[/color][*][color=#ffffff][size=3]Download and extract the latest version of the mod from the files tab.  Be sure to double check that you are downloading the correct version for your platform (Steam or Xbox App)[/size][/color]
[*][color=#ffffff][size=3]Move the contents of the extracted mod folder to the [b]LogicMods[/b] folder from above ([size=2][size=3][b]...\Pal\Content\Paks\LogicMods\[/b][/size][/size]).  Be sure to only copy the contents and not the entire folder.[/size][/color]
[*][color=#ffffff][size=3][size=3][color=#f6b26b][size=4][size=2][color=#ffffff][size=3]That's it!  Press '[b]Shift + O[/b]' while in game to open the options menu.[/size][/color][/size][/size][/color][/size][/size][/color]
[color=#ffffff][size=3][size=3][color=#f6b26b][size=4][size=2][color=#ffffff][size=3]
[/size][/color][/size][/size][/color][/size][/size][/color]
[/list][color=#f6b26b][size=4][size=2][color=#ffffff][size=3][size=2][size=2][size=3][size=3][size=2][size=3]If you have issues please check [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/palworld/articles/67]here[/url]﻿ for possible solutions.  If you're still having issues find me on [url=https://discord.gg/W6Y4ps699z]Discord[/url]﻿.[/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/color][/size][/size][/color][/size][/size][/size][/color][size=2]

[/size][color=#6aa84f][size=5][b]Configuration Options[/b][/size][/color][color=#dd7e6b][size=5][b]
[/b][/size][/color][size=3]Settings can be configured in game by pressing '[b]Shift + O[/b]' on your keyboard while loaded into a level.  Keep in mind that this keybind ('[b]Shift + O[/b]') can be changed in the settings menu.

Configuration file is stored here:
[quote][b]...\Pal\Content\Paks\LogicMods\PalAnalyzerConfig.json[/b][/quote]
If you would like to restore your settings to default, delete this configuration file.[/size][color=#dd7e6b][size=5][b]

Find me on[/size][/b][/size][color=#f6b26b][size=4][b][size=4] [url=https://discord.gg/ddXAhVu4ZF][/url][url=https://discord.gg/T9TbacH5Ca]Discord[/url][url=https://discord.gg/T9TbacH5Ca][/url]﻿[url=https://discord.gg/T9TbacH5Ca].[/url]﻿[/size][/b][/size][/color][/center]

name: T-Box Pal Dressing Facility Skin Swap
summary:
Basically, this adds my skins to the Palworld Pal Dressing Facility.

Steam Only.
description:
This is a pack that will contain all my mods in one (literally). You won't need the other mods for this one to work. Just keep in mind that since it's the pal dressing facility, you need to manually apply the desired skin to the desired pal. You can keep the previous mods as a swap for the wild pal.

[size=5]Multiplayer[/size]
[b]The latest version of this mod works on dedicated.[/b]

This mod has been tested on Steam, both single player and dedicated and works provided both the client and the server have the mod installed, [b]and the server has UE4SS 3 installed.[/b]

For coop P2P multiplayer, it may work if everyone installs the mod on their client. If you do attempt to get it working in multiplayer, sharing your outcome (whether successful or failure) in the comments section helps.

[size=5]Incompatibilities[/size]
This mod will most likely be incompatible with any other mod that changes the same values

[size=5]Credits
[/size]Thanks to [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/palworld/users/187864722]Eld3rBrain[/url] for their testing and providing a solution to get the mod working properly on dedicated.

[/size]Finally, if you are experiencing a bug you believe is part of this mod please view the steps on [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/palworld/mods/11]Carry weight increase[/url] and make sure to include the information under the 'Bugs' heading.

name: PalInfo
summary: PalInfo is a UE4SS-based mod for Palworld that displays detailed information about any Pal when you aim at it. This mod provides comprehensive stats including HP, attack power, defense, individual values (IVs), soul bonuses, trust levels, passive skills, active skills, and work suitability all displayed in real-time as you hover over Pals.
description:
[center][size=6][b][/b][/size][/center][center][size=6][color=#FFD700][b]PalInfo[/b][/color][/size][/center][color=#87CEEB][size=4]━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━[/size][/color]
[size=5][b][color=#FFD700]Overview[/color][/b][/size]
[b]PalInfo[/b] is a UE4SS-based mod for Palworld that displays detailed information about any Pal when you[color=#ff0000][size=3][b] aim[/b][/size][/color] at it. This mod provides comprehensive stats including HP, attack power, defense, individual values (IVs), soul bonuses, trust levels, passive abilities, active skills, and work suitability all displayed in real-time as you hover over Pals.
[size=5][b][color=#FFD700]Features[/color][/b][/size]
[list]
[*] [b]Real-time Pal information[/b] display while [b][u][color=#ff0000][size=3]aiming[/size][/color][/u][/b]
[*] [b]Customizable[/b] stat display options
[*] Support for [b]custom Pal nicknames[/b]
[*] [b]Status indicators[/b] (injured/sick)
[*] [b]Element type[/b] display
[*] [b]Condensation rank[/b] information
[*] [b]Trust and friendship[/b] tracking
[*] [b]Passive skill[/b] and [b]Active skill[/b]
[*] [b]Work suitability[/b] information
[*] [b]Special tags[/b] for rare Pals (Lucky, Alpha, Tower)
[/list]
[color=#00ffff][size=5][b]Need on Client Side / Player PC | Works on servers also directly[/b][/size][/color]
[color=#87CEEB][size=4]━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━[/size][/color]
[size=5][b][color=#FFD700]Installation[/color][/b][/size]
[size=4][b]Requirements[/b][/size]
[list]
[*] [b]Palworld[/b] (Steam version)
[*] [b]UE4SS[/b] installed : [url=https://github.com/Okaetsu/RE-UE4SS/releases/download/experimental-palworld/UE4SS-Palworld.zip][color=#00ff00]Click here to download[/color][/url]﻿
[*] The mod folder structure must be in: [i]Pal/Binaries/Win64/ue4ss/Mods/[/i]
[/list]
[size=4][b]Installation Steps[/b][/size]
1. [b]Download and locate the mod folder[/b] at:
[code]C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Palworld\Pal\Binaries\Win64\ue4ss\Mods\
[/code]
2. [b]Drop PalInfo folder in Mods folder and verify structure[/b]:
[code]?ue4ss
┣ ?Mods
┃ ┣ ?PalInfo
┃ ┃ ┣ ?Scripts
┃ ┃ ┃ ┣ ?config.lua
┃ ┃ ┃ ┣ ?localized.lua
┃ ┃ ┃ ┗ ?main.lua
┃ ┃ ┗ ?enabled.txt
┣ ?imgui.ini
┣ ?LICENSE
┣ ?MemberVariableLayout.ini
┣ ?UE4SS-settings.ini
┣ ?UE4SS.dll
┗ ?UE4SS.log
[/code]
author: AYNJ

name: LevelScaledExp
summary: Automatically scales EXP rate based on player level milestones. As you reach new level thresholds, EXP increases for everyone — keeping lower level players competitive. Includes a server version (tracks highest level player) and a solo version. Fully configurable milestones and multipliers.
description:
LevelScaledExp is a catch-up EXP mechanic for Palworld that automatically scales the EXP rate based on player level milestones. As you or your teammates reach new level thresholds, the EXP rate increases for everyone — keeping lower level players competitive and making late game progression feel rewarding.
Comes in two versions — a [b]server-side mod[/b] for dedicated servers that tracks the highest level player online and adjusts EXP for the whole server, and a [b]solo version[/b] for single player that scales based on your own character level. Both versions are fully configurable with custom level thresholds and EXP multipliers.
author: kuronekoo1337

name: Chaos World Engine
summary: Chaos World Engine is a tactical in-game cheat hub for Palworld. Press F4 to open a custom UI and instantly adjust player speed, gravity, and collision (No-Clip to walk through walls). It also features God Mode, Infinite Stamina, and World Time control. Designed for single-player via UE4SS.
description:
[b]Chaos World Engine[/b] is a lightweight, highly stable, tactical cheat hub built specifically for Palworld single-player. Running natively through UE4SS, it injects a custom-themed "Crimson" UI directly into your game, allowing you to manipulate the physics, environment, and your character's vitality in real-time.
Built with stability as the primary focus, this mod bypasses bloated features in favor of core, rock-solid enhancements that work seamlessly without crashing your game.
[line][b]?️ Core Features[/b][b]MOVEMENT & ENVIRONMENT[/b]
[list]
[*][b]Speed Multiplier:[/b] A real-time slider (1.0x to 15.0x) that overrides your base movement speed. Zoom across the map in seconds. [i](See important note below regarding sprinting).[/i]
[*][b]Gravity Scale:[/b] Adjust the world's gravity on your character (0.1 to 2.0). Lower the gravity to execute massive moon-jumps over mountains or safely glide down from massive heights.
[*][b]Time of Day Slider:[/b] Instantly change the world clock. Slide it forward to skip the night, or lock it to your preferred time for base building.
[/list][b]TACTICAL OVERRIDES[/b]
[list]
[*][b]No-Clip (Flying):[/b] Toggle collision entirely off. This custom script doesn't just let you walk through walls—it safely shifts your character into a "Flying" state so your WASD movement continues to work perfectly even when you have no floor beneath you.
[*][b]God Mode:[/b] Total and complete invincibility. Take zero damage from Pals, syndicate thugs, or fall damage.
[*][b]Infinite Stamina:[/b] Your stamina bar will instantly refresh. Climb the tallest cliffs, swim across oceans, and glide indefinitely without ever running out of breath.
[/list][line][b]⚙️ Installation Instructions[/b][b]Prerequisite:[/b] You [i]must[/i] have [url=https://github.com/UE4SS-RE/RE-UE4SS]UE4SS[/url] installed in your Palworld directory for this mod to work.
[list=1]
[*]Download the [code]ChaosWorldEngine_v1.0.0.zip[/code] file.

[*]Extract the contents of the zip file.

[*]Locate your Palworld Mods folder. The default Steam path is usually:[code]C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Palworld\Pal\Binaries\Win64\ue4ss\Mods\[/code]

[*]Drag and drop the entire extracted [b]ChaosWorldEngine[/b] folder into your [code]Mods[/code] folder.

[*][i](Optional but recommended)[/i] Open the [code]mods.txt[/code] file in your UE4SS folder and add [code]ChaosWorldEngine : 1[/code] to the bottom of the list to ensure it loads cleanly.

[*]Launch Palworld!

[/list][line][b]? Controls & Usage[/b][list]
[*][b][F4][/b] - Toggle the Chaos World Engine Hub Open / Closed.
[*]The menu is completely click-and-drag. Tweak the sliders and check the boxes as needed; the engine updates your character instantly.
[/list][line][b]⚠️ IMPORTANT WARNINGS (Please Read)[/b][list]
[*][b]NOT TESTED ON MULTIPLAYER[/b]
[*][b]THE SPRINTING QUIRK (DO NOT HOLD SHIFT):[/b] The Speed Multiplier drastically increases your [i]base[/i] walking speed. If you hold the [code]Shift
[/code] key while the speed mod is active, the game's default sprint logic will kick in and actually make you run [b]slower[/b] than the modded speed. Just use WASD!
[/list]
author: Enoch

name: Balanced Solo Arena Rewards (PalSchema)

[heading][font=Comic Sans MS][color=#e69138]Balanced Arena Solo Rewards[/color][/font][/heading]
[font=Arial][size=3]This is my first mod on Nexus! It enhances the rewards you earn from the Pal Arena's Solo mode Bronze through Master. Both first clears and repeat victories are made more rewarding. It's a great mod for anyone who enjoys the Arena and wants their effort to feel properly recognized! I think the values are properly balanced and thought out. author: Zarqueam

name: Legendary Pouch (Pal Schema)
summary: Adds a legendary pouch, an upgrade beyond the giant pouch that greatly increases your inventory capacity.
description:
[font=Calibri][center][img]https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xberkth/xberkth-stuff/refs/heads/main/endorsement_up.png[/img][/center]
[color=#0099FF][b][size=5]DESCRIPTION[/size][/b][/color]
Adds a new legendary pouch that surpasses the giant pouch, greatly increasing your carrying capacity. Perfect for long adventures and heavy resource gathering, this pouch represents the ultimate upgrade in storage, allowing players to carry more items than ever before.

[color=#0099FF][b][size=5]COLOSSAL POUCH[/size][/b][/color]
[size=2]?[/size] Unlock in the technology tree at level 64.
[size=2]?[/size] Cost 5 ancient technology points.
[size=2]?[/size] Provides 6 extra inventory slots.

[img]https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xberkth/xberkth-stuff/refs/heads/main/palworld-modding/colossal_pouch.png[/img]

[color=#0099FF][b][size=5]INSTALLATION[/size][/b][/color]
[color=#0099FF][b][size=3]1. PALSCHEMA[/size][/b][/color]
[size=2]?[/size] Install [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/palworld/mods/2361]PalSchema[/url]. Follow the official PalSchema [url=https://okaetsu.github.io/PalSchema/docs/installation]installation instructions[/url] to avoid any issue. Skip this step if you already have it installed.

[color=#0099FF][b][size=3]2. UE4SS[/size][/b][/color]
[size=2]?[/size] Install the latest [url=https://github.com/Okaetsu/RE-UE4SS/releases/tag/experimental-palworld]experimental UE4SS[/url]. Skip this step if you already have it installed.

[color=#0099FF][b][size=3]3. MOD[/size][/b][/color]
[size=2]?[/size] Go to your Palworld installation directory. On Steam, you can right-click the game, select Manage > Browse local files. If you are using the file explorer, path should be some like this: [code]C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Palworld[/code]

[size=2]?[/size] Download the [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/palworld/mods/3313?tab=files]mod[/url] and extract it directly to the Palworld directory that you just opened. Make sure to download the version of the mod for your platform.

[size=2]?[/size] After extracting the files, you should have the following structure:
[font=courier]? Palworld

author: xBerkth

name: Ultimate Engine Tweaks (Anti-Stutters - Lower Latency - No Film Grain - No Chromatic Aberration - Lossless)
summary: Definitive Unreal "Engine.ini" changes with the goal to remove most stutters, improve performance and stability, decrease input latency, improve picture clarity. All with no visual loss.
description:
[img]https://i.imgur.com/v16KHXx.jpeg[/img]

[b][size=6][center][font=Lucida Sans Unicode]Palworld - Ultimate Engine Tweaks[/font][/center][/size][/b]
After over [url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/P40L0X/mods]1 year of iterative work[/url] with 40+ other UE5 games, I would like to share my definitive, optimized, custom "Engine.ini" for the game.

My goal was to just include as many optimizations as possible (both for CPU/GPU/RAM/SSD) in order to remove most stuttering, improve performance, reduce input latency and improve picture clarity (e.g. by optimizing Film Grain, Motion Blur & removing Chromatic Aberration, optimizing DLSS/FSR Upscaling and Anti-Aliasing, reducing ghosting and shimmering) all without any visual loss nor introducing glitches or crashes.

If you want to know [u]exactly[/u] what every single optimization does and what their combined result is, have a look [url=https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1A71Q9jd0sm0MaCRWib8SJEY_urNk-Wuyl2pK5UI_4DI/edit?usp=sharing]here[/url]


[b]What to choose between the "VRR" and "No VRR" mod variants?[/b]
VRR stands for [url=https://www.rtings.com/tv/tests/motion/variable-refresh-rate]Variable Refresh Rate[/url]﻿, which can translate to G-Sync for NVIDIA or AMD Freesync for AMD.
You need to cross check if it is enabled both on your NVIDIA/AMD Control Panel and the Display itself (Monitor/TV)
If yes on both, use the "VRR" variant of the mod.
If no, use the "No VRR" variant instead.

Open the game at least once and close it, then you can directly download/extract my [b]Engine.ini[/b] to paste in:

[center][i][quote]%localappdata%\Pal\Saved\Config\Windows[/quote][/i]
(Press Win + R , type the path above and click Enter to open the folder directly)

then Right-Click on it -> Properties -> make sure the "[b]Read-Only[/b]" box is selected -> Apply/OK


[u][size=3]

For Xbox PC version only:[/size][/u][size=3][/size]

Go and paste it into:

[i][quote]%localappdata%\Pal\Saved\Config\WinGDK[/quote][/i]
(Press Win + R , type the path above and click Enter to open the folder directly)

then Right-Click on it -> Properties -> make sure the "[b]Read-Only[/b]" box is selected -> Apply/OK


[b](To Uninstall just delete the modded Engine.ini, the game will use its defaults on next launch)


[/b][u]
DO NOT USE TOGETHER WITH OTHERS "OPTIMIZATION" MODS
[/u](any other mod will be compatible)




For additional PC/Windows optimizations, I also highly recommend to follow and apply my:

[url=https://www.patreon.com/posts/88124101]PC Gaming: Optimized - The Ultimate Guide (including NVIDIA/AMD CP + G-Sync/Freesync + HDR Best Settings)[/url][list]
[/list]

[size=3]
All of this is [b]FREE[/b] but, if you found it useful and want to support my work, please consider to:[/size] 

﻿[url=https://www.paypal.com/donate/?hosted_button_id=9N5Z6W6BDTWBE][img]https://i.imgur.com/XiHaimn.png[/img]﻿[/url]

or

[url=https://www.patreon.com/techoptimized][img]https://i.imgur.com/SihtTss.png[/img][/url]

[b]Thank you![/b] ﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿??


Also don't forget to [url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/P40L0X/mods]check out my other mods[/url]﻿ ;)

Enjoy!
-P[/center]
