author: Convergence Team

name: Elden Ring Ultimate Cheat Engine Table (Best Table)
summary: Hexinton's Elden Ring Cheat Table offers extensive control over your gameplay experience. Features item adding items, player status editing, scripts for various in-game actions, player enhancements (infinite HP/FP/SP, etc.), game manipulation (speed, time), and advanced features like Param manipulation and Character Spawning.
description:
If you want to make Elden Ring your own game, create custom magic, spawn your own boss fights, and edit the world around you, then this table made by the top modders in ds3 is for you! 

This Elden Ring table has become well beyond what we expected in only 1 month after the game’s release, and has been making constant progress due to the many skilled members we have working daily in our community. Our table contains many basic features, but MANY MORE advanced scripts that have been refined over the years in Dark Souls 3, such as: Magic/Bullet Editor, ItemGib (GUI Version), script generator(with which you can easily make your own scripts), and Custom SummonSpirits.

Allows you to add every single Item in the Game

The Flag Logger allows you to Log Events like NPC Progression Boss states and doors opened and lets you add them directly to the table for you to Save

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Features Include (Too many features, it's impossible to list all of them here,so you can ignore this and download the table to check yourself):

->ItemGib/GigaGib add all/specific Items
->StatMultiplier
->WorldFlags
->Character Data
->UnlockAllGestures
->Scripts
->ParamPatcher
-Edit Weapons 
-Edit Armor
-Edit Attacks/Bullets
-Edit Effects
-Edit NPCs and Spirit Summons (Summon Bosses as Summons)
-Make your own Magic
->NPC Manager
->Display Target´s Status
->Teleport
->NoClip
->FreeCam
->Force TP
->Edit your own Stats and LVL

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

All ID Lists are compiled on our [url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240628035113/https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/u/3/d/e/2PACX-1vQ0LUsF2rBNa55jMq8KNVYFeyxnV_TinvJ9-xh6nzeWhp3OOnPYu_yNCslI2yorP7hFl47Bel4YE82G/pubhtml]Spreadsheet[/url] 

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Note: This table was only made with OFFLINE play in mind, we do not condone cheating online. Easy Anti-Cheat was implemented into ER so you will not be able to use this during online play.

How do I use Cheat Engine / Table and how do I bypass EAC Offline

Incase you have followed a different guide (e.g. WeMod Fearless, Nexus) undo any changes you have made so far.

[list=1]
[*]Head to [url=https://www.cheatengine.org/]https://www.cheatengine.org/[/url] click the big green button to download the latest version of Cheat Engine and run the setup file.
[*]Download the latest Table version from ⁠[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/48?tab=files]Nexusmods[/url]
[*]unzip the file and place the offline launcher inside your game folder: steamapps\common\ELDEN RING\Game
[*]Start the game directly from the game folder by double clicking the offline_launcher.bat
[*]Open the ct file downloaded from nexus and press [ Enable ]
[/list]

Common Questions

Q: Do I need to delete the bat file after I am done?
A: No starting the game via steam will launch it Online

Q: Does disabling EAC protect me from getting banned?
A: No Fromsoft uses an internal anti cheat made by them

Q: What could get me banned?
A: Currently the only confirmed things that ban/warn you are unavailable/cut content items or editing your stats to something that aren't possible by playing the game legit. Adding dlc spells without owning the dlc has not been tested yet but it banned in previous games so you should avoid that too.

Q: It still says EAC is not disabled
A: Make sure EAC isnt running anymore by checking your processes with task manager or simply restart your pc

Q: What does getting banned mean?
A: The game doesnt ban you from playing the game it softbans you meaning its going to restrict your online experience. You will only be able to play with others/see signs from others that are softbanned. (You can still play Seamless Co op without issues)

Using any Method to disable EAC other than the one we provide will end in you not being able to use some features of the Table.

Should you require any additional assistance, please feel free to contact us via Discord. We have a 24/7 AI Helper for general questions and an active support staff.
author: Hexinton

name: Elden Ring Item and Enemy Randomizer
summary: Randomizes bosses, enemies, and items, including key items and shops
description:
It's an item and enemy randomizer for Elden Ring which modifies the files loaded on game startup, formerly "Elden Ring Key Item Randomizer".

Items found in the world, shops items, enemy and boss drops, and character starting loadouts are all randomized. Key item randomization is supported. Most enemies can be randomized as well, with extensive customization for different enemy types.

Normally, all you need to complete the game is two Great Runes and the Rold Medallion. The key item randomizer can chain key items together, especially when the bias slider is higher. There are also options to add, change, and remove Great Rune requirements. In the most restrictive mode, you must collect all 7 Great Runes before you can challenge the final boss.

If you use this mod in a casual livestream or published video, please link this mod page from a chat command or video description if possible. If you use this mod in a unique live event like a showcase or a contest, please credit me (thefifthmatt) for the mod if it's reasonable to do so. There isn't a team behind this mod. I created it for people to use it, so please facilitate that if you manage to create engaging content from it. Thank you!

If you would like to provide feedback or playtest or see detailed changelogs, you can join the discord server at [url=https://discord.gg/QArcYud]https://discord.gg/QArcYud[/url] (also for Fog Gate Randomizer, Sekiro Randomizer, DS3 Static Randomizer). This mod is under development, and it will update when the game updates, so please check this mod page and the server for updates.

If you're a native speaker of languages supported by Elden Ring without existing mod translations or notice partially missing translations, you can add yourself to [url=https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1NfW_qniivBZgwP1O6BupFgcqUl1s2hIUliDuUzcgPAQ/edit]this spreadsheet[/url] and fill in the files in [font=Courier New]diste\Messages[/font] to help make this mod available in other languages. Also let me know if features are needed like font changes or space adjustments.

If you'd like to support my work you can use [url=https://ko-fi.com/thefifthmatt]my Ko-fi page[/url]. There is no obligation at all, and randomizer will always be free.

[size=5]Installation[/size]

These instructions are a bit long, but mainly to account for all of the different ways you can use the randomizer with other mods and with different modding setups. For the simplest installation process, use Automatic Mod Engine. If you run into issues, check out the Troubleshooting section below.

There are mainly two types of Elden Ring mods: dll mods and file mods. dll mods hook into the game's memory while it's running, similar to what Cheat Engine scripts do. For example, [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/510]Seamless Co-op[/url] or [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/97]Item and Param Randomiser[/url]. File mods provide an altered version of the data files used by the game, such as regulation.bin (containing game params) or files packed inside archives like Data0.bdt. For example, this randomizer or [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/541]Elden Ring Reforged[/url].

File mods like this randomizer [i]cannot hook into the game on their own[/i], and require either [url=https://github.com/soulsmods/ModEngine2/releases]Mod Engine 2[/url] or [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/1651]Elden Ring Selective UXM[/url] to do this for them. Both of these tools are supported in the FromSoftware modding server ?ServerName? ([url=https://discord.gg/mT2JJjx]https://discord.gg/mT2JJjx[/url]). On the other hand, dll mods can be installed with something like Elden Mod Loader, or used standalone with their own launchers.

As a result, using this randomizer requires one of these three options (more details in Step 2):
1. Automatic Mod Engine (recommended). In this case, you can download the randomizer anywhere, and you do not have to download anything else. When you run the randomizer, it outputs hundreds of game files, and also outputs a [font=Courier New]config_eldenringrandomizer.toml[/font] file. You can click the "Launch Elden Ring" button in the randomizer to launch a built-in Mod Engine. Mod Engine may not work unless Steam is running, Elden Ring is installed in the default Steam library, and [font=Courier New]eldenring.exe[/font] has [i]not[/i] been modified to run as administrator.
2. Manual Mod Engine. In this case, you download standalone Mod Engine, and the randomizer is a subdirectory within Mod Engine. After running the randomizer program, you can use the Mod Engine launcher to launch the game. It will use those randomized files and automatically disable EAC, assuming all of the aforementioned Mod Engine requirements are met.
3. UXM. In this case, unpack the entire game from its archives (requiring ~50GB extra disk space) and then patch it to use the unpacked files. When you run the randomizer program in UXM mode, it replaces those unpacked game files with randomized versions. Then after disabling EAC, you can launch eldenring.exe (or any other launcher) and it will use the randomized files.

Randomizer is partly compatible with [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/510]Seamless Co-op[/url]. There are two main ways to set up randomizer so everyone has the same seed: either everyone separately downloads randomizer and runs it using the same options and seed (use Options > Import/Export in the randomizer's menu bar), or one person downloads the randomizer, runs it, and distributes a zip file to everyone else. Both of these are covered in the detailed instructions below. The second approach is usually simpler since it requires only one person to set up randomizer. [i]Note: Enemy Onslaught (boss multiplier) is not fully compatible with Seamless Co-op. If any player dies in Enemy Onslaught, all players must disconnect and reconnect.[/i]

If you're using [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/3295]Elden Ring Fog Gate Randomizer[/url], read that page [i]first[/i] for the required installation steps. The overall idea is to first install Item/Enemy Randomizer with specific options, and then set up Fog Gate Randomizer to merge the Item/Enemy Randomizer directory.

[size=4]1. Go offline on Steam (optional)[/size]

If you don't want randomizer runs to be interrupted or invalided, consider going offline on Steam for the duration of the run to prevent Elden Ring from automatically updating (Steam > Go Offline). This is not required, but otherwise your playthrough may not be completeable in the case of a game update.

(After the game updates, all regulation.bin edits based on the previous game version will be ignored. This includes all item location edits, character edits, and enemy attribute edits. It will be necessary to download a new version of the randomizer whenever one is available, and if possible, rerunning it with the same seed and options.)

Regarding online server bans: using mods will not ban your Steam account if you're taking necessary precautions. In particular, after you're done using mods, before logging back into game servers, restore all game files to normal (and/or stop using the Mod Engine Launcher) and delete any progress made in all save slots while the mod was active. This can be done by using a launcher with an alternate save file, or deleting all save file slots used with the mod, or restoring a backup of ER0000.sl2. More details in the "Uninstall" step below.

[size=4]2. Install .NET 6.0 Desktop Runtime[/size]

This is needed to prevent errors about framework versions. If you don't have it, Windows will send you to a page with a bunch of download links. Most of them will not work. You specifically need the following link for the [i]Desktop[/i] Runtime from Microsoft.

[url=https://aka.ms/dotnet/6.0/windowsdesktop-runtime-win-x64.exe]https://aka.ms/dotnet/6.0/windowsdesktop-runtime-win-x64.exe[/url]

[size=4]3a. Automatic Mod Engine[/size]

[b]Skip this step if you're using UXM or manual Mod Engine.[/b]

To use this option, [b]manually[/b] download the randomizer zip from the [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/428?tab=files]Files tab[/url], and place it anywhere. Unzip it using "Extract here" in 7-Zip. This will create a directory named [font=Courier New]randomizer[/font].

Do [b]not[/b] use Vortex Mod Manager, because apparently it does not know how to properly install game files generated by the randomizer.

If you want to use randomizer with other file-based mods, you will be able to specify other mods in the randomizer program using "Merge other mod" before randomizing. See the "Select options" step below for more details. When randomization is applied, randomizer will automatically merge the mods' files. When the game launches, it will load both the merged files and the mods' other files.

If you want to use randomizer with dll-based mods, including Seamless Co-op ([font=Courier New]ersc.dll[/font]), you can specify them using "All dll mod" and they'll be loaded through Mod Engine after clicking "Launch Elden Ring". In the "Misc Options" tab, built-in dll mods which come with randomizer can be selected as well. This includes [font=Courier New]RandomizerCrashFix.dll[/font], which is strongly recommended if base game and DLC enemies are randomized together.

[size=4]3b. Manual Mod Engine[/size]

[b]Skip this step if you're using UXM or automatic Mod Engine.[/b]

This option requires using [url=https://github.com/soulsmods/ModEngine2/releases]Mod Engine 2[/url] for Elden Ring. Download it anywhere, for instance the Downloads folder or Desktop, and extract it using 7-Zip or a similar tool. Then, [b]manually[/b] download the randomizer zip from the [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/428?tab=files]Files tab[/url], place it inside of the [font=Courier New]ModEngine-2.1.0.0-win64[/font] directory you just created, and unzip it using "Extract here" in 7-Zip. This will create a directory named [font=Courier New]randomizer[/font] inside of Mod Engine, alongside the other directories [font=Courier New]mod[/font] and [font=Courier New]modengine2[/font].

This approach is covered by [url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3GdUwpzYf8o]Elden Ring Key Item Randomizer: Installation Instructions[/url] by Nax.

Do [b]not[/b] use Vortex Mod Manager, because apparently it does not know how to properly install game files generated by the randomizer.

For a basic installation of just randomizer, edit [font=Courier New]eldenring.config.toml[/font] (you can open it with Notepad) so that the mod path at the bottom of the file is called "randomizer". You will want [url=https://i.imgur.com/xhkz2Cn.png]this exact setup[/url] of the config file and directories. This approach is covered by [url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3GdUwpzYf8o]Elden Ring Key Item Randomizer: Installation Instructions[/url] by Nax.

If you want to use randomizer with other file-based mods, including regulation.bin edits, the randomizer supports this by using a second directory containing the mods. In particular, you must install that mod in a separate [font=Courier New]mod[/font] directory, and then add both the [font=Courier New]randomizer[/font] and [font=Courier New]mod[/font] directories to [font=Courier New]eldenring.config.toml[/font]. You will end up with config file [url=https://i.imgur.com/sFflndU.png]like this[/url].

If you want to use randomizer with dll-based mods with a manual [font=Courier New]eldenring.config.toml[/font], Mod Engine 2.1.0.0 supports specifying dlls in the [font=Courier New]external_dlls[/font] field. To use built-in randomizer dlls with a manual config, they can be added to the config like [font=Courier New]"randomizer\\dll\\RandomizerCrashFix.dll"[/font] or [font=Courier New]"randomizer\\dll\\RandomizerHelper.dll"[/font], and in the case of RandomizerHelper, options for this dll can be configured using the "Edit RandomizerHelper options" menu in the "Misc Options" tab.

[size=4]3c. Set up UXM[/size]

[b]Skip this step if you're using Mod Engine.[/b]

To use UXM, unpack and patch the game using UXM. Unpacking the game requires around 50 GB of disk space.

The recommended UXM version is [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/1651]Elden Ring Selective UXM[/url]. You should also make sure you can bypass EAC when you launch the game, or else Elden Ring with refuse to start with the modified exe. You can find guides for this online.

Download the randomizer zip from the [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/428?tab=files]Files tab[/url]. It doesn't matter where you download it to do, because you will specify the game install path inside of it. Unzip it using "Extract here" in 7-Zip.

[size=4]4. Select options[/size]

[b]If you're rerunning randomizer, check the [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/428?tab=files]Files tab[/url] to make sure you have the latest version with additional softlock fixes and features. Update it if not.[/b]

Open EldenRingRandomizer.exe and select your Elden Ring game exe. Select "Output files for UXM" only if you're using UXM. When this is checked, the randomizer will output game files into the game directory, creating backups of all of the files it replaces. Otherwise, it will output game files directly into the [font=Courier New]randomizer[/font] directory.

If you have other file-based mods to merge with randomizer, use "Merge other mod". Select either a directory a regulation.bin file to merge another mod's directory. (With Manual Mod Engine, you'll also need to edit the config file [url=https://i.imgur.com/sFflndU.png]like this[/url], as mentioned in Step 2b). Select a Mod Engine toml file to merge all of the mod directories listed in the toml file. This uses the same resolution order as Mod Engine: if a file exists in multiple mods, randomizer only merges the file which is found first in the mod order. Be warned that some mods cannot be merged with item randomizer currently, and will always result in errors.

Then, select randomization options in randomizer tabs, which can be enabled or disabled with the corresponding checkboxes. In item randomizer, "Important locations" are all of the places you might have to check to finish the game or get essential upgrades. See more information about key items and locations below. By default, enemies get randomized within predefined categories (dragons bosses become other dragon bosses), but this is highly configurable with custom enemy placement.

To get the same run as someone else, you can Export/Import options files from the Options menu. These contain your seed, options, and enemy preset. To get the same enemies as someone else but different items, use different "Overall seed" numbers but the same "Separate enemy seed" (in the Enemy Randomizer tab). You can instead zip up the mod files after randomizing, covered in the next section.

[size=4]5. Randomize![/size]

Click the "Randomize items and enemies" button to output game files for a randomized run. The button's text may change depending on whether item/enemy randomizer are enabled, or whether you've checked options like "Reroll seed". In particular, "Run with fixed seed" means it will try to make a run based on the seed(s) you provided or which were populated based on an existing run.

The randomizer creates a file in the [font=Courier New]spoiler_logs[/font] directory which contains hints and spoilers for the run you just generated.

After this, [b]do not manually copy and paste individual files![/b] In addition to regulation.bin, hundreds of files in the event, map, msg, script, and sfx directories are required to make the item and/or enemy randomizer function correctly, and the list of required files can change every time you randomize. The randomizer already puts all of these files in the correct place and adds/deletes them as necessary, so [b]never manually copy and paste them[/b].

If you plan to use Seamless Co-op, and you're generating the mod files for everyone else (as opposed to Export/Import options), you can now send the files. In particular, randomizer can create a zip file using File > Zip mod files, and you can send the zip file. The zip file contains the randomized game files, any merged file mods, and a [font=Courier New]launchmod_rando.bat[/font] file to launch Elden Ring with the modded files. Remember that everyone still needs to install Seamless Co-op separately using [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/510]its install instructions[/url].

[size=4]6. Run the game[/size]

Close the game if it's currently running, then launch the game. (If using Automatic Mod Engine, press the "Launch Elden Ring" button. If using Manual Mod Engine, launch [font=Courier New]launchmod_eldenring.bat[/font].)

Mod Engine's launcher seems to be incompatible with running Elden Ring as administrator, so make sure "Run this program as an administrator" is unchecked in the Compatibility Properties for eldenring.exe while using Mod Engine. If you run into other problems, see Troubleshooting below.

If items are randomized, the [i]second[/i] character creation screen should have different weapons and armor (the first has not changed), and the item beside the Stranded Graveyard Site of Grace should be different. If enemies are randomized, Grafted Scion should be a different enemy.

[size=4]7. Uninstall[/size]

If you used UXM to install randomizer, open EldenRingRandomizer.exe and select "File > Restore UXM backups" to uninstall. Review the files and click "OK" to restore all of the backups. This is also required if you're switching from UXM to Mod Engine.

If you used Mod Engine, simply don't launch the game with Mod Engine anymore, or edit the Mod Engine config to remove it or change it back.

Finally, if you plan on going back online, make sure you use restore a save file from before you started using mods, or else you will definitely get banned! Some launchers will handle this for you, so see their documentation if this is the case. Otherwise, randomizer creates a save file backup when it first does randomization, so browse [font=Courier New]C:\Users\<user name>\AppData\Roaming\EldenRing\<save id>[/font] from local disk. The file to restore is ER0000.sl2, the randomizer's default backup name is ER0000.sl2.randobak.

[size=5]Troubleshooting[/size]

[i]I launched the game with Mod Engine and it immediately closed.[/i]

In order for Mod Engine to launch with the recommended setup, the game must be installed from Steam and Steam must be running. Due to current limitations of Mod Engine preview3, you may also run into issues if Elden Ring is installed on a different disk from the main Steam library, and may need to be reinstalled into the main Steam library location. Finally, make sure you're running [font=Courier New]launchmod_eldenring.bat[/font] and not [font=Courier New]modengine2_launcher.exe[/font].

Mod Engine's launchmod script seems to be incompatible with running as administrator, so make sure "Run this program as an administrator" is unchecked in the Compatibility Properties for eldenring.exe while using Mod Engine. Otherwise, see "I've tried everything else" below.

[i]I launched the game with Mod Engine and item randomizer, but none of the items or starting classes are random.[/i]

This could indicate two things: either [font=Courier New]config_eldenring.toml[/font] has not been edited with the right values, or the game has updated and so it no longer can use modded [font=Courier New]regulation.bin[/font] files meant for older versions of the game. If you're using a version of the randomizer from at least the latest game patch, then double-check Step 3a to make sure the config is correct.

This issue may arise if merged mods are from an older version of the game, in which case those mods need to be updated. This can still work in some cases, by loading into the main menu without any mods installed and then quitting out, or sometimes by moving the current ER0000.sl2 and letting the game re-create it.

[i]I loaded into the game and I keep seeing "?EventTextForMap?" or "Error: Mismatch between regulation.bin and other randomizer files".[/i]

This is a failsafe in the randomizer when it's not properly installed, meaning it only detected some of the files it needs. It can happen when the required game files are not kept together (please see the warning in Step 4: [b]do not manually copy and paste individual files[/b]), or when files are copy-pasted on top of the randomizer, or when the randomizer is not at the top of the mod list in [font=Courier New]config_eldenring.toml[/font]. It can also happen when the game files are re-randomized without fully shutting down the game, in which case you should exit to desktop and re-launch the game.

This can also indicate the case from "none of the items are random" where the randomizer version (or a merged regulation.bin mod) mismatches the game version.

If the issue is due to partial installation, the best way to resolve this is to rerun the randomizer and let it output files to their intended locations, and remove any previous instances of copy-pasted files. If you want to use other file-based mods with the randomizer, follow the instructions for using the separate "mod" directory. With the exception of other file-based randomizers and installers, [b]the randomizer should always be the last-installed mod[/b].

[i]I launched the game with Mod Engine and item/enemy randomizer, and item randomizer seems to work, but enemy randomizer doesn't.[/i]

Make sure you followed the above installation instructions, including manually downloading the randomizer and extracting its files in the appropriate location. Do [b]not[/b] use Vortex Mod Manager, because apparently it does not know how to properly install game files generated by the randomizer.

Assuming none of the other troubleshooting sections apply, this may indicate a bug in Mod Engine preview3, so see the "I've tried everything else" option.

[i]I've tried everything else and Mod Engine still doesn't work.[/i]

Mod Engine is still in beta and there are a bunch of issues, and it is somewhat complicated to set up, so there are a few failsafes which seem to work for most people.

If you can spare the extra 50GB of disk space, you may want to consider using UXM installation. This almost always seems to work (assuming the game version matches the randomizer version), but has the disadvantage of being difficult to share with other people, and difficult to uninstall or merge with other mods.

An alternate Mod Engine setup involves putting Mod Engine in the game directory directly, which seems to work for unknown reasons. In this case, copy the files and directories inside of [font=Courier New]ModEngine-2.0.0-preview3-win64[/font] (including the [font=Courier New]randomizer[/font] directory) into the [font=Courier New]ELDEN RING\Game[/font] directory, then run [font=Courier New]modengine2_launcher.exe[/font] ([i]not[/i] the bat file in this case) as administrator. Any confirmation of this is appreciated.

[size=5]Item logic[/size]

Important changes (and non-changes):

- Volcano Manor has an area you can access by dying to a grab attack at the bottom of Raya Lucaria. However, you can also get there from Volcano Manor's prison town, and vice versa. See [url=https://streamable.com/je5mdi]this video[/url] for both paths.
- Shop contents are randomized. All shop NPCs drop their own Bell Bearings which will match the contents of their shops.
- Optional NPC questlines are not required for key items. It is also never necessary to kill an NPC (so don't kill Patches!), and no good items are obtainable through NPC death. It is also never necessary to repeatedly farm enemies for key items.

If you find any bugs, especially those which make a run incompletable, you can upload your spoiler log in the discord server and I will take a look.

[size=4]Locations[/size]

Important locations are unmissable item locations that come in these required categories:

- Vanilla locations of key items: Locations of items you need to access all of the base game. See the key item list below. (Excludes the locations for Pureblood Knight's Medal and Carian Inverted Statue, because they're given by NPCs you can anger.)
- Major bosses: Every boss with [url=https://www.trueachievements.com/game/Elden-Ring/achievements]an achievement[/url] for defeating them. (Excludes Lichdragon Fortissax because he is missable.) In the DLC, this is defined as every remembrance boss plus Bayle and Golden Hippopotamus.

And these optional categories:

- Golden Seed trees: Every glowing golden tree with a Golden Seed under it.
- Sacred Tear churches: Every church with a statue that has a Sacred Tear under it.
- Merchant shops: Every shop with normal items, excluding those which only have sorceries or incantations or puppets.
- Other bosses: Every enemy with a boss healthbar that drops an item.
- Shadow Realm Blessing pickups: Anywhere you normally find a Scadutree Blessing or Revered Spirit Ash on the ground in the DLC. This includes Miquella's Crosses, tutelary deities (the withered corpses with Revered Spirit Ashes), the Scadutree Chalice, and various random pickups. However, it excludes enemies which normally drop these items.

Patches is an edge case in a few ways. He's not considered a boss and he doesn't drop anything valuable. He is considered a shop, but only if you don't select the "Exclude caves" option.

All categories have blue links beneath them in the randomizer. Click on them to open a popup window with a full list of locations broken down by area.

There are over 3200 distinct item locations in Elden Ring. As of randomizer version v0.11, around 1900 have handwritten descriptions and can contain important or noteworthy items. All of the rest currently have an automatically generated description referencing the closest map landmark.

[size=4]Key items[/size]

Key items are defined as items which unlock other unmissable items. They are only placed in important locations. These items, and no others, can block your progress in the randomizer until you find them. It's possible to use glitches to bypass some of these.

- Academy Glintstone Key: Used to access the Academy of Raya Lucaria. There are two versons of this item. Only the version that says "a glintstone key will remember its user" works here.
- Carian Inverted Statue: Used to progress in Carian Study Hall and reach the Divine Tower of Liurnia.
- Cursemark of Death: Used to access the Deathbed Dream in Deeproot Depths. (Note that the fight itself is missable and cannot have key items.)
- Dark Moon Ring: Used to progress to Moonlight Altar from Ainsel River.
- Dectus Medallion (Left and Right): Used to ascend the Grand Lift of Dectus. Other routes to enter Altus Plateau are also possible.
- Discarded Palace Key: Used to unlock the treasure chest in the Raya Lucaria Grand Library.
- Drawing-Room Key: Used to access the Volcano Manor Drawing Room and the lava area hidden within.
- Great Runes: By default, two of seven are required to enter Leyndell, either though Capital Outskirts or Deeproot Depths. With modified rulesets, any number of these can be required to enter Leyndell, enter Mountaintops, or access the final boss.
- Haligtree Secret Medallion (Left and Right): Used to access Consecrated Snowfield, the hidden path to the Haligtree.
- Imbued Sword Key: Used to unlock a Sending Gate at the Four Belfries in Liurnia. There are three of these in the game.
- Pureblood Knight's Medal: Used to immediately access Mohgwyn Palace without using the Sending Gate in Consecrated Snowfield.
- Rold Medallion: Used to access Mountaintops of the Giants after Leyndell. With modified rulesets, it can be replaced with a Great Runes requirement.
- Rusty Key: Used to progress into Stormveil Castle. This is optional, unless Gostoc dies before he opens the main gate for you.

The DLC has these key items:

- Messmer's Kindling: Used to burn the Sealing Tree in Rauh Ruins. With modified rulesets, this can be split into some number of Messmer's Kindling Shards with a minimum shard count required to burn the Sealing Tree.
- O Mother: Used to access Hinterland by using the gesture in front of the Marika statue behind Shadow Keep.
- Hole-Laden Necklace: Used at Finger Ruins to progress Count Ymir's quest and access the Metyr boss fight.
- Well Depths Key: Used to access the Belurat swamp.

[size=4]Other important items[/size]

By default, the following items are not randomized: Flask of Crimson Tears, Flask of Cerulean Tears, Flask of Wondrous Physick, Spectral Steed Whistle, Spirit Calling Bell, Crafting Kit, Whetstone Knife, Lantern, Serpent-Hunter, and all map fragments. Some of these can be randomized using custom item placement.

Talisman Pouches, Memory Stones, Whetblades are not key items but they're placed in the key item pool by default. Golden Seeds, Sacred Tears, and upgrade material Bell Bearings can also be assigned to important locations when selected in the randomizer.

When Bell Bearings are randomized and DLC Start is disabled, they are placed in the following locations, matching their vanilla locations:

Smithing-Stone Miner's Bell Bearing [1]: In Liurnia
Smithing-Stone Miner's Bell Bearing [2]: In Altus Plateau
Smithing-Stone Miner's Bell Bearing [3]: In Mountaintops of the Giants
Smithing-Stone Miner's Bell Bearing [4]: In Farum Azula
Somberstone Miner's Bell Bearing [1]: In Caelid
Somberstone Miner's Bell Bearing [2]: In Altus Plateau
Somberstone Miner's Bell Bearing [3]: In Mountaintops of the Giants
Somberstone Miner's Bell Bearing [4]: In Farum Azula
Somberstone Miner's Bell Bearing [5]: In Farum Azula
Glovewort Picker's Bell Bearing [1]: In Mt. Gelmir
Glovewort Picker's Bell Bearing [2]: In Mountaintops of the Giants
Glovewort Picker's Bell Bearing [3]: In Farum Azula
Ghost-Glovewort Picker's Bell Bearing [1]: In Nokron, Eternal City
Ghost-Glovewort Picker's Bell Bearing [2]: In Ainsel River
Ghost-Glovewort Picker's Bell Bearing [3]: In Haligtree

Smithing Stones themselves can be distributed using the "Smithing Stone availability similar to base game" option. This places [i]most[/i] stones of a given tier in the portion of the game appropriate to their level, but there is still a chance to pick up any stone anywhere. When this option is disabled, their placement is completely random, and you may be stuck at lower upgrade levels for a long time, especially for regular Smithing Stone weapons. The option also adds a few new stones to guarantee 40-50 total item pickups per tier, slightly more if collectible materials are also randomized.

These are the main locations for Smithing Stones when the option is enabled. Again note that a handful of Smithing Stones of each tier [i]will[/i] be placed outside of these areas to allow getting better weapons early with more thorough exploration.

Smithing Stone [1] and [2] can be found mainly in Limgrave, Liurnia, and Caelid.
Smithing Stone [3] can be found mainly in Liurnia and Caelid, and occasionally in Limgrave, Altus Plateau, and early underground areas.
Smithing Stone [4] can be found mainly in Liurnia and Caelid, Altus Plateau, and underground areas.
Smithing Stone [5] can be found mainly in Altus Plateau, Leyndell, Sewers, and later underground areas.
Smithing Stone [6] can be found mainly in Leyndell, Sewers, later underground areas, and Mountaintops or later.
Smithing Stone [7] can be found mainly in Sewers and Moonlight Altar and Mountaintops or later.
Smithing Stone [8] can be found mainly in Moonlight Altar and Mountaintops or later.
Somber Smithing Stone [1] and [2] can be found mainly in Limgrave, Liurnia, Caelid, and early underground areas.
Somber Smithing Stone [3] and [4] can be found mainly in Liurnia, Caelid, and early underground areas.
Somber Smithing Stone [5] can be found mainly in Caelid, Altus Plateau, and Leyndell.
Somber Smithing Stone [6] can be found mainly in Altus Plateau, and Leyndell, and later underground areas.
Somber Smithing Stone [7] [8] and [9] can be found mainly in Mountaintops or later, with a few in Altus Plateau or later.
Ancient Dragon Smithing Stones and Somber Ancient Dragon Smithing Stones can be found throughout the game.

[size=4]Hints[/size]

There is an optional feature to purchase map marker hints when you get stuck. If enabled, they are available for purchase from Kalé in the Church of Elleh after reaching the Altus Plateau.

In short, if there is a required key item which is accessible but you haven't found it yet, you can purchase a hint for where to search for it. The first Site of Grace in the item's area will be marked, along with the overall name of the area. This costs 200 runes times your Rune Level, or 10k runes, whichever is higher.

There is also an option to mark item locations, down to the exact map coordinates of the treasure or enemy that drops it. You can purchase these only once you defeat the main healthbar boss of the item's area. (Usually this is the boss at the "end" of the area. For Liurnia overall it's Royal Knight Loretta, for Dragonbarrow and Altus Plateau it's their Godskin Apostle, for Mt. Gelmir it's Fallingstar Beast.) This costs 500 runes times your Rune Level, or 25k runes, whichever is higher.

OOT Randomizer-style hints are planned in the future, in the style of "a catacombs boss drops a key item" or "there are no key items in Leyndell".

[size=5]Enemy randomization[/size]

Enemy randomizer shuffles enemies and bosses within categories. By default, major bosses replace other bosses, minor bosses replace other minor bosses, regular enemies replace other regular enemies, and so on. Minibosses like night bosses, overworld dragon bosses, and evergaol bosses each have their own categories.

The categorization of major bosses in enemy randomizer is slightly different from the categorization in item randomizer. Some additional major bosses are added in enemy randomizer, like Fia's Champions, Fortissax, Gideon. Elemer of the Briar is a major boss in item randomizer, because he has an achievement, but is downgraded to a minor boss in enemy randomizer because room very small.

The warp from Raya Lucaria to Volcano Manor is changed so that getting consumed by the abductor enemy is no longer required. Instead, the only requirement is to die at the bottom of the elevator through any means.

Aside from default enemy categories, customization is possible if you check "Custom enemy placement" and then click "Edit custom enemy placement". Each custom configuration is called a preset and is saved as a file in the [font=Courier New]presets[/font] directory. Most common types of customization like "Merge non-major bosses" and "Replace wildlife with regular enemies" can be achieved by clicking on Quick Edit links at the bottom.

For each category, custom enemy lists can specify which enemies are randomly selected. Within a category, you can have several source groups (also known as "pools") whose percentages add up to 100%. Click the "+" button to add a new source group. You can add enemy types to source groups, and when a group is used, an enemy in that group will be randomly selected based on its frequency in the vanilla game. "Same category" is synonymous with whichever category you're editing, so by default, all categories are replaced with 100% same category. There's also a special source group called "Not randomized" which gives a % chance for some enemies in a category to be randomized and for others to not be randomized.

Finally, custom enemy placement also has "Oops All" mode, which replaces all enemies with a single boss or enemy type, and "Enemy Onslaught" which duplicates (or more) each enemy and boss. Onslaught can be used with or without randomization, by adding or removing "All Enemies and Bosses" from being randomized or not.

[size=5]Customization for merged mods[/size]

If you get errors merging a mod you've created and want to try for compatibility with randomizer, you can place a config file named [font=Courier New]randomizer_merge_config.yml[/font] in the mod's root directory (the same directory which contains [font=Courier New]regulation.bin[/font]) which randomizer will read when merging the mod. There is an example of this file inside of the [font=Courier New]diste[/font] directory distributed with the randomizer.

At the moment, customization is geared towards basic compatibility, containing lists of items/enemies locations in the merged mod to avoid randomizing. There is no configuration for additional randomization of new items/enemies yet, as this can get extremely complicated. The supported fields are [font=Courier New]NorandomEntities[/font], [font=Courier New]NorandomLots[/font], [font=Courier New]NorandomEnemyLots[/font], [font=Courier New]NorandomLotFlags[/font], and [font=Courier New]NorandomShops[/font]. These can be given individual numbers or inclusive number ranges as a YAML list, as follows:

NorandomLots:
- 10811
- 1038540500-1038540599
NorandomShops:
- 100945-100949

Note that enemies which are added by mods, with brand new entity ids, are already not randomized and don't need to be listed. For items, [i]avoid specifying vanilla lots and shops[/i] as much as possible and prefer to only include ids added by your mod. Some item randomization features require being able to randomize specific items, especially key items. Supporting custom randomization is conceivable in the future but depends on the needs of each mod. If there are multiple merged mods, all of their configs will be combined together, even if only the first one's lots are used.

[size=5]Special thanks[/size]

Thanks to TKGP and katalash for SoulsFormats, Meowmaritus for SoulsFormats EMEVD editing and initial UXM, and HotPocketRemix for EMEVD instruction reversing. Thanks to everyone in ?ServerName? for being helpful and informative, and everyone in the rando discord for brainstorming ideas.

RandomizerCrashFix.dll has convenience patches for the game based on SoulSplitter by FrankvdStam and alt-saves by vswarte, with some modifications to remain compatible with those mods.

Finally, thanks to Saltyfish_King, Fababfan, Plante, proteh, Meliar, Leekos, Kuroko, 刀子, SaniTOS, HiJacked, frioosin, and Cogumellow for contributing translations and permission to use them in this mod.

Source code is published to [url=https://github.com/thefifthmatt/SoulsRandomizers]https://github.com/thefifthmatt/SoulsRandomizers[/url] and updated occasionally.
author: thefifthmatt

name: ERR - ELDEN RING Reforged
summary: A thorough overhaul of ELDEN RING, promoting deep engagement with the world, your ideal character fantasy, and revised combat mechanics.
description:
[center][/center][center][youtube]ACvCZNfg7F8[/youtube][/center]
[size=5][center][b][color=#ffff00]For information on how to install the mod, please read the [/color][color=#ff7700]"README"[/color][color=#ffff00] files provided with the mod download![/color][/b][/center][/size]

[img]https://erreforged.com/files/promo/1AWorldReforged.jpg[/img]
[size=5][b]
A World Reforged[/b][/size]

[size=3]ELDEN RING Reforged is an overhaul about giving the player a great variety of reasons to engage with the game world, imagine and realize a compelling character fantasy through balanced and powerful build choices, and refining ELDEN RING's combat system with rich, impactful moment-to-moment decisions and a variety of tools at the player's disposal.[/size]

[b][size=4]Please note before playing:[/size][/b]

[list]
[*][size=3]Reforged is best enjoyed in thorough, long playthroughs, as if playing again for the first time. Popular "get strong quick" routes are absent.[/size]
[*][size=3]Reforged is rebalanced in deeply fundamental ways, meaning that knowledge and comparisons to vanilla game balance should be left at the door.[/size]
[*][size=3]To promote build variety and fantasy, many previously weak and under-used strategies are now fun and competitive, while some of the more oppressive ones are equalized.[/size]
[*][size=3]Reforged is a polished experience which is fully ready to play, but receives frequent updates and development.[/size]
[*][size=3]Due to the nature of Souls modding, and the quick release schedule, compatibility with other mods is very low.[/size]
[*][size=3]Reforged realizes a strong vision when it comes to its goals and the experience it provides, but all feedback is welcome and accounted for.[/size]
[/list]
[b][size=4]Reforged is for you if:[/size][/b]

[list]
[*][size=3]You love ELDEN RING's game world, the Lands Between, and want to take in all of its untapped potential.[/size]
[*][size=3]You value a seamless and cohesive experience, where every addition effortlessly slots into the game setting.[/size]
[*][size=3]You want to enjoy challenging, fun combat that is a reward in its own right, instead of avoiding encounters.[/size]
[*][size=3]You wish to experience ELDEN RING without the pressure of following "the best strategy" or rushing for "the best weapon".[/size]
[*][size=3]You love designing your own character to suit your strengths, carefully deliberating over many competitive choices.[/size]
[*][size=3]You are looking for a longer, more thorough playthrough, either by yourself or with your friends.[/size]
[/list]

[img]https://erreforged.com/files/promo/2NewAndEngagingCombat.jpg[/img]﻿
[size=5][b]
New and Engaging Combat[/b][/size][list]
[*][size=3]Guard and (Perfect) Deflect with any weapon, rewarding good timing with a powerful advantage.[/size]
[*][size=3]Vary your evasive actions beyond rolling with Sliding, Ducking and Jumping along with vastly improved enemy hitboxes.[/size]
[*][size=3]Time your inputs precisely to trigger Perfect Actions, improving your speed and resource management.[/size]
[*][size=3]Ambidextrous action: Perform any special moves such as rolling, dashing or backstab attacks with your left-hand weapon, while retaining guarding ability.[/size]
[*][size=3]Ebb and flow: Generate FP through weapon attacks, then spend it on powerful weapon skills.[/size]
[*][size=3]Fully rebalanced enemy negations and resistances, with Madness and Death Blight status effects now affecting most foes, even bosses.[/size]
[/list]

[img]https://erreforged.com/files/promo/3FreedomOfChoice.jpg[/img]

[size=5][b]Freedom of Choice[/b][/size]

[list]
[*][size=3]Embody a strong character fantasy with Fortunes, a class and sub-class system promoting varied archetypal builds.[/size]
[*][size=3]Employ dozens of new weapons, talismans, and fully revamped existing equipment with all-new stats and balancing.[/size]
[*][size=3]Choose from an expanded list of 24 Weapon Affinities with varied scalings and bonuses, to fit your unique character.[/size]
[*][size=3]Advance with Reworked Character Stats, boasting new scalings and secondary effects, along with a maximum level of RL200.[/size]
[*][size=3]Gain power beyond levels with Runeforging, granting passive stats which may be re-assigned at any time.[/size]
[*][size=3]Wield passive bonuses with Enkindled Ashes of War, and apply them even to unique somber weapons.[/size]
[*][size=3]Scour the Land of Shadow for powerful Curios, each rewarding a type of combat action with an impactful effect.[/size]
[*][size=3]Take full advantage of a Revamped Crafting System where recipes are less restrictive, and consumables can power a whole play style.[/size]
[/list][size=3]
[/size]
[img]https://erreforged.com/files/promo/4APersonalJourney.jpg[/img]

[size=5][b]A Personal Journey[/b][/size]

[list]
[*][size=3]Explore updated and new locations, and fight many new bosses and enemy types with a familiar feel.[/size]
[*][size=3]Tailor the challenge to your own needs through the Difficulty system, for both laid-back and die-hard players.[/size]
[*][size=3]Express your own style with Transmogrify Armor, Armor Dyes and Nightfarer Postures.[/size]
[*][size=3]Discover 1200+ Rune Pieces and Ember Pieces, collectibles manually placed in the hidden corners of the world that fuel the Runeforging and Curio systems.[/size]
[*][size=3]Challenge overhauled Enemy Camps to collect equipment normally reserved to random drops.[/size]
[*][size=3]Track all the world's foes in the Codex of the All-Knowing, learn their weaknesses and unlock powerful rewards.[/size]
[*][size=3]Spend rare Starlight Tokens to unlock build capstone items early in the playthrough at the Altar of Anticipation.[/size]
[/list]
[img]https://erreforged.com/files/promo/5AdvancedMagecraft.jpg[/img]

[size=5][b]Advanced Magecraft[/b][/size]

[list]
[*][size=3]Discover dozens of new spells that pad out existing spell schools in fitting, unique ways.[/size]
[*][size=3]Maintain your FP economy through Generator Spells and magical Catalyst Heavy attacks.[/size]
[*][size=3]Cast spells faster, with shorter recovery times, and further increase your speed by investing in Mind instead of Dexterity.[/size]
[*][size=3]Wield weapons reimagined as Weapon Catalysts, casting with Guard+Light Attack without sacrificing guarding or heavy attack capabilities.[/size]
[*][size=3]Slot spells into your catalysts with Mystic Ashes, and easily access them with the Ash-of-War input.[/size]
[*][size=3]Find passive assistance from Rebalanced Spirit Ashes, then use Spirit Fury to let them command enemy attention.[/size]
[/list]

[img]https://erreforged.com/files/promo/6CommunityAndOnlinePlay.jpg[/img]

[size=5][b]Community and Online Play[/b][/size]

[list]
[*][size=3]Experience fully-featured FromSoftware multi-player on Reforged's Custom Multiplayer Server, with separate match-making.[/size]
[*][size=3]Enjoy advanced Seamless Coop support and (some) bug fixes when playing in the separate offline mode.[/size]
[*][size=3]Play in your desired language with community translations, mostly or partially complete, especially Japanese, German and French.[/size]
[*][size=3]Chat with your fellow players and the developers in the ERR Discord, post feedback, and find help and advice.[/size]
[*][size=3]Contribute to the mod by editing the community wiki, or join the team if you are a 2D or 3D artist who loves ER's art style![/size]
[/list]

[img]https://erreforged.com/files/promo/7TooMuchToCover.jpg[/img]
[size=5]
[b]Too Much to Cover[/b][/size]

[size=3]ELDEN RING Reforged is a truly colossal project, and the hundreds of thousands of changes that make up its whole can't be put into words. Still, here's some other things you can expect:

[list]
[*][size=3]An easy installation process, including native support for Linux and Steam Deck.[/size]
[*][size=3]A gorgeous, more readable UI with more information and new icon graphics.[/size]
[*][size=3]Resurrect and re-challenge bosses at any time.[/size]
[*][size=3]Much improved camera lock-on dynamics for large bosses.[/size]
[*][size=3]Overhauled weight and stamina system, where "Overloaded" is a viable, truly weighty play style.[/size]
[*][size=3]Unique game modes such as Adventure Mode (no grace travel) and Eternal Return (a Majora's Mask tribute).[/size]
[*][size=3]New and remixed movesets for many existing weapons.[/size]
[*][size=3]Tens of thousands of fixes to asset placements, clipping and raytracing lighting bugs.[/size]
[/list]
Many, many other minor fixes, adjustments and improvements that have piled up over the years are not listed.[/size]

[size=5][b]Wiki[/b][/size]
﻿
[size=3]While there are far too many changes in ERR to meaningfully summarize them, the community has been maintaining a nice community wiki which contains a lot of useful information:

[/size][url=https://err.fandom.com/wiki/ELDEN_RING_Reforged_Wiki][size=4]Link[/size][/url][url=https://docs.google.com/document/d/112cAdBAQqf6XbuGXSNltfUzwxn-GOdpIS-_QEBpeLHY/edit?usp=sharing][size=4]

[/size][/url][b][size=5]Feedback/Discord[/size][/b]

[size=3]We are very open to feedback and discussion (which does not mean we will agree with all of it). If you wish to chat with the community or give your feedback you can do so at our Discord server:

[/size][url=https://discord.gg/eldenringreforged][size=4] ﻿﻿Link[/size][/url]

[size=5][b]How to install[/b][/size]

[size=3]ELDEN RING Reforged is incredibly simple to install for Souls mod standards. It comes bundled with a launcher program that takes care of everything for you.

[color=#ffff00][size=5]After downloading the mod and unpacking the archive, please read the "README" files in the unpacked folder![/size][/color]

To avoid issues, please unpack the mod into a folder such as "C:\ERR" on an SSD hard drive, and add an exception to that folder for your anti-virus programs.[/size][size=3]
[/size][size=5]
[/size][b][size=5]Development team[/size]

[/b][list]
[*][size=3][b]Kirnifr:[/b] Project director, lead developer.[/size]
[*][size=3][b]ivi:[/b] Co-director, wearer of many hats.[/size]
[*][size=3][b]poeconcept:[/b] Principal concept artist.[/size]
[*][size=3][b]hanalove:[/b] Principal asset artist.[/size]
[*][size=3][b]Cavou:[/b] Map editor, quality assurance.[/size]
[*][size=3][b]Rayan:[/b] VFX editor.[/size]
[*][size=3][b]Pina Colada:[/b] Spell designer, icon artist, VFX editor.[/size]
[*][size=3][b]cultist:[/b] 3D model and texture artist.[/size]
[*][size=3][b]remu:[/b] Icon artist.[/size]
[*][size=3][b]Tlarok:[/b] Cloth physics specialist.[/size]
[/list][b]
[size=5]Support[/size]

[/b][size=3]ELDEN RING Reforged is a free passion project.

You can support the lead developer on Ko-Fi if you want:[/size]

﻿[url=https://ko-fi.com/kirnifr][size=4]﻿Link
[/size][/url]
[size=3]Ko-Fi links of other team members are available on the Discord.[/size]

[b][size=5]Credits[/size]
[/b]
[size=3]A lot of people have contributed to Reforged over the years.
You can find the full credits list in the separate CREDITS.txt file.[/size]
author: Kirnifr and the Reforged team

name: Glorious Merchant
summary: (DLC compatible) Free access to every item for quick build making. Like Grand Merchant, but no merging necessary to use with other mods.
description:
This is an alternative to the [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/129]Grand Merchant[/url] mod that doesn't contain any files that require merging or unpacking. Compatible with ELDEN RING version 1.16 downloaded through Steam.

[list]
[*][b]Compatible with other mods without merging anything [/b]- just install a single DLL file
[*][b]Accessible even if Kalé is dead [/b]- he will be temporarily revived and non-hostile while the mod is active
[*][b]Auto-upgrade weapons [/b]- weapons are upgraded to the highest level you've already attained on a given character, so no need to run back and forth between Hewg and Kalé (optional - can be disabled via config file)
[*]Supports all languages supported by the vanilla game. Some languages have partial English text - let me know if you'd like to contribute to or fix a localization.
[/list]
Mods that add new items, such as weapons and armor in [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/3419]The Convergence[/url] and [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/541]ELDEN RING Reforged[/url], will also have these new items available in the appropriate shop menus.

Items from the Shadow of the Erdtree expansion are available under the "Browse Inventory" > "DLC Items" submenu. You can avoid this menu if you'd like to avoid spoilers or don't own the DLC.

[img]https://i.imgur.com/SXcBnWi.png[/img]

[size=5][b]Installation (Option #1 - ModEngine2)[/b][/size]

[list=1]
[*]Download the latest version of [url=https://github.com/soulsmods/ModEngine2/releases/latest]ModEngine2[/url] and extract it somewhere.

[*]Download the mod and extract [b]ermerchant.dll[/b] and [b]ermerchant.ini[/b] into the [b]ModEngine-2.1.0.0-win64[/b] folder[i] (see screenshot below[/i])
[*]Open [b]config_eldenring.toml[/b] in Notepad and look for the line starting with "external_dlls = ...". Add the mod to the list:
[font=monospace][color=#efefef]    external_dlls = [[/color]
    [color=#00ff00][b]"ermerchant.dll",[/b][/color]
[color=#efefef]    ][/color][/font]
If you're using multiple DLLs, make sure they're separated by commas. For example:
[font=monospace][color=#efefef]    external_dlls = [
        "ertransmogrify.dll",[/color][b][color=#efefef][/color][color=#00ff00]
        "ermerchant.dll",[/color][/b]
[color=#efefef]    ]
[/color][/font][*]Double-click [b]launchmod_eldenring.bat [/b]to start the game.
[/list]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/1xmb1Sq.png[/img]
To add other mods, follow their instructions to add them to your ModEngine2. For example, other DLL mods like Seamless Coop can be added to the "external_dlls" section in the same way.

[size=3][b]Installing on Steam Deck[/b][/size]

This also works on Steam Deck, but instead of running [b]launchmod_eldenring.bat[/b] you need to launch through Steam. See [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/articles/654]How to install Elden Ring mods on Steam Deck[/url].

[size=5][b]Installation (Option #2 - Elden Mod Loader)[/b][/size]

If you can't get ModEngine2 to work or don't care about the additional features, you can also run the mod with Elden Mod Loader. (I don't use personally use this method, so I can't provide support for it).

[list=1]
[*]Install [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/117]Elden Mod Loader[/url]﻿ according to the instructions in the linked Nexus page.
[*]Extract [b]ermerchant.dll[/b] and [b]ermerchant.ini[/b] into the [b]ELDEN RING[/b] > [b]mods[/b] folder.
[*]Launch the game normally.
[/list]
[size=5][b]Installation (With The Convergence)
[/b][/size]
To use this with The Convergence mod:

[list=1]
[*]Extract [b]ermerchant.dll[/b] and [b]ermerchant.ini[/b] into the [b]ConvergenceER [/b]folder[i] (the same folder that contains [b]config_eldenring.toml[/b][/i])
[*]Open [b]config_eldenring.toml[/b] in Notepad and look for the line starting with "external_dlls = ...". Add the mod to the list
[font=monospace][color=#efefef]    external_dlls = [[/color][color=#efefef]
        "mod/example.dll",
        "mod/example.dll",
        "mod/[font=monospace][color=#efefef]example[/color][/font].dll",
        "mod/example.dll",[/color]
        [b][color=#00ff00]"ermerchant.dll",[/color][/b]
[color=#efefef]    [/color][color=#efefef]][/color][/font]
[*]Double-click [b]Start_Convergence.bat [/b]to start the game
[/list][color=#ffff00][size=3][b]NOTE: This isn't an official addon for The Convergence - please don't report issues to the convergence developers and community.[/b][/size][/color]

[size=5][b]Installation (With ELDEN RING: Reforged)[/b][/size]

To use this with ERR, follow this process for adding DLL mods instead.

[list=1]
[*]Extract [b]ermerchant.dll[/b] and [b]ermerchant.ini[/b] into the [b]dll > active [/b]folder
[*]Double-click [b]Launch ELDEN RING Reforged [/b]to start the game.
[/list]
This mod has been verified to work with Reforged versions up to 1.4.9N. If future Reforged updates stop working, please report this in the discussion tab so it can be fixed.[color=#ffff00][b][size=2]

[/size][size=3]NOTE: This isn't an official addon for ELDEN RING: Reforged - please don't report issues to the reforged developers and community.[/size][/b][/color]

[img]https://i.imgur.com/nltrBg6.png[/img]

Speak with Kalé in the Church of Elleh (Kalé is always alive and non-hostile while the mod is loaded).

Choose the "Browse Inventory" option, and browse the available item types. You can buy any item for 0 runes, and auto unlock all gestures.

Alternatively, choose "Browse Cut Content" to obtain items that aren't normally available in the game. Note that going online with cut content may result in a ban.

[img]https://i.imgur.com/f20RPI0.jpeg[/img]

[img]https://i.imgur.com/1IbCnYi.png[/img]

[list]
[*]Mod by Tom Clark
[*]Brazilian Portuguese localization by LucasGabranth
[*]Japanese localization by Furisuke
[*]Simplified Chinese and Traditional Chinese localization by quantumfallout
[*]Mod idea completely stolen from Grand Merchant by Vawser and Honest Merchant by Church Guard. Please consider giving the original GM an endorsement if you enjoy this mod.
[*]Thumbnail art by [url=https://twitter.com/Herheim731/]@Herheim731[/url]﻿
[/list]
[b]Source code[/b]: [url=https://github.com/ThomasJClark/elden-ring-glorious-merchant]https://github.com/ThomasJClark/elden-ring-glorious-merchant[/url]﻿

[size=5][b]More Mods[/b][/size]

[list]
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/6927]Armor and Weapon Dyes[/url]﻿ - Alter the color of your equipment using customizable dye options
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/3596]Transmogrify Armor[/url]﻿ - Change your appearance to any armor while keeping the stats and bonuses from the armor you actually have equipped
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/8088]Player List Overlay for Seamless Co-op[/url]﻿
[/list]
author: Tom Clark

name: Unlock the framerate
summary: Unlocks the FPS and lets you set a custom FPS limit (default is 300 FPS).
description:
[size=4][b][size=3]Check out my other mods:
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/179]Remove chromatic aberration[/url], [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/177]Remove vignette[/url], [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/175]Remove black bars[/url], [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/118]Fix the camera[/url], [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/325]Adjust the FoV[/url], [b][size=4][size=3][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/349]Increase animation distance[/url][size=4], [b][size=4][b][size=4][size=4][b][size=3][size=4][size=3][size=4][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/376/][size=3]Disable rune loss[/size][/url], [b][size=4][size=3][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/421/]Skip the intro logos[/url]﻿.[/size][/size][/b][/size][/size][/size][/size][/b][/size][/size][/b][/size][/b][/size][/size][/size][/b][/size][/b]
[/size][b][size=4]
Requirements:
[/size][/b][list=1]
[*][size=3][b]Turn off vsync or any other graphics card setting that may lock the FPS:[/b] [url=https://github.com/uberhalit/EldenRingFpsUnlockAndMore?tab=readme-ov-file#follow-these-steps-on-nvidia-see-below-for-gsync]https://github.com/uberhalit/EldenRingFpsUnlockAndMore?tab=readme-ov-file#follow-these-steps-on-nvidia-see-below-for-gsync[/url]﻿ (the mod on the linked page is not mine).[/size]

author: TechieW

name: Transmogrify Armor
summary: (DLC compatible) Change your appearance to any armor while keeping the stats and bonuses from the armor you actually have equipped. Compatible with game version 1.16 / Shadow of the Erdtree, The Convergence Mod, ELDEN RING Reforged, and most other mods.
description:
[size=3][url=https://wowpedia.fandom.com/wiki/Transmogrification]Transmogrification[/url] (AKA transmog) allows you to modify the cosmetic appearance of your armor while keeping the original stats intact. You can make armor pieces resemble any other piece in the game, or make them invisible to benefit from their effects without covering up your character.

[img]https://i.imgur.com/PL384Hl.jpg[/img]
[size=3]
The mod is tested on Elden Ring patch 1.16 downloaded via Steam with Shadow of the Erdtree purchased. It's included by default in [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/541]ELDEN RING Reforged[/url] and [/size][/size][size=3][size=3][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/3419]The Convergence mod[/url], and also works with[/size][/size][size=3][size=3] [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/510]Seamless Co-op[/url],[/size] [size=3][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/2202]ELDEN Ring Reborn[/url], and most other mods. This mod doesn't impact Seamless Co-op matchmaking, so you can still co-op or invade with people who don't have it installed.[/size]
[/size][color=#ffff00][size=3]
[/size][/color][color=#ffff00][size=4][b]DLC Info[/b][/size][/color][size=3][color=#ffff00][size=3]
[/size][/color][color=#ffff00]
This mod displays armor names and descriptions from the Shadow of the Erdtree expansion. If you wish to hide DLC items, edit the [b]include_dlc_armor[/b] line in your ertransmogrify.ini file, changing [b]true [/b]to [b]false[/b].[/color]

[img]https://i.imgur.com/SXcBnWi.png[/img]

[list=1]
[*]Download the latest version of [url=https://github.com/soulsmods/ModEngine2/releases/latest]ModEngine2[/url] and extract it somewhere.
[*]Download the mod and extract [b]ertransmogrify.dll[/b] and [b]ertransmogrify.ini[/b] into the [b]ModEngine-2.1.0.0-win64[/b] folder[i] (see screenshot below[/i])
[*]Open [b]config_eldenring.toml[/b] in Notepad and look for the line starting with "external_dlls = ...". Add the mod to the list:
[font=monospace][color=#efefef]    external_dlls = [[/color]
    [color=#00ff00][b]"ertransmogrify.dll",[/b][/color]
[color=#efefef]    ][/color][/font]
If you're using multiple DLLs, make sure they're separated by commas. For example:
[font=monospace][color=#efefef]    external_dlls = [
        "ermerchant.dll",[/color][b][color=#efefef][/color][color=#00ff00]
        "ertransmogrify.dll",[/color][/b]
[color=#efefef]    ]
[/color][/font][*]Double-click [b]launchmod_eldenring.bat [/b]to start the game.
[/list]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/U9u8oE8.png[/img][b]
Important[/b]: Before switching back to the vanilla game, untransmogrify any armor you changed with this mod. You could also back up and restore your save file before and after playing the mod. Playing online with transmogrified armor won't work, and might get you banned if you try anyways.
[b]
[size=3][b]Installing on Steam Deck[/b][/size]

[/b]This also works on Steam Deck, but instead of running launchmod_eldenring.bat you need to launch through Steam. See [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/articles/654]How to install Elden Ring mods on Steam Deck[/url].[b]

[/b][/size][img]https://i.imgur.com/nltrBg6.png[/img][size=3]
[size=4][b]Transmogrif[size=3]ying Armor[/size][/b][/size]

[/size][list=1]
[*][size=3]Choose the [b]Transmogrify armor[/b] option while resting at a Site of Grace.[/size]
[*][size=3]Select the armor slot you want to change ([b]Transmogrify head[/b], [b]Transmogrify chest[/b], [b]Transmogrify arms[/b], or [b]Transmogrify legs[/b])[/size]
[*][size=3]Select an armor piece for that slot.[/size]
[/list][size=3]
Any armor in the game can be chosen for transmogrification, even if you don't have it in your inventory. This includes unobtainable armor (e.g. Deathbed Smalls) and armor added by other mods you have installed (e.g. Dreadnought Armor from The Convergence or the Chelonian Mitre from ELDEN RING: Reforged). You can also pick [b]Invisible[/b] to make that slot appear empty.

If you prefer, you can lock armor that you haven't acquired yet from transmogrification by editing the supplied .ini file.

If you don't pick anything for a slot, it'll default to your current equipped armor.

[img]https://i.imgur.com/agBThAB.jpeg[/img]

[/size][size=4][b]Undoing Transmogrification[/b][/size][size=3]

[/size][list=1]
[*][size=3]Choose the [b]Transmogrify armor[/b] option while resting at a Site of Grace.[/size]
[*][size=3]Select [b]Dispel transmogrification[/b][/size]
[/list][size=3]
Your appearance will change back to the armor you're actually wearing.

[img]https://i.imgur.com/adAgKhY.jpeg[/img]
[/size][size=3][size=3]
[size=4][b]Configuration options[size=3][size=3][size=3][b]
[/b][/size][/size][/size][/b][/size][/size][/size]
[size=3]You can customize a few aspects of the mod by editing the supplied .ini file and relaunching the game. See the file for more details.[/size][size=3][size=3][size=4][b]

Language Support[/b][/size][size=3][b]
[/b][/size]
This mod supports all languages that Elden Ring is localized in. Please let me know if you'd like to report translation errors in your local language.
[/size][/size]

[img]https://i.imgur.com/1IbCnYi.png[/img]
[list]
[*][size=3]Mod by Tom Clark[/size]
[*][size=3]Event scripting and VFX help from Lord Exelot[/size]
[*][size=3]Polish translation by Lumitko[/size]
[*][size=3]Simplified Chinese and Traditional Chinese translations by 兵器君 (shintheweapon)[/size]
[*][size=3]Japanese translation by Furisuke[/size]
[*][size=3]German translation by ivi[/size]
[*][size=3]Italian translation by antro97[/size]
[*][size=3][size=3]Korean translation by Eka001[/size][/size]
[*][size=3][size=3]Russian translation by kotovsky410 and NazgulXCI[/size][/size]
[*][size=3][size=3]Arabic translation by 2Pz[/size][/size]
[*][size=3][size=3]Spanish translation by Yonchi Chikito[/size][/size]
[*][size=3][size=3]Thai translation by Perapat Chainpattanakij[/size][/size]
[/list]
[size=5][b]More Mods[/b][/size]

[list]
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/6927]Armor and Weapon Dyes[/url]﻿ - Alter the color of your equipment using customizable dye options. Armor dyes also apply to transmogs.
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/5192]Glorious Merchant[/url]﻿ -  Free access to every item for quick build making. Compatible with all other mods without merging.
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/8088]Player List Overlay for Seamless Co-op[/url]﻿
[/list]

author: ap926012

name: Nightreign's Surge Sprint
summary: Simple port of Nightreign's Surge Sprint to Elden Ring
description:
Simple port of Nightreign's Surge Sprint to all weapon categories in Elden Ring. Instead of adding it as a new sprint cycle, I simply replaced the default sprint and related animations with Nightreign's faster counterparts.

Requirements: Mod Engine 2

Installation: Simply place the "chr" folder from my mod into your "mod" folder from Mod Engine 2.
Example: "steamapps\common\ELDEN RING\Game\ModEngine-2.1.0.0-win64\mod\chr"
Then start the game with launchmod_eldenring.bat

Make sure that inside of the "chr" folder you can find these files: c0000_a00_hi.anibnd.dcx, c0000_a0x.anibnd.dcx and c0000_a1x.anibnd.dcx

Please note that I'm a complete beginner in modding, the mod works nicely but you don't get the complete Nightreign experience, such as a little animation for entering surge sprint (Since Elden Ring doesn't have an intro for sprinting like Nightreign, so there's nothing to replace), and also you won't get the climbing, it's just a simple surge sprinting mod.

If you find any bugs, let me know either through the posts or the bugs tab.
author: Artorio

    Steam\steamapps\common\ELDEN RING\Game
   
    СТЪПКА 4 - Стартирате играта от modengine2_launcher.exe
   
    СТЪПКА 5 - Играта ви е на български играете.
   
    ИГРАТА СЕ ИГРАЕ НА БЪЛГАРСКИ ОФФЛАЙН !
   
    ВЛИЗАНЕТО В ИГРА ОНЛАЙН МОЖЕ ДА ДОВЕДЕ ДО БАН И Е НА ВАША ОТГОВОРНОСТ !!!

    2.Всички имена на оръжия на български(английски)
    3.Част от главното меню + някой команди

author: Ceco_g

name: Golden Age - English Localization (translation only)
summary: English translation for the Golden Age mod. Used translation software, then manually adjusted for better flow/readability.  (DOES NOT include the full mod, just the translation files)
description:
[size=2]To preface, this translation does not contain the original mod. You will need to download the mod from the original creators, or acquire it elsewhere (I won't be helping with the latter). Here's the [url=https://afdian.com/a/moke16864]official download link[/url].[/size]

I'm also not committing to updating this translation as the mod gets more content. Maybe one or two more updates, maybe not. But others can use mine as a base and carry it forward.

And if the original creators see this and want to, they can include my translations in future updates.

[size=3]如果原作者看到此内容并有意愿，可以将我的译文收录进未来的更新中。[/size]

[size=5][b][u]Process/scope:[/u][/b][/size]

I used the [url=https://soulsmods.com/mods/cmksthov0000109jpeinq0cqw/er-golden-age-rough-english-translation]ER Golden Age ROUGH English Translation[/url] as a starting point, and from there modified nearly all item descriptions and menus, and a lot of subtitles for the new content.

My main priorities for this project were:

- Make menus understandable so people know what they're selecting.
- Match character names, locations, etc. to base Elden Ring.
- Any weapon, item, spell, or boss cameos use their official translations from their respective games/media, with minor adjustments when appropriate.
- Ensure item descriptions aren't too long, so text doesn't overlap or bleed off the bottom of the screen.
- Proper capitalization and formatting.

Beyond that I adjusted item descriptions to be more in line with FromSoft's style (things get lost in translation, and thus can be a little dry). As I can't read Chinese, I used both Google Translate and DeepL to get multiple translations of all text, so I could best approximate the original creator's intent. I occasionally add an extra sentence or two of flavor text if the direct translation does not seem to complete a train of thought.

There are also some very obscure references (like to predominantly Chinese mobile games that have been discontinued for years) that most English-speaking Elden Ring players won't get, so I added some breadcrumbs for those who want to follow them. Some of these took a LOT of research to track down, so I figured I'd make it easier for everyone else.


[u][size=5][b]Installation:[/b][/size][/u]

1) Download and unzip the translation mod.

2) Navigate to your Golden Age mod folder.

3) Drop the "engus" folder included in this translation into GA\msg. Replace the folder if one already exists.

若合并失败或想尝试手动合并，可参考以下步骤

省略...
<text id="27000263">灵体</text>

最后将新的menu_dlc02-msgbnd.dcx替换原有文件即可
author: Lbuki-S

name: Multi-Instance - Run Two Copies on One PC
summary: Removes the single-instance restriction so two people can play Elden Ring on the same PC. Essential for ASTER, Sandboxie, and other multi-seat setups. Drop-in DLL proxy — no game files modified, survives updates. Includes PowerShell fallback script.
description:
[size=4][b]What This Does[/b][/size]

Elden Ring blocks multiple instances by creating a system-wide mutex (Global\SekiroMutex). Even if you run two copies under different Windows users or desktops, the second one refuses to launch.

This mod removes that restriction with a tiny DLL proxy (48KB). Two people can play on the same machine using ASTER, Sandboxie, or any multi-seat / split-desktop setup.

[size=4][b]How It Works[/b][/size]

The mod uses Windows DLL search order — the game loads our dinput8.dll from its folder before the system one. On load, it hooks the mutex creation call and returns a dummy handle instead of a real named mutex. Each instance thinks it's the only one.

[list]
[*]No game files modified
[*]Survives game updates (hooks Windows API, not game code)
[*]No scripts to run — just drop the file and launch
[*]Compatible with Seamless Coop
[/list]

[size=4][b]Installation[/b][/size]

[list=1]
[*]Download [b]dinput8.dll[/b] from the Files tab
[*]Copy it to your Elden Ring [b]Game/[/b] folder (next to eldenring.exe)
[*]Launch the game from both environments
[/list]

To find your Game folder: Steam → right-click Elden Ring → Manage → Browse Local Files → Game/

[size=4][b]Uninstallation[/b][/size]

Delete dinput8.dll from the Game/ folder. That's it.

[size=4][b]Verifying It Works[/b][/size]

Each launch writes a small DINPUT8.log in the Game folder. If you see "BLOCKED SekiroMutex", it's working.

[size=4][b]Compatibility[/b][/size]

[list]
[*][b]Seamless Coop[/b] — Compatible, no conflict
[*][b]ModEngine2[/b] — Conflict (both use dinput8.dll). Use the included kill_mutex.ps1 fallback instead
[*][b]EAC[/b] — Not compatible. Use offline or with Seamless Coop launcher
[*][b]Game updates[/b] — Unaffected, version-independent
[/list]

[size=4][b]Fallback Script[/b][/size]

If the DLL doesn't work for your setup, a PowerShell script (kill_mutex.ps1) is included:
[list=1]
[*]Launch Elden Ring (first instance)
[*]Run as Administrator: [font=Courier New]powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File kill_mutex.ps1[/font]
[*]Launch second instance
[/list]

[size=4][b]Source Code[/b][/size]

[url=https://github.com/dpcprince/elden-ring-multi-instance]GitHub[/url] — source code, build instructions, and issue tracker.
